title: Reconstructing Boolean Functions From MAGIC Logic-in-Memory Using Power Side-Channel Attacks
authors: Lorenzo Pfeifer, Rainer Leupers, Jan Moritz Joseph
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Memristors have become an important post-CMOS technology that enables new computing paradigms such as logic-in-memory (LIM) with MAGIC. With MAGIC, binary switching states are used to implement basic logic functions that offer a promising alternative to conventional FPGAs with better energy efficiency. These new processing paradigms come with new hardware security challenges. In this article, we investigate how the netlist of confidential intellectual property (IP), implemented using LIM, can be reverse-engineered. We present a power side-channel in MAGIC and show that structural information about the IP is leaked. We propose a novel recovery algorithm, which combines several side-channel attacks (SCAs) to recover the netlist of the IP. On the ISCAS85 benchmarks, the algorithm needs just 350 SCAs to identify circuits with 2000 + gates.

title: Language Supervised Multi-Camera Multi-Object Tracking
authors: Kaige Mao, Xiaopeng Hong, Xiaopeng Fan, Wangmeng Zuo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Recent multi-camera multi-object tracking (MC-MOT) algorithms are primarily trained using per-detection identity annotations, which are complicated to obtain. In contrast, labeling a language description per-object is a more natural and human-friendly way. In this paper, we explore MCMOT in a language-supervised manner (LS-MCMOT) and propose a novel approach LaVST, which performs language-to-vision weakly-supervised learning based on reliable pseudo-labels generated via tracklet-level cross-modality matching. In addition, we design an ID-aware projection self-correction mechanism to correct inaccurate image-to-ground projection in a self-supervised manner. The models trained with our approach exhibit promising performance in LS-MCMOT. Surprisingly, they perform favorably against state-of-the-art identity-supervised methods, especially in cross-dataset evaluation (with an average gain by 20.0% in IDF1), underscoring the potential of language annotations in MCMOT. Codes and language annotations will be available here.

title: Intuitive Tele-Operated Robotic Catheter System with Wrist-based Controller
authors: Satoshi Miura, Motoi Hamashima, Victor Parque, Tomoyuki Miyashita
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Catheter ablation realizes radical surgery for arrhythmia. However, it is too challenging to manipulate the catheter accurately because the catheter’s motion is quite different from human hands due to the flexible and non-haptic structure. This study presents a novel tele-operated robotic catheter system for using human wrist embodiment. We developed the wrist-based controller, and the catheter advancement robot adapting to most catheters. The catheter’s tip moves according to the motion and twist of the operator’s hand at each direction due to the robot system. We conducted two experiments as follows: First, the motion of the catheter tip was measured by three-dimensional magnetic positioning sensor. Second, five participants conducted the experiment to insert the catheter into the vessel and heart model each six times randomly by both manual hand and tele-operated system, respectively. As results, the master’s input matches the catheter’s motion with high precision in each direction, respectively (R2>0.99). Furthermore, the contact force decreased significantly (p=0.02), the achievement time decreased 45.7%, and the oxy-Hb in parietal area activated 54.8% higher in tele-operated system than manual by fNIRS. It was concluded that the tele-operated system could perform precise, quick, and intuitive operation.

title: Rotor Electromagnetic Structure Optimization for Improving the Efficiency of Permanent-Magnet Synchronous Motors in New Energy Vehicles
authors: Hucheng Chen, Peiyuan Ma, Yunchong Wang, Bensheng Xu, Hui Jing, Guo Ma, Weizhong Mo
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Rotor electromagnetic structure optimization (RESO) has emerged as a key approach for improving the efficiency of permanent-magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs), resulting in a substantial volume of published studies. This paper systematically reviews the major RESO strategies targeting efficiency improvement. The rotor electromagnetic-structure characteristics and efficiency evaluation indicators are identified. Then, according to the different optimization mechanisms of the rotor structure, RESO techniques are classified into several classic categories. The most popular optimization methods are summarized in terms of their mechanisms, characteristics, and complexities. Finally, current research limitations and future directions are highlighted. This review indicates that RESO is an important pathway for improving efficiency, but its effectiveness depends on motor topology, operating conditions, performance requirements, manufacturing feasibility, and cost constraints. It is expected that this review will provide useful guidelines for designers and researchers seeking to enhance PMSM efficiency and offer insightful prospects, indicating where extra efforts are needed.

title: Information Control in Networked Multi-User Discrete-Event Systems Using State Estimates
authors: Fei Wang, Feng Lin, Jun Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of information control in networked multi-user systems, where agents such as robots, sensors, and software entities interact via a communication network to achieve individual or shared goals. Information control involves deciding which state estimates to share or broadcast, balancing cooperation among friends and privacy from adversaries. Since each user has only partial knowledge of the system, efficient protocols for sharing relevant data to balance privacy, security, and transparency is needed. This study models multi-user systems as discrete-event systems where agents need to distinguish certain state pairs in order to perform their tasks. We systematically study and solve critical problems to address the key aspects of information control: determining the necessity of shared information, minimizing communication for security, and maximizing public information release when required. A framework that addresses private communications, public broadcasting, and adversarial dynamics, offering strategies to meet both security and transparency requirements is introduced. Solutions and algorithms are proposed to solve these problems.

title: Design and Evaluation of an Educational Virtual Agent Based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation for the Entrepreneurship Culture Course in Higher Education_supp1-3700935.pdf
authors: Isabel Guerrero Garcia
publish: N/A
abstract: The digital transformation of higher education has driven the incorporation of intelligent systems aimed at academic support and competency development, particularly in courses related to entrepreneurial training. In this context, this article aims to design and evaluate a prototype of an AI-based educational virtual agent built upon a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture, intended to support the learning process in the Entrepreneurial Culture course. The prototype was designed and developed using an Agile methodology based on SCRUM, enabling an iterative and incremental construction process centered on the pedagogical and technological requirements of the institutional context. The virtual agent was implemented in a web environment and integrates semantic retrieval mechanisms, natural language generation, and contextual knowledge management to provide personalized guidance in business plan development and academic inquiry resolution. To evaluate the prototype’s quality, black-box functional testing and the System Usability Scale (SUS) were applied in accordance with the criteria established in the ISO/IEC 25010 standard. This analysis enabled assessment of the system’s performance in terms of usability, functionality, efficiency, and reliability from the end-user perspective. The results indicate an average SUS score of 72.96, reflecting an acceptable level of usability according to international software quality standards for educational systems.

title: PCPSI: Efficient Unbalanced Private Set Intersection Using Homomorphic Encryption with Plaintext–Ciphertext Multiplication
authors: Dongju Lee, Sungyeon Lee, Youyeon Joo, Kevin Nam, Yunheung Paek
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Private Set Intersection (PSI) allows two parties to compute the intersection of their datasets without revealing anything beyond the result. Many real-world applications arise in unbalanced settings, where a small client set is compared against a much larger server database. Homomorphic encryption (HE)–based PSI protocols provide strong privacy and a non-interactive workflow, but existing designs rely on compressing comparison results into a single ciphertext, requiring costly ciphertext–ciphertext multiplications. As the server dataset grows, this leads to deeper circuits, larger parameters, and substantial overhead. We present PCPSI, a new HE-based PSI protocol that replaces product-based compression with a depth-1 plaintext–ciphertext comparison strategy, eliminating ciphertext–ciphertext multiplications altogether. This shallow circuit scales efficiently without bootstrapping and enables a two-dimensional batching technique that reduces communication cost and increases parallelism. To further improve accuracy, PCPSI isolates computations per hash function and adaptively selects the number of hash functions based on dataset characteristics. Empirical evaluation under the semi-honest model shows that PCPSI outperforms prior HE-based PSI protocols across both large and small client sets. For large client sets, PCPSI achieves substantial speedups over strong HE-based PSI baselines, reaching up to 8.9× improvement under real (actual) cloud instance evaluation environments.

title: Adaptive Model-Free Predictive Current Control for SPMSM Drives Using Algebraic Estimator: Enhanced High-Frequency Disturbance Rejection
authors: Yang Shen, Zhenxiao Yin, Xueyan Wang, Fobao Zhou, Yujia Zhang, Hang Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Deadbeat predictive current control (DPCC) is widely used in permanent magnet synchronous motor current control for fast dynamic response and strong current tracking, but its predictive model accuracy relies on accurate motor parameters. Model-free predictive current control (MFPCC) based on algebraic estimators (AE) exhibits excellent parameter robustness and disturbance rejection capability, serving as a data-driven method with parameter robustness that relies solely on input-output data. However, disturbances caused by proportional factor mismatch and high-frequency disturbances still limit its performance. To fully exploit the rich historical data from the AE and further enhance the disturbance rejection performance, an AE-based adaptive model-free predictive current control (AMF-AE) for SPMSM drives is proposed in this paper, which is inherently constructed with two tiny neural networks (NNs) to address both parameter mismatch and high-frequency disturbances. First, a tiny neural network for adaptive proportional factor is proposed to suppress disturbances caused by proportional factor mismatch. Second, another tiny neural network for the suppression of high-frequency disturbances is constructed, which is independent from specific motor or inverter parameters. Experimental results on a 1 kW surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous motor (SPMSM) test bench verify the effectiveness of the proposed strategy. When there is a parameter mismatch with the proportional factor set to twice its nominal optimal value <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha _{o}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, the proposed method achieves a 26.26% reduction in current total harmonic distortion (THD) compared with the conventional AE-based MFPCC under rated load conditions.

title: LIME-Based Analysis of Adversarial Feature Manipulation in Cloud Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Humberto Goncalves, Zakaria Alomari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Cloud-based intrusion detection systems face persistent threats from adversarial attacks that introduce imperceptible perturbations to malicious traffic, enabling evasion and compromising network security. While ML-based detection systems demonstrate strong performance under normal conditions, their vulnerability to adversarial manipulation and the mechanisms behind such evasion remain insufficiently understood. This work investigates how adversarial perturbations manipulate feature importance in cloud-based intrusion detection using XGBoost on the CIC-IDS2017 dataset. HopSkipJump attacks were applied to generate adversarial DoS samples, followed by LIME-based interpretability analysis to reveal manipulation patterns. Results show that adversarial attacks strategically suppress DoS-indicative features such as min_seg_size_forward, flow_packets/s, and init_win_bytes_forward by up to 32%, while elevating benign-resembling attributes including destination_port, bwd_packet_length_max, and bwd_header_length. Analysis of misclassified samples reveals non-uniform adversarial strategies that divert model attention from core intrusion indicators through targeted feature weight redistribution. This exploratory study offers actionable insights into vulnerable features and evasion patterns, supporting the development of more robust cloud-based intrusion detection systems that are resilient to adversarial manipulation.

title: An AI-Driven Optimization Framework for Sustainable E-Waste Management and Resource Utilization
authors: Priyanka Chauhan, Deepak Kumar Adhana, Mayank Saxena
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: In the given paper, an AI-based optimization model of sustainable e-waste management and resource use is presented. The research falls in the fields of circular economy engineering, intelligent decision analytics, and intelligent urban sustainability systems. The framework creates a spatio-temporal digital twin of e-waste flows, which incorporates graph neural prediction of waste generation, deep reinforcement learning prediction of adaptive collection and routing, and multi-objective evolutionary optimization prediction of processing and material recovery. Lifecycle sustainability assessment is incorporated to pursue, all at the same time, cost, environmental impact, and circularity goals. Experimental assessment on the city scale shows that there are very high increments in performances relative to traditional methods that depend on rules to use in collection, logistics systems relying on optimization and, hybrid AI logistics systems. The efficiency of the proposed framework is 93% material recovery efficiency, 23.1% reduction on greenhouse gases, 25.6% operational costs efficiency and 27.4% reduction on waste which is not controlled. Further measurements ensure better routing effectiveness, increased service completion, greater prediction performance, increased processing throughput, greater circularity gain, and greater stability of policy compliance. These findings prove the potential of the framework to convert e-waste ecosystems into smart, resilient, and sustainability-oriented resource infrastructures of the contemporary smart cities.

title: Deepfake Video Forensics Using ResNeXt–LSTM Visual Modeling and DSP-Enhanced Audio Spectrogram Analysis
authors: Tanish Lal O, Sijo Devassy V J, P. Deepa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The technology for creating deepfakes has advanced rapidly, and this allows for realistic modifications to both audio and video. Many current forensic systems only use frame-based visual artefacts, while modern deepfake pipelines use temporal smoothing, high-quality generative models, and synthetic voice, which makes unimodal detectors less dependable. This research introduces a multimodal deepfake detection framework that integrates a ResNeXt50 video feature extractor, an Long Short Term Memory (LSTM)-based temporal analysis module, and a Digital Signal Processing (DSP)-enhanced audio spectrogram classifier. Visual characteristics find differences in faces, temporal modelling finds differences between frames, and the audio stream finds problems in synthetic speech, like odd harmonic patterns and smoothed spectrum energy. A fusion module takes predictions from all branches and merges them to give a single authenticity score. Experiments show that using both DSP-based audio cues and temporal modelling makes things far more stable than only using video. The system is set up using a Django web interface that is built for automatic real-time deepfake verification.

title: Investigating Genomic Insights and Smart Biomaterial–Based Biosensing Using Data Science Approaches for Advancing Precision Oncology Treatments
authors: Sadaf A. Ansari, Irfan Altaf Ansari, Shabina Anjum, Nikhil Mangrulkar, Hrushikesh Madhukar Panchabudhe, Prashant Khobragade
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Cancer is a very heterogeneous disease which is caused by complicated genomic changes that restrict the use of the traditional one-size-fits-all therapy. In precision oncology, the solution to this dilemma is to design treatment plans based on the unique molecular characteristics of each patient; but the large scale, multidimensional, and heterogeneous nature of next-generation sequencing data complicates accurately analyzing and converting this information into clinical practice with only traditional statistical methods. The objective of the study was to explore how integrated methods of data science and smart biomaterial-based bio sensing can support precision oncology, in terms of better biomarker discovery, predicting treatment-response, and providing real-time clinical decision support. It suggested an inclusive model that brings together genomic data preprocessing, statistical variant modelling, machine learning and deep learning based prediction and intelligent biomaterial bio sensing to continuously monitor the tumor. Its methodology included rigorous genomic data cleaning, probabilistic variant discovery, multi-omics integration of features, and predictive modeling with support vector machines, random forests, convolutional neural networks, and transformer architectures, wearable, and implantable biosensors to get longitudinal data on biomarkers. It was shown that deep learning models performed better than classical in genomic-based cancer prediction with an accuracy of up to 96.1 and an F1-score of 95.1. Predictive treatment outcome modeling yielded higher treatment response prediction rates of more than 94 % on breast cancer datasets.

title: Deep Learning-Based Intelligent Intrusion Detection System For The Internet Of Medical Things
authors: B. Rama Subba Reddy, Munagala Kotthakapa Sarath Kumar Reddy, Ponnaganti Baba Ganesh, Raghavarapu Naga Sai Rusheek, Soleti Venkatasatyakarthik
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The project mentioned as the HIDS-IoMT A Deep Learning-Based Intelligent Intrusion Detection System of the Internet of medical Things explains a safe and intelligent approach of securing IoMT against the new cyber threats. In this system, machine learning frameworks are trained on a highly performing level i.e. Random Forest, Extra Trees, Decision Tree, MLP, and LSTM classifiers to study the main characteristics of the network flow i.e. flow duration, length of packets, idle time and transmission rate. The models are trained in a manner that they may distinguish normal and abnormal traffic patterns such that it is able to adequately detect potential attacks in real time. The web page is developed on a Flask web framework with MySQL database integration in such a way that, users can easily register, upload network traffic data, choose prediction model and see the result. Once the abnormal patterns have been detected, the system will give actionable security insights and compliance recommendations, including the initiation of an investigation or ongoing monitoring. The platform is developed to have a simple interface, enabling even the non-technical users to communicate with highly developed AI-driven tools. This system can be used to make medical networks resilient by automating threat monitoring and providing contextual solutions, which can be applied to the overall critical infrastructure protection.

title: A Study on Mechanical Behaviour of Ternary Blended Geopolymer Concrete
authors: Ayushi, Pradeep Kumar Goyal, Amit Kumar Srivastava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The building sector plays a major part in economic expansion; however, it also has a serious environmental footprint, basically due to the significant release of carbon dioxide associated with cement manufacture, which is the most widely used material over many years, has necessitated the exploration of greener material options with less of an impact on the environment. These alternative materials include Geopolymer Concrete, which are produced by alkaline activation of materials rich in silica and alumina. Geopolymer Concrete, which incorporates Alccofine, Ground Granulated Blast Furnace Slag along with Fly Ash, has been studied less which limits its use in practical settings. This research delves into the creation of six mixes of Ternary Blended Geopolymer Concrete featuring various combinations of Alccofine, Ground Granulated Blast Furnace Slag and Fly Ash, to assess the mechanical behaviour and to compare all the properties with conventional concrete. Samples underwent tests for compressive, split tensile and flexural strength after 7 and 28 days of curing. The findings indicates that the mix GPC 5 shows highest compressive strength as 40.44 N/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and 53.19 N/mm2, split tensile strength as 5.23 N/mm2 and 6.02 N/mm2 and flexural strength as 4.46 N/mm2 and 6.46 N/mm2 at 7 and 28 days age respectively.

title: RE-FIDS: Robust and Explainable Federated Learning-based Intrusion Detection System
authors: Pranav Prajapati, Atharva Karambe, Ruchi Kharab, Nilesh Mane, Parikshit Singh, Sunita Dhavale
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Modern cyber-threats continue to evolve across distributed infrastructures where centralized Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) face critical challenges related to data privacy, scalability, and localized reliability. Federated Learning (FL) offers a decentralized alternative for collaborative model training; however, traditional FL-IDS frameworks remain vulnerable to Non-IID data distributions, gradient poisoning attacks, and a lack of decision-making transparency. To address these limitations, we propose RE-FIDS, a Robust and Explainable Federated Intrusion Detection System. Our methodology integrates Dirichlet-based heterogeneous data modeling with a multi-stage Hybrid Aggregator that combines Krum filtering and Trimmed Mean smoothing to neutralize adversarial updates. Furthermore, we incorporate a SHAP-based interpretability layer to provide feature-level transparency. Empirical evaluations on the CIC-IDS2017 and UNSW-NB15 datasets demonstrate that RE-FIDS significantly outperforms FedAvg and standalone robust aggregators under 20% poisoning pressure. The framework achieves stable convergence and superior F1-scores, effectively preserving model integrity in hostile environments. This study provides a scalable solution for secure, interpretable network monitoring, bridging the gap between high-performance anomaly detection and operational trust in decentralized security operations centers.

title: Explainable AI-Driven Intrusion Detection System Using Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Secure Network Infrastructure
authors: B. Vijay, Chandra J, Nagendra N, Prabhakaran M V, Rajiv S, Parul
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The network security threats have not been fixed hence the need to adopt more sophisticated intrusion detectors (IDS) that in addition to being highly accurate, should offer decision-making processes that are understandable. Conventional IDS systems are not typically transparent and cannot readily comprehend how attacks are processed by the system and the reaction by the security analysts. To make the integrated deep learning architecture, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, and explainable AI models, such as SHAP (SHapley Additive explanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations), easier to understand, this paper proposes to use an integrated deep learning architecture. The system was evaluated using NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 datasets through comprehensive preprocessing, feature selection, and cross-validation techniques. The hybrid model proposed has a higher accuracy of detecting 98.7 and precision of 97.3 and a recall rate of 96.8 and an F1-score of 97.05, which is 12-15 superior to the traditional machine learning models, however, the model possesses a high explainability (score 89) confidence rate. The integration of explainable AI with hybrid deep learning models significantly enhances both detection performance and interpretability, providing security professionals with actionable insights for proactive threat mitigation.

title: BrainArcNet: Attention-Enhanced InceptionResNetV2 with ArcFace for Accurate Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Scans
authors: Snega S, Manikandan J, Shalom Judi Pushpam L
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Brain tumors are among the more fatal neurological problems, where early and accurate diagnosis is a major factor in the success of the treatment. MRI is the most suitable method for tumor detection because of its high-resolution soft-tissue contrast. Still, manual interpretation of MRI scans is a source of inter-observer variability and requires a great deal of expertise. In order to alleviate these issues, we present a deep learning framework BrainArcNet that fuses InceptionResNetV2 as the backbone network merged with a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for the additional feature refinement and an ArcFace classification head to facilitate the angular margin-based separability in the embedding space. The method starts with the carefully planned preprocessing of brain MRIs that include Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), gamma correction, and z-score normalization. These steps help in enhancing contrast and standardizing intensity distributions. The backbone network gets the different-level features from the input images, and CBAM helps the network to selectively focus on the tumor-relevant spatial and channel information. The extracted embeddings are projected into a discriminative hypersphere using ArcFace, leading to improved inter-class margins and intra-class compactness compared to conventional softmax classifiers. Experiments were conducted on the Kaggle Brain Tumor MRI Dataset, which includes 7023 T1-weighted contrast-enhanced images across four types they are glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and no tumor. Preliminary results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves ~90– 96% classification accuracy, which is a realistic and achievable performance range for beginner-level implementations when using transfer learning and moderate computational resources. With further finetuning, data augmentation, and hyperparameter optimization, the model has the potential to give more accuracy.

title: Dynamic Data-Driven Framework for Forecasting Employment Trends in the AI Era
authors: Mayank Singh Negi, Vansh Raheja, Ketan Kumar, Meena Chaudhary, Gunjan Chandwani, Narender Gautam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping global labor markets by automating repetitive tasks while creating new opportunities in data-driven and technology-intensive sectors. This paper presents a Dynamic Data-Driven Employment Forecasting Model (DDEFM) that predicts employment trends using a hybrid machine learning framework integrating time-series forecasting, natural language processing, and sectoral analysis. The model combines real-world labor datasets, AI adoption metrics, and investment indicators to evaluate employment volatility across sectors. The research contributes a technical methodology to forecast job creation and decline across industries such as healthcare, IT, education, and green energy. Experimental results demonstrate the model’s ability to reduce prediction error by 12–18% compared to baseline statistical models. This study provides a scalable framework for policymakers and industries to plan workforce development in the AI era.

title: Deep Learning-based Smart Traffic Signal Control
authors: T Thilagam, Siva Rama Lingham N, T Praveen Kumar, Ritish Patro
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The Indian cities have very complicated traffic control processes because of high density of roads, the vehicles mixed in terms of their types, unpredictable drivers, and the varying amount of traffic at different times of the day. The traditional fixed-time or sensor-based traffic systems are not able to respond to such dynamic conditions, and it results in the increase of congestion and waiting time. To solve this problem, the proposed research suggests an intelligent traffic control system that combines both an LSTM network to predict short-term traffic and a Q-learning system to optimize the signal phases in real-time. LSTM model predicts the impending traffic density and traffic flow in the near future according to the historical records, whereas the Q-learning optimizes signal timing to reduce delays and traffic congestion. The Kaggle Urban Traffic Dataset and IEEE Indian Vehicle Dataset are used to train the model and capture the nature of the Indian traffic. The application of performance evaluation through the SUMO simulator shows that there is a 32 percent decrease in the average vehicle waiting time and a 19 percent increase in efficiency of the traffic flow. Also, the system has the priority of emergency vehicles as it allows free circulation of intersection clearance without affecting the safety. The proposed deep learning-based framework can be applied to the field of urban mobility to the benefit of the Indian urban environment, by predicting congestion proactively and optimizing signals dynamically, to enhance the mobility of the Indian population in the city.

title: Using Generative AI to Improve Cities, Energy, Farming, and Healthcare for the Environment
authors: Jitendra Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Generative AI is changing sustainability initiatives in big ways by leveraging advanced machine learning frameworks like GANs, transformer-based models, and diffusion networks. This paper offers an exhaustive examination of generative AI applications across four principal sectors: smart cities, energy systems, agriculture, and healthcare. We show that generative AI applications lead to: (1) a 23–45% improvement in optimizing city traffic, (2) a 15–30% improvement in the accuracy of predicting renewable energy, (3) an 18–35% increase in crop production forecasts, and (4) a 40–60% decrease in the time it takes to find new drugs. This research paper looks at 47 important deployments and presents a unified Generative Sustainability Framework (UGSF) that brings together smart cities, energy, agriculture, and healthcare. It also enables an easy and flexible approach to choose case studies based on certain parameters.

title: An Efficient Tuberculosis Detection from Chest X-ray Images using Lightweight Deep Learning Models
authors: K.V.D.S. Santhosh, Jayadeep Pakala, Songa Manikumar, K.V Mounika, A.Lakshmanarao, P.Chandra Sekhar Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Tuberculosis (TB) remains a major global health concern, and chest X-ray imaging is widely used for large-scale TB screening due to its accessibility and low cost. However, manual interpretation of chest X-rays is time-consuming and subject to observer variability, motivating the need for reliable automated detection systems. This paper presents a progressive learning framework for automated tuberculosis detection from chest X-ray images, systematically transitioning from classical machine learning to deep and transfer learning paradigms under a unified experimental setup. Initially, handcrafted texture features are classified using conventional machine learning models to establish baseline performance, followed by an artificial neural network to capture nonlinear feature relationships. A custom convolutional neural network is then employed to learn spatial representations directly from images. Finally, a proposed lightweight transfer learning framework utilizing MobileNetV2, ShuffleNetV2, and EfficientNetB0 is introduced to achieve higher accuracy with reduced computational complexity. Experimental results on a publicly available tuberculosis chest X-ray dataset demonstrate consistent performance improvements across learning stages, with lightweight transfer learning models achieving the best balance between diagnostic accuracy and efficiency. The proposed framework offers a practical and effective solution for automated TB screening, particularly suitable for deployment in resource-constrained healthcare environments.

title: Literature Review on Neural Networks based Security in Multimedia with Application and Emerging Trend
authors: Yashaswini K, Sharath M N
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Neural networks have emerged as a transformative tool in multimedia security, addressing the increasing challenges posed by complex and dynamic threats to digital media. In the digital age, the dissemination of images and videos on social media has emerged as a prevalent privacy concern. It has been challenging to create safe and effective Image steganography, image cryptography, and image authentication to guard against the eavesdropping and destruction of sensitive information. Deep learning offers a resolution for digital image security. This survey provides a comprehensive review of neural network-based techniques applied to secure multimedia content, focusing on applications such as watermarking, steganography, biometric authentication, and encryption. It explores how neural networks, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), and Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), enhance the robustness of multimedia security systems by learning intricate patterns, modeling non-linear relationships, and adapting to evolving attacks. The survey highlights the advantages of neural networks in areas like real-time threat detection, automated feature extraction, and privacy-preserving methods. Furthermore, emerging trends such as adversarial training, multimedia cryptography, and deep fake detection are discussed, along with their potential to redefine the future of multimedia security. The review concludes by identifying open research challenges, particularly in the scalability, robustness, and ethical use of neural network models in multimedia applications.

title: OkraSqueezeNet: A Lightweight CNN with Bayesian Optimization for Real-Time Ladies Finger Leaf Disease Detection
authors: R. Renugadevi, Divyasri M, Rithikaa K, Siby R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Ladies finger (Abelmoschus esculentus), also name as okra or bhindi, is one important vegetable crop what grow mostly in tropical and subtropical place like India. Farmers is facing big loss many times because of leaf disease cause by fungal, bacterial, and viral thing. This disease not easy to see in starting time, and old way of checking by hand is slow, not correct, and need expert person which many small farmers not have. This work makes OkraSqueezeNet, a light weight brain like network (CNN) for find six common diseases in ladies finger leaf, train with Bayesian setting and can work on phone picture without any man help. A new set of picture name Ladies Finger Leaf Disease (LFLD), having 1,700 farm taken photo in seven group (Good, Alternaria Leaf Spot, Cercospora Leaf Spot, Downy Mildew, Leaf Curly Virus, Phyllosticta Leaf Spot, and Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus), was get from real farm in India. Trick with picture like turn, flip, blur, cut, and light change was use on teach data. The model gets 97.14% right answer with good score in all measure, do better than EfficientNetB0, MobileNetV3, ResNet, InceptionV3, and VGG16. With size only 4.82 MB, it good for put in mobile. An app make with Flutter was also build for farmer to check disease right in field in real time.

title: BIM–AI Integration for Sustainable Urban Planning in Smart Cities: Framework, Challenges, and Future Trends
authors: Noorsan Abu Ja’far, Mohammad Alauthman, Amer Al-Jokhadar, Ali Al Maqusi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Progress toward Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 11 depends on planning processes that connect building-level performance to city-level dynamics. Building Information Modelling (BIM) provides semantically rich, quantitative descriptions of assets, yet these models are commonly retained within project repositories and are not systematically used in municipal workflows. Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and 3D city models contribute spatial context at urban scale, but they often lack the granularity required to evaluate embodied carbon, energy demand, accessibility, and resilience at the level of individual buildings. Recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI)—including graph learning, large language models, and neuro-symbolic approaches—make it increasingly feasible to integrate these sources and implement urban digital twins that can support policy formulation and design evaluation. This paper presents a governance-aware conceptual framework that integrates BIM, GIS/CityGML, and urban sensing through a layered pipeline for data ingestion, semantic integration, AI analytics, and SDG-aligned decision support. In lieu of a prototype, the paper reviews work reported during 2021–2025 and critically examines persistent barriers and emerging directions, including semantic interoperability, privacy-preserving data governance, generative urban design, and automated SDG compliance checking. Because data availability varies widely across cities, we also discuss data-readiness tiers and fallback strategies for contexts where BIM, IoT streams, or semantic 3D city models are incomplete, fragmented, or unavailable.

title: An AI-Driven Cyber-Physical Control System for Real-Time Environmental Monitoring and Disaster Management
authors: Sorabh Lakhanpal, Navneet Kumar Rajpoot, Poornima Ediga, Manjula G, Dilraj Preet Kaur, Ashish Kumar Singhal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: This paper presents a proposed AI-based Cyber-Physical Control System (AI-CPCS), which is used to monitor the environment and manage disasters in heterogeneous sensing infrastructures in real time. It will be designed with the help of multimodal IoT sensors, edge-intelligent computing nodes, and a blockchain-secured data integrity layer to provide credible and low-latency decision streams. The hybrid deep learning pipeline that uses spatiotemporal convolutional encoders and sequence predictors based on transformers are constructed to approximate environmental dynamics and identify abnormally deviant patterns emphasizing the signs of floods, landslides, and atmospheric instabilities. Distributed control loop uses reinforcement learning by automatically adjusting resource allocation, communication bandwidth, and actuation feedback as the environmental loads change. The system has a priority-based message routing protocol that minimizes the end-to-end delay with resilience to node failure. Synthetic testing of a multi-hazard benchmark shows a vastly better predictive performance, event capture ability, and control responsiveness than traditional CPS models. The suggested AI-CPCS creates a scalable base of the autonomous disaster mitigation systems of the next generation.

title: Dual-Path Motion Encoding for Robust Violence Detection
authors: Abderrahim Ouza, Mohamed El Ghmary, Ali Choukri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In this paper we tackle the problem of violent event detection in realistic crowd scenes, which combine small scale individual actions with large scale group movement. We propose a dual-path motion encoding framework that jointly models fine-grained human micro-motions and global crowd dynamics. The local branch captures spatiotemporal human-centric features using 3D convolutions, while the global branch models scene-level temporal evolution through a bidirectional recurrent encoder. A stable training strategy combining warm-up scheduling and adaptive learning rate reduction ensures reliable convergence. Experimental results on the Real-Life Violence Situations dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms CNN, LSTM, and 3D CNN baselines in terms of accuracy and F1score. Visualization analysis confirms that the two branches learn complementary motion patterns. The method is suitable for realtime surveillance applications.

title: Cloud-based Intelligent Brain Tumor Detection and Segmentation Framework using Hybrid Deep Representation Learning Approach
authors: K. Anitha, T. Priyaradhikadevi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: A brain tumor (BT) is an abnormal development in the brain, which destroys its functions and poses a major danger to human lifespan because of weakened neurons. Earlier recognition and identification of BTs are more important for averting health-related problems and sustaining better health conditions. Popular technologies like artificial intelligence and cloud computing are gradually making their way into the healthcare industry, enabling superior decision-making for radiologists. The technology of cloud computing aids in automatically analyzing BTs. Besides, recent progress in deep learning systems has exposed vast potential in image classification and segmentation for BT. This paper presents an Intelligent Cloud-Based Brain Tumor Detection and Segmentation Framework using a Hybrid Deep Learning Model (CBTDS-HDL). The major intention is to develop an advanced cloud-enabled system that can accurately perceive and visualize BTs from medical imaging. To perform that, the proposed CBTDS-HDL model initially pre-processes the input image using a Wiener filter (WF) for noise removal and CLAHE for contrast enhancement (CE) to enhance the image clarity and quality. For tumor segmentation, an R2UNet model optimized using the RMSProp optimizer is utilized to precisely determine tumor boundaries. Following that, the multi-scale vision transformer model that uses a base (B16) is employed for feature extraction to capture detailed information from segmented images. Finally, the tumor detection is carried out using a temporal convolutional bi-directional long short-term memory optimized with RMSProp, which enables accurate tumor classification. To show an improved performance of the proposed CBTDS-HDL method, a complete experimentation study is made. The comparative outcomes stated the improved performances of the CBTDS-HDL method with maximum accuracy of 96.65%.

title: Optimized Microenvironments for Battery Cell Production: Space-Efficient Handling Systems and Economic Feasibility
authors: Johannes Scholz, Jan-Niklas Sturm, Florian Kößler, Jürgen Fleischer
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The increasing demand for battery cells, driven by market volatility and rapid technological advancements, necessitates agile and energy-efficient production strategies. Traditional large-scale dry rooms, which are essential for moisture-sensitive battery manufacturing, pose significant challenges due to their high energy consumption and limited adaptability. As an alternative, microenvironments as small dry rooms designed as robot cells, offer a more flexible and efficient solution by creating localized, controlled atmospheres that minimize energy usage while maintaining strict environmental conditions. A key advantage of microenvironments is the elimination of human operators within these enclosed spaces, requiring fully automated handling processes. However, standard industrial robots, often employed for automation, demand substantial space, leading to an increase in the overall volume of the microenvironment and, consequently, higher operational costs. To address this limitation, the microenvironment is analysed systematically to identify the interfaces and functions of the microenvironment. Based on the functional description, a space-optimized handling system is developed that enables material manipulation while minimizing the required footprint. This study explores the design and implementation of such a system, using a dry coating process as a practical application example. Beyond the technical development, the economic viability of microenvironments and the proposed handling system is assessed. By evaluating energy savings, material efficiency, and operational flexibility, the study highlights the potential of microenvironments to enhance the sustainability and competitiveness of battery cell production. The findings suggest that integrating compact, automated handling solutions within microenvironments can significantly reduce costs and improve adaptability to fluctuating market conditions. The paper presents the potential of an optimized handling system inside microenvironments regarding the required space. Furthermore, a standardized functional description of microenvironments is given as a basis for the design of optimized microenvironments.

title: Classification of Edible and Poisonous Mushrooms Using Lightweight Deep Learning Models on Mobile Devices
authors: Arif Sefa Bölükbaşı, Elif Eroğlu, Bora Tok, Enes Ayan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Edible wild mushrooms are valued for their nutritional and health benefits. However, distinguishing edible species from poisonous ones remains challenging due to their strong visual similarities and high species diversity. This can lead to serious health risks in cases of misidentification. This study proposes a lightweight, mobile compatible, deep learning based system that can classify mushrooms in real time and offline using image data. The system employs transfer learning with pre-trained convolutional neural network architectures to achieve high performance with low computational cost. Experimental results show that EfficientNet-based models obtained better classification performance. EfficientNetV2S achieved the best overall results: an accuracy of 0.89, a precision of 0.91, a recall of 0.87, and an F-score of 0.89. Its model size was 79.2 MB. For mobile deployment, EfficientNetB2 was a more compact alternative, achieving an accuracy of 0.84, a precision of 0.81, a recall of 0.88, and an F-score of 0.84 with a model size of 31 MB. The selected model was integrated into a Kotlin-based mobile application that operates without an internet connection. This application enables fast and reliable inference on low-end mobile devices, offering a practical solution for identifying mushrooms and preventing poisoning in the real world.

title: Intracavity Temperature Sensor Based on Phase-Shifted Fiber Bragg Grating in a Fiber Laser
authors: Boqiang Lin, Yihua He, Renan Xu, Weihao Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: This paper proposes and validates a temperature sensing system based on phase-shifted fiber Bragg grating (PSFBG) for monitoring minor temperature changes within the range of $20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. In the experiment, the PSFBG was integrated as both a filtering and sensing element inside the fiber laser cavity. As the ambient temperature changed, the laser’s output wavelength exhibited linear drift. The experimental results show that the sensing system has a sensitivity of $8 \mathrm{pm} /{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Additionally, the output signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of the laser cavity exceeded 50 dB, and the laser linewidth was less than 0.1 nm. This sensor demonstrates promising potential for precise temperature monitoring in biomedical applications.

title: Rotor load decoupling method based on geometric modal projection
authors: Feng Cao, Lei Liang, Shu Dai, Weijian Zhong, Tianmingxuan Cao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: To address modal decoupling failure and crosstalk caused by sensor installation deviations in rotor load measurement, a robust identification method based on geometric modal projection and stiffness correction is proposed. Unlike traditional methods, this approach eliminates the reliance on perfect physical symmetry by utilizing redundant information from multiple sensors to construct an observation matrix in modal space. Combined with load calibration, geometric installation deviations are automatically absorbed into an equivalent stiffness matrix. Simulation results for a nonlinear pre-twisted blade show that under a 50 mm chordwise deviation, the lead-lag load RMSE of the traditional bridge method reaches 578.47 Nm, while the proposed method maintains an RMSE of only 1.03 Nm. The identification error for sectional principal stiffness is only $\mathbf{0. 7 \%}$.

title: CityPulse 360 – AI-Powered Real-Time Urban Happiness and Sentiment Analysis
authors: Sai Charan Naidu P, J Naveen Krishna, A Akash, J Vijayarani
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rapidly growing urbanization in India has introduced a demand of intelligent tools that will be used to monitor the well-being of the city in real time. Traditional urban performance indicators utilize stagnant or lagging data and can be unable to reflect the present public feeling. CityPulse 360 brings in an AI-based system merging social media mood, environmental factors, price-of-living information, and infrastructure indicators and produce a real-time mood of happiness in Indian cities. The system uses Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) to process live streams in the format of weather and air-quality data provided by OpenWeatherMap API, sentiment-based data provided by Twitter, Reddit, and YouTube, and structured data in the form of safety, healthcare, affordability. These multimodal inputs are aggregated into composite scores and represented with the help of interactive Power BI dashboard. In this paper, the architecture of the system, data acquisition, preprocessing, feature engineering, and dashboard design will be presented to monitor the urban happiness. The framework provides assistance to policy makers, researchers and citizens by providing an ever-present and multi-dimensional picture of urban well-being.

title: Time-Series Forecasting of Cloud Resource Utilization Using Hybrid LSTM Models
authors: Vijayachandar Sanikal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Cloud resource utilization time-series forecasting is highly important in smart workload management and cost-effective cloud operations. Broadly speaking in the field of cloud intelligence and predictive computing, the accurate forecasting has been created useful in proactive scaffolding, SLA assurance, and energy-conscientious decision-making. The proposed work presents a new Hybrid LSTM forecasting model that combines stacked temporal learning, attentive refinements with a residual correction unit to increase the prediction strength. This model is successful in capturing long-range temporal correlated effects, dominant utilization patterns, and intra does not force the cumulative error drift in the dynamic workloads. The experiments that have been carried out using actual cloud workload traces prove that the suggested framework performs significantly better as compared to ARIMA, GRU, basic LSTM, and CNN Hybrids of LSTM. The model records significant improvements in performance, such as: reduction in RMSE, MAE, and MAPE; extending R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, forecast stability, reducing inference latency and robustness in the face of noisy fluctuations in workload. Altogether, the suggested Hybrid LSTM based forecasting model offers a more credible, accurate and deployable solution to the problem of cloud utilization prediction, supporting intelligent autoscaling, performance optimization and sustainable cloud resource management.

title: A Radar-Based Contactless Stethoscope for Phonocardiogram Reconstruction
authors: Simone Pasquariello, Giulio Montanari, Marco Mura, Elia Vignoli, Giorgio Guerzoni, Pasquale Di Viesti, Giorgio Matteo Vitetta
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Continuous monitoring of cardiac activity is often required in clinical scenarios in which wearable sensors cannot be employed (e.g., during magnetic resonance imaging or computerized axial tomography). In this manuscript, the development of a contactless stethoscope based on a multiple-input multipleoutput frequency modulated continuous wave radar is illustrated. The proposed processing chain combines a deterministic preprocessing stage with a convolutional neural network trained on a dataset of real-world measurement. Our results, generated for a given test set, show that the phonocardiogram reconstructed from radar data exhibits only minor deviations from that provided by a reference digital stethoscope.

title: IAC-TecMed Database for Infrared Thermography-Based Diabetic Foot Monitoring
authors: Badiaa Ait Ahmed, Pilar Montañés-Rodríguez, Otman Aghzout, Juan Ruiz-Alzola
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This publication presents a publicly available subset of a database created to assess diabetic foot anomalies using infrared thermography, aimed at enhancing early detection of complications in diabetic patients. The initial study involved 22 healthy subjects (9 females and 13 males) to develop a standard morphological model for evaluating temperature asymmetry in the plantar aspects of both feet. The database comprises thermographic and RGB-D images captured at four time points (T0, T5, T10, T15) under controlled conditions (temperature: $\mathbf{2 5}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}} \mathbf{C}$, constant luminosity, and humidity), resulting in a longitudinal dataset that reflects natural thermal variations. This methodology is clinically relevant as it identifies thermal anomalies that may prevent complications in diabetic foot. Images were subjected to segmentation masks, manually delineated by three experienced researchers, and validated via the STAPLE algorithm to establish a ground truth. Statistical analyses utilized Wilcoxon’s matched-pairs test, with adjustments for multiple comparisons via Hochberg and false discovery rate methods, maintaining a significance level of 5%. These analyses revealed significant temperature variation areas, reinforcing the validity of the results. From the cohort, 19 participants (7 females and 12 males) consented to share their data for research. This subdataset (IAC-TecMed database, hereafter IAC-TMBD) is a valuable resource for exploring thermal patterns and their implications in foot disorder monitoring. This subset retains the integrity and scope of the original data, providing a resource for further investigation into temperature patterns and their implications for early detection and monitoring of foot disorders.

title: Non-Invasive Diagnosis of Circulating Blood or Cardiac Inflammatory Diseases Through Microwave Technology
authors: Antonio Curcio, Giovanni Lopes, Sandra Costanzo
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The adoption of microwave technology is proposed in the present work to perform the identification and distinction of different inflammation states associated to cardiac diseases, such as myocarditis, endocarditis, or pericarditis. A well distinguished reflection response is observed when performing non-invasive microwave sensor measurements on plasma samples, thus revealing a correlation between the pathological states and the variation in the dielectric properties of plasma (dielectric constant and conductivity). Experimental results obtained from a measurement campaign conducted on 60 critically ill patients are discussed to validate the proposed approach.

title: Evaluating Data Poisoning Resilience of Large Language Models for Cybersecurity Applications
authors: Navjot Kaur, Nityanand Kumar, Shivangi Shekhar, Ch. Rajendra Prasad, Mohit Garg, Meenakshi Munjal
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are also being integrated in cybersecurity processes like the analysis of cyber threat intelligence (CTI), security log triage, and interpretation of alerts. Nonetheless, they are susceptible to data poisoning attacks, where adversarial constructed or manipulated training data silently weakens downstream security decision making. Although there is a rising interest in 2022-2025, the current analyses of the strength of LLM poisons are still disjointed, frequently based on generic NLP tests, implicit threat assumptions, or single-point accuracy scores that are not indicative of operational cybersecurity risk. This paper is a systematic assessment checklist on data poisoning resistance of LLMs used in cybersecurity situations. We model a realistic threat of poisoning both pretraining and fine-tuning data sources that are regularly employed in CTI reports, system logs, and text corpora related to their malware patterns. We test common LLM families (encoders, decoders) between 2022 and 2025 on representative security-specific datasets at varying degrees of poisoning. The trends in performance degradation have been found to be consistent across experimental results, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.7+-1.9$</tex> percent to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.4+-3.2$</tex> percent losses in task-level robustness varying according to the poisoning strategy used and type of task. We note amplification of variance and asymmetry of robustness in the tasks, which has led to the importance of statistically informed resilience assessment prior to the implementation of the LLMs in securitycritical settings.

title: PSO-Based Optimization of a CMOS Switched-Capacitance Current-Starved Ring VCO in 0.18-μm Technology
authors: Elhafidi Sanae, Aymen Hal-Houssine, Maaroufi Ismail, El Beqal Asmae, Benhala Bachir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents the design and optimization of a switched-capacitance current-starved ring voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO). The proposed architecture relies on a current-starved topology combined with switched MOS capacitance tuning. The circuit is implemented in TSMC 0.18 μm CMOS technology. It operates at a center oscillation frequency of 1.5 GHz under a 1.8 V supply voltage. The main performance metrics considered are phase noise and power consumption. Design trade-offs inherent to multi-stage ring VCOs are thoroughly investigated. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is adopted and applied as a metaheuristic optimization strategy to identify optimal transistor sizing and tuning parameters. The optimization process targets low phase noise and reduced power dissipation. The complete VCO is designed and simulated using the Cadence design environment. Simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed methodology. The optimized VCO achieves a phase noise of –80 dBc/Hz at a 1 MHz offset. The total power consumption is limited to 15.8 mW. These results demonstrate the suitability of PSO for highperformance CMOS Current-Starved ring VCO design.

title: Follow the Sun and Catch the Wind: A Spatio-Temporal GNN-DRL Routing Framework for Carbon-Neutral Geo-Distributed Data Centers
authors: Alicia Chai, Page Zhang, Xuehan Sun
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The explosive growth of cloud computing and Large Language Model (LLM) training has propelled data centers to become primary consumers of global electricity. While temporal workload shifting within a single data center can partially align compute demand with renewable energy availability, its efficacy is fundamentally constrained by local meteorological volatility and strict Service Level Agreements (SLAs). In this paper, we propose a paradigm shift from temporal to spatio-temporal scheduling by exploiting geo-distributed data centers. We introduce a "Follow the Sun, Follow the Wind" spatial routing framework that dynamically migrates computational workloads to global regions currently experiencing abundant, low-carbon renewable energy generation. However, migrating data across continents incurs significant Wide Area Network (WAN) bandwidth costs and transmission latency. To resolve this complex multi-objective trade-off, we formulate the network as a dynamic graph and propose a novel Spatio-Temporal Graph Proximal Policy Optimization (STG-PPO) algorithm. The framework utilizes Graph Neural Networks (GNN) to capture the topological correlations of global renewable energy grids and WAN congestion, empowering a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) agent to execute microsecond-level routing decisions. Comprehensive experiments on real-world Google Cluster traces and multi-continent NREL weather data demonstrate that our STG-PPO approach reduces global carbon emissions by 41.2% compared to state-of-the-art temporal schedulers, while strictly bounding WAN transmission latency and bandwidth expenditures, offering a robust blueprint for global sustainable smart city infrastructures.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Prompting Strategies for LLM-Based Combinatorial Optimization
authors: Taha Chadli, Asmaa Mourhir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate remarkable performance on natural language understanding tasks but struggle with mathematical optimization problems, primarily due to calculation hallucinations and constraint tracking failures. The LLM-based Formalized Programming (LLMFP) framework addresses the above problems by shifting the role of the LLM from a solver to a translator that converts the natural language problems into executable Python code utilizing the Z3 solver. While the framework achieves up to 95% accuracy, its monolithic design introduces limitations such as error propagation, the lack of validation between stages, and parsing difficulties. Error analysis shows that the majority of LLMFP failures are due to formatting and parsing issues rather than reasoning errors. This paper presents the Parser-CoderValidator (PCV) technique, a three-stage modular architecture. The technique decomposes the optimization problem into three stages. First, we establish a new baseline through a comprehensive comparative analysis of the performance of the four state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-OSS-20B, GPT-OSS-120B, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Grok-Code-Fast-1) on 687 optimization problems across three problem domains: Coffee Supply Chain, Facility Location, and Workforce Scheduling. We then present a comparison of several prompting strategies, demonstrating that PCV achieves up to 39.71% overall accuracy improvements over the LLMFP baseline, with the most dramatic gains in Facility Location. The results underscore the significance of the PCV framework, which demonstrates that careful design of the prompting and validation process is as critical as model selection for achieving reliable LLM-based optimization.

title: Mitigating Misalignment of Excitation Sources in Low-Frequency Mammography
authors: Ghazaleh Tashtarian, Hamid Akbari-Chelaresi, Mauricio Hernandez, Farzan Mirshekari, Ahad Tavakoli, Omar M. Ramahi
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Any movement of the transmitter antenna during breast tumor detection can cause localization errors. This paper presents a technique to mitigate misalignment and accurately detect and localize tumors in a 2-D impression. The proposed method employs cross-correlation and Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) analysis, and simulation results confirm its effectiveness.

title: Efficient Assessment Method for the Temperature Rise Limit of Degaussing Coil Systems Based on Sparse Simulation and Model Extrapolation
authors: Zhixin Li, Weibo Li, Yuanbin Yang, Qixing Shang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Large pulsed degaussing coils usually require full-cycle thermal simulation to determine the temperature rise limit, but the associated computational cost is extremely high and severely restricts engineering design efficiency. To address this issue, this paper proposes an efficient evaluation method based on sparse simulation and model extrapolation. A first-order lumped-parameter thermal model is first established and rapidly calibrated using finite-element results from only a few key nodes, so that the dynamic thermal behavior of the system can be captured with limited simulation effort. A quantitative guideline for selecting sparse sampling nodes is further introduced by partitioning the pulse sequence into calibration, validation, and equilibrium-oriented zones and assigning explicit range and error criteria to each zone. The calibrated model is then employed to predict the temperature evolution over the entire operating cycle, and the temperature rise limit is finally obtained by solving the thermal equilibrium condition. An electro-thermal coupled degaussing coil model is used as a case study for validation. In addition, a sensitivity analysis with respect to the equivalent thermal resistance, equivalent thermal capacitance, and ambient temperature is performed to examine the robustness and transferability of the proposed framework. The results show that the proposed method achieves a prediction error of less than 5% while reducing the computational cost by about one order of magnitude compared with conventional full-cycle simulation. The sensitivity analysis further indicates that the saturation temperature is mainly governed by the equivalent thermal resistance and ambient temperature, whereas the equivalent thermal capacitance primarily affects the transient heating rate. The proposed method therefore provides an accurate and efficient tool for evaluating the temperature rise limit of pulsed degaussing coil systems and offers a reproducible practical solution for thermal design and safety margin assessment of electrical equipment under massive pulsed operating conditions.

title: Deep Learning-Based Prediction of Pulsed Electric Field and Biological Parameters from In Vitro Experiments
authors: Nicolò Colistra, Arianna Casciati, Simone Samperna, Raffaele Crusi, Francesca Camera, Mirella Tanori, Luciano Tarricone, Marco Zappatore, Caterina Merla
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Pulsed Electric Fields (PEFs) have emerged as a minimally invasive and highly controllable therapeutic modality for cancer treatment, capable of inducing reversible or irreversible electroporation depending on pulse parameters. Recent advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) offer new opportunities to optimize PEF protocols by exploiting complex, nonlinear relationships between electrical parameters, cellular properties, and biological outcomes. In this work, we present a novel AI-based framework for the optimization of standalone PEF therapeutic protocols. Unlike standard approaches relying on literature-derived parameter selection, the proposed methodology is grounded on experimental in vitro data obtained from six human tumor cell lines with distinct molecular profiles exposed to various PEF conditions. Multiple Artificial Neural Network (ANN) models were specifically developed to predict cell viability and proliferation and optimize the electrical signal characteristics. The results demonstrate the ability of AI-driven models to accurately capture PEF-induced cellular responses and to guide the selection of effective, tumor-specific therapeutic protocols, paving the way toward data-driven and personalized oncological therapies.

title: A Machine Learning-Assisted Approach for High-Precision PV Module Characterization
authors: Sanat Kumar Patro, Deepak Kumar Barik, Saumyaranjan Sahoo, Gunamani Jena
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: A machine learning-assisted approach for determining the parameters of a dual-diode photovoltaic module model is introduced, utilizing an enhanced differential evolution method with a two-phase mutation technique, referred to as Multimodal Multi-Objective Differential Evolution with Two-Stage Mutation and Modified Maximum Extension Distance (MMODE-TSM-MMED). This strategy is designed to enhance diversity and accelerate convergence. The effectiveness of the proposed technique is assessed by comparing the derived outcomes with those of alternative algorithms. Observations indicate that the optimal solution stabilizes at 6.682 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> across all iterations, with a maximum iteration count of fewer than 15.

title: Performance Comparison of Radiative Transfer Simulation Tools Based on Land Surface Radiance in Beihang Green Garden
authors: Yunda Yang, Huaiwen Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: To evaluate the performance of radiation transfer simulation tools in ground object radiance modeling, this study conducted research at Beijing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics' Lvyuan campus, selecting typical ground objects including vegetation, water bodies, soil, and buildings. Radiance data were extracted from Gaofen-1 satellite imagery and ASTGM TM V003 elevation data, with simulation scenarios constructed using three projection methods: fisheye camera, parallel projection camera, and perspective camera. Three mainstream radiation transfer simulation tools—LESS, DART, and ERADIATE—were employed for radiance modeling. A quantitative evaluation system was established, including absolute radiance error, relative error, root mean square error (RMSE), and coefficient of determination, combined with qualitative dimensions such as morphological fidelity and scene adaptability to systematically compare simulation accuracy under various solar geometric conditions. Results demonstrated that the LESS model consistently outperformed others across all observation conditions, maintaining a stable coefficient of determination above 0.99 and achieving the lowest RMSE across all experimental groups, showcasing superior numerical stability and morphological fidelity. Leveraging ray tracing algorithms, LESS demonstrated significant advantages in complex 3D scene simulations (vegetation-water-body-building mixtures), particularly excelling in fine-grained vegetation canopy structure representation, making it an ideal tool for scientific quantitative remote sensing inversion. The DART model exhibited balanced performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> generally above 0.95 and RMSE slightly higher than LESS but still within acceptable ranges. DART demonstrates robust performance in multiscale simulations (from individual blades to landscape scales) and multi-angle radiative transfer analysis, particularly exhibiting strong adaptability when handling heterogeneous surfaces, making it suitable for engineering simulation validation and parameter inversion applications. The ERADIATE model shows significant uncertainty in complex projection conditions, with RMSE values notably higher than the other two models. While its high-precision radiative transfer algorithm performs well in idealized simple scenarios, simulation accuracy and stability are relatively limited in highly heterogeneous urban environments like Beihang Green Park due to constraints in scene modeling flexibility and computational resource availability. However, ERADIATE demonstrates lower coefficient of variation under weak signal conditions (e.g., cloudy days or water bodies), indicating its advantages under low-energy input scenarios. This study provides quantitative evidence for selecting and optimizing radiation transfer simulation tools in complex surface environments, while establishing methodological foundations for precision evaluation and parameter calibration of simulation tools in quantitative remote sensing applications.

title: IoT-Enabled EV Parking Station Powered by Renewable Energy
authors: Sirisha Annam, K. Vishali, P. Geethika, Sk. Abdul Asif, Y. Adharsh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) has created a significant demand for sustainable and intelligent charging solutions. Traditional charging stations mostly depend on grid power, which increases load demand and indirectly contributes to carbon emissions. To address this challenge, this project proposes an IoT-Enabled EV Parking Station Powered by Renewable Energy. The system utilizes solar photovoltaic panels (or other renewable sources) as the primary energy supply, supported by a battery storage unit to ensure continuous operation. The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technology makes the station intelligent and user-friendly. IoT modules and sensors are employed to monitor parameters such as energy generation, battery status, charging rate, and slot availability. Real-time data is transmitted to a cloud platform, where users can access it through a mobile application or web interface. This allows EV owners to identify available parking slots, track charging progress, and receive notifications. The proposed system reduces dependency on non-renewable sources, promotes green energy utilization, and enhances user convenience through smart monitoring. Additionally, the project demonstrates scalability for deployment in smart cities, universities, commercial complexes, and residential areas. Thus, this work provides a sustainable and innovative solution to support the growing EV ecosystem.

title: GridInsu-Net: An Efficient Algorithm for Defect Detection of Transmission Line Insulators
authors: Yuxi Liao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Transmission line insulators are critical components of power systems, and their condition directly affects grid safety and stability. However, practical inspection images often involve scale variations, subtle defect features, and complex backgrounds, which make accurate detection challenging. To address these issues, this paper proposes GridInsu-Net, an improved insulator defect detection model based on YOLOv12. The model introduces three enhancements: Multi-Scale Feature Fusion for Insulators (MSFFI) to improve multi-scale feature representation, Power Grid Contextual Attention (PGCA) to emphasize insulator-related features while suppressing background interference, and Micro-Defect Enhancement Refinement (MDER) to strengthen the representation of fine-grained defects. Experimental results show that GridInsu-Net achieves an mAP of 96.7% on the test set, outperforming the original YOLOv12 while maintaining real-time performance, demonstrating its effectiveness for insulator defect detection in complex power grid environments.

title: New approach to improve low-noise amplifier performances for 2.45 GHz wireless applications
authors: Moustapha Séré, Jamal Zbitou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This article describes a new approach to improving the performance of low-noise amplifiers (LNA) operating at 2.45 GHz. It focuses on the use of novel circuit topologies, advanced techniques to reduce the noise figure (NF), and simultaneous gain enhancement. Such improvement is made possible by advancements in CMOS technologies, such as gallium arsenide (GaAs), and the careful selection of impedance matching network components. The GaAs-based ATF-21170 transistor is used as the active element in the proposed LNA. This LNA operates in class $\mathbf{A B}$ and is designed using the Advanced Design System (ADS) software. Simulations yield a gain of 15.918 dB and a noise figure of $\mathbf{1. 1 4 3 ~ d B}$. The input (S11) and output (S22) reflection coefficients are -20.136 dB and -15.205 dB, respectively. The implementation layout measures $\mathbf{4 0} \times \mathbf{8 3. 5} \mathbf{~ m m}^{\mathbf{2}}$. The final results demonstrate enhanced performance for receivers used in wireless communications, such as radar systems, Wi-Fi, and satellite communications…
