title: Energy-Efficient AI Models for Large-Scale Technical Data Collection in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Kiran Saripudi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Large-scale Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) produce diverse, high-velocity technical data that necessitate constant acquisition, processing, and transmission. When using traditional fixed-rate data collection methods, energy is wasted, communication is slowed down, and sampling is done too often, especially in distributed sensor networks. This paper presents an energy-efficient AI-driven data acquisition framework that incorporates Reinforcement Learning (RL), adaptive sampling, and lightweight deep learning models to dynamically optimize sampling rates according to sensed context, signal variability, and operational constraints. When tested on a publicly avaialble data set Intel Lab Data with 120 sensor nodes used to monitor temperature, the proposed model uses 41.6 % less energy, 37.2 % fewer redundant data samples, and 22.4 % more accurate data than static sampling. The framework shows promise for being used on a large scale in smart grids, the industrial Internet of Things, precision agriculture, and strong CPS infrastructures.

title: Deep Hybrid Architecture for Cybersecurity Threat Detection in IIOT Networks: CNN–BiGRU Approach
authors: Rajni Jindal, Valluri Daneesha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The rapid growth of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) technologies has transformed industrial automation, manufacturing, and cyber-physical systems. However, the increased connectivity among different devices creates significant cybersecurity risks. Traditional intrusion detection systems struggle to manage the dynamic and large amounts of data generated in IIoT settings. This paper presents a Deep Hybrid Architecture that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Units (BiGRU) to effectively detect cybersecurity threats in IIoT networks. CNNs efficiently pull spatial features from packet-level data, while BiGRU layers capture bidirectional temporal dependencies in industrial data streams. Tests on the UNSW-NB15 dataset show notable improvements in performance by class: our model achieves a 70% increase in F1-score for backdoor attacks, a 246% improvement in recall for backdoor detection, and a 10-12% increase for shellcode. Additionally, it shows a 7.4% improvement for reconnaissance and a 30% improvement for DOS attacks. Overall accuracy improved by 3.02%, climbing from 71.81% to 74.83%. The significant gains for rare and stealthy attack types—which present the highest risk in industrial settings—confirm that modeling temporal context is vital for identifying evolving IIoT intrusions. The CNN-BiGRU architecture improves generalization without substantially increasing computational demand (a 12% rise in training time), maintains over 96% accuracy for benign traffic, and positions itself as an effective, scalable, and context-aware intrusion detection solution ideal for real-time IIoT cybersecurity.

title: A Dual-Level Data-Driven Framework for Reducing Financial Fraud in Banking
authors: Nusrat Jahan, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Anika Sayeema, Sabiha Firdaus, Jannatul Maua, Hashibul Ahsan Shoaib
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Financial fraud detection remains a major challenge in modern banking due to evolving adversarial manipulation, data imbalance, and the narrow focus of conventional detection strategies. This paper introduces a dual-level data-driven framework that combines account-level behavioral risk modeling with transaction-level anomaly detection to enable more accurate and context-aware fraud identification. The proposed system achieves an ROC-AUC of 0.995 and AUPRC of 0.907 under the final combined inference model, surpassing traditional classifiers such as Logistic Regression (AUPRC 0.716) and even deep MLP models (AUPRC 0.843). Under stringent operating requirements of 90% precision, the proposed model maintains a recall of 0.611 compared to 0.519 for deep MLP and 0.381 for Logistic Regression. Additionally, the fairness-aware account model reduces demographic prediction disparity from 0.178 to 0.081. These results, validated on both the Credit Card Fraud dataset and the BAF account dataset, demonstrate that multi-scale risk reasoning substantially improves operational fraud detection performance and provides practical benefit for real-world banking applications and fraud monitoring workflows.

title: Reliability-Guided Frequency Compensation Network for Multi-Exposure Image Fusion
authors: Xudong Li, Zhongqiu Zhao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Multi-exposure fusion (MEF) is a key technique for generating high-quality high-dynamic-range images by fusing complementary information from differently exposed images. However, existing methods lack explicit modeling of exposure-induced degradation, leading to fine detail loss in saturated regions and poor global semantic consistency. To address this, we propose a Reliability-Guided Frequency Compensation (RGFC) framework with a Progressive Dual-Stream Interaction (PDI) backbone and an RGFC module. The PDI backbone enables stage-wise fusion of under/over-exposed features to avoid premature feature entanglement, while the RGFC module adaptively recovers high-frequency details via multi-scale frequency modulation regulated by a luminance-texture dual-reliability mechanism. Extensive experiments on SICE and MEFB datasets against 9 state-of-the-art methods verify that RGFC outperforms competitors in both quantitative metrics $(1.2 \%-5.3 \%$ average improvement on SD/SF/VIF) and subjective visual quality.

title: Research on Construction and Virtual Commissioning of Intelligent Warehousing System Based on PQFactory Platform
authors: Runsheng Hao, Guohao Feng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Digital twin virtual simulation technology plays a significant role in the virtual commissioning of intelligent production lines. Based on the PQFactory platform, this paper conducts virtual commissioning research on the intelligent warehousing system design. A digital model of the warehousing system is designed, a digital twin scenario is built on the PQFactory platform, and relevant parameters are configured according to the process flow. The robot program and PLC control program are compiled, and the KingIOServer gateway is used to realize communication between the physical PLC and the PQFactory platform, completing the integrated digital twin commissioning of the entire system. The virtual simulation verifies the accuracy and reliability of the intelligent warehousing system design, which helps improve the design and commissioning efficiency and reduce the development cycle and cost.

title: Inchiscope: A Modular Triple-Balloon Soft Robotic Endoscope for Navigating the Small Bowel
authors: Jackson Williams, Charles DeLorey, Dominic Jones, Pietro Valdastri, James Avery, James H. Chandler
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Access to the small bowel remains challenging due to its length, diameter, and curvature, making conventional push endoscopes insufficient for deep exploration. Improved tools that can travel throughout the small bowel without risking mucosal trauma, maintain stable visualization, and offer fine distal manipulation are needed for diagnosis and targeted treatment of small-bowel diseases. This work presents the Inchiscope, a miniaturized modular self-propelled soft robotic endoscope developed to meet the challenges associated with small-bowel access. The presented configuration combines nine independent actuation units to deliver three anchoring balloons and two parallel bellows actuator (PBA) segments for extension/contraction and steering. The modular approach facilitates a minimized diameter (13 mm) while enabling three operation modes including full-body inchworm-inspired locomotion, independent distal segment control for traversing tight bends, and proximal anchoring for stabilized inspection. Characterization highlights stable anchoring at forces up to 12 N, a PBA elongation ratio of 300% and controlled omnidirectional bending to 110<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Experiments in rigid and compliant phantoms demonstrate self-propelled locomotion at up to 11.4 mm/s and within tubular environments <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$< $</tex-math></inline-formula>25 mm in diameter and with bending radii between 30–45 mm, while the independently actuated distal tip delivered continuous 3-D control for observation and positioning in complex geometries.

title: Efficient Minimum $k$-Truss Search: A Decomposition-Based Approach
authors: Qifan Zhang, Yang Liu, Kaiqiang Yu, Shengxin Liu, Cheng Long, Xun Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Cohesive subgraph mining has been extensively studied and finds numerous graph mining applications such as link farm identification, community detection, and product recommendation. Among various cohesive subgraph structures, the $k$-truss is particularly notable for its strong structural cohesiveness based on triangles. However, the classical $k$-truss problem aims to find the $k$-truss with the maximum number of vertices, which is often extremely large and complex in practice. To fully leverage the benefits of the $k$-truss, we consider a novel problem called the minimum $k$-truss problem, which seeks to identify a $k$-truss with the minimum number of vertices, where $k\geq 2$ is a positive integer. We first formally prove the NP-hardness of the problem. We then design a baseline algorithm MTEnum that is based on the vertex enumeration and a heuristic method for computing an upper bound. Despite these efforts, MTEnum still faces practical efficiency issues which may be due to the fact that the $k$-truss lacks the hereditary property. To address this issue, we develop a novel decomposition-based framework DSA, which elegantly transforms the problem into a sequence of problems that are based on a new cohesive subgraph model called edge-based $s$-plex ($s$-eplex). With the hereditary property of $s$-eplex, we design a branch-and-bound algorithm with several customized techniques for the newly formulated problem. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our studied problem and the efficiency of our proposed algorithm DSA. In particular, DSA runs up to five orders of magnitude faster than the baseline MTEnum.

title: Hybrid Consistency Policy: Decoupling Multi-Modal Diversity and Real-Time Efficiency in Robotic Manipulation
authors: Qianyou Zhao, Yuliang Shen, Xuanran Zhai, Duidi Wu, Jin Qi, Ce Hao, Jie Hu, Qiaojun Yu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: In visuomotor policy learning, diffusion-based imitation learning has become widely adopted for its ability to capture diverse behaviors. However, approaches built on ordinary and stochastic denoising processes struggle to jointly achieve fast sampling and strong multi-modality. To address these challenges, we propose the Hybrid Consistency Policy (HCP). HCP runs a short stochastic prefix up to an adaptive switch time, and then applies a one-step consistency jump to produce the final action. To align this one-jump generation, HCP performs time-varying consistency distillation that combines a trajectory-consistency objective to keep neighboring predictions coherent and a denoising-matching objective to improve local fidelity. In both simulation and on a real robot, HCP with 25 SDE steps plus one jump approaches the 80-step DDPM teacher in accuracy and mode coverage while significantly reducing latency. These results show that multi-modality does not require slow inference, and a switch time decouples mode retention from speed. It yields a practical accuracy–efficiency trade-off for robot policies. Project website: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://sites.google.com/view/hybrid-cp</uri>.

title: H-VSJ: A Hybrid Stiff-Compliant Variable Stiffness Joint With Decoupling Position and Stiffness Modulation
authors: Lei Yan, Junpeng Gu, Ziye Zheng, Sheng Liu, Shilong Sun, Fujun Peng, Wenfu Xu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: The wide spectrum of robotic applications motivates the intrinsic stiffness modulation of the robot besides compliance control, especially for highly dynamic manipulation. Variable Stiffness Actuators (VSAs) are designed for improving impact resistance, stiffness adaptability, and dynamic performance, while many existing variable stiffness mechanisms still face coupling between position and stiffness modulation. This paper presents a Hybrid Stiff-Compliant Variable Stiffness Joint (H-VSJ) that employs a decoupling mechanism to achieve independent control of joint position and stiffness as well as hybrid stiff-compliant modes. By adjusting the force-transmission ratio between the elastic element and the output link along the joint axis, H-VSJ achieves the decoupled and accurate stiffness modulation. The decoupling mechanism also allows the joint to smoothly switch between the stiff mode and compliant mode. The stiffness model of H-VSJ is established, and the influence of key design parameters on stiffness is further analyzed. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed H-VSJ is validated through experiments on buffering motion range, stiffness-position decoupling, stiffness analysis, dynamic stiffness modulation, and impact-buffering capability, which demonstrate H-VSJ has a maximum deflection of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$30.20^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the low-stiffness state, an average stiffness modulation error of 7.00%, a stiffness range of 23.57–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$260.36~\mathrm{N\ m/rad}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the compliant mode, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8630.25~\mathrm{N\ m/rad}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the stiff mode.

title: A Review of Vision-Based Vehicle Detection for UAV-Based Traffic Monitoring: Experimental Insights and Future Directions
authors: Jianlin Ye, Christos Kyrkou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In Intelligent Transportation System (ITS), uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV)-based surveillance offers an innovative solution to traffic surveillance with wide coverage and real-time data collection capabilities. In comparison to fixed ground-based infrastructure, UAVs are able to respond to dynamic traffic but present challenges such as vehicle detection at varying altitudes, compensation for motion-induced image variations and efficient processing of high-resolution images. Deep learning has been largely beneficial on improving the detection accuracy; however, for practical deployment, a critical assessment of the accuracy, latency, and harmonization with current transportation systems needs to be carefully considered. This survey reviews recent advancements in the UAV-based traffic monitoring, with a primary focus being deep neural network models for traffic analytics in various urban settings. Three main challenges identified in the literature are ensuring compatibility with traffic control systems, achieving real-time processing to optimize traffic flow, and maintaining robust detection in different environmental conditions. Existing solutions often lack comprehensive frameworks for utilizing UAV captured data to respond to incidents and manage traffic effectively. Future research should focus on optimal detection models, edge processing, and adaptive control integration to improve the responsiveness of urban traffic management.

title: Real-Time Detection of Subsurface Defects in CFRP Using Line-laser Scanning Thermography and CSDNet
authors: Jixiang Luo, Zhijie Zhang, Wuliang Yin, Guangyu Zhou, Haoze Chen, Gaokun Wang, Chaofan He, Qianfang Xie, Yonghua Xun
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Carbon fiber reinforced polymers (CFRP) are widely used in aerospace and other fields but are susceptible to defects during manufacturing and service. Infrared thermography-based non-destructive testing enables non-contact, large-area inspection; however, most existing approaches rely on offline processing, limiting their applicability to online scenarios. To address this limitation, a real-time subsurface defect detection method for CFRP based on line-laser scanning thermography is proposed. First, real-time differential preprocessing is applied to suppress the thermal trailing effect. Then, a lightweight detection network, CSDNet (CFRP Subsurface Defect Detection Network), is developed, consisting of three core components: the C2f-RepViT-EMA module, the Lightweight Multi-branch Modulated Feature Pyramid Network (LM²FPN), and the Lightweight Quality-aware Detail Head (LQDH). These components reduce noise interference in infrared images, enhance multi-scale feature representation, and suppress low-quality detection boxes, respectively. Experiments on the T300 CFRP dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mAP50 of 0.984 with 1.4 million parameters, 5.0G FLOPs, and a deployment inference speed of 30 FPS. Compared with various lightweight detection models, the proposed method achieves a better trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, enabling synchronized scanning and detection and providing effective support for real-time online inspection of CFRP subsurface defects.

title: Pointing Calibration Study for the HADAR Experiment
authors: Huiying Sun, Xiangli Qian, Tianlu Chen, Maoyuan Liu, Cheng Liu, Youliang Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: HADAR is an ultra-wide-angle atmospheric Cherenkov telescope that employs a water-lens optical system, offering significant advantages in detecting gamma-ray sources, including bursts, transients, and extended sources. Given its implementation of an innovative transmissive optical technology, the precise pointing accuracy of both the telescope and imaging system is critical, as it directly influences the reconstruction accuracy of gamma-ray events based on Cherenkov light detection. Consequently, rigorous calibration is essential. In this study, we present a calibration method to determine the optical axis orientation of the HADAR imaging system. This method leverages the distinct signal variations observed in the imaging system’s pixel units when bright stars transit through the field of view of the water lens, thereby enabling the precise calibration of the telescope’s geometric properties. Applying this method to the 0.9-meter HADAR prototype system, we analyzed the imaging characteristics induced by bright stars and performed an accurate calibration of the prototype’s imaging system alignment. Our results confirm the effectiveness of this calibration approach. This technique will be further employed for the alignment and calibration of future large-aperture water-lens telescopes in the HADAR experiment.

title: Robust Beamforming Design for STAR-RIS Assisted MISO Secure Communication System
authors: Bin Qiu, Chunni Deng, Zhongshan Zhang, Jianqing Li, Zhen Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: As a novel reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) architecture, the simultaneous transmitting and reflecting RIS (STAR-RIS) provides full-space coverage and has strong potential to enhance communication security in future 6G networks through optimized beamforming. Motivated by this, we investigate the physical-layer security issues in STAR-RIS-assisted multi-user multiple input and single output (MISO) communication systems. Considering the difficulty in obtaining the channel state information (CSI) of eavesdropping users, we propose a robust beamforming optimization scheme with artificial noise (AN) under imperfect CSI. Specifically, we aim to maximize the system weighted sum secrecy rate by jointly optimizing the base station (BS) beamforming, AN vector and STAR-RIS transmission-reflection coefficients, subject to system transmit power and user information rate constraints. Due to the non-convex constraints and strong coupling of variables, the problem is difficult to solve directly. To address this, we propose an alternating optimization algorithm using successive convex approximation (SCA) and penalized concave convex process (PCCP), and utilize S-procedure to handle the uncertainty of eavesdropping CSI. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm exhibits excellent convergence and robustness. Moreover, it outperforms other benchmark schemes in terms of secrecy performance.

title: Untethering EIT: An Integrated Wireless Tactile Interface for Human&#x2013;Machine Interaction_supp3-3688715.mp4
authors: Shili Liang
publish: N/A
abstract: Large-area tactile sensing is a core requirement for intelligent robots. However, traditional high-density sensor arrays face challenges due to complex wiring and high integration difficulty. These issues limit their industrial application. This article proposes a stable and flexible tactile sensing system based on electrical impedance tomography (EIT). At the hardware level, we developed a highly integrated and miniaturized data acquisition (DAQ) system. The developed DAQ system weighs 34 g, complementing a conformable flexible sensor with a thickness of 700 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m. This design improves portability and achieves real-time dynamic mapping at 11.1 FPS. At the algorithmic level, we adopted a U-Net architecture to solve the nonlinear reconstruction challenges of EIT. Within a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$58\times 58\,\,\text{mm}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> sensing area, the model achieves a two-point spatial resolution limit of 7 mm and a mean multipoint localization error of 1.33 mm under multitouch scenarios. Furthermore, we utilized a CNN-BiLSTM model to decode complex spatiotemporal signals, achieving 96% accuracy for dynamic handwriting recognition and 85% in cross-user tests with ten participants, thereby demonstrating strong generalization capability. Finally, we deployed the system on an industrial robotic arm and a bionic hand. The system demonstrates high-fidelity contact sensing. It can also precisely identify small objects of 5 mm. The proposed EIT system provides a lightweight and scalable solution for industrial tactile perception and intelligent interaction.

title: Surrogate-Assisted Framework for Log-Periodic Feedlines based Filter Optimization
authors: Amartya Paul, Jintu Borah, Hemant Kumari, Shubhankar Majumdar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: This work presents a computer-aided design (CAD)-based methodology for the design and behavioral modeling of a log-periodic microstrip ring filter. The filter is structured with log-periodic feedlines capacitively coupled to a hollow ring resonator and fabricated on an RT/Duroid 5880 substrate. It operates over a wideband range from 0.3 GHz to 1 GHz, with dominant resonances at 0.4 GHz, 0.6 GHz, and 0.9 GHz, achieving measured insertion loss below −1.1 dB and return loss exceeding −27 dB. A data-driven surrogate modeling framework is proposed to predict S11(f) and S21(f) responses based on 13 different ensemble learning models. Among these, XGBoost outperformed others, achieving an R2 score of 0.99 and RMSE of 0.00022 on the test set, while also providing over 105× speed-up compared to full-wave electromagnetic (EM) simulation. Parametric sensitivity analysis and feature importance quantification reveal the influence of geometric factors on spectral response, while heatmap visualizations guide optimal design regions. Experimental repeatability across four fabricated units shows consistent S21 traces, validating fabrication robustness. A fully integrated CAD loop is presented, combining geometry modeling, ML-based prediction, design optimization, and feedback from physical measurements, enabling rapid and reliable RF filter design.

title: Hybrid Bit and Semantic Communications for UAV-Enabled Wireless Power Transfer Networks: A Decision-Assisted Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Jingfu Li, Jingjing Cui, Chong Huang, Jing Zhu, Zheng Chu, Mingzhe Chen, Pei Xiao, Rahim Tafazolli
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Semantic communications which can significantly reduce spectrum consumption in wireless networks, have recently become a popular research area. When combined with wireless power transfer (WPT), semantic communications can help achieve high spectral efficiency for energy-limited devices in wireless communications. In energy-constrained and link budget-limited scenarios such as UAV networks, the integration of semantic communications and WPT enables highly energy-efficient transmission mechanisms. In this paper, we investigate semantic communications in UAV-enabled WPT networks. To achieve adaptability to varying signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and task requirements, we introduce a multi-layer hybrid bit and semantic communication framework. We adopt a semantic communication efficiency metric and aim to maximize it by jointly optimizing UAV trajectory, energy harvesting base station (EHBS) selection, user association, semantic mode selection, and energy harvesting time allocation. To address this complex long-term optimization problem, we introduce the distributional soft actor-critic (DSAC) algorithm and introduce a decision assistant to further enhance the convergence performance of DSAC. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method and framework and demonstrate that our algorithm can achieve superior long-term optimization performance in dynamic network environments.

title: FDSCBAM-YOLO: Integrating Frequency-Domain Attention with Depthwise Separable Convolution for Data-Scarce SAR Vehicle Detection
authors: Dan Gao, Shuo Yu, Caoyi Mei, Xiaofang Wu, Zhijin Wen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The intelligent advancement of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) Automatic Target Recognition (ATR) is frequently hindered by the scarcity of high-quality annotated data. To address this fundamental challenge, we propose FDSCBAM-YOLO, a lightweight detection network incorporating a novel Frequency-domain Depthwise Separable Convolutional Attention Module (FDSCBAM). By synergistically integrating depthwise separable convolutions with both frequency-domain and spatial attention mechanisms, FDSCBAM effectively suppresses inherent coherent speckle noise and accentuates target scattering features in the frequency domain without compromising computational efficiency. Furthermore, we introduce a frequency-domain enhanced loss function specifically tailored to improve small target detection. Extensive experiments conducted under data-scarce conditions demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior detection accuracy while maintaining low model complexity.

title: When Does Artificial Intelligence Matter for Knowledge Management? Empirical Insights from Indonesian Organizations
authors: Beata Asty Daneswari, Devi Ciyen, Stephanie Devithawaty, Maria Grace Herlina
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) is changing how organizations manage their knowledge. This study examines the influence of AI utilization on knowledge creation, sharing, and renewal among employees in Greater Jakarta. A cross-sectional survey design was utilized, and data were analyzed through Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The results show that using AI has statistically significant negative effects on the creation and sharing of knowledge, but not on the renewal of knowledge. Furthermore, AI accounts for only a small part of the differences in how people manage knowledge, suggesting limited predictive capability. These results contradict the presumption that implementing AI inherently improves organizational learning and collaboration. Instead, AI might cause behavioral problems, such as relying too much on automated outputs, not thinking critically enough, feeling insecure about jobs, and having less trust, which can slow down knowledge processes. The research enhances the literature by analyzing knowledge management behaviors as a cohesive system and emphasizing the contingent nature of AI’s influence. In practice, the results indicate that organizations should enhance AI implementation through employee training, psychological safety initiatives, and knowledge governance mechanisms to optimize benefits.

title: Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Dynamic Analysis Predictions of Regular Building Structures in Morocco
authors: Mouna El Mkhalet, Hicham Lamouri, Nouzha Lamdouar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The growing importance of advanced predictive techniques in civil engineering has led to the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning to address complex challenges. In Morocco, recent earthquakes have highlighted the need for advanced predictive methods. This study focuses on the application of artificial intelligence for predicting seismic forces and structural displacements in regular structures, aiming to provide additional scientific value. The proposed methodology begins with Monte Carlo data simulation using nine inputs and two outputs for regular structures, based on the Moroccan RPS standards for four distinct sites. Three topology selection methods are examined, including one utilizing Principal Component Analysis and Kmeans clustering. Optimal topologies for artificial neural networks are determined for each of the four sites. The method simplifies seismic studies and provides practical solutions for Moroccan companies and design offices.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Metal and Plastic Waste Segregation
authors: Roopashree V, Seejal Sivakumar, Vedhavarshini J, Balaji M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of deep learning framework for an automatic dry waste segregation. The objective of the framework is to design an image-based system that can accurately classify between metal and plastic waste. The framework uses the Convolutional Neural Network concept using the MobileNetV2 architecture via transfer learning. A custom dataset comprising of images across two classes curated from Kaggle and open repositories. The model was trained and evaluated in Google Colab using GPU acceleration and the performance of CNN was analyzed by means of accuracy, loss, precision and recall metrices. The results obtained showed that the model attained 91% of accuracy in classifying metal and plastic waste. The trained model was further integrated with Arduino-based embedded control unit where servo motors are connected to a mechanical flap to physically direct waste into their respective bins. The framework has the potential for scalability and extension into smart waste management solutions. Future work can include the extension of framework using the concept of edge computing.

title: Quaternary Reversible Circuit Optimization for Scalable Multiplexer and Demultiplexer
authors: Y. Sunanda, M. Ravi Kishore, K. Shankar, Y. Pavan Kumar Reddy, P. Syamala Devi, P. Mounika
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Reversible computing is starting to establish itself as a practical solution for the energy dissipation problem in digital systems and is anticipated to be the future by pushing the limits of information loss. There are certain areas where quaternary reversible logic (multi-valued reversible logic) has an edge over irreversible binary (two-valued reversible logic) including information density, interconnect complexity, and overall scalability. We describe the design and implementation of a quaternary reversible Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) which contains large functional units and which operates like those in the modern ALU's; offering such functions as arithmetic, logic, memory, decoders, and multiplexers. The system will be affecting the movement of data and control of operation to scale (like a flexible control unit) and even adopts the use of a selection-line design. The proposed ALU shows some major advances in quantum cost, garbage output, and steady-state inputs in comparison to earlier designs, and yet, it can be a compatible one to design scalability for larger reversible and quantum computing systems overall. The achieved results through simulations and comparisons indicate that the proposed system is more efficient and has a significantly lower power consumption than the already proposed designs; therefore, it is reasonable to say that it could be incorporated into the future of computing technologies

title: Evaluating the Importance of Marine Ecological Protection: A Case Study of the Dalian Sea Area, Liaoning Province
authors: Wenhu Lu, Long He, Lu Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Marine ecological protection is one of the global common concerns. Scientific identification of conservation priority areas and the evaluation of the importance of ecological protection is one of the important routes to achieve the best protection effect with limited investment. In this study, taking the sea area of Dalian City, Liaoning Province as an example, a qualitative and quantitative evaluation index system and method for the importance of marine ecological protection were constructed. Through the criteria of the importance of marine ecosystem service and ecological vulnerability, conservation priority areas were identified in Dalian, and their relative importance levels were evaluated. Results showed that the area of extremely important ecological protection regions was approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6,867 \text{km}^{2}$</tex>, accounting for 22.8% of the total sea area of Dalian, including the distribution areas of Phoca largha and key protected or threatened seabirds, distribution areas of aquatic germplasm resources, seagrass meadows, estuaries and saltmarshes. The area of important ecological protection regions was approximately 16,679 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{km}^{2}$</tex>, accounting for 55.54% of total sea area of Dalian, including tidal flats and shallow sea waters, migration channels and winter grounds for fishery resources. The evaluation of the importance of marine ecological protection is the most fundamental work for the delimitation of marine ecological red line and ecological zone. The research can provide scientific guidance for marine ecological zoning and marine biodiversity conservation.

title: Multi-Scale Object Detection for Autonomous Driving Based on Multimodal Feature Fusion
authors: Jiafu Chen, Xiguang Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: Object detection is a critical component in autonomous driving systems. However, traditional detection algorithms suffer from significant performance degradation in complex traffic scenarios. This paper proposes YOLO-Vehicle, a multi-scale object detection model based on multimodal feature fusion, specifically designed for autonomous driving scenarios. The model employs multi-scale feature map extraction to handle vehicle targets of different sizes and distances, while integrating text semantic information to enhance scene understanding. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLO-Vehicle achieves 92.1% accuracy on the KITTI dataset with 226 FPS detection speed, meeting real-time autonomous driving requirements.

title: Emotionally Adaptive Conversational AI for Personalized Learning
authors: Eman Alduweib, Essa Hezzam, Mohammed Noman
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Emotions like confusion, curiosity, and boredom deeply influence learning quality through their impact on attention, motivation, and cognitive regulation. However, virtually all current emotionally adaptive tutoring systems have been developed as reactively designed systems that respond to an individual's emotions once those emotions have occurred, rather than proactively preventing extended frustration or learner disengagement from the learning process. This paper presents a multimodal, uncertainty-aware architecture for Emotionally Adaptive Conversational AI (EACAI), which is designed to move beyond reactively-designed systems and to provide predictive and preventative emotional intelligence in support of enhanced student engagement and learning. The proposed architecture includes four primary components: (1) Multimodal sensing modalities including text-based input, spoken input, behavioral cues, and optionally facial expressions, (2) Confidence weighted multi-modal affective inference models that can accurately infer an individual's emotional state based on the multimodal input data collected during interaction with the EACAI, (3) A Cognitive-Affective Learner Model (CALM-X) that enables longitudinal tracking of an individual's mastery trajectory and emotional states across multiple interactions, and (4) A Predictive Affective Risk Module (PARM) that uses historical data to predict when an individual will be at risk for disengaging, experiencing boredom, or experiencing frustration. Additionally, our architecture integrates several important ethical design elements: consent, transparency, and human oversight. It is also aligned with good educational psychology frameworks, such as Self-Determination Theory and Control-Value Theory, to ensure that the system is both academically sound and ethically acceptable. Finally, evaluation strategies, case studies and ethical considerations are provided.

title: Investigating the Synergy between Industry 4.0 Technologies, Lean Tools, and Lean Wastes
authors: Rashid Alnaqbi, Zehra Canan Araci
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The topic Lean 4.0 has gained an interest among researchers and practitioners in recent years as the tools and methods it provides can help organizations to improve the productivity of their processes and operations. Lean 4.0 is the integration of Lean principles and Industry 4.0 technologies. There seems to be a relationship between Lean principles and the digital technologies of Industry $\mathbf{4. 0}$. This research proposes investigating this relationship to understand how Lean tools and methods would synergize with Industry 4.0, in order to eliminate or reduce Lean wastes. The methodology used in the research is a systematic literature review and a bibliometric analysis. Understanding the relationship between Industry 4.0, Lean tool, and Lean wastes conjointly will be beneficial to organizations of different sizes and sectors, as it can provide insights and perspectives on how to tackle a specific waste or inefficiency in their processes and activities.

title: Harnessing Large Language Models for Next-Generation Artificial Intelligence Applications
authors: Navin Garg, Sasikanth Yangalasetty, Tharakesavulu Vangalapat, Mina Kumari, Mahesh Basavaraj, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) usage is the innovation capable of introducing the new generation of the artificial intelligence system with adaptive thinking, multimodal understanding, and grounded knowledge synthesis. Transformer architecture can be developed; less supervised scalable cognitive automation can be enabled by combining developed augmented retrieval-based generation and parameter-efficient tuning. This paper presents a technically valid and conceptually coherent hybrid-grounded LLM model that has cross-modal embedding alignment, external-knowledge recall, symbolical equivalent validation, and, the hallucination-controlled decoding. The methodology guarantees the semantic integrity and logic-sensitive decision-support in the different fields. The experimental evaluation of a group of complex reasoning demonstrates a considerable improvement as the number of perplexities was decreased to 7.1 instead of 16.8, the level of overall accuracy was increased by 63.4 to 83.8 percent, and the level of F1-score improved by 65.1 to 85.4. Generative metrics also show a significant improvement as BLEU scores 36.9, ROUGE-L scores 56.4 and exact match scores also goes to 63.2 and expectation calibration error also goes down to 3.6 and hallucination error also drops to 5.1. These findings indicate that grounded LLM design is an experimental catalyst of the future AI ecosystems with central interests on reliability, knowledge validity and high-fidelity reason.

title:
Interface and Transport-Layer Engineering for CdS / BaZrS
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    / CuSCN Solar Cells: Device Simulation, Band Alignment and Defect Effects
authors: Abhijit Kumar Kar, Mnisha, Chandni Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: This simulation study provides an important blueprint for the development of high-efficiency, lead-free BaZrS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> perovskite solar cells. Through a thorough SCAPS-1D analysis of a FTO/CdS/BaZrS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>/CuSCN/Au stack, we determine that the most important levers of performance are interface defect passivation and the precise band alignments. The front HTL/absorber interface (IDL1) is quite sensitive to defects, showing very large voltage and fill factor losses for defect densities > 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> cm⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, while the rear ETL/absorber interface (IDL2) limits the current for higher MT (defect density). Our simulations predict that a peak power-conversion efficiency of 16.77% is possible with the best designs, using a 700-800 nm thick absorber, very small interface traps (IDL1 ≤ 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup> -10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> cm⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>) and with the band structure tuned so as to have a small positive conduction band offset at the ETL interface and an HTL work function of 5.05-5.15 eV. We conclude that the experimental investigations necessary will need to accelerate passivation of the front interface and fine-tuning of the absorber processing necessary in order to bring defect densities down to the bulk levels of below 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</sup> cm⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. This work will translate the innately large promise of the BaZrS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> system into more precise, actionable targets for the realization of high performance, non-toxic perovskite photovoltaics.

title: A Unified Deep Learning-Based Architecture for Real-Time Monitoring and Classification of Fungal and Viral Crop Infections in Precision Agriculture
authors: Mukesh Singh, Pooja Vats, Shiva Sumanth Reddy, Sowmini Bandaru, R. P. Ram Kumar, Amit Dutt
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Precision agriculture requires fast, precise, and scalable technologies to identify and group crop infections, especially fungal and viral leave infections that can ruin the crops. This study proposes the Multi-Stage Unified Deep Crop Disease Detection Network (MU-DCDNet) unified deep networks architecture to accomplish this in real-time using multimodal images in detection, classification, and tracking progression of crop diseases detection. In the suggested system, aerial UAV pictures and ground-level sensing on the basis of IoT will be combined to obtain RGB, NIR, and thermal photos, making them spatially aligned with the aid of a Multimodal Image Registration Module. Spectral-Spatial Attention Encoder is used to perform the task of feature extraction and focus on the wavelength patterns and spatial texture anomalies that can be found in the infections. The feature of a dual-head classifier is to locate healthy or infected crops and simultaneously identify the type of the infection being either of a fungal or of a viral nature. In order to facilitate early intervention, a Temporal Disease Progression Tracker that is built through a ConvLSTM model is used in order to capture the spatial evolution of infection as it occurs across time. Its whole architecture is post-training quantized in order to optimize it on edge devices, allowing to make decisions in the field in real time. Experimental tests show that MU-DCDNet promotes accuracy, the granularity of classifications, and the time awareness drastically as against the conventional CNN-type models. It is a framework to provide a scalable and powerful solution to sustainable crop health management to enable the timely data-driven decision-making by farmers and agronomists in precision farming settings. The suggested MU-DCDNet approach has an overall accuracy of 94.7%.

title: Subgroup and Longitudinal Measurement Invariance of the Computational Thinking Test Short (CTt-S)
authors: Joseph C. Tise, Monica McGill
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Conference on Research on Equity and Sustained Participation in Engineering, Computing, and Technology
abstract: None

title: Project SmartAvatar: A Human-Avatar Interface
authors: Renato Araujo Lima, Leonardo da Conceição Estevam, Marcos César da Rocha Seruffo
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM International Conference on Interactive Media Experiences
abstract: None

title: Detection of Arbitrary Polarization States Based on Spoof Surface Plasmon Polaritons
authors: Yi Ren, Jun Xia, Yuxiang Wang, Dianjie Wang, Jingjing Zhang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Polarization detection is crucial for advanced electromagnetic applications, yet conventional Stokes-based methods are often bulky and complex. This letter proposes a universal, compact approach for detecting arbitrary polarization states on the Poincaré sphere using a spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) platform. Leveraging the spin-momentum locking property of SSPPs, the design incorporates only two spiral shaped scatterers coupled to a single SSPP waveguide. Incident polarization is decomposed into left- and right-handed circular components, which are separated directionally and collected at two output ports. The signal ratio between these ports yields the full polarization state via a derived analytical relation, eliminating the need for multi-element cascades or Stokes parameter retrieval. Simulated and measured deviations are below 0.9 ° and 8.5 °, respectively. The proposed platform offers a fabrication-friendly, planar architecture suited for on-chip integration, providing a robust miniaturized solution for polarization sensing, communication, and imaging.

title: Unfolded Coprime Padded L-Shaped Array Design From Fourth-Order Difference Co-Array Concept
authors: Hua Chen, Yifan Zhang, Wei Liu, Qing Wang, Ye Tian, Ming Jin, Hing Cheung So
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Sparse array design leveraging the fourth-order difference co-array (FODCA) can significantly enhance the total count of uniform degrees-of-freedom (uDOF). The unfolded coprime L -shaped array (UCLsA), owing to its high sparsity and reduced mutual coupling effect, has become an attractive structure for high-resolution two-dimensional (2D) direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation. However, the FODCA of UCLsA exhibits numerous holes in its central region, resulting in constrained uDOF. To mitigate the aforementioned limitation, this paper introduces an unfolded coprime padded L -shaped array (UCPLsA), which is constructed by augmenting the UCLsA with two additional subarrays to achieve a substantially larger number of uDOF. First, a systematic analysis of the FODCA of UCLsA is conducted, and the exact representation of the central-hole locations is derived. Then, the position distribution of the newly generated fourth-order virtual elements in UCPLsA is analyzed, followed by the computation of its key weight functions. When compared to current 2D sparse array designs, the proposed UCPLsA provides two major augmented proficiencies, offering a larger number of uDOF and effectively mitigating the mutual coupling effect. Numerical experiments are presented to corroborate the superior performance of UCPLsA in relation to alternative 2D sparse arrays.

title: Construction of Digital Terrain Maps from Multi-view Satellite Imagery using Neural Volume Rendering
authors: Josef X. Biberstein, Guilherme Cavalheiro, Juyeop Han, Sertac Karaman
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Digital terrain maps (DTMs) are an important part of planetary exploration, enabling operations such as terrain relative navigation during entry, descent, and landing for spacecraft and aiding in navigation on the planetary surface. As robotic exploration missions become more ambitious, the need for high-quality DTMs will only increase. However, producing DTMs via multi-view stereo pipelines for satellite imagery, the current state-of-the-art, can be cumbersome and require significant manual image preprocessing to produce satisfactory results. In this work, these shortcomings are addressed by adapting neural volume rendering techniques to learn textured digital terrain maps directly from satellite imagery. The proposed method, neural terrain maps (NTM), only requires the locus for each image pixel and does not rely on depth or any other structural priors. NTM is demonstrated on both synthetic and real satellite data from Earth and Mars encompassing scenes on the order of 100km2. The accuracy and precision of the output terrain maps is evaluated by comparing with existing high-quality DTMs produced using traditional multi-view stereo pipelines. NTM shows promising results, with the precision of terrain prediction almost equal to the resolution of the satellite images even in the presence of imperfect camera intrinsics and extrinsics. As compared to high quality reference terrain maps, NTM results ranged from an error standard deviation of 498m for ASTER imagery with a grounds sample distance (GSD) of 15m to 19m for MRO CTX imagery with a GSD of 6m.

title: A High-Throughput Custom Accelerator Architecture for Transformer Models on Edge Devices
authors: Ramakrishna Penaganti
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Transformer models have become the most popular type of architecture in deep learning today. They can do the best work in natural language processing, computer vision, and multimodal tasks. But their use on edge devices is limited by high computational costs, high memory bandwidth needs, and high energy use. This paper introduces a high-throughput custom accelerator architecture specifically engineered to facilitate efficient Transformer inference in resource-constrained edge environments. The proposed architecture incorporates a systolic tensor processing array, a specialized multi-head attention engine, and a hierarchical on-chip memory subsystem to minimize data movement and optimize parallelism. Low-precision arithmetic (INT8/INT4) and quantization-aware execution make models even more energy-efficient without losing accuracy. By using a co-optimized hardware-software stack and a pipelined execution strategy, the accelerator makes big gains in latency, throughput, and power use. Tests on typical Transformer models, such as BERT-Tiny, MobileViT-Transformer, and DistilGPT, show that they can process data up to 18.4 times faster and use 7.2 times less energy than optimized CPU and commercial NPU baselines. These results show that specialized Transformer-centric accelerators could make real-time edge intelligence possible for applications like on-device NLP, smart sensing, aerial perception, and embedded vision systems.

title: Stability Study of Advance Mining Roadways in Fault Zones Under Mining Disturbance
authors: Hongyuan Tu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: In addressing the instability of fault-affected advance haulage roadways in deep mining, we performed a thorough investigation that encompassed theoretical evaluations, numerical modeling, and field testing to analyze the evolution of stress and the deformation properties of the surrounding rock within the 402 haulage roadway. On this basis, differentiated control strategies incorporating 'anchoring-grouting-support' were subsequently developed based on a risk assessment of instability across different zones. Our results indicate that the risk of roof instability significantly increases under low fault dip angles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20^{\circ}-40^{\circ}$</tex>) or when the working face approaches within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 m}$</tex> of the fault. Furthermore, when the fault throw exceeds the coal seam thickness, both the peak abutment pressure and the deformation of the surrounding rock in the footwall advance roadway decrease as the throw increases. The roadway is categorized into three distinct risk levels: high, medium, and low. Further validation through field tests demonstrate that the proposed control scheme effectively reduces deformation and ensures roadway stability, thereby providing a theoretical foundation and practical guidance for similar conditions.

title: Beyond Three Doors: A Comprehensive Analysis of Generalized Monty Hall Problem
authors: ElMehdi Benmassoud, Salem Nafiri, Noureddine Semane, Abdelhak Elidrissi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper develops a unified probabilistic framework for the generalized Monty Hall problem with n doors and m prizes, explicitly incorporating the role of host information. The proposed ($n, m, k, r$) model describes scenarios in which the host opens k doors revealing r prizes, enabling a direct comparison between informed and uninformed strategies. Closed-form expressions for the winning probabilities of switching and staying are derived. In the informed-host case, switching is advantageous if and only if $n\lt \frac{m k}{r}$, while in the uninformed case, conditioning on the observed reveal preserves symmetry, making both strategies equivalent. These results unify several classical and extended formulations within a single framework. Monte Carlo simulations corroborate the theoretical analysis and demonstrate how optimal strategies vary with system parameters, providing further insight into decision-making under uncertainty.

title: Robust Variational Bayesian Inference for Full-Duplex Computational Microwave Imaging
authors: Tianzhuo Yang, Qian Zhang, Ke Huang, Lixin Ran
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Metasurface-based computational microwave imaging offers a cost-effective and high-resolution solution for biomedical monitoring and security screening. While transitioning to full-duplex architectures enhances system compactness, it inevitably introduces signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) degradation due to self-interference arising from transmitted signal leakage. In this work, a full-duplex computational microwave imaging (FDCMI) system architecture employing a 1-bit phase reconfigurable transmitarray (PRTA) is presented, integrated with a robust reconstruction algorithm based on variational Bayesian inference (VBI). Unlike conventional compressive sensing methods yielding point estimates, the VBI framework incorporates statistical modeling and sparse priors to obtain Bayesian estimates of all unknown parameters, significantly enhancing robustness against noise and model mismatch while maintaining computational efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate the high-resolution capability of the FD-CMI system and the robustness of the proposed VBI, with an imaging resolution of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.27 \lambda$</tex> under a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5} \mathbf{d B}$</tex> SNR, indicating its promise for practical imaging scenarios.

title: Cost-Constrained Real-Time Optimization of Multi-Chip Interconnection Architecture for Power Load Forecasting
authors: Rongzi Cong, Yaoyang Guo, Hao Men, Junxiang Bu, Ming Li, Xuanzhi Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Power load forecasting is a critical function for intelligent power system dispatch, demand response, and stability control. With the growing complexity of data-driven forecasting models, single-chip platforms often cannot meet strict real-time execution requirements under power grid dispatching deadlines. Multi-chip parallel architectures offer scalable computing capability, yet they introduce additional inter-chip communication overhead and hardware deployment costs. To address this issue, this paper proposes a cost-constrained real-time optimization framework for multi-chip interconnection architectures dedicated to power load forecasting.First, a computation-communication coupled latency model is established to capture the total execution time of parallel forecasting tasks, including computational delay, synchronization latency, and data transmission overhead. A hardware cost model is further constructed to quantify chip deployment and interconnection expenses. On this basis, the hardware configuration problem is formulated as a constrained optimization issue, which aims to minimize the total system cost while satisfying the 1-second real-time deadline of power dispatching.Numerical simulation results show that under typical power edge parameters, the optimal chip scale is determined to be 26 chips with a minimum total cost of 5072.54. Comparative analysis verifies that 16 chips fail to meet the real-time constraint, while 32 chips cause 26.7% extra cost with negligible performance gain. In addition, increasing interconnection bandwidth from 10 GB/s to 200 GB/s reduces the minimum chip requirement by 78.7%, with diminishing returns beyond 100 GB/s.The proposed framework reveals the quantitative trade-off among workload scale, chip performance, interconnection bandwidth, and system cost, and provides clear engineering guidance for cost-efficient and real-time guaranteed hardware configuration in intelligent power load forecasting platforms.

title: Koopman Neural Operator for Multi-Task Modeling in Optical Fiber Communications with NTK Analysis
authors: Yiming Meng, Xiao Luo, Xiaotian Jiang, Wenbin Chen, Shengnan Li, Min Zhang, Danshi Wang
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Optical fiber communication networks serve as a critical backbone of modern information infrastructure and have undergone rapid development in recent decades. To optimize transmission dimension resources, extensive research has focused on accurately modeling key linear and nonlinear effects, including chromatic dispersion, Kerr nonlinearity, stimulated Raman scattering, laser transition processes, and light field evolution, all governed by partial differential equations (PDEs). Traditional numerical solvers and black-box data-driven neural networks are commonly used to solve these PDEs but suffer from high computational complexity and strong data dependency, respectively. This paper explores the Koopman neural operator (KNO) for multi-task modeling in optical communications. The framework is validated across four representative tasks: 16QAM waveform propagation, quality of transmission (QoT) estimation, optical amplification modeling, and light field evolution. In all benchmarks, KNO outperforms Fourier neural operators (FNO) and convolutional neural operators (CNO), reducing mean squared error by up to one order of magnitude in certain tasks, requiring approximately three times fewer parameters. Branch ablation studies on spectral components reveal that KNO's dual-branch architecture adaptively balances low-frequency (LF) global dynamics and high-frequency (HF) local details. Furthermore, generalization tests under unseen physical conditions, such as launch powers and cross-device generalization, demonstrate KNO's robustness in handling conditions not observed during training. Neural tangent kernel (NTK) analysis highlights KNO's superior spectral properties, such as higher effective rank, lower condition number, and improved kernel ridge regression performance, which underpin its enhanced trainability, modal response, and generalization. This work establishes KNO as a scalable surrogate for PDE-related problems in optical fiber communication systems.

title: Geometry Optimization of Non-Ideal Through-Glass Vias (TGVs) for Enhanced Electrical and Mechanical Performance
authors: Zhen Fang, Jinxu Liu, Borui Li, Wenlei Li, Jihua Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Through-glass vias (TGVs) have attracted considerable attention in advanced packaging because they offer low dielectric loss and good electrical insulation. However, the tapered sidewalls formed during fabrication can affect the reliability of glass-based packages. These sidewalls increase the parasitic inductance at high frequencies and cause nonuniform stress under thermal loading. Existing TGV inductance models still have limited ability to describe skin-current effects in nonvertical vias. This study presents an analytical model for tapered TGVs that includes skin current and defines the frequency range in which the model is applicable. The effect of taper angle on stress distribution at 200 °C is also examined. The results show that the taper angle redistributes stress in the copper column and reduces stress concentration at the Cu/glass interface. By further optimizing laser power, ultrasonic power, HF concentration, etching temperature, and ammonium fluoride concentration, precise control of the TGV taper is achieved. This work provides a design reference and an optimization strategy for high-performance glass interposers.

title: Parameter Efficient Emotional Transfer for Fine-Grained Emotional Audio-Driven Talking Portrait Generation_supp1-3699022.mp4
authors: Chi-Man Pun
publish: N/A
abstract: Facial expression, especially emotion, plays a crucial role in building a realistic talking portrait video from audio. Previous emotional talking head methods train lip-synchronization with video-level annotated emotion labels jointly. However, the video-level annotated emotion labels make it hard to learn the accurate frame-wise emotions. Besides, since the emotional dataset is much smaller than the neutral faces, it becomes harder to generate lip-synchronized portrait videos with accurate expressions. To address these challenges, we propose a new method for efficient emotional transfer and detailed emotion capture. In detail, we first consider emotions as the residual of the neutral expression and train additional adapters (LoRAs in our case) for each emotion individually. Then, we use the frame-wise emotional annotations as input and design a novel method to jointly optimize the multiple adapters. After training, our method can produce high visual quality results by driving audio and fine-grained emotion guidance with negligible additional parameters. Extensive experiments also demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in synthesizing photo-realistic, emotionally expressive talking portraits that are well-synchronized with the input audio compared with other state-of-the-art methods.

title: Improving Lightweight Convolutional Neural Networks Performance for Colorectal Cancer Diagnosis via Knowledge Distillation
authors: Sümeyye Karatekin, Enes Ayan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Colorectal cancer is one of the most common and deadly types of cancer worldwide, and early diagnosis significantly improves treatment success. Computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) systems are gaining increasing importance in supporting physicians in endoscopic image analysis. However, large deep learning models require significant computational resources, which limit their use in computationally limited clinical environments. This study proposes a teacher-student architecture based on Knowledge Distillation to improve the classification performance of lightweight Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) models on endoscopic images. Twenty-one CNN models were trained using transfer learning and fine-tuning methods on the Kvasir v2 dataset. EfficientNetV2L, ResNet152V2, EfficientNetV2M, and EfficientNetB2 were selected as teacher models, while EfficientNetB1, MobileNetV2, and NASNetMobile were determined as student models. Knowledge distillation parameters were optimized to effectively transfer knowledge from teacher models to student models. The results showed that EfficientNetB1 achieved the highest F1-Score under all teacher models, reaching 0.9290 (1.42% improvement over baseline) when trained with EfficientNetV2L. MobileNetV2 and NASNetMobile also demonstrated notable F1-Score improvements of 4.46% and 19.34%, respectively, particularly with the EfficientNetV2M teacher model. These findings demonstrate that knowledge distillation enables lightweight models to achieve competitive performance while maintaining computational efficiency, making them suitable for use in clinical applications with limited resources.

title: Optimizing Cloud Resource Allocation with Predictive Big Data Models
authors: Mohammad Al Khaldy, Amjad Aldweesh, Ahmad Al-Qerem
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Cloud platforms increasingly host mixtures of latency-sensitive microservices and data-intensive analytics whose workloads vary at time scales comparable to autoscaler actuation delays. Reactive scaling policies therefore operate with inherent lag, leading to transient service-level objective (SLO) violations during demand surges and unnecessary overprovisioning when demand drops. Predictive autoscaling can reduce this lag, but many deployed and published approaches either (i) rely on point forecasts integrated through heuristic rules, or (ii) solve cost minimization without explicit treatment of forecast uncertainty and SLO risk. This paper presents Predictive Big-Data Model Resource Allocation (PBDMRA), a cloud-native control stack that couples large-scale telemetry processing with probabilistic forecasting, lightweight uncertainty calibration, and risk-bounded allocation. PBDMRA ingests metrics and traces (and can incorporate structured logs when available), constructs features using stream processing, produces multi-horizon predictive quantiles, calibrates empirical coverage via rolling residual adjustments, and computes scaling actions by solving a constrained optimization problem that trades cost against SLO risk and scaling churn. Trace-driven replay across microservice, streaming, and batch workloads indicates that PBDMRA reduces SLO violation minutes by 33-55relative to Kubernetes HPA and a point-forecast baseline, while also reducing scaling actions.

title: The Capacity of the Weighted Read Channel
authors: Omer Yerushalmi, Tuvi Etzion, Eitan Yaakobi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Nanopore sequencing is emerging as a powerful tool for storing digital information in DNA molecules. Its advantages over traditional sequencing methods such as support for long reads, scalability, and relatively low cost, making it an attractive and growing area of research. A fundamental aspect of this technology is the nature of its output: instead of reading individual bases in isolation, the sequencer observes overlapping segments of the DNA sequence due to inter-symbol interference (ISI). Similar readout mechanisms also arise in emerging memory technologies such as domain wall (racetrack) memory, where transverse-read techniques can sense the number of stored bits within a region without shifting any domains. Motivated by this analogy, we study theoretical models that capture the fundamental structure of such overlapping read processes. In this work, we study a simplified model of the nanopore sequencing process, known as the (ℓ, δ)-read channel. This channel operates by applying a sliding window of length ℓ across a DNA sequence and shifting it by δ positions after each read. The output of each window is a numerical value, which, in our model, corresponds to the sum (or Hamming weight) of the bases within that window. The collection of these outputs forms what we refer to as the read vector. Due to overlapping windows, multiple input sequences can map to the same read vector, leading to ambiguity. The main object of interest in this work is the capacity of the (ℓ, δ)-read channel, defined as the number of distinct output sequences that can be generate by this channel. Previous studies have determined the capacity for specific combinations of ℓ and δ, such as when ℓ ≤ δ, when ℓ is a multiple of δ, and for certain small values of ℓ and δ. In this study, we extend the current knowledge by addressing more general cases. Our first main result is a closed-form expression for the capacity when δ < ℓ < 2δ, given by: 1/δ log2 ( 1/2 (ℓ + 1 + √(ℓ + 1)2 − 4(ℓ − δ)(ℓ − δ + 1) )). This result is derived by analyzing a deterministic finite-state representation of the read channel and applying tools from graph theory, particularly the Perron–Frobenius Theorem. Next, we consider the case where ℓ ≥ 2δ and δ does not divide ℓ. For this setting, we construct a constrained code that yields an upper bound on the capacity, improving upon known bounds and further refining our understanding of this more complex regime. Finally, we extend the model to the two-dimensional setting. Motivated by the physical layout of some sequencing platforms and potential future applications, we propose and analyze a two-dimensional version of the weighted read channel and we present initial results on its capacity.

title: A Novel Deep Learning Framework with Feature Engineering for Stroke Risk Prediction
authors: Ahmad Al Athamneh, Mahmoud E. Farfoura, Abdel Fawaz Bagoudou, Saba Alshatali
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Stroke is the principal cause of mortality and long term disability all over the world with the known risk factors including old age, high blood pressure, diabetes, obesity, and tobacco use. The proper identification of the people at risk is a key to successful prevention. In this work, a residual-gated deep neural network is suggested as the model to predict the stroke risk in the cases of structured healthcare data, using clinically-motivated feature engineering, class-imbalance management, and interpretability evaluation. In order to overcome the extreme class imbalance that can occur in model training, Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE) is used. Interaction terms are added to the feature space, and the interaction-based composite clinical risk scores are utilized to increase the representational capacity. The suggested architecture uses gating mechanisms, residual connections, batch normalization and dropout so that it can be trained steadily. There is experimental analysis on a public healthcare dataset that has shown to perform well in terms of discriminative evaluation reaching a ROC-AUC of 0.976 with balanced performance in terms of precision and recall. The model interpretability can be provided based on permutation feature importance and probability distribution analysis, and calibration curves can also be used to evaluate the credibility of the risk score that has been predicted. These findings present the possibilities of integrating clinically informed feature engineering with state-of-the-art neural designs to predict the risk of stroke in controlled experimental contexts.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Multi-Head Self-Attention and Axial Attention in Transformer Architectures
authors: Amine Berquedich, Lahbib Ajallouda, Ahmed Zellou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Transformer architectures have become foundational models across natural language processing, computer vision, and multimodal learning, primarily due to the effectiveness of self-attention mechanisms. However, the quadratic computational complexity of standard self-attention limits scalability for high-dimensional inputs, particularly in vision tasks requiring high-resolution processing. Axial attention has emerged as a promising alternative that factorizes attention along individual axes to improve efficiency while maintaining global receptive fields. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of Multi-Head SelfAttention and Axial Attention, examining their theoretical foundations, computational properties, representational capacity, and practical trade-offs across domains. Through empirical evaluation on medical image segmentation using a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Transformer architecture, we demonstrate that while MHSA achieves marginally higher accuracy, Axial Attention provides substantial computational efficiency gains, enabling processing of higher resolution inputs with significantly reduced memory footprint. Our analysis provides practical guidelines for selecting appropriate attention mechanisms based on task requirements, computational constraints, and input characteristics.

title: Finite-Time LQR Control With Redundant Inputs for Continuous-Time Systems
authors: Jianzhou Liu, Ze Zhang, Hongzheng Quan, Tianliang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article investigates the effects of redundant control inputs on the finite-time linear quadratic regulator (LQR). Despite the widespread application of finite-time LQR control, characterizing the impact of redundancy remains challenging due to its time-varying nature. To address this gap, we first derive novel lower and upper bounds for the solution of the Riccati matrix differential equation by leveraging key matrix and eigenvalue inequalities. Building on these bounds, we establish a sufficient condition that guarantees a simultaneous reduction in both the optimal quadratic performance index and the controller gain. Furthermore, a deterministic algorithm is presented to facilitate the practical design of the weighting matrices. The effectiveness and engineering applicability of the proposed approach are demonstrated through numerical simulations and a case study on a fluid catalytic cracking reactor.

title: Target-centered Micro-motion Tracking and Measurement with Commodity WiFi
authors: Zijun Han, Zixuan Huang, Shurong Ge, Zhaoming Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Wireless signals-based radio tracking and measurement has emerged as a significant avenue for innovation in human-computer interaction. Among various wireless sensing modalities such as acoustic signals, visible light, Radio Frequency (RF), etc., the WiFi-based technique holds significant promise owing to its pervasiveness and cost-effectiveness. However, constrained by the limitations ofWiFi’s low frequency, small antenna array and bandwidth, current state-of-the-art systems can only achieve an accuracy on the order of decimeters. Many sophisticated micro-motion applications, such as aerial handwriting tracking, necessitate a significantly higher level of accuracy than what current state-of-the-arts can provide. In this paper, we present MicroFi, which targets to measure the micro-motion of WiFi radios in view of the Channel State Information (CSI). To be specific, we devise an innovative inside-out incremental motion tracking model to convert the tracking task from a transceiver view to a target-centered view. This model operates independently of any prior knowledge regarding anchor locations and demonstrates robustness against occlusions. Additionally, we propose a two-way communication protocol for CSI bidirectional collection, and eliminate clock asynchronization-induced random frequency offsets by channel reciprocity. We employ a 3D Multiple Signal Classification algorithm to jointly estimate the arrival angle, time of flight and Doppler velocity of the tracking target, and then derive the motion traces by multi-anchor aggregation. We have prototyped MicroFi utilizing commercially available WiFi radios, and comprehensive experiments confirm that MicroFi is capable of achieving an accuracy of 1.8 cm across a variety of user scenarios and environmental conditions, positioning it as a promising solution for numerous motion measurement applications.
