Existing Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) often struggle with asynchronous traffic and lack interpretability. To overcome these limitations, we propose AegisNet, a novel sequence-based IDS that reformulates intrusion detection as a sequence classification task on fixed-length windows of T consecutive observations (sequence length), augmented with explicit time-delta features to capture irregular traffic intervals. AegisNet introduces a novel Temporal-Aware and Self-Adaptive Multi-Head Attention (TA-SAMHA) mechanism. It introduces learnable temporal bias projections to directly encode irregular time intervals into the attention process, enabling the system to capture fine-grained temporal dependencies and evolving behaviors. It also employs a Dual-Gate Residual Encoder (DGRE) for bidirectional sequence modeling with dual gating, enabling enriched contextual representations and stable learning across deep temporal layers. Additionally, we introduce the IDS-specific adaptation of Temporal SHAP (T-SHAP), enabling time-resolved feature attributions that expose how influential factors shift during sequential intrusion detection, offering a new dimension of temporally aware explainability for evolving attack patterns. Experimental results demonstrate that AegisNet achieves a detection accuracy of 99.63% on the CICIoV2024 dataset and 99.13% on the CICIoT2023 dataset, demonstrating its effectiveness for securing Industrial environments.

title: IoT-Enabled SnO₂-Based Humidity Sensor for Real-Time Monitoring in Neonatal Incubators
authors: Poundoss Chellamuthu, Kirubaveni Savarimuthu, Gulam Nabi Alsath Mohammed, Biancaa Ramesh
publish: IEEE Transactions on NanoBioscience
abstract: This research presents the development and optimization of a Tin Oxide (SnO₂)-based resistive humidity sensor for accurate monitoring in incubator systems. SnO₂ nanomaterials were synthesized using hydrothermal synthesis technique, followed by comprehensive characterization through X-ray diffraction (XRD), field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM), current-voltage (J-V) analysis, ultravioletvisible (UV-Vis) spectroscopy, and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). The best-performing sensor exhibited a flower-like nanostructure with a response time of 18 s, a recovery time of 14 s, and a sensitivity of 72.4%. The sensor functioned effectively within the required incubator humidity range of 40–60% relative humidity (RH) and demonstrated superior performance compared to the commercial DHT11 sensor. To ensure accuracy and reliability, machine learning algorithms were applied to estimate error and validate the correctness of the sensed humidity data. The optimized SnO₂ sensor was integrated into an incubator system, with real-time data transmission enabled through an Internet of Things (IoT) platform. These findings highlight the potential of SnO₂-based sensors for high-precision humidity monitoring in biomedical applications, particularly for neonatal care where stable humidity levels are critical.

title: A Continuous Finite-Time Tracking Controller for Nonholonomic-Unicycle Vehicles
authors: Angel I. Paredes, Emmanuel Cruz-Zavala, Antonio Loría, Emmanuel Nuño
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a tracking controller for first-order nonholonomic-unicycle vehicles that guarantees stabilization in finite time. Besides the perks of finite-time stabilization (such as robustness), a significant advantage of our controller is its mathematical simplicity; while existing finite-time controllers often rely on discontinuous switching terms ours is continuous. This is important since the robot is velocity-controlled. Furthermore, in contrast to other controllers achieving finite-time stabilization ours relies only on the knowledge of the desired reference velocities and the system maintains stable in the presence of unknown, non-zero reference accelerations. We establish uniform global finite-time stability (UGFTS) via Lyapunov theory and illustrate our theoretical findings with numerical simulations.

title: Wavelength Dependence and Transient Photoresponse Mechanisms in Aerosol-Jet Printed ZnO Nanowire Ultraviolet Photodetectors
authors: Chenkun Lyu, Haoyi Lin, Joseph Andrews
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation poses significant health risks, motivating the development of reliable UV sensing systems. This paper presents an aerosol-jet-printed ZnO nanowire UV photodetector fabricated on a Si/SiO₂ substrate using printed interdigitated Ag electrodes and a ZnO nanowire sensing layer. The device exhibits strong wavelength- and intensity-dependent photoresponse under 340 nm and 375 nm UV illumination at a 1 V bias. At the same irradiance of 60 μW·cm⁻², the photocurrent reached ∼0.9 mA at 340 nm and ∼0.015 mA at 375 nm, corresponding to responsivities of 31.71 A/W and 3.67 A/W, respectively. Transient measurements reveal pronounced persistent photoconductivity (PPC) and strong dependence of recovery behavior on illumination conditions. In addition, thermal cycling experiments and a 10-nm Al₂O₃ atomic layer deposition (ALD) encapsulation layer were used to investigate the role of oxygen-related surface processes in carrier transport and recovery dynamics. These results provide insight into the transient photoresponse mechanisms of ZnO nanowire UV photodetectors and the influence of surface interactions on device behavior.

title: Clustering of Equipment for Maintenance in BIM 7D Using the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) Approach Applied to Data Reduced by t-SNE (t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding)
authors: Adnane Abdellaoui Alaoui, Mouna El Mkhalet, Nouzha Lamdouar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Building Information Modeling (BIM) in its 7D dimension incorporates maintenance management and life cycle cost of technical equipment. Optimizing this maintenance requires intelligent methods capable of grouping equipment according to their operational behaviors and characteristics. This article proposes a supervised clustering approach using the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm applied to dimensionally reduced data using the t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) technique. The objective is to visualize and classify equipment based on aging criteria, failure frequency, temperature, duration, and intensity of use. The results show that this $t-S N E+K N N$ combination allows for distinguishing three main groups of equipment with similar maintenance behaviors, paving the way for predictive models integrated into 7D BIM.)

title: Exploration of Engineering Educational Models based on CDIO and TPACK
authors: Meriem El Batoul Eddaif, Mohamed Tabaa, Souad Ahriz, Es-Saadia Aoula
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The 21st century has witnessed a profound digital transformation in higher education, particularly within engineering education institutions. While the CDIO (Conceive–Design–Implement–Operate) framework promotes experiential and project-based learning grounded in real engineering practice, there is no clear approach for operationalizing technology, pedagogy, and content knowledge to enhance these experiential learning opportunities in a way that aligns seamlessly with CDIO standards. Conversely, the TPACK (technological, pedagogical and content knowledge) framework offers a theoretical foundation for teaching but lacks contextual anchoring in authentic engineering workflows. In order to facilitate the methodical creation of successful engineering learning experiences, this paper proposes an integrated CDIO–TPACK pedagogical model that integrates TPACK competencies with CDIO standards. The model provides educators with an organised approach to curriculum design, instructional planning, and assessment by clearly mapping CDIO standards to content knowledge, pedagogical knowledge and technological knowledge. A structured workflow for a Python programming course given to first-year engineering students demonstrates the applicability of the proposed framework into instructional designs that promote active learning and close the gap between theory and practice.

title: Utilizing Academic Analytic Dashboards in Improving Student Graduation Rates
authors: Walid Ibrahim
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The growing complexity of higher education institutions has elevated the need for academic analytics dashboards as a critical tool for enhancing institutional effectiveness, facilitating evidence-based decision-making, and supporting continuous quality improvement. This paper discusses the importance of academic analytic dashboards and presents the United Arab Emirates University (UAEU) as a case study of effective institutional implementation. The UAEU dashboard provides a comprehensive suite of interactive pages designed to support data-informed decision-making across multiple dimensions of academic performance. Among these, this paper focuses on three core pages that are particularly critical for academic planning and enhancing student graduation rate. These pages deliver real-time, insights into course offerings, student registration, as well as graduation, and retention patterns. The dashboard provides academic administrators with the needed insights to adjust course capacities, add or consolidate sections, and allocate instructional resources more efficiently based on emerging trends. Furthermore, the timely detection of registration anomalies or at-risk patterns allows for targeted interventions that help students stay on track with their academic plans. Collectively, these features enhance institutional responsiveness, promote better resource allocation, and ultimately contribute to improving student progression and graduation outcomes.

title: Optimal Control for Managing the Dynamics of Two Computer Virus Co-Infection
authors: Bashir Abdullahi Baba
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study presents a mathematical model describing the simultaneous spread of two distinct computer viruses within a networked system, taking into account vertical transmission for one virus as well as treatment strategies for both infections. The model is used to derive the basic reproduction number of the combined system, which helps determine the conditions under which the infections either die out or persist. We analyze the disease-free and endemic equilibrium states to better understand the longterm dynamics of the system. To identify effective mitigation strategies, optimal control theory is incorporated into the model, and Pontryagin’s Maximum Principle is applied to establish the necessary conditions for minimizing infection levels and associated intervention costs. Numerical simulations are performed to demonstrate how the proposed control measures influence the spread of infection. The results indicate that combining equilibrium analysis with carefully designed control strategies can substantially reduce infection prevalence while keeping costs at a reasonable level, providing useful insights for managing co-infections in computer networks.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Large-Scale Water Quality Assessment
authors: Anuj Kumar Jain, Manu Midha, Narinder Kaur, Geetanjali Sharma, Arushi Vaish, Shaveta Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Water quality monitoring is crucial for environmental sustainability as well as public health and traditional laboratory-based water quality assessment methods are time-consuming and costly and difficult to scale. This study introduces a machine learning based framework for efficient classification of water quality using a large scale environmental data. A dataset with more than 2.5 million data records after cleaning containing 8 most important physicochemical parameters, namely: pH, temperature, turbidity, nitrate, phosphate, hardness, chlorides and dissolved oxygen is used. Comprehensive preprocessing steps such as Cleaning of the data, Normalizing of data, Feature selection and Handling Imbalance in classes are taken up to make the data set rely-able data. Multiple machine learning models namely Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest and Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) are evaluated. Experimental results show that the LightGBM model is better than other methods and its accuracy is 98.6% and other evaluation indexes (precision, recall and F1-score) are better. Comparative analysis is a method to prove the robustness and scalability principle of the proposed mechanism in the case of big data. Furthermore, the trained model deployment is done using a Streamlit-based web interface for performing the water quality prediction in real-time. The proposed framework is a timely and cost-effective way of tackling the issue of water quality assessment with possible applications in environmental monitoring, policy making and public health management.

title: Enhancing Small and Medium Sized Enterprises Sustainability: Material Flow Cost Accounting as an Enabler for Circular Economy Thinking
authors: Rahajeng Simpony Santoso, Ang Swat Lin Lindawati, Agustinus Winoto
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of Material Flow Cost Accounting (MFCA) and Green Accounting on circular economy practices among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in West Jakarta, Indonesia. Data were collected from 410 SME owners and managers through a structured questionnaire and analyzed using the Smart- PLS technique. The results reveal that both MFCA and Green Accounting exert positive and significant effects on SMEs’ adoption of circular economy principles. The integration of these two accounting approaches enhances operational efficiency, reduces production waste, and strengthens environmental accountability. The study contributes to sustainability accounting literature by simultaneously testing MFCA and Green Accounting within the SME context-an area that remains underexplored in developing economies. Practically, the findings provide insights for SMEs, policymakers, and academics in promoting resource-efficient and environmentally responsible business practices

title: Learning-Driven Job Recommendation Systems: A Comprehensive Review of Deep Semantic, Transformer-Enhanced, Ontology-based and NLP-Bidirectional Approaches
authors: Velusamy A, Aishwarya S, Chellam N, Devipriya R, Prabhu R, Vasudevan I
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Deep learning, semantic matching, natural language processing (NLP), and transformer-based systems have significantly improved job recommendation systems. Such developments respond to the increased complexity of the job market, the large data about high dimensional skills, dynamically changing employed requirements, and the need of increasingly individualized employment support. The review is based on the contributions of recent literature in the area of matched representation learning, semantic structure modeling, attention mechanisms, transformer-based framework, reciprocal recommendation model, ontology-enhanced system, and resume fusion system. Ten advanced methodologies are compared in terms of data modeling strategy, interpretability, bidirectionality, scalability, adaptability to labor-market development, and relevance to different groups of people such as college graduates, migrants, and vocational learners. The paper also outlines some of the major constraints of classical job-matching pipelines and suggests a hybrid architecture, which combines semantic skill extraction, contextual modeling through transformers, ontology-based filtering, and the two-way resume-job matching. Comparative findings demonstrate higher accuracy, relevance ranking, explainability, and robustness. Future directions include multimodal-resumes-understanding, adaptivity through reinforcement-learning, cross-regional-labor-market-intelligence-engine-based.

title: AI-Augmented Healthcare Communication: A Review of Large Language Model Healthcare Agents and Generative AI in EHR Integration
authors: Shivam Tyagi, Amit Kumar Goyal, Deepti Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) and large language models (LLMs) are among artificial intelligence (AI) innovations that have reshaped the concept of healthcare communication. Such technologies are being used to facilitate both synchronous and asynchronous communications: LLCM-based healthcare agents making structured consultations with patients, and GenAI-generated drafts helping clinicians respond to electronic health records (EHR) messages. The current review brings together recent research literature on these two paradigms that are complementary with regard to their effectiveness in clinical work, implications on workflow, and dilemmas related to them. An experimental and randomized research shows that healthcare agents significantly improve the inquiry proactivity, diagnostic relevance, and communication safety over baseline LLMs, whereas GenAI drafts augment reply empathy and length, but less frequently at the expense of increased review time. A comparative study shows that neither of the two paradigms can fully address issues of physician workload, personalization and medico-legal accountability. Rather, an intermediate system between patient-facing actors and physician-facing EHR draft can provide a moderate solution. Other challenges that are open to review in the paper are the personalization of physician style, integration of multi-modal data, and adherence to privacy rules including HIPAA, and GDPR. We conclude by a research roadmap highlighting the aspects of hybrid integration, personalization, and stringent assessment schemes of AI-assisted healthcare communication.

title: A Multi-Stage XAI-Enhanced Transformer Model for Bias-Aware Decision Making in Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Yuvaraj Duraisamy, Rabins Porwal, Kshamta Chauhan, P. Ramya, Kunal, Sribidhya Mohanty
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Loss of transparency and bias in the detection of financial fraud presents two significant problems, namely, poor adherence to the standards of fraud detection, and lost user confidence. In order to address these issues, we have come up with a Multi-Stage XAI-Enhanced Transformer Model that is used to deal with bias-conscious decision making in fraud detection. We use a combination of three aspects Transformers to identify complex pattern of temporal frauds, adversarial debiasing to make fair predictions across the various demographic groups and explainability by visualization of attention and SHAP analysis. We achieve 97.3% accuracy, 98.1% recall, and 99.2% AUC-ROC, better than current algorithms, such as XGBoost (95.1%) and Random Forests (93.8%). Better still, we maintained demographic parity disparity at less than 0.02 which reduced bias by 89.8 per cent relative to conventional systems. The SHAP analysis revealed that the most decisions are made basing on the Transaction Amount, Time of Transaction, and Merchant Category which provides the stakeholders with a clear understanding of the operation of the model. Such a balance between explainability and bias minimization forms a fraud protection system that is both non-discriminatory and credible enough, as required by the regulations and ethical principles of the fintech sector.

title: Deep Semantic Feature Learning and Efficient Indexing for Content-Based Image Retrieval
authors: Khushbu Devi Tiwari, Swati Khanve, Nitya Khare, Vinay Lowanshi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A lot of organizations and people have huge amounts of images. These pictures can be used for training data, visualization, marketing, etc. Searching through picture archives can become an overwhelming task because of the amount of data. Deep features require efficient indexing and storage methods for image retrieval that is scalable and efficient. Content-based image retrieval (CBIR) is the system that allows access to and search for images based on their contents. CBIR makes it easier for people to look for information or products by efficiently searching and browsing the world of images on the internet. Manually extracting images of interest as per query is a tedious process. CBIR plays an important role a better alternative to retrieval of images on a large scale. It also helps for quicker and easier detection than manual detection. The system generates feature vectors for every pixel in the image for further analysis of the image and the object. The system does not rely on any content (text or metadata) surrounding the image but on the actual content in the image.Systems based on handcrafted features of CBIR do not have sufficient semantic representation and scalability. We need to find a better way to extract content from images. Additionally, efficient and scalable algorithms for indexing are also needed.

title: Optimizing Linux Kernel Scheduler Parameters Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hitesh Makka, Kartikey, Preeti Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The Linux kernel’s Completely Fair Scheduler (CFS) is a cornerstone of modern operating systems, but its static, heuristic-based design often fails to deliver optimal performance for specialized, high-demand workloads. This paper investigates the application of a modern reinforcement learning algorithm, Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (A3C), to dynamically tune nine critical CFS parameters. An agent was trained in a live kernel environment on a PostgreSQL OLTP workload to learn an optimal scheduling policy. The evaluation reveals a significant performance trade-off. The A3C-optimized parameters significantly improved throughput for CPU-intensive tasks, achieving a 47.4% faster completion time on the Hackbench scheduler stress test and a 14.8% increase in requests-per-second for an Apache high-concurrency test. However, this gain came at a significant cost to latency-sensitive applications, with the same policy causing a 7.6% performance regression and a seven-fold increase in instability on the Redis benchmark. The principal contribution of this work is the empirical evaluation of the A3C algorithm for live kernel parameter tuning, characterizing its effects and revealing trade-offs between throughput gains and low-latency performance.

title: Cognitive Networking using Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Optimization
authors: Jobin Thomas, Riya Sanjesh, Devi Priya V.S., Galiveeti Poornima
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Modern communication networks are becoming more complex due to factors such as the rapid proliferation of varied devices, changing traffic patterns, and tight quality-of-service standards. It is challenging to respond in real time with rule-based or model-driven network management in such dynamic contexts. Cognitive networking holds great promise as it enables networks to see, understand, and adjust autonomously. This study presents a cognitive networking framework for optimising networks in real-time that is based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL). Intelligent and adaptive optimisation of routing, resource allocation, and congestion control is possible when network control is viewed as a sequential decision-making issue. By interacting with the network environment and learning the necessary rules from experience, the framework adapts to new scenarios without network models. The experimental results demonstrate that cognitive networking based on DRL outperforms typical optimisation methods in terms of latency, throughput, and packet loss, all while being scalable and stable.

title: Ensemble Strategies for LLM-based Text Summarization: A Timing-Based Framework
authors: Rooz Munjal, Sonali Goyal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: One of the biggest problems with big language models (LLMs) is finding the right balance between accuracy, cost, and latency while also cutting down on hallucinations. This research presents a temporal categorization of ensemble techniques (EMs) into Ensemble Before Inference (EBI), Ensemble During Inference (EDI), and Ensemble After Inference (EAI). A systematic review of 11 ensemble studies (2022–2025) employing transformers in multimodal domains reveals distinct performance–cost trade-offs. Ensemble approaches significantly enhance hallucination detection, medical and legal summarization, multi-domain tasks, and low-resource language processing, yielding performance improvements between 2% to 33% compared to single models. The temporal taxonomy shows that each strategy has its own strengths: EBI reduces runtime overhead while keeping quality, EDI improves output quality at a higher computational cost, and EAI is a balanced and versatile option. This framework gives academics and practitioners a structured way to choose the best ensemble techniques depending on the needs of the application and the resources available.

title: EchoGuard: Biometric Voice-based Audio Watermarking with Blockchain-Enabled Ownership Verification
authors: Padmini Devi B, Vishnu M S, Saaidarun S, Vinith M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital audio content has made ownership verification and tamper detection increasingly challenging. Existing approaches, including conventional watermarking and blockchain-based timestamping, fail to establish a reliable and identity-linked association between audio content and its creator. To address this limitation, this paper proposes EchoGuard, a hybrid AI–blockchain framework for secure audio ownership verification. The proposed system integrates biometric speaker embeddings, neural audio watermarking, and decentralized blockchain registration into a unified pipeline. A creator-specific voiceprint is first extracted using a deep speaker representation model and converted into a compact watermark payload. This payload is then embedded into the host audio using a deep learning-based steganographic model, ensuring imperceptibility while maintaining robustness against common signal processing attacks. The system further stores cryptographic hashes of the watermarked audio on a blockchain to provide immutable and verifiable ownership records. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves high watermark extraction accuracy and strong robustness under compression, noise, and resampling attacks, while preserving audio quality. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining biometric watermarking with blockchain to enable reliable, tamper-resistant audio authentication.

title: A Deep Learning Approach to Improving Multilingual Neural Machine Translation using Semantic and Linguistic Features
authors: Yaxi Su, Mohamad Yusof Darus, Norizan Mat Diah, Yuxia Du, Ling Li, Yang Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study presents a novel semantic-linguistic feature decoupling framework designed to significantly enhance the performance and generalization capabilities of multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT). By employing a specialized dual-channel feedforward network, the proposed approach explicitly separates language-agnostic semantic representations from language-specific linguistic features. This structural decoupling facilitates more robust cross-lingual knowledge transfer and effectively mitigates interference between diverse languages. Furthermore, the framework is integrated with a lightweight Transformer architecture that leverages dynamic sparse attention mechanisms and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). This combination ensures both exceptional computational efficiency and high-quality translation outputs. Comprehensive experimental evaluations on standard benchmarks such as IWSLT2017 demonstrate remarkable gains: the model achieves a substantial BLEU score improvement of +3.74 in zero-shot translation directions. Simultaneously, it optimizes operational efficiency by reducing memory usage by 62.5% and increasing inference speed threefold. Ultimately, this framework successfully addresses the critical issue of off-target generation, offering a scalable solution suitable for deployment in diverse resource-constrained environments.

title: Early Detection of Hypokinetic Rigid Syndrome Through Vocal Biomarker Analysis Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Rohan Dhumane, Rahul Bhandekar, Prashant Tarone, Nilesh Nagrale, Jayesh Bisen, Jiwan N. Dehankar, Nutan Dhande
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Hypokinetic Rigid Syndrome leaves vocal traces before visible symptoms appear. This study demonstrates how artificial intelligence detects these changes, enabling earlier diagnosis. We analyzed 400 voice recordings examining 23 acoustic characteristics revealing hidden disease patterns. Using multiple machine learning approaches, Convolutional Neural Networks achieved 97% accuracy distinguishing healthy from Parkinson's-affected voices, outperforming K- Nearest Neighbors and Support Vector Machines. CNNs overcome classical limitations by automatically learning complex patterns without handcrafted features. Voice recordings are non-invasive, inexpensive, and easily collected, ideal for widespread screening and telemedicine. While data standardization and validation challenges remain, this research advances accessible early detection tools deployable via smartphones, giving patients crucial intervention time.

title: Toward Portable and Energy-Efficient Home Hemodialysis: Clinical, Economic, and Technical Review with Engineering Perspectives
authors: Ilyasse Boujraf, Yassine Zahidi, Mohamed El Moufid, Hassan Gziri, Wafaa Dachry, Hicham Medromi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Home hemodialysis (HHD) offers a promising alternative to in-center hemodialysis (ICHD), providing improved quality of life and treatment flexibility. This review presents a comparative analysis of HHD versus ICHD with respect to cost-effectiveness and quality of life, based on a structured literature search in the Scopus database. In addition, a comprehensive technical evaluation of four commercial HHD systems (Quanta SC+, NxStage System One, Physidia $\mathrm{S}^{3}$, and Tablo) is conducted, focusing on key design parameters including weight and dimensions, dialysate delivery strategies, circuit configurations, consumables requirements, and portability characteristics. The analysis highlights three major, interconnected technological challenges in current HHD systems: high dialysate consumption, significant energy demand, and extensive use of disposable consumables, collectively impacting system cost and portability. Proposed directions are intended to serve as a structured engineering perspective to guide future research. Experimental validation, system modeling, and prototype implementation are identified as necessary steps toward developing cost-effective, energyefficient, and portable HHD technologies.

title: Ontology-Enriched Abstract Topic Modelling for Biomedical Trend Discovery: A MeSH-based LDA Framework with BI Visual Analytics
authors: Ahmad Altarawneh, Stephen Swift, Allan Tucker, Mahir Arzoky
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The explosive growth of biomedical literature increasingly makes it difficult to identify incipient topics and long-term developments. Citation-based indicators are slow to capture developments and often fail to represent influence. Whilst Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) supports content-based trend discovery, its bag-of-words assumption can overlook synonymy, polysemy, and hierarchical relations that are central to biomedical terminology. Moreover, recent work provides limited direct evidence on how far MeSH-enriched abstract representations improve LDA-based trend analysis when compared against the original abstract text. To fill this gap, an ontology-driven methodology for the pre-topic modelling of article abstracts by enriching them with hierarchical Medical Subject Headings (MeSH) terms is introduced in this study. We evaluate four representations, namely original abstracts, MeSH parent categories, MeSH-matched terms with parents, and a fully enriched representation, using a decade-long Scopus corpus of 624,486 biomedical journal articles (2015–2024). Model performance is assessed using coherence, distinctiveness, perplexity, and stability. The fully enriched representation achieves the strongest overall results (coherence 0.606, distinctiveness 0.996, stability 0.725, perplexity 1782.040) relative to the original abstract baseline. Temporal topic analysis further reveals interpretable patterns, including the COVID-19 surge and longer-term structural shifts such as the rise of machine-learning diagnostics. These findings indicate that MeSH-guided abstract enrichment can strengthen topic quality and support more effective biomedical trend discovery through business-intelligence visual analytics.

title: Assessing the Efficacy of Pre-trained Deep Learning Models for Facial Expression Recognition
authors: Maryam Alkandari, Alaa Eleyan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition (FER) is a fundamental problem in affective computing and human– computer interaction, with applications in healthcare, surveillance, and intelligent user interfaces. Recent advances in deep learning, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs), have significantly improved FER performance by leveraging transfer learning from large-scale image datasets. This paper presents a comparative performance analysis of four state-of-the-art pre-trained CNN architectures, namely, ResNet152, EfficientNetV2-L, Xception, and DenseNet201 which are applied to facial expression recognition using the Extended Cohn–Kanade (CK+) dataset. The experiments were conducted on eight emotion classes with data augmentation applied to enhance generalization. All models were fine-tuned using the Adam optimizer under identical experimental conditions. Performance evaluation was carried out using confusion matrices and class-wise, micro-averaged, and weighted-averaged metrics. Among the evaluated models, DenseNet201 achieved the highest accuracy of 89.25%, outperforming EffiecientNetV2-L by 1.08% under identical training conditions. This indicates that dense feature reuse is particularly effective for capturing subtle facial expression patterns, while EfficientNetV2-L provides a balanced trade-off between accuracy and model efficiency

title: Deep Learning-Based Solar Energy Forecasting Using Environmental Time-Series Data
authors: Geethanjali Guthi, Pavan Kumar Didde, Zuber Shaik, Siva Mohan Yadav Vangala, Muralidhar Kurni
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: With the global transition toward renewable energy, accurately forecasting solar energy production is pivotal for enhancing grid reliability, optimizing energy storage, and facilitating sustainable distribution. This project presents a comprehensive analysis of energy prediction using three distinct models: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, Linear Regression, and Random Forest. Leveraging environmental inputs such as temperature and humidity, hourly time-series data were processed and normalized using a 24-hour input sequence to capture daily patterns. Model performance was rigorously evaluated using Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE). Comparative results highlight the superior accuracy of the LSTM model, reflecting its ability to learn long-term dependencies in sequential weather data. Furthermore, the project includes a 7-day (168-hour) future energy forecast and aggregated daily estimates, offering actionable insights for solar energy planning and optimization. This work demonstrates how deep learning approaches can outperform traditional models in complex, time-sensitive domains, such as energy forecasting.

title: Radiation Resistant Reinforcement and Integration Technology for 650V Half Bridge Gate Drive circuit
authors: Ning Tang, Liqiang Ding, Wei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Within the space electronics industry, several strategies have been implemented to mitigate radiation effects in integrated circuit fabricating processes, including radiation resistant reinforcement through process technology alterations and through circuit design, layout, and architecture. In this work, multi-method has been used to reinforce the high voltage half bridge driving circuit radiation resistance ability. These methods include fabricating in 1.0 m 650 V SOI (Silicon-On-Insulator) BCD (Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS) process, using ring gate devices, add negative feedback regulation loop and layout optimization. This work analyzes the total irradiation dose (TID) induced performance shifts in three critical circuits of the half bridge gate driving chip and uses techniques to repair and improve the gate driving circuit performance. The three circuits include: 15 V/650 V level shift circuit, 650 V ring LDMOS (Laterally-Diffused-Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor) and high/low side driving circuit. Experiment results shown that the proposed design circuit with a chip area of 3.88 mm × 2.80 mm can achieve a TID close to 100 krad(Si).

title: Wearable-Based Emotion Recognition Using Electrocardiogram and Galvanic Skin Response and Instrumentation Audit: A Systematic Review
authors: Ayse Kosal Bulbul, Muhammad Irfan, Maria K. Jaakkola, Abdulhamit Subasi, Riku Klén
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Simple and unobtrusive wearables, particularly watches and wristbands, now provide continuous monitoring with no inconvenience and are widely used by elderly people. Effective emotion recognition relies not only on advances in models but also on methods for signal acquisition and specification. In response to this imperative, we performed a systematic review of emotion recognition utilising wearable physiological signals, focusing on two practical modalities: electrocardiography (ECG), which indicates cardiac dynamics and emotional arousal, and galvanic skin response (GSR), a measure of electrodermal activity (EDA) reflecting sympathetic nervous system activation and conveniently recordable at the wrist, which reflects sympathetic nervous system activation and can be conveniently recorded at the wrist. We examined four prominent publishers (ScienceDirect, SpringerLink, Taylor & Francis Online, IEEE Xplore) and identified 549 papers; after excluding 90 review articles and 75 papers focused solely on electroencephalography (EEG), 384 were subjected to thorough screening. After excluding 20 studies focused solely on stress and 316 papers without ECG/GSR data, 48 studies utilising machine learning were identified (2019–2025). Traditional models like support vector machines remain prevalent, though deep learning techniques, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and hybrids, are more effective at discerning complex temporal patterns. Simultaneously, we conduct an instrumentation and measurement (I&M) audit on commonly utilised datasets. Protocols and sampling rates are generally documented; however, essential measurement details are frequently absent: electrode type and positioning, skin–electrode impedance, signal quality indices (SQIs), calibration, repeatability, and interdevice comparability. These limitations hinder equitable performance attribution (sensing versus modelling), limit uncertainty assessment, and complicate hardware transfer and cross-study evaluation. Standardising I&M metadata in public releases, when combined with AI evaluation practices, improves comparability and dependability in healthcare predictions based on wearable physiological information.

title: Single-Stage Switched Capacitor and Inductor-based Modified Totem-Pole PFC Converter with High Step-Down Voltage Capability for LEV Chargers
authors: Sushant Gupta, Vignesh Kumar Vethanayagam, Karthikeyan Anbalagan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: In this article, a single-stage bridgeless switched-capacitor and inductor (SCI)-based modified totem-pole (MTP) PFC converter is proposed for light electric vehicle (LEV) charging applications. The proposed converter enables high step-down voltage conversion over an extended output range by integrating switched-capacitor and switched-inductor techniques within a totem-pole structure. Conventional boost-type PFC converters inherently provide step-up operation, requiring an additional DC-DC stage to obtain low output voltages, which increases component count, losses, and cost. To address this limitation, the proposed topology achieves high step-down conversion in a single stage, while reducing device stress and improving efficiency. A simplified control strategy using a single PWM signal further reduces implementation complexity. The feasibility of the proposed converter is validated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations and a 1-kW hardware prototype. Experimental results demonstrate high efficiency, low THD, and stable operation under steady-state and dynamic conditions with a 230 V, 50-Hz input supply.

title: A Soft Worm Robot for Sub-Centimeter Scale Pipeline Inspection_supp6-3699247.mp4
authors: Yitong Zhou
publish: N/A
abstract: Inspection of sub-centimeter pipelines with continuous spatial curvatures remains challenging due to extreme geo metric confinement. This paper presents an ultralight, trunkless worm-inspired robot actuated by a single twisted and coiled actuator (TCA) specifically optimized for short-range inspection within localized extreme spatial bottlenecks. By integrating actuation and structural support into a single TCA and eliminating rigid trunks and auxiliary recovery mechanisms, the robot achieves a compact and lightweight design, with a mass of 0.5–1.1 g, a length of 32 mm, and a diameter of 4–10 mm. Directional peristaltic locomotion is enabled by a wedge-based micro silicone frustum foot that provides passive frictional anisotropy without complex control. A simplified mechanics-based anchoring model captures the dominant directional friction asymmetry induced by foot geometry and guides parameter optimization. Experiments demonstrate a maximum friction anisotropy ratio of 4.75, enabling stable crawling at 0.40 mm/s horizontally and 0.13 mm/s vertically in 4–9 mm pipelines, including complex three dimensional curved configurations. Blind inspection experiments with an onboard micro-camera further validate the robot's potential for inspection in highly confined pipeline environments.

title: Integrating Lateral Transportation and Collaborative Energy Scheduling: A Bi-Level Programming Approach for Port Waste-Energy Management
authors: Junyu Yu, Ji Quan, Xianjia Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This study introduces a novel port waste transportation and energy scheduling coupled system, addressing existing research limitations that primarily focus on isolated waste transportation or disposal processes and lack integration of waste-to-energy technology with port energy management systems. The system integrates multiple treatment facilities equipped with waste-to-energy technology and multiple waste collection stations, establishing a dynamic link between solid waste transportation quantities and the energy output of treatment facilities, while considering collaborative transportation of solid waste and collaborative energy scheduling among facilities. To balance the conflicting objectives of port authority and treatment facilities, a bi-level programming model is proposed. After transforming the asymmetric Nash bargaining game and applying Karush-Kuhn-Tucker conditions, the model is solved using commercial solvers. Comparative analysis of different cases under uncertain renewable energy generation and electrical load conditions shows that integrating lateral transportation and collaborative energy scheduling achieves the lowest economic costs and highest environmental benefits. Specifically, compared to the baseline Case 1, Case 4, which integrates both lateral transportation and collaborative energy scheduling, reduces the average cost of the port authority by 0.95%, the average total cost of treatment facilities by 54.39%, the average carbon emissions of the port authority by 3.63%, the average total carbon emissions of treatment facilities by 11.94%, and the average electricity purchased by treatment facilities from the distribution grid by 31.44%. The proposed system offers viable solutions for the sustainable development of port waste management and energy scheduling.

title: Vehicle Classification Based on Multi-Frequency Impedance Magnetic Profiles in Distance Domain
authors: Zbigniew Marszalek, Filip Hallo, Dominik Rzepka, Krzysztof Duda
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Vehicle magnetic profile (signature) acquired from inductive loop embedded in a road is a well-established signal for vehicle classification. This paper demonstrates how classification errors can be almost entirely suppressed by the Multi-Frequency Impedance Measurement (MFIM) system along with the proposed application of data fusion and artificial neural network. Two slim and two wide inductive loops operating at different measurement frequencies are employed to acquire the magnetic signatures, extracting not only the reactance but also the resistance component. The influence of vehicle speed is eliminated by scaling the magnetic profile from the time domain into the distance domain. This allows efficient pattern learning application directly on the signature samples, without the necessity of feature extraction. Furthermore, a comprehensive optimization of the classification process is presented, including the impact of signal resolution in distance domain. It is confirmed, by means of extensive tests, that the number of samples, that are the classification features, in distance domain can be reduced significantly up to 40 times, in respect to original 1 cm resolution, and still a high classification accuracy is preserved. Data fusion from different channels, i.e. different inductive loops and measurement frequencies, is investigated using an artificial neural network classifier. Finally, an energy-efficient spiking neural network is employed, and tested.

title: CFP-WMP: A Coarse-to-Fine Environmental Perception Framework for Walking Mode Prediction
authors: Chaoyu Song, Chao Chen, Xiaoxu Zhang, Jian Xu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Walking mode prediction is a critical intelligent capability for powered lower-limb prostheses to achieve smooth transitions across different terrains. Inspired by human visual anticipation, this paper proposes CFP-WMP, a hierarchical coarse-to-fine environmental perception framework. The system operates through four integrated modules. Initially, a coarse perception module identifies potential transition-critical terrains, triggering a fine perception module for high-precision local mapping only when a target enters the projected heading area; this selective activation ensures computational efficiency via a low-power standby state. Concurrently, a foothold prediction module estimates subsequent contact points based on historical trajectories. Finally, a locomotion mode prediction module fuses terrain geometry with these predicted footholds to anticipate gait transitions (e.g., from level ground to stair ascent), providing essential feed-forward guidance for gait control. Deployed on an NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin, the framework achieved an average prediction accuracy of 98.92% across diverse lighting and weather conditions. With a processing latency of 92 ms per frame, the proposed framework demonstrates significant potential for real-time control, ensuring safer and more natural gait transitions for amputees in complex environments.

title: Toward Efficient Uplink Access in 6G LEO Satellite Networks: A Heartbeat-Based Design
authors: Zhenyu Cao, Swades De, Hu Jin, Jun-Bae Seo
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite integration is a key component of 6G non-terrestrial networks, but uplink (UL) access in LEO is fundamentally constrained by long round-trip time (RTT), rapid Doppler variation, and finite beam dwell time. For intermittent session traffic, the dominant cost is no longer a single random-access (RA) collision, but the repeated cold-start re-entry required after idle periods. Existing approaches therefore fail at opposite extremes: contention-based random access suffers from RTT-amplified retries, while terrestrial contention-free schemes assume warm synchronization or persistently reserved resources. This article identifies an intermediate UL access regime for session-oriented intermittent traffic and proposes a connection-lite (CL) mechanism that maintains lightweight access readiness rather than persistent resource reservation. By coupling synchronization refresh with access signaling through a periodic heartbeat, CL enables micro-grant-based UL transmission while reducing repeated RA and idle resource waste. Numerical results show that, for bursty session traffic, CL achieves substantially lower access delay and resource overhead compared to conventional schemes, making it a promising UL-access mode for satellite-enabled 6G networks.

title: Flood Inundation Extent Extraction Based on Multi-source Synergistic Features from GF-3 Dual-polarization SAR Data
authors: Rong Li, Denghua Yan, Wei Jiang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: In the context of global climate change, flood disasters triggered by extreme rainstorms occur frequently, posing a severe threat to the safety of people's lives and property. Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR), with its all-time and all-weather imaging capabilities, has become a critical tool for emergency flood monitoring. However, complex terrain and interference from ground features often limit the accuracy of traditional singlefeature extraction methods. This paper proposes a technical framework for the precise extraction of flood inundation extents based on Gaofen-3 (GF-3) dual-polarization SAR data, following a “preprocessing - multidimensional feature extraction - machine learning classification - post-processing optimization” workflow. Building upon radiometric calibration and terrain correction, this method constructs a 21-dimensional multi-source collaborative feature vector that includes polarimetric scattering, texture features, temporal changes, and terrain constraints. An optimized Random Forest algorithm is then utilized to achieve probabilistic water body classification. Focusing on the “ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23 \cdot 7$</tex> ” catastrophic flood event in the Haihe River Basin in 2023, the Dongdian Flood Storage and Detention Area was selected as the study area, and GF-3 imagery from August 11, 2023, was used for experimental validation. The results indicate that this method effectively overcomes interference from complex backgrounds, and the extracted inundation extent aligns closely with the geographical boundaries of the storage and detention area. Compared with concurrent high-resolution optical imagery, the Overall Accuracy (OA) reached 94.67 %, with a Kappa coefficient of 0.89. The findings confirm that this technical scheme possesses both high accuracy and high timeliness under extreme weather conditions, providing reliable spatial information support for flood control decision-making and post-disaster assessment.

title: Cascaded FOC-Based Speed Control of a PMSM Traction Drive for Electric Vehicles
authors: Mohamed Bounagui, Tamou Nasser, Ahmed Essadki
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper focuses on the control of high-power Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) speed for traction drive in electric vehicles. The electromechanical dynamic behavior is formulated in state space representation that can be readily controlled. A cascaded Field-Oriented Control (FOC) architecture is then applied, where the outer speed loop generates the torque producing current, and inner current regulators ensure a decoupled current. The PMSM is fed by a voltage source inverter with a Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) control, and an output filter is connected to increase the quality of the motor terminal voltage. Simulation results under variation speed reference and load torque disturbance are provided to show tracking, disturbance rejection, stable regulation, and balanced three phase stator currents. The inverter phase voltages show the expected switching behavior, while the filtered waveforms are very close to sinusoidal, confirming the effectiveness of the modulation and filtering block.

title: A Review of Error Analysis and Compensation Methods Affecting the Measurement Performance of All-Optical Current Transformers
authors: Wenxing Sun, Haiyun Tang, Duanjiao Li, Congwei Yao, Kunyu Song, Wei Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The All-Fibre Optic Current Transformer (AFOCT) is an innovative electrical measurement device based on the Faraday magneto-optic effect. It offers distinct advantages including excellent insulation properties, strong resistance to electromagnetic interference, a broad measurement bandwidth, compact size, and ease of digitalisation. It holds significant application potential in fields such as ultra-high voltage transmission, renewable energy grid integration, power electronics equipment, and traction power supply for rail transit. However, during prolonged operation and under complex conditions, the measurement performance of AFCTs is susceptible to multiple factors including fibre material characteristics, optical component drift, environmental disturbances, and signal processing algorithms. These factors induce non-negligible measurement errors, limiting their further adoption in high-precision metering and protection applications.

title: In Silico Study of Resonant Magnetoelectric Micro-Transducers for Implantable Magnetic Sensing
authors: Giulia Caiani, Valentina Galletta, Marta Bonato, Emma Chiaramello, Serena Fiocchi
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The detection of weak biomagnetic signals remains underexplored in clinical practice due to the limitations of current bulky and expensive measurements system. Recently, magnetoelectric (ME) materials have emerged as compelling alternatives for highly sensitive, room-temperature sensing. This work presents a computational investigation of resonant ME micro-transducers designed for fully wireless operation via the delta-E effect. We analyse and compare the performance of core-shell microparticles and thin-film disks composed of Cobalt Ferrite and Barium Titanate, specifically optimized for MHz and kHz frequency regimes to minimize electromagnetic attenuation in biological tissues. Finite element simulations reveal that thin film disk can achieve maximum sensitivities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 5 ~ H z} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{n T}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4 1. 5 ~ m V} \boldsymbol{/}$</tex> Oe, for the first radial mode, at low bias field <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<60 \text{Oe})$</tex>, while the core-shell configuration a sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$214 \text{mV} / \text{Oe}$</tex> at 350 Oe. Collectively, these results pave the way for the development of minimally invasive, wireless microsensors that overcome the limitations of current wired technologies in clinical diagnostics.

title: A Digital Twin of Schooling Instability: Active Matter and Machine Learning for Climate-Resilient Marine Systems
authors: Fatima-Ez-Zahra Grini, Souad Tayane, Jaafar Gaber, Walid Abouzoul, Safa El Kordy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Climate change is increasingly destabilizing marine ecosystems, yet existing ecological models often fail to capture the nonlinear collapse of collective animal behavior under environmental stress. This paper presents an integrated framework combining active-matter physics and machine learning to model and predict climate-induced instability in fish schooling, with a regional focus on the Red Sea one of the fastestwarming marine basins globally. Using a Vicsek-type model of self-propelled agents, we simulate fish school dynamics across a grid of density $\rho$ and climate stress $\eta$ parameters, capturing transitions between ordered, milling, and disordered states. We introduce a Schooling Stability Index (SSI) that couples collective alignment (polarization) with environmental variability to quantify resilience. A Random Forest classifier predicts schooling phases with $\mathbf{9 6. 8 \%}$ accuracy, supporting ML-assisted earlywarning indicators, supporting ML-assisted early-warning indicators. Results reveal sharp thresholds beyond which behavioral collapse occurs, particularly under high-stress regimes consistent with climate-forcing scenarios reported for warming basins. This physics-informed digital twin prototype contributes to climate-resilient monitoring and AI-for-sustainability applications, with scalable relevance to vulnerable coastal regions.

title: Salt Typhoon and the Limits of “Quantum Security”: A Technique-Level Audit_supp2-3688536.csv
authors: Juljan Krause
publish: N/A

title: Motion analysis of a multi-stiffness concentric tube magnetic continuum robot and its remote constraint application for minimally invasive surgical operations_supp1-3699226.mp4
authors: Wenguang Yang
publish: N/A
abstract: Conventional magnetic continuum robots (MCRs) show great potential for minimally invasive surgery due to their simple structure and ease of manipulation. However, their monolithic continuous structure and single-stiffness design limit kinematic performance and manipulation accuracy. To address these limitations, we developed a concentric tube magnetic continuum robot (CT-MCR) system with replaceable concentric tubes and adjustable magnetic particle positions. Motion analysis and modeling were performed using the Cosserat rod model to optimize structural characteristics and enhance manipulation accuracy. Within a 270° driving range, the operating end-effector achieves stable trajectory following with a fit rate exceeding 90%. The distal constraint effect minimizes end-effector hopping, improving stability by over 50% compared to conventional MCRs. Navigation and foreign body retrieval experiments in a complex branching vascular model within a 3D Helmholtz coil workspace demonstrate the CT-MCR’s potential for targeted drug delivery and minimally invasive cardiovascular surgery.

title: SpecEStop: Self-Supervised Hyperspectral Mixed Noise Removal via Deep Spectral Prior
authors: Ruobing Zhang, Michael K. Ng, Lianru Gao, Marina Ljubenovic, Lina Zhuang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Hyperspectral remote sensing images often suffer from mixed noise—Gaussian, stripe, and impulse—due to atmospheric interference, solar variability, and sensor imperfections. These noises are typically band-dependent and diverse in distribution, making unified denoising particularly challenging. Existing deep denoising methods rely on clean/noisy pairs, which are unavailable in real-world remote sensing, while traditional approaches require manual tuning and lack adaptability. We propose SpecEStop, a fully self-supervised spectral vector denoising framework that requires only a single noisy HSI for training. Leveraging a novel deep spectral prior, SpecEStop exploits the spectral bias of neural networks, which tend to learn low-frequency (clean) signal components with Gaussian noise before overfitting to non-Gaussian noise ones. An adaptive early stopping strategy halts training before non-Gaussian noise is learned, enabling effective suppression of complex noise patterns. To address remaining Gaussian noise, we purposely design the network architecture to preserve its statistical properties in the latent space, allowing the use of off-the-shelf Gaussian denoisers during inference. Without any clean supervision, SpecEStop achieves effective, stage-wise removal of mixed noise, as validated across diverse real-world scenarios. Code will be released at https://github.com/ruobing-Zhang<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: Lightweight Deep Learning for Concealed Person Detection and Tracking in Airplane Cargo Holds
authors: Bachir Khalfi, Rim Gasmi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: One of the major security challenges, particularly in airports with high passenger and cargo traffic, is the concealment of humans inside aircraft cargo holds as a means of illegal immigration. Traditional preventive methods, such as manual inspections of cargo areas and the use of canine units to detect human presence, are often insufficient or difficult to apply effectively. To address this issue, the present work proposes an artificial intelligence–based approach that employs lightweight YOLO models—such as YOLOv8n, YOLOv9t, YOLOv10n, YOLOv11n, and YOLOv12n—as well as an SSD-based lightweight detector (SSDLite320 with a MobileNetV3 backbone),to detect individuals attempting to hide in cargo holds under varying lighting conditions, viewing angles, and positions. The selected model was further integrated with a tracking algorithm and virtual counting lines to monitor entry and exit events, after being optimized to balance detection accuracy with computational efficiency. Experimental results show that YOLOv9t provides the best trade-off between mean average precision and inference speed, making it well suited for real-time deployment. The proposed solution demonstrates strong potential to improve airport security protocols and reduce the risks associated with illegal immigration.

title: Skin Lesion Segmentation and Cancer Classification using U -Net and Vision Transformer Models
authors: Shantini K, Shalini V, E Ahila Devi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Skin cancer is one of the most common and dangerous dermatological conditions in the world, where early and correct diagnosis is very important for increasing the survival rate among patients. Manual analysis of dermoscopic images is time-consuming and prone to human error, thus sparking interest in the use of automated diagnosis techniques. In this paper, a two-stage deep learning model is proposed for the segmentation of lesions and probabilistic diagnosis of skin cancer from dermoscopic images using the U-Net and Vision Transformer model. The proposed work utilizes the U-Net model for precise lesion segmentation by eliminating background noises along with retaining necessary structural details. The resultant lesion images are then further processed with Vision Transformer for calculating the probability score for malignancy instead of obtaining a crisp diagnosis result. The proposed model is evaluated using publicly available ISIC datasets, and it is shown that lesion segmentation improves the result for risk prediction and that the proposed work is superior to results attained using convolutional neural networks.

title: Overcoming Data Scarcity in EV Battery Disassembly with Augmented Deep Learning and Structural Reasoning
authors: Marina Baucks, Haoran Sun, Florian Koessler, Juergen Fleischer
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The steadily increasing demand for electric vehicles (EVs) has led to a heightened need for efficient, automated battery disassembly systems for sustainable recycling. However, the variability of battery designs and the insufficient availability of training data pose significant challenges for machine learning in the context of disassembly automation. In this study, we propose an image-based approach that integrates deep learning and structured reasoning to enable the autonomous generation of disassembly sequences for EV battery packs. A YOLOv8-based pipeline for object detection and instance segmentation is trained using two different approaches to data augmentation: conventional image transformations are compared with synthetic image generation using Segment Anything Models (SAM). Object-specific augmentation using SAM leads to higher precision in object recognition than general, conventional augmentation techniques. Structural relationships between components are then derived using both bounding box heuristics and pixel-level segmentation masks. This enables reliable extraction of spatial and connection data. The information extracted from the image data can then be further processed to derive well-founded and adaptable disassembly sequences. This process is possible without the use of CAD models or extensive prior knowledge. The experimental results showed improved recognition accuracy by using SAM-based data augmentation compared to data augmentation with conventional image transformation (mean Average Precision at an Intersection-over- Union threshold of 0.5 (mAP@0.5) increase: 2.0 %) and compared to not using augmentation techniques (mAP@0.5 increase: 3.3 %) on a small-sized dataset containing 190 images (original size, + 50 images for SAM-based augmentation; + 160 images for conventional augmentation).

title: An Interval-Valued Fuzzy Inference System for Disease Diagnosis
authors: Adnan Shaout, Sai Arunanshu Govindarajula, Neha Basugade
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Clinical data that is noisy, partial, and overlapping is frequently used by medical diagnostic systems. A lot of traditional diagnostic models produce clear results. A false sense of security may result from these results. Classical fuzzy inference systems also have this drawback. This paper will demonstrate that interval valued fuzzy inference system provide a more practical and informative approach of medical diagnosis than the conventional fuzzy or non-fuzzy methods. To explicitly model uncertainty in disease diagnosis, this paper presents an Interval-Valued Fuzzy Inference System (IVFIS). The Pima Indians Diabetes Dataset is used to evaluate the system. As baseline models, traditional Mamdani and Sugeno fuzzy inference systems are used. Mesiar's interval-valued fuzzy set methodology is then used to build an interval-valued extension. The propose IVFIS enhances robustness in borderline situations, as simulation data show. Additionally, it improves interpretability. Instead of using single numerical values to describe illness risk, the system uses intervals to produce more meaningful diagnostic findings.

title: AUV Path Planning Under Ocean Current Disturbances Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Bingbing Cai, Fanghua Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: To address three-dimensional path planning for autonomous underwater vehicles in marine environments with seabed, obstacles, and ocean-current disturbances, this paper proposes an improved deep reinforcement learning method termed NPD-D3QN. A three-dimensional marine environment is constructed using a bathymetric digital elevation model, spherical obstacles, and real ocean-current data with a first-order GaussMarkov disturbance term. The path-planning problem is formulated as a Markov decision process with a designed state space, discrete action space, and reward function. Based on D3QN, NoisyNet, prioritized experience replay, and a distillation replay pool are introduced to enhance exploration, sample efficiency, and training stability. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves faster convergence, higher success rates, and lower overall cost than the comparison methods. Dynamicobstacle experiments further demonstrate its adaptability and response capability in time-varying environments. Therefore, the proposed method provides an effective solution for threedimensional AUV path planning in complex marine environments.

title: A Comparative Study of Various Urban Change Detection Techniques in Northern Jordan using Sentinel and TerraSAR-X images
authors: Nawras Shatnawi, Bashar Altheeb, Aslam Al-Omari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The high rate of urbanization in the northern governorates in Jordan has necessitated the need to detect urban change in time with the planning being needed by the high numbers of refugees entering the country. Hence, it has been assessed that a multi-temporal Sentinel-2 (optical) and SAR (Sentinel-1 and historical TerraSAR-X/ASAR) multi-image analysis compares urban growth in the Northern Jordan in 2003 and 2024 to quantify the growth. The image preprocessing, which involves radiometric calibration, speckle filtering and terrain correction was used to meet this aim and object-based classification was done in eCognition Developer. Further, spectral indices have been utilized including: Normalized Difference Built-up Index (NDBI) and Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) and a Built-up Area Index (BAI), as attributes in a supervised maximum likelihood classification and in unsupervised (K-means) clustering. The coded results were then transformed into raster to calculate built-up area (BUA). The findings reveal that the BUA expanded by 53.8 km 2 (3.3%) in 2003 to $84.6 \mathrm{~km} 2(4.8 (\%))$ in 2024 with most of it being located at the urban Belt and thus turning agricultural lands into built space, increasing the population demand on infrastructure and services. Moreover, similar maps were obtained by the SAR-based and the optical-based methods, and even supervised classifications tended to be more accurate than unsupervised because with the increase in control of the definition of classes performance improved. Therefore, the results demonstrate that urban sprawl can be effectively monitored by satellite change detection that will guide land-use policy. Moreover, the research points at the implication of the findings on the planning of studies because the profound growth of study territory sets the importance of a sustainable development strategy and free access to high-resolution images and analysis tools to continue monitoring of the city. Lastly, the research paper suggests the need to avail free satellite images, which spans a vast region within the area, by government and private organizations that support such kind of researches.

title: Intelligent Multi-Source Data Fusion for AI-based Precision Crop Recommendation using Soil, Environmental, and Market Intelligence
authors: S.Vinoth Kumar, E. Rama Seshu, G. Guru Gopi Pavan Harsha Vardhan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The classical crop recommendation methods mainly use soil and climatic factors without taking into account important economic factors like demand in the market and fluctuation of prices. This constraint commonly leads to agronomical but not economical recommendations. To fill this gap, this research proposes a market-conscious crop recommendation model, that is, incorporating soil nutrients, environmental factors, and past market patterns into a single machine learning model. The formulation of the problem is a supervised multi-class classification problem based on a random forest classifier. The model is trained using a multi-source dataset with soil, climatic and market characteristics. Experimental findings indicate that the use of market intelligence can greatly enhance the performance of prediction with an overall accuracy of 95.2%. The analysis of the feature importance shows the role of both environmental and economic characteristics in making a decision. The suggested framework offers a viable, and cost-effective decision-support system in precision agriculture, which allows farmers to make effective decisions in crop planning in dynamic environmental and market conditions.

title: A Review on Advancing Immersive Experiences through Animation Technology in Virtual Reality
authors: Faris Abuhashish, Mohammed Atoum, Waheeb Abu-Ulbeh, Isma H. Ismail
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Animation in virtual reality is often conceptualized as a layer on top of content, empirical observations suggest that the virtual reality animation is infrastructure which takes up latency budgets, stabilizes sensorimotor synchrony, and which determines whether a virtual environment is experienced as if inhabited or be simply observed. Drawing upon a Scopus/ERIC-based systematic review of VR-animation investigations, we characterized animation technology as a coupled pipeline that involves motion generation, constraint management, multimodal synchronization, and real-time rendering within some stringent frame-time constraints. The consequences of this group of pipelines, reported, fall into three fields: user experience (presence, immersion, engagement), educational and training contexts in which animation is embedded in interactive tasks, in industrial applications for collaboration, configuration and decision support. The evidence basis is heterogeneous; many studies make assuming designs but make use of non-comparable measures, and are constrained because of hardware, space, or methodological differences. To address this, we put forward a taxonomy that separates between perception-coupled motion and purely decorative motion and propose an evaluation framework for performing an animation selection based on the latency variance, risk of cybersickness and impact on the execution of the related task. The following research outline presents the focus on reproducibility, cross-device strength, and privacy-conscious avatar animation.

title: Exploring the Moderating Effect of Artificial Intelligence Adoption on Technological Self-Efficacy and Digital Communication in Relation to Digital Leadership Competencies
authors: Vania Zeisa Ardini, Gita Chinara Maharani, Kenni Natali Talaata Anggraeni, Maria Grace Herlina
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The rapid adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) is changing how organizations work. It means organizations need to be ready for the technology and have good leadership and communication. This research examines the relationships among digital leadership, digital communication, technological self-efficacy, and the use of AI in organizational settings. Utilizing survey data from 241 employees across various sectors in Greater Jakarta, the study employs structural equation modeling (SEM) to evaluate both mediating and moderating mechanisms. The results demonstrate that digital leadership can significantly enhance digital communication, further increasing employees’ technological self-efficacy. Digital communication is further validated as a critical mediating mechanism through which digital leadership impacts technological self-efficacy. It confirms the essential role of social and communicative processes in digital transformation. Moreover, the utilization of AI significantly impacts the relationship between digital communication and technological self-efficacy, exhibiting a negative interaction effect. This outcome suggests that excessive dependence on $A I$ might harm the confidence-enhancing function of human-centered digital communication. It highlights a significant boundary condition in AI-integrated workplaces. The study enhances the literature on digital leadership by illustrating that AI adoption does not consistently improve psychological outcomes and must be complemented by leadership-driven communication. The findings indicate that organizations should incorporate AI as an augmentative tool while maintaining meaningful leadership communication to preserve employees’ technological confidence.

title: Affective-Aware Learning Systems: Bridging Emotion and Education through AI
authors: Khalil Omar, Jorge Marx Gómez
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Most digital learning platforms fail to address the critical role of emotions in educational outcomes. This study presents an emotionally adaptive learning framework that uses multi-modal emotion recognition to dynamically adjust instructional content and interaction patterns based on real-time student affective states. Our system achieves 87.3% accuracy in recognizing seven key learning-related emotions across diverse datasets including DAiSEE (9,068 educational videos), AffectNet (450,000 facial images), and MOOC behavioral data (200,000+ learners). The hierarchical decision-making architecture balances immediate emotional responses with long-term pedagogical objectives, demonstrating a 34% improvement in predicted knowledge retention and 42% reduction in stress indicators compared to static interfaces. Cross-dataset validation shows significant improvements in engagement metrics (p < 0.001). The framework employs privacy-preserving edge computing and cultural adaptation mechanisms to address key challenges in non-invasive emotion sensing. These findings establish foundations for next-generation learning systems that respond to both cognitive and affective dimensions of individual learners.
