The current models are analyzed in-depth with the assistance of the following metrics of RMSE, R 2, Precision, Recall and IoU, which assist in identifying the predictive performance and the classification performance. The review concludes by formulating the new trends in the future of smart agriculture which are multi-sensor data fusion, edge computing and explainable AI. The proposed paper will assist policy makers, developers and researchers to develop more resilient, scalable and farmer friendly systems of agricultural intelligence by synthesizing and mapping gaps of this area.

title: Weighted Ensemble Learning for Defensive Intrusion Detection
authors: Mohammad Adnan Aladaileh, Abdullah Ahmed Bahashwan, Ahmed J. Hintaw
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Network intrusion detection systems (IDSs) complement preventive controls by monitoring traffic and raising alerts when malicious activity is suspected. While signature-based IDSs remain effective for known threats, they struggle with novel or obfuscated attacks and typically require continuous manual rule engineering. Machine-learning-based IDSs can learn complex traffic patterns, but published results often suffer from limited transparency, inconsistent experimental protocols, and weak reproducibility—especially on legacy benchmarks. In this paper, we present a reproducible, defensive pipeline for multi-class intrusion detection using the NSL–KDD benchmark derived from KDD’99. We evaluate four classical supervised learners (logistic regression, random forest, gradient boosting, and a shallow multi-layer perceptron) and propose a weighted soft-voting ensemble where weights are proportional to validation macro-F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> scores to emphasize minority classes. On the official NSL–KDD test split, the ensemble attains accuracy 0.774, macro-F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> 0.588, and weighted-F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> 0.738, offering competitive performance relative to individual models. We further include an Isolation Forest baseline for binary anomaly detection and provide interpretable analyses including confusion matrices, ROC/precision– recall curves, feature importance, and learning curves. Overall, our study provides a transparent baseline and a defensively oriented reference implementation for future IDS research.

title: Optimal User-Driven Performance Governance for Server-Side Rendered Web Applications
authors: Harshendra Gite
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: To enhance the first load time, search engine visibility, and user experience, server-side rendered (SSR) web applications are commonly used but uniform behavior with variable user behavior and server workloads are difficult to maintain. The current performance assessment methods are very dependent on fixed benchmarks or synthetic testing that do not reflect the actual user interactions and are not adaptively optimized. In order to address these shortcomings, this paper proposes the best user-based performance governance to server-side rendered web applications which combine real-user performance measures with intelligent learning and optimization. The systematic feature identification, expert-based screening, and correlation analysis is used to produce a compact and meaningful set of performance measures based on real-user monitoring data to create a refined dataset. Deep linear perceptron (DLP) model is employed in learning relationships between the performance indicators and the overall quality of the page in order to predict the performance accurately. The feature selection and model parameter tuning are performed by a modified pelican optimization (MPO) algorithm that ensures a higher accuracy of prediction as well as less redundancy. These findings demonstrate that the EPO-optimized DLP model has a high accuracy, stability and computational efficiency as compared to non-optimized models. The results show that the use of user-driven analytics and metaheuristic optimization allows the proactive performance governance and facilitates adaptive decision-making in SSR environments.

title: Learning Context-Aware Neural ODE Dynamics for Adaptive Robotic Control
authors: Shao-Yi Yu, Jen-Wei Wang, Maya Horii, Masayoshi Tomizuka, Vikas Garg
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Robotic systems deployed in uncertain and dynamically changing environments often face variations in contact conditions, aerodynamic effects, and external disturbances that challenge reliable control. To remain effective under modelbased control, these systems require dynamics models that can adapt to such changes, especially when direct access to complete environmental information is limited. To enable adaptability and facilitate integration with model predictive control, we propose a context-aware dynamics model based on neural ordinary differential equations, which infers environmental factors from state-action histories using a two-phase training procedure. We validate the approach across diverse robotic platforms, including a quadrotor in simulation, as well as a Sphero BOLT robot and a Fanuc manipulator in real-world experiments. The results demonstrate that our method effectively adapts to temporally and spatially varying environmental changes across different tasks. Videos are available here, and the source code is available at our GitHub repository.

title: PanSR: An Object-Centric Mask Transformer for Maritime Panoptic Segmentation
authors: Lojze Žust, Matej Kristan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Environmental panoptic perception and obstacle detection are key components in enabling autonomy for autonomous surface vehicles (ASVs). Recent studies have demonstrated that general panoptic methods, currently dominated by mask transformer approaches, are unsuitable for maritime environments, as they perform poorly in crowded scenes and in scenes with a wide range of object scales, highlighting the need for domain-specific panoptic methods. In this work, we identify several fundamental shortcomings of these recent methods and rethink architecture design choices from the perspective of perception for autonomous navigation. We introduce PanSR, a new panoptic segmentation method with the following main contributions: 1) an object-centric proposal extraction module for better handling of object size variety, 2) an improved ground-truth matching scheme consistent with proposal extraction, and 3) object-centric mask prediction for better instance separation of similar objects. With these enhancements, PanSR effectively mitigates instance merging, enhances small-object detection and increases performance in crowded scenes, delivering a notable +3.4% PQ improvement over state-of-the-art on the challenging LaRS benchmark and +8.4% PQ on WaterScenes, while retaining competitive performance on the autonomous driving perception benchmark Cityscapes. The code and models are publicly available on GitHub.

title: Event-triggered optimal tracking control for nonlinear networked Markovian switching systems: Q -learning approach
authors: Haiyang Sun, Wenhai Qi, Ju H. Park, Zheng-Guang Wu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This paper proposes a fuzzy optimal tracking control scheme for networked nonlinear Markovian switching systems under an event-triggered mechanism. An augmented system is constructed by integrating system dynamics with the reference signal. Then, an actor-critic Q-learning method achieves adaptive control without requiring prior knowledge of system matrices or transition probabilities. An event-triggered mechanism is introduced to update control actions on demand, significantly reducing network communication load while preserving tracking performance. An online actor-critic neural network algorithm is designed. The critic network approximates the value function, and the actor network updates the control policy in real time, enabling collaborative optimal tracking control. Theoretical analysis shows that the resulting neural network-driven closed-loop system exhibits bounded weight estimation errors and is uniformly ultimately bounded. Simulations performed on a tunnel diode circuit model confirm the effectiveness and resource efficiency of the proposed method in a networked environment.

title: Free-space Optical Accelerometer with High Dynamic Range Using Phase Generated Carrier Demodulation Achieved via Integrated Coil Modulation
authors: Yuqi Meng, Shivansh Madan, Neal A. Hall
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This work demonstrates a free-space optical displacement sensor based on a Michelson-type interferometer employing the phase generated carrier demodulation technique. The sensor utilizes a vertical-cavity surface-emitting laser (VCSEL) and a proof mass comprising a coil-wound bobbin and a diffraction grating serving as a beam splitter. The bobbin is modulated sinusoidally out-of-band to encode the proof mass displacement resulting from seismic ground motion. Signal demodulation is achieved by extracting in-phase and quadrature components of the proof mass displacement and phase unwrapping, enabling displacement measurement across multiple optical fringes. Experimental results show that the sensor accurately recovers ground motion when excited by single-frequency and broadband test signals, with performance validated against a reference geophone. The presented scheme eliminates the need for active force-feedback control, thereby enabling designs with higher mechanical compliance and higher SNR than would otherwise be achievable. Future work will address comprehensive noise and dynamic range analysis, including the effects of shot noise and laser RIN.

title: Robust Multiplicative Control in Chemical Reaction Networks
authors: Emmanouil Alexis, Clarence W. Rowley, José L. Avalos
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: Achieving complex multi-species control objectives is essential for engineering advanced autoregulated biomolecular devices. This paper addresses the problem of robust steady-state tracking for outputs defined as multiplicative combinations of biomolecular species concentrations. We first introduce a control architecture realized via chemical reaction networks that steers the product of two target species concentrations in the controlled network to a prescribed value. A robust stability analysis is provided for closed-loop system families with distinct structural characteristics. The proposed framework is then extended to a more general formulation capable of regulating arbitrary monomial outputs involving multiple species. Numerical simulations of representative examples corroborate the theoretical results and illustrate the effectiveness of our approach.

title: Adaptive Sliding Mode Path Following Control for Autonomous Ground Vehicles with Uncertain Mass and Cornering Stiffness
authors: Hongliang Li, Heng Wang, Gang Liu, Qing Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a novel integrated estimation-control framework to solve the path following problem for autonomous ground vehicles under coupled uncertainties in mass and cornering stiffness. The proposed method is characterized by the synergistic integration of three key innovations: 1) a fuzzy confidence-enhanced recursive least-squares algorithm (FC-RLS-MFF) that employs a rule-based mechanism to dynamically assess excitation sufficiency, ensuring robust online mass estimation; 2) a tightly-coupled identification scheme, where the estimated mass actively refines cornering stiffness in real time through tire vertical load updates, departing from the use of fixed nominal values; and 3) an adaptive fast nonsingular terminal sliding mode controller (AFNTSMC) that leverages this real-time parameter stream to enable model-based compensation, rather than mere disturbance rejection. Co-simulations, including ablation studies, validate the framework and demonstrate that this tight integration yields emergent performance gains, such as a significant reduction in path-tracking error.

title: A Novel Fractional-Order Second-Order Sliding Mode Control For Nonlinear Systems Under Matched and Mismatched Uncertainties
authors: Weiqi Zhang, Chuanyu Sun, Yanmin Wang, Hany M. Hasanien, Kai Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: To overcome traditional limitations in handling complex disturbances, a novel fractional-order second-order sliding mode control (FOSOSMC) is developed for a class of nonlinear systems in the presence of matched and mismatched uncertainties, ensuring the properties of stability and dynamic performance. The main novelties are highlighted as follows. Firstly, a structurally flexible continuous SOSMC manifold incorporating FO calculus is constructed by taking into account the design of a family of sliding variables, which ensures that the state trajectory can converge to the equilibrium point accurately in a finite time and address the chattering problem owing to its memory and hereditary properties, while uniformly compensating for mismatched uncertainties. Furthermore, the straightforward sliding manifold structure allows for diverse designs of control laws. For simple single-input single-output systems, their fast convergence and high precision under lumped uncertainties can be guaranteed by combining the FOSOSM manifold and a fast-terminal sliding mode-type reaching law. For complex multi-input multi-output systems, a state-dependent adaptive gain is fitted to the FOSOSM manifold to obtain stronger robustness without sacrificing control accuracy or complicating parameter tuning. The global finite-time stability of the closed-loop dynamics is demonstrated by means of Lyapunov functions. Finally, simulation and experimental results verify the excellent control performance of the proposed method for nonlinear systems with uncertainties.

title: Decentralized AI-Driven Cyber-Resilient Control for Real-Time Intrusion Detection and Mitigation in Inverter-Dominated Grids
authors: Uzair Asif, Harsha Vardhan Reddy Modugu, Hamza AL-Hallaj, Mohammad B. Shadmand
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: Distributed energy resources rely on secure and robust communication between hierarchical control layers to ensure stability, reliability, and optimal coordination. It makes the system vulnerable to cyberattacks, such as false data injection (FDI) and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks, especially on communication channels. To address this problem, this paper proposes an artificial intelligence (AI)-driven, decentralized, and resilient control framework for real-time detection and mitigation of potential cyberattacks. Specifically, this work presents a hybrid AI model comprising a convolutional neural network (CNN) and a transformer that estimates control signals locally based on time-series measurements of power, frequency, and the rate of change of frequency. Moreover, an autoencoder is employed to detect and differentiate the presence of an attack on the received and locally predicted control signals. If the attack is detected, the proposed framework substitutes the manipulated signals with attack-free values to mitigate its impact and ensure nominal operation of the power-electronics-dominated grid, thereby minimizing reliance on the communication channel. The developed cyber-resilient control framework is evaluated in a network of adaptive droop-controlled grid-forming inverters (GFMIs), where a supervisory controller sends updated droop coefficients to all GFMIs to enhance frequency response and improve power sharing. Multiple case studies are conducted on a modified IEEE 14-bus system and a small-scale hardware setup to validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed intrusion detection and mitigation scheme.

title: Equity Analysis of Statewide Longitudinal High School CS Coursework Data: An Application of the CAPE Framework
authors: Stephanie M. Werner, Ying Chen

title: A WhatsApp-Based Administrative Chatbot for Academic and Service Processes at Astra Polytechnic
authors: Muhammad Hanif Abdulfattah, Aris Dwi Hanggoro, Niken Ayu Tirtaningtyas, Suzanna
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The Informatics Department of Astra Polytechnic serves approximately $\mathbf{5 0 0}$ academic community members through various administrative services that still rely heavily on manual interaction, limiting consistency, response speed, and operational efficiency. This study presents the development of a WhatsApp-based administrative chatbot designed to automate three core service processes room reservation, overtime submission, and academic Q&A. The system integrates WhatsApp using Baileys, an unofficial reverse-engineered library, demonstrating that non-API mechanisms can support institutional automation. The chatbot was developed using a prototyping-based Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) supported by Object-Oriented Analysis and Design (OOAD). Functional evaluation through black-box testing showed that all scenarios operated without errors, while a user satisfaction survey involving 88 respondents yielded an average score of 3.72 out of 5. A before-after performance assessment further indicated notable improvements, with response times reduced from $2-5$ minutes to $1-3$ minutes for room reservations and from 1-3 minutes to 10-30 seconds for academic Q&A. These results demonstrate that the proposed chatbot enhances administrative workflow efficiency and improves the accessibility and consistency of campus services.

title: Development and performance analysis of a highaccuracy six-axis force sensor
authors: Sen Liu, Kun Jiang, Huanhuan Yan, Na Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: The six-axis force sensor can simultaneously measure 3-dimensional force and 3-dimensional torque, and is a core device for achieving tasks such as precision assembly, collaborative work, and safe interaction. It is widely used in fields such as robotics, medical equipment, aerospace, automotive, and scientific research, and is a key supporting technology for the intelligence, precision, and high safety. Aiming at the problem of poor measurement accuracy of multi-dimension force sensors caused by coupling effects, a high-accuracy strain-type six-axis force sensor with small inter-dimensional interference and high sensitivity is designed. Based on the optimization design of the “cross-beam” structure, the problems such as the pasting position of strain gauges and circuit bridging are determined through finite element simulation analysis. Finally, the decoupling matrix is obtained through sensor calibration and decoupling operation. The nonlinear error, repeatability and hysteresis of each unit are less than 0.2% F.S, and the interference error is better than 1% F.S.

title: Toward an Inclusive Cognitive Banking System: Empathetic Voice Assistants for the Digital Autonomy of Elderly and Visually Impaired People in Africa
authors: Bertrand Alain Yonso, Freddy Oscar Songo-Kette Mogambo, Edgard Ndassimba, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The digital transformation of the banking sector has significantly improved access to financial services and operational efficiency. However, it also reveals a persistent usability gap, particularly affecting elderly and visually impaired individuals who often struggle with complex digital interfaces. This paper introduces an inclusive cognitive banking approach based on an empathetic voice assistant, enabling users to access financial information through a simple phone call without relying on graphical interfaces. The proposed architecture integrates GSM telephony, an Asterisk-based call management system, realtime audio processing through LiveKit, and a conversational agent adapted to users’ cognitive abilities. A key contribution lies in the integration of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) as a secure intermediary layer between the intelligent agent and the banking database, ensuring controlled access, structured interactions, and full traceability of requests. Experimental results confirm the feasibility of the system, highlighting intuitive voice interaction, robust speech understanding, and a secure framework for accessing sensitive financial data. The study demonstrates that combining telephony, voice-based artificial intelligence, and MCP provides an effective solution to reduce barriers to financial services in constrained environments. Future work includes multilingual system extension, real-world large-scale evaluation, and strengthening MCP-based security mechanisms.

title: Fabrication of copper-titanium composite layers on alumina using laser-based powder bed fusion
authors: Christoph Hecht, Jonas Heidkämper, Mario Sprenger, Anton Frank, Jörg Franke, Florian Risch
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: Laser-based powder bed fusion has emerged as a key technology in additive manufacturing in recent years. This study investigates the application of laser-based powder bed fusion for the metallization of alumina substrates using pre-alloyed copper-titanium powder. To achieve a strong bond between ceramics and copper-based alloys, active elements such as titanium are commonly used. However, since copper-titanium alloys exhibit relatively low thermal and electrical conductivity, additional copper layers are applied to ensure efficient metallization for electronic applications. The primary objective is to develop an alternative metallization method for ceramics that could replace or complement conventional techniques such as Direct Copper Bonding or Active Metal Brazing. This additive metallization approach provides the opportunity for mechatronic integrated metallizations for electronics in harsh and high-power application. The research analyzes the specific challenges and opportunities in processing copper and copper-titanium alloys on ceramic substrates using laser-based powder bed fusion. Two laser systems, operating in the green and infrared spectral range, are used to examine material- and wavelength-specific properties.

title: Near-Infrared LED Reflow Soldering for Flexible Hybrid Electronics
authors: Thomas Wenger, Jewgeni Roudenko, Marcus Reichenberger
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: Reflow soldering of printed flexible low-cost electronics continues to pose a challenge, which is hampering the growth of the technology. The specific process parameter requirements severely limit the available soldering technologies. This study focuses on the suitability of a new photonic soldering process based on individually controllable near-infrared light emitting diodes (NIR LEDs) for processing screen-printed silver (Ag) structures and etched copper (Cu) structures on polyethylene terephthalate (PET) with low-temperature solders of the Tin-Bismuth-Silver (SnBiAg) group. The approach enabled various component sizes to be successfully soldered under more favorable conditions. An analysis of the shear strength shows comparable behavior for the copper structures compared to samples that were convectively soldered. A significant improvement in the shear strength of the printed silver structures was achieved using the new NIR process.

title: CKD: Contrastive Knowledge Distillation for Cross-Dataset EEG Classification
authors: Ziwei Wang, Xingyi He, Hongbin Wang, Dongrui Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Cross-dataset transfer in electroencephalography (EEG)-based brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) remains challenging due to substantial distribution shifts across datasets, including differences in subjects, acquisition devices, and recording protocols. This study aims to improve cross-dataset EEG decoding by enhancing knowledge transfer beyond conventional output-level distillation. Methods: We propose a contrastive knowledge distillation (CKD) framework for cross-dataset EEG classification. CKD follows a two-stage transfer strategy, consisting of cross-dataset teacher pretraining and cross-subject online adaptation, and jointly exploits logit-level distillation and feature-level contrastive alignment. In this way, the student model is encouraged to inherit both the predictive behavior and representation structure of the teacher. Results: Experiments on five motor imagery EEG datasets showed that CKD consistently outperformed twelve conventional training, representative knowledge distillation, and domain adaptation baselines under both single-source and multi-source transfer settings. Additional visualizations and quantitative analyses further confirmed that CKD improves teacher-student alignment in terms of feature geometry and distribution consistency, and can be further enhanced by explicit domain adaptation. Conclusion: The proposed CKD provides an effective solution for cross-dataset EEG decoding by jointly improving predictive knowledge transfer and latent feature alignment under severe dataset shifts. Significance: This work improves the robustness and generalizability of EEG decoding across heterogeneous datasets, which is important for practical BCI deployment under real-world acquisition conditions.

title: Multi-Step Knee Joint Angle Prediction for Lower-Limb Exoskeletons Using Temporal Convolutional Networks
authors: Xiao Zhang, Shuting Cao, Kangqian Cai, Yuanyuan Zu, Xiao Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Accurate anticipatory prediction of knee joint motion is essential for smooth human-exoskeleton coordination. This paper proposes a multi-step knee joint angle prediction framework for lower-limb exoskeletons based on Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN). Using past 1.5 s of inertial measurement unit (IMU) and motor sensor data (150 points at 100 Hz), the model simultaneously predicts knee joint angles at six future horizons (30, 50, 80, 100, 150, and 200 ms). To address the asymmetric mounting of right-leg IMUs, a coordinate correction preprocessing pipeline is introduced. Furthermore, a locomotion-mode-weighted random sampling strategy is employed to mitigate class imbalance across different motion patterns (level walking, stair ascent/descent, slope walking, and fast walking). Experiments on 65 trials collected from multiple subjects and two distinct lower-limb exoskeleton devices demonstrate that the proposed method achieves RMSEs of $\mathbf{1. 7 8}^{\circ}$ and $\mathbf{7. 2 2}^{\circ}$ at 50 ms and 200 ms horizons, respectively, outperforming LSTM (2.17 ${ }^{\circ} / 7.75^{\circ}$), GRU (2.16 ${ }^{\circ} / 8.35^{\circ}$), and plain 1D-CNN (2.70 ${ }^{\circ} / 9.80^{\circ}$) baselines. Crucially, the held-out test set comprises data from subjects unseen during training, confirming the model’s ability to generalize across individuals. Furthermore, deployment benchmarking on an embedded RK3588 platform confirms that the model meets the strict real-time requirements of 100 Hz exoskeleton control. The results suggest that TCNs offer an efficient, parallelizable, and deployable solution for anticipatory exoskeleton control.

title: Untethering EIT: An Integrated Wireless Tactile Interface for Human&#x2013;Machine Interaction_supp6-3688715.mp4
authors: Shili Liang
publish: N/A
abstract: Large-area tactile sensing is a core requirement for intelligent robots. However, traditional high-density sensor arrays face challenges due to complex wiring and high integration difficulty. These issues limit their industrial application. This article proposes a stable and flexible tactile sensing system based on electrical impedance tomography (EIT). At the hardware level, we developed a highly integrated and miniaturized data acquisition (DAQ) system. The developed DAQ system weighs 34 g, complementing a conformable flexible sensor with a thickness of 700 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m. This design improves portability and achieves real-time dynamic mapping at 11.1 FPS. At the algorithmic level, we adopted a U-Net architecture to solve the nonlinear reconstruction challenges of EIT. Within a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$58\times 58\,\,\text{mm}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> sensing area, the model achieves a two-point spatial resolution limit of 7 mm and a mean multipoint localization error of 1.33 mm under multitouch scenarios. Furthermore, we utilized a CNN-BiLSTM model to decode complex spatiotemporal signals, achieving 96% accuracy for dynamic handwriting recognition and 85% in cross-user tests with ten participants, thereby demonstrating strong generalization capability. Finally, we deployed the system on an industrial robotic arm and a bionic hand. The system demonstrates high-fidelity contact sensing. It can also precisely identify small objects of 5 mm. The proposed EIT system provides a lightweight and scalable solution for industrial tactile perception and intelligent interaction.

title: Countering AI-Powered Threats in IoV: A Secure Task Offloading and Resource Allocation Scheme with RIS and Quantum Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wenjing Xiao, Shixin Chen, Xin Ling, Miaojiang Chen, Min Chen, Ahmed Farouk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The advanced Internet of Vehicles (IoV) integrates autonomous driving, intelligent navigation, and in-vehicle consumer electronics, relying on efficient and secure communications to ensure reliable services. While Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) offer enhanced support for high-rate vehicular communication, existing RIS-aided scheduling schemes remain inefficient in delay-sensitive multi-task IoV scenarios and lack resilience against emerging AI-driven threats in consumer electronic systems. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a secure and communication-enhanced task offloading and resource allocation scheme for vehicular consumer electronic networks. First, we introduce an intelligent RIS-assisted vehicle model that jointly optimizes task dependencies, resource allocation, and RIS configuration under adversarial disturbances to improve transmission rate and communication quality. Second, to efficiently solve the resulting high-dimensional and non-convex optimization problem involving RIS phase shifts, we develop a Quantum-based Reinforcement Learning (QRL) framework. QRL encodes state spaces with quantum bits and embeds quantum circuits into neural networks, accelerating convergence and strengthening robustness against adversarial attacks. Experimental results show that QRL outperforms the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) method in jointly optimizing offloading and allocation under security constraints. The proposed framework demonstrates strong generalization potential for various RIS-assisted mobile computing applications, especially in environments vulnerable to AI-powered threats.

title: FACDA: Federated Learning-based Autonomous Cyber Defense for Edge–Cloud Systems
authors: Prajwalasimha S N, Dilip Kumar Jang Bahadur Saini, Sahana G, Yeddula Likith Neeraj Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The establishment of the future applications such as smart cities, intelligent transportation systems, and cyber physical systems lies in the smart computing infrastructures that combines edge and cloud computing resources. These infrastructures however are increasingly vulnerable to AI-driven, adaptive multi-vector cyber-attacks exploiting their distributed intelligence and real-time operation requirements. The paper will suggest a Federated Autonomous Cyber Defense Agent (FACDA) to defend the smart edge cloud infrastructures by collaborative or privacy intelligence. The framework applies autonomous defense agents on cloud and edge layers to learn local threat behavior with heterogeneous data sources. Federated Learning (FL) enables the development of common models without revealing raw data, and this consideration is both privacy-compliant and scale-compliant to large-scale smart environments. It is also efficient in simplifying the attack scenarios, such as coordinated Denial-of-Service (DoS), malware proliferation, and data exfiltration attacks on smart systems, because the proposed solution integrates adaptive policy coordination and cross-layer attacks correlation. Thorough testing of the structure of the typical smart computing situations has demonstrated that the offered structure provides superior detection performance, reduced response time, and increased resilience compared to the centralized defense mechanisms. The results imply that federated autonomous cyber defense provides a scaleable platform of trusted and robust smart computing systems.

title: Continuous-Time Stability of Networked Cyber-Physical Systems Under Denial-of-Service Attacks: A Lyapunov–LMI Framework with Sample-and-Hold Control
authors: Rachid Boutssaid, Said Kririm, Bensalem Boukili, Naoual Armi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Cyber-physical systems (CPSs) increasingly rely on shared communication networks to close feedback loops, which exposes the control layer to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks that intermittently block sensing and actuation updates. This paper develops a continuous-time stability framework for linearized CPSs operating under DoS by combining a sampleddata networked control model with deterministic constraints on DoS activation frequency and denial duration. Under a zero-order-hold implementation, the closed loop evolves as a timevarying sampled-data system driven by the sequence of successful update instants. We derive an explicit upper bound on the inter-success time and establish a Lyapunov/contractivity-based sufficient condition that guarantees global exponential stability for all admissible DoS patterns. A nominal stabilizing state-feedback gain is obtained via a quadratic Lyapunov LMI, and the DoS-resilience certificate is verified on a finite grid over the admissible interupdate interval. Simulations on a Jacobian-linearized autonomous-vehicle model (used only for illustration) demonstrate that the proposed ZOH-hold implementation preserves regulation under bursty DoS, while a naive controldropout strategy exhibits degraded transients.

title: Synthetic Brain Tumour Classification: A Complete Experiment
authors: Faris Abuhashish, Mohammed Atoum, Isma H. Ismail
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Brain tumour classification from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) supports early diagnosis and treatment planning, yet many benchmark datasets are gated, limiting reproducibility in low-resource settings. This work develops a reproducible pipeline for four-class tumour recognition when pixel-level MRI slices are unavailable. We emulate the MRI Brain Tumour collection (7,023 contrast-enhanced T1-weighted slices from Figshare, SARTAJ and Br35H) by synthesising 7,023 labelled samples with 50 features (30 informative, 10 redundant, 10 noise) and preserving class proportions across glioma, meningioma, pituitary and no-tumour categories. A stratified 75/25 train-test split is applied; features are standardised for multinomial logistic regression, while gradient boosting is trained on raw features. All experiments were executed in Python using scikit-learn with fixed random seeds to enable exact replication. We report class-weighted metrics to avoid bias under mild imbalance and include paired significance testing of prediction errors for transparent baseline comparison only. Accuracy, weighted precision, weighted recall, weighted F1 and confusion matrices quantify performance. Gradient boosting attains 0.9003 accuracy and 0.9003 weighted F1 (precision 0.9006), outperforming logistic regression (accuracy 0.8098, F1 0.8091). Baseline errors concentrate between no-tumour and meningioma, whereas boosting shifts most predictions to the diagonal, leaving only residual boundary confusions. The findings show that non-linear ensembles exploit complex feature interactions in surrogate medical data and provide a practical testbed for later studies using real MRI pixels, attention-augmented networks and explainable decision support.

title: Comparative Study of Deep Transfer Learning Models for Classifying Different Types of Diseases in Paddy
authors: S. Vinoth Kumar, G. Prashanth Kumar Reddy, Y. Venkata Sudheer
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The early identification of rice leaf diseases protects food supplies and enhances farm productivity. The traditional approach to manual inspection takes too much time while delivering results that depend on personal judgment and cannot handle inspection needs for extensive farming territories. This study introduces HybridRiceNet which functions as a deep transfer learning system featuring hybrid attention mechanisms to achieve precise identification of paddy leaf diseases. The system uses pre-trained convolutional neural networks together with an attention system to enhance its ability to identify essential features needed for disease detection. The researchers applied advanced preprocessing methods together with data augmentation techniques to achieve better results on small dataset testing. The researchers assessed the model using a public database of rice leaf diseases which contains four categories: Brown Spot, Leaf Blast, Hispa, and Healthy. The experimental results show that HybridRiceNet achieves better results than standard transfer learning models through its 97.1% accuracy rate which exceeds the performance of VGG16, ResNet50, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2 models. The system uses explainable AI methods to make its operations easier for users to understand. The results demonstrate that HybridRiceNet functions as a dependable and effective system for use in smart agricultural systems which require immediate processing capabilities.

title: Wearable Companion Robot for Assistive Support
authors: Prasanna Raj C, Sai Kumar R, Robinson J, Chitra S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: This paper presents a wearable companion robot designed to provide assistive support for elderly, differently-abled, and general users through multimodal human–robot interaction. The primary challenge addressed is the deployment of computationally intensive artificial intelligence functionalities within the limited resources of wearable devices. To overcome this, a hybrid edge–server architecture is proposed, where the edge device manages sensing, interaction, and actuation, while a centralized server performs complex cognitive tasks such as speech processing, visual perception, and dialogue generation. The system integrates multiple open-source modules, including speech recognition, face recognition, object detection, and optical character recognition, to enable comprehensive assistive capabilities. Experimental evaluation demonstrates stable real-time performance, with efficient latency across speech and vision modules and an intent recognition accuracy. The proposed system achieves a balance between responsiveness, scalability, and functionality, making it suitable for real-world assistive applications. This work highlights the feasibility of combining edge computing with centralized intelligence to develop scalable and intelligent wearable robotic systems.

title: Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMRs) for Warehouse and Production-Line Logistics
authors: Prachur Gupta, Bhuyash Chahar, Sanchay Tuteja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and execution of a cost-effective Autonomous Mobile Robot (AMR) for warehouse and production-line logistics. The system employs a wheeled robot controlled by an ESP32 microcontroller, utilizing a single ultrasonic sensor for perception and WiFi for telemetry and control via a Blynk-based mobile application. We implement custom lightweight algorithms for SLAM-based mapping, dynamic collision avoidance using time-to-collision metrics, and frontier-based path planning. By optimizing these computational processesfor resource-constrained hardware, we achieve precise navigation and obstacle avoidance at a significantly lower cost compared to traditional LiDAR-based solutions. This work demonstrates a scalable pathway for interoperable logistics automation using commodity electronics.

title: Radiobiological framework for pediatric radiotherapy plans using an automated Python-based NTCP modeling tool
authors: Meriem Tantaoui, Fatimzahra Chehab, Souha Sahraoui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In pediatric radiotherapy, predicting radiationinduced normal tissue complications remains challenging because of the high radiosensitivity of developing tissues and the limited availability of pediatric-specific radiobiological models. Normal Tissue Complication Probability (NTCP) has emerged as a valuable endpoint for radiobiological treatment plan evaluation using dose-volume information extracted from treatment planning systems. This study describes the development of an automated Python-based software platform for calculating Equivalent Uniform Dose (EUD) and NTCP values for pediatric organs at risk based on the Lyman-Kutcher-Burman (LKB) model. The implemented workflow supports cumulative and differential dose-volume histogram inputs and integrates automated data preprocessing, bin conversion, radiobiological parameter assignment, EUD computation, and NTCP estimation within a unified framework. Model parameters were obtained from established radiobiological datasets reported in the literature and conservatively adapted for pediatric research applications. The tool was tested on representative, clinically approved pediatric treatment plans involving major organs at risk, enabling consistent radiobiological comparison between competing planning strategies. This platform provides a reproducible, transparent, and flexible solution for research-oriented NTCP assessment and supports research-oriented radiobiological evaluation while facilitating future incorporation of pediatricspecific modeling developments.

title: Omnidirectional Path Finding Algorithm using Single Ultrasonic Sensor for Robotic Cars
authors: M Jyothirmai, Panathula Thanmai, Pikkili Harshitha, Mohebbanaaz, Neravati Sai Samanvitha, B.Geetha Rani
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Robotic Navigation has a reliance on accurate environmental Awareness, Obstacle Detection, and a highly effective means of planning Paths through the unpredictable environments within which the robots must operate. Ultrasonic Sensor-Based Navigation Systems typically have very limited ability to provide a Directional Perception of obstacles, due to the fact that Ultrasonic Sensors have fixed positions when mounted onto robots. Fixed mountings significantly impair the robots’ ability to determine the Direction of an obstacle and therefore their Decision Making Ability and increase the likelihood of collision. The research described in this document will develop a new technique to produce Omnidirectional Paths based on a single Ultrasonic Sensor, that will be mounted to a Servo Motor, allowing the Robots the ability to assess their immediate environment with a far wider range of degrees of Freedom than existing Ultrasonic Sensor-based Systems, which rely on multiple Ultrasonic Sensors and provide continuous scaning over 360 degrees. By performing multiple Distance Measurements at different angles, the system can capture and combine Distance Information and create the Spatial Model of their Environment without needing to incorporate Multiple Ultrasonic Sensors into the Autonomous Robotics System. A mean-based Distance Estimation Approach, based on Distance Estimates obtained at Various Angles, will create Accurate Filtered Distance Estimates that are not adversely affected by Noise and Spurious Responses from Reflected Signals, or Environmental Interference, when they are acquired.

title: Research on Cooperative Detection Methods Based on Target Angle Information
authors: Mengjun Zhang, Feihong Sun, Shuaishuai Liu, Jiajun Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: This paper derives and establishes an angle-based information transfer method in the passive detection scenario. Utilizing the target azimuth and elevation angle obtained by the single station, this method cues other geographically separated detection assets to search and track the target, thereby achieving cooperative detection and triangulation localization. Simulation verification demonstrates that the algorithm enables detector to cue other detectors for cooperative detection and complete target distance calculation in the passive tracking scenario, with the cuing success rate exceeding 85% in the simulation experiment. In physical environment validation, all conducted three tests were successful. Furthermore, the application of the Kalman Filtering algorithm to process the detection datas are valid to remove outliers, smooth the measurement datas, and improve success rate of target cuing.

title: Sensitivity Analysis-Based Comparative Evaluation of Text Classification Models for Automotive Customer Reviews
authors: Chaimae Faiz, Mouna El Mkhalet, Mohammed Sbihi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of three text classification models-linear SVM, Gaussian SVM (RBF), and Naive Bayes-applied to customer reviews in the automotive sector. The objective is to evaluate the performance of each model using accuracy and recall metrics, and to visualize the results through confusion matrices and heat maps. The results show that linear SVM excels at detecting all positive mentions, Gaussian SVM achieves maximum accuracy, and Naive Bayes offers a balanced compromise between accuracy and recall. This study demonstrates the value of combining multiple models and metrics for reliable analysis of customer reviews and opens up avenues for research toward advanced models such as BERT and Transformers for a better semantic understanding of texts.

title: Simulation-driven polymer selection for eco-efficient plastic tray design
authors: Zineb Achor, Souad Tayane, Jaafar Gaber
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study presents a mold flow analysis of five polymers-Low-Density Polyethylene (LDPE) from two manufacturers, High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Homopolymer (HP), and Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC)-used in the production of plastic trays. Using Moldex3D, key parameters including warpage, filling pressure, and filling time are evaluated, which directly affect product quality and manufacturing efficiency. The results revealed significant differences among the materials. One polymer consistently showed superior behavior, with a 50% reduction in warpage and a 15% improvement in filling time compared to the average, indicating enhanced dimensional stability and reduced defect risk. These findings emphasize the importance of material selection and simulation in improving injection molding outcomes. This research contributes to the development of high-precision, environmentally friendly plastic components for industrial applications.

title: Fuzzy Inference-Based Artificial Intelligence Approach for Optimizing Machining of LM25-10 wt.% SiC Composites
authors: N. Senthilkumar, B. Deepanraj, B. Ratna Sunil, Feroz Shaik
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study examines an LM25 alloy reinforced with $\mathbf{1 0} \mathbf{w t. \%} \mathbf{S i C}$, which was machined under sustainable dry conditions on a CNC turning center. An uncoated CNMG120404 insert was utilized to assess tool wear and surface integrity under controlled cutting parameters, aiming to enhance productivity. A Taguchi L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</inf> orthogonal array was utilized to examine the effects of cutting speed (CS), feed rate (FR), and depth of cut (DoC) on flank tool wear (TW) and surface roughness (SR). The experimental results indicated that TW increased from 0.08 to 0.26 mm and SR varied from 2.08 to $5.25 \mu \mathrm{~m}$, with deterioration primarily observed at elevated FR and DoC. FR was identified as the predominant factor, attributed to increased chip load, thrust force, vibration, and reinforcement-induced abrasion. Additionally, higher CS exacerbated thermal-mechanical effects, including adhesion/BUE instability and oxidation. Predictive models were constructed utilizing linear regression and ANFIS with 54 data sets as the experiments were replicated thrice. The regression models attained $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ values of $\mathbf{9 6. 5 1 \%}$ for TW and $\mathbf{9 6. 8 \%}$ for SR. In contrast, ANFIS demonstrated superior capability in capturing nonlinear interactions and exhibited closer alignment with experimental trends. This study presents statistically validated parameter response relationships aimed at minimizing tool wear and enhancing surface finish during the turning of SiC reinforced LM25.

title: Descriptor: Two-Way-Ranging-Based Cooperative Localization with an Open UWB Dataset from Bern-University (TWR-CLOUD-Bern)
authors: DIMITRIS XENAKIS, ANTONIO DI MAIO, TORSTEN BRAUN
publish: IEEE Data Descriptions
abstract: This paper introduces Two-Way Ranging-based Cooperative Localization with an Open Ultra-Wideband Dataset from Bern-University (TWR-CLOUD-Bern), a multi-node Ultra-Wideband (UWB) Two-Way Ranging (TWR) dataset and, to our knowledge, the largest open dataset of its kind to date. Collected across 12 scans covering more than 500m2 with 40 Qorvo DWM3001C nodes, the dataset provides ground-truth coordinates, Time of Flight (ToF) measurements, and reception statistics. To support fair, transparent, and reproducible comparisons, we also release a companion evaluation framework. In this framework, 11 scans serve as training partitions for data-driven ranging-model development, while one independent scan with distinct node placements is reserved for testing. The framework fixes node selections and measurement configurations, while varying node counts and locations. It supports single-and multi-sample ranging and covers anchor-free cooperative, anchor-aided cooperative, and anchor-only non-cooperative localization under real-world node-to-node connectivity. The dataset, framework, and documentation are openly available through the Zenodo archival release, making TWR-CLOUD-Bern an immediately usable testbed for UWB localization research.

title: FlowAdapt-GS: Dual Optical Flow Supervision with Adaptive Keyframe Sampling for Endoscopic 4D Reconstriction
authors: Zhihao Yang, Zhonghua Liu, Hao Xiong, Weihua Ou, Kaibing Zhang
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: In image-guided robotic surgery, achieving automation fundamentally depends on accurate 3D modeling of soft tissue structures. Against this backdrop, the emerging 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) framework and its temporal extension (4DGS) offer a promising solution for dynamically deforming surgical scenes. However, existing methods struggle with incomplete reconstruction. To address these issues, this paper proposes FlowAdapt-GS, a novel method for deformable endoscopy reconstruction via spatial-temporal flow guidance. Its core is a dual optical flow supervision strategy (DOFS) that decouples the optical flow field into spatial-geometric and temporal-consistency supervision. In addition, we develop an Adaptive Keyframe Sampling (AKS) method that effectively selects frames containing significant deformation information, thereby focusing optimization on the most informative moments. Extensive experiments on two widely used surgical datasets demonstrate that FlowAdapt-GS not only enables real-time rendering but also achieves superior rendering quality compared to existing benchmark methods.

title: FLoRA: Fusion-Latent for Optical Reconstruction and Flood Area Segmentation via Cross-Modal Multi-Task Distillation Network
authors: Jagrati Talreja, Tewodros Syum Gebre, Leila Hashemi-Beni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate flood-water mapping is critical for disaster management, yet current methods struggle to fully exploit the potential of spaceborne imagery. Optical data offers high interpretability but is limited by environmental conditions, whereas SAR provides reliable all-weather coverage with reduced visual interpretability. FLoRA (Fusion-Latent for Optical Reconstruction & Area Segmentation) is a cross-modal multi-task framework that jointly reconstructs high-fidelity optical imagery and segments flood-water regions from Sentinel-1 SAR by fusing the complementary strengths of optical and SAR data. During training, a lightweight optical teacher (driven by RGB and NDVI priors) provides pyramidal features that guide SAR representations into a fusion-latent space via multi-scale windowed cross-attention and FiLM conditioning, with gated residuals preventing over-correction. This design enables multi-task learning across two complementary objectives: (i) SAR-to-optical translation for fine-grained RGB reconstruction and (ii) flood-water region segmentation for hydrologic interpretation. The dual decoders are optimized using Charbonnier + SSIM for structural fidelity, edge + FFT-magnitude losses for spectral realism, and Dice + BCE + hydrology-aware edge alignment for precise flood-water delineation. A feature-distillation constraint further aligns fused SAR features with the optical teacher’s manifold. Evaluations on SEN1FLOODS11, DEEPFLOOD, and SEN12MS demonstrate that FLoRA surpasses fusion baselines in PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS, demonstrating that multi-modal fusion within a teacher-guided latent space yields semantically faithful and physically consistent flood-water intelligence from spaceborne observations. https://github.com/JagratiTalreja01/FLoRA.

title: NEMO: Neuro Hydrodynamic Model-Based Trajectory Planning for Autonomous Surface Vehicles With Reinforcement Learning
authors: Tongjie Yuan, Tao Huang, Xiadong Lv, Zhuo Liu, Chuang Guo, Yong Liu, Weiwei Liu
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Autonomous surface vehicles (ASVs) require accurate trajectory planning to ensure safe and efficient navigation in complex maritime environments. Traditional planning algorithms, including search-based and sampling-based methods, often have limitations such as trajectory nonsmoothness, high computational cost, and poor adaptability to environmental disturbances. Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a promising alternative due to its ability to learn optimal policies from interaction data without relying on precise system models. However, most RL-based methods remain confined to simulation due to the challenges posed by sim-to-real transfer and unmodeled hydrodynamic disturbances. This article proposes a novel trajectory planning framework integrating data-driven hydrodynamic modeling and RL. A transformer-based model is employed to learn the coupled hydrodynamic characteristics of ASVs from real-world state data, including ASV states influenced by wind, waves, and currents. The learned model is then incorporated into a RL-based planner to generate safe and dynamically feasible trajectories. Experimental evaluations in simulated and real-world water environments demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves trajectory reachability, target-point accuracy, and overall navigation safety.

title: Quasi-SMAP: A SMAP-referenced long-term daily surface soil moisture record from 1981 to 2022
authors: Yunjia Wang, Hao Sun, Jinhua Gao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Long-term daily surface soil moisture records are important for hydrological, ecological, and drought-related applications. However, existing satellite soil moisture products still face a trade-off among temporal length, daily spatial continuity, and consistency with the SMAP era. This study develops Quasi SMAP, a SMAP-referenced long-term daily surface soil moisture record from 1981 to 2022. A two-stage reconstruction framework was designed to generate this multi-decadal daily global SSM record. In the first stage, cross-era bias correction is applied to align the long-term ESA CCI record with SMAP-era behavior through distribution alignment and conditional residual correction. In the second stage, daily gap filling is used to improve the spatial continuity of the aligned record. Quasi-SMAP is evaluated against in situ measurements, and reanalysis datasets are used for independent consistency checks. During 2015–2022, Quasi-SMAP achieves an overall R of 0.65 and an ubRMSE of 0.06 m³ m⁻³. For 1996–2014, Quasi-SMAP shows consistently higher median correlation than ESA CCI across three sub-periods. Additional analyses show that the reconstructed record improves daily spatial continuity, remains broadly stable across the SMAP transition, and preserves consistent temporal variability relative to external benchmarks. As an example application, Quasi-SMAP was used to characterize drought-related soil moisture anomalies. The SPEI-3-based analysis showed that stronger climate drought conditions were generally associated with broader and stronger negative soil moisture anomalies in Quasi-SMAP. Representative cases further demonstrated its ability to describe monthly anomaly patterns and daily rapid-drying evolution. At 0.25° spatial resolution, Quasi-SMAP provides a SMAP-referenced long-term daily SSM baseline for soil moisture anomaly analysis and event scale applications.

title: Non-Enzymatic Glucose Sensing with GO/rGO-Modified Gold Electrodes: Interplay of Surface Functionalization and Conductivity
authors: Jacob A. Collazo-Vázquez, José L. Flores-Arciniega, Verónica Perez-Luna, Mildred Quintana Ruiz, Gilberto Martínez-Saucedo, Osvaldo Del Pozo-Zamudio, Iker R. Chávez-Urbiola, Edgar A. Cerda-Méndez
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: We report on non-enzymatic glucose sensors based on gold (Au) electrodes modified with graphene oxide (GO) and reduced graphene oxide (rGO), without the use of metallic nanoparticles (NP). This simplified architecture contrasts with conventional Au NP–GO composites, where the contribution of the GO layer is often masked, and may enable scalable fabrication. GO-modified electrodes exhibit the highest sensitivity, with two linear amperometric regimes (0–0.5 mM and 1–22 mM) and sensitivities of 18.7 and 502 μA mM⁻¹ cm⁻², respectively, allowing detection below 1 mM. In contrast, chemically reduced GO shows higher current density but poor sensitivity to glucose concentration changes. These results suggest that sensing performance is not governed solely by conductivity, but by the balance between sp² network connectivity and oxygen-containing functional groups. The enhanced response of GO highlights the important role of defect-mediated surface chemistry, supporting the hypothesis that direct GO modification of Au electrodes provides an effective alternative to NP-based systems.

title: Seizure Prediction Using Channel-Optimized Intracranial EEG with Variable Interictal and Preictal Durations
authors: Xiaoshuang Wang, Meng Li, Tommi Kärkkäinen, Hailong Liu, Jianwen Lin, Fengyu Cong
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Engineering in Medicine and Biology
abstract: Objective: Predicting epileptic seizures using electroencephalogram (EEG) signals can provide a critical window for intervention to suppress impending seizures. However, the impact of varying interictal and preictal durations on seizure prediction remains unclear and warrants further exploration. Considering this, this study focuses on seizure prediction using intracranial EEG (iEEG) signals with variable interictal and preictal durations. Method: In this work, we discuss six pairwise combinations in total, involving two interictal durations (24 h and 48 h) and three preictal durations (30 min, 60 min and 90 min), during seizure prediction analysis. Furthermore, we also compare three classification strategies: the all-channel strategy (Strategy 1), the single-channel-based majority voting strategy (Strategy 2) and the channel-optimization-based majority voting strategy (Strategy 3), across each pairwise combination. Results: The long-term SWEC-ETHZ iEEG dataset is utilized to evaluate our method. The results reveal that our method achieves up to 87.7% sensitivity, 94.6% specificity, 92.8% accuracy, 0.912 Area Under the Curve (AUC) and 0.858 F1-score at the segment-based level, along with 95.9% sensitivity and 0.05/h false prediction rate (FPR) at the event-based level. Moreover, the overall performance of Strategy 3 surpasses that of Strategies 1 and 2 in each pairwise combination, indicating that channel optimization is beneficial for improving seizure prediction performance. Conclusion: This work shows high effectiveness in predicting seizures using iEEG signals, with the ability to forecast upcoming epileptic events up to 90 min in advance across both 24-h and 48-h interictal durations.

title: Parallel-Elastic Actuation with Reactive Latch Elevates Robotic Hopping Performance: Jump Height and Continuity
authors: Songnan Bai, Runze Ding, Song Li, Ruihan Jia, Ruobing Wang, Zhiyuan Zhang, Fangzheng Wang, Pakpong Chirarattananon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Robotics
abstract: While many animals exhibit impressive hopping capabilities, machines have struggled to match their performance. Current hopping robots face limitations in power density, energy efficiency, and control stability. Here, we present a parallel-elastic actuation mechanism with a reactive latch that optimizes energy transfer, enabling a legged robot to achieve hopping heights and continuity previously unattainable. This mechanism efficiently stores and releases energy, extending the actuation period over the aerial phase while minimizing stance time. Our robot achieves a maximum hopping height of 3.6 meters, surpassing both human and animal records while demonstrating sustained, high-frequency hopping cycles with minimal power requirement. By integrating inertia-based onboard sensorimotor autonomy, we demonstrate stable, controlled hopping in environments without external aid. These results represent a step toward bridging the performance gap between biological and robotic locomotion, with potential to influence the design of future legged systems.

title: A Knowledge-Based Semi-Supervised Crystal Property Prediction Framework with Consistency Regularization
authors: Haomin Yu, Jilin Hu, Yunyao Cheng, Chenjuan Guo, Yizhou Zhu, Bin Yang, Christian S. Jensen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: In the field of material science, the analysis of the properties of crystalline materials is of key importance. Recently, machine learning has become a prominent tool for predicting the properties of materials based on their structure. However, the application of machine learning to crystal property prediction faces two significant challenges. The first is the scarcity of labeled data, due to the time-consuming and resource-intensive process of crystal property labeling. The second is the importance of leveraging specialized knowledge when performing crystal structure analysis, which requires adapting machine learning methods specifically for the crystal domain. In this paper, we propose a new semi-supervised framework, a Knowledge-Based Semi-Supervised crystal property prediction (KBSS) framework, which employs consistency regularization to leverage both labeled and unlabeled data while incorporating crystal knowledge guidance. Specifically, to use unlabeled data efficiently, the KBSS framework incorporates two key modules: a knowledge-guided augmentation (KGA) module and an adaptive pseudo-label filtering (APF) module. The KGA module utilizes the Monte Carlo method to leverage knowledge from the crystal domain to guide weak and strong augmentations of crystal structures. The APF module enhances the pseudo-labeling process for unlabeled crystal data by enabling task-guided uncertainty adjustment and category-aware pseudo-label selection. The experimental results show that KBSS achieves state-of-the-art performance. All code is publicly available at https://github.com/HaominYu0/KBSS.

title: Toward Unified Interpretable Decision-Making for Autonomous Driving: A Safety Interval Reserve Approach
authors: Xiaofeng Xiao, Wen Hu, Huazhen Fang, Ruiyi Wu, Yu Meng, Qing Gu, Wei Li, Dongpu Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The application of autonomous vehicles (AVs) requires a safe and efficient decision-making approach for diverse and complex traffic environments. Most existing methodologies focus on specific scenarios or tasks, and are not sufficiently effective for real-world driving situations. This paper proposes a Safety Interval Reserve (SIR) model to quantify safety time margin, which is inspired by human driving behavior. Concurrently, a SIR network is developed to parametrically delineate the changes of SIR under dynamic traffic conditions. Consequently, a SIR network based decision-making approach (DMA-SIR) is designed to unify the macro path planning and micro behavior decision-making. The macro layer optimizes the global path with considering driving efficiency, while the micro layer generates local driving behavior to ensure safety during decision-making. The DMA-SIR facilitates multi-task management through a unified model and is grounded in motion mechanism. Besides, dataset validation demonstrates the anthropomorphic characteristics of DMA-SIR. As a result, it offers an interpretable method that effectively avoid the black box problem. Finally, extensive simulations experiments are conducted using 48 complex scenarios to verify the performance of DMA-SIR. The results show that DMA-SIR can efficiently handle multi-vehicle scenarios and generate driving trajectory with significant greater efficiency, safety and comfort compared to other methods.

title: SpinJORR : Spin actuated Jumping Omnidirectional Rolling Robot
authors: Venkata Ravindhra Reddy Varikuti, ArunSai Kammari, Ali Mohseni, Phanindra Tallapragada
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents the design of a compact, spin actuated rolling and jumping robot. The robot can use the rolling modality for efficient motion and jumping over obstacles. The robot consists of a fully 3D-printed circular hoop enclosing an internal pendulum-like unbalanced rotor and a tip-mounted rotor that together enable rolling, steering, and jumping through controlled actuation. The design is based on a dynamic model that exploits unconventional mechanics to achieve the versatile capabilities. Unlike traditional jumping robots that rely on elastic components to store and release energy, the proposed design employs a purely momentum-based actuation mechanism, reducing relaxation times between jumps to almost zero. Experimental results demonstrate stable steering, high forward jumps of more than four body lengths (0.9 m), long forward leaps reaching nine body lengths (1.8 m), and reverse jumps reaching approximately five body lengths (1.1 m). The robot can recover from a fall again solely through the gyroscopic action of the internal rotors, coupled with ground contact forces. Experiments demonstrate its ability to traverse a variety of terrains. The simplicity and compactness of the design, as well as its versatility and agility, make the system suitable for applications requiring locomotion over unstructured terrains.

title: StereoMulti3DPose: Multi-Person 3D Pose Estimation via Lightweight Asymmetric Stereo Learning on the Edge
authors: Deyu Zhang, Kangqi Wei, Huan Yang, Yin Tang, Fan Wu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Multi-person 3D human pose estimation is essential for applications such as behavior analysis, mobile robotics, healthcare monitoring, and intelligent IoT sensing. Driven by the paradigm of Edge Computing, there is an increasing demand to migrate these tasks from the cloud to the edge to ensure low latency and privacy. Although monocular and multi-view methods have advanced significantly, their high computational cost, memory usage, or deployment complexity make them unsuitable for resource-constrained edge nodes. Binocular systems offer a more practical trade-off by providing explicit geometric cues using only two cameras, yet existing stereo approaches still suffer from redundant dual-branch processing or heavy disparity computation, preventing real-time performance on embedded hardware. To address these limitations, we propose StereoMulti3DPose, a lightweight and IoT-oriented stereo 3D pose estimation framework. The system adopts an asymmetric dual-branch architecture, where a full-scale left branch extracts high-quality geometric features while a compact right branch minimizes redundant computation across stereo views. To further enhance depth estimation under strict resource budgets, we design an Epipolar Feature Enhancement Module (EFEM) for selective cross-view fusion and a Pose-Depth Refinement Module (PDRM) that recovers fine-grained depth within a constrained disparity range. A feature distillation strategy aligns the lightweight branch with the full-scale branch, improving accuracy without increasing inference cost. Experiments demonstrate that our model achieves 51.0 mm MPJPE in real-world scenarios with a fixed latency of 116 ms on embedded IoT platforms. The proposed framework outperforms existing monocular and stereo methods, achieving real-time, scalable performance even in crowded multi-person scenes.

title: Low-Power LECTOR-Based Current-Starved VCO in 90 nm CMOS for Wireless IoT Networks
authors: Md Raisul Islam Ratul, Anindya Chanda Tirtha, Md Tawfiq Amin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The growth of Internet of Things (IoT) networks and next-generation wireless systems has increased the need for voltage-controlled oscillators (VCOs) that achieve both low power consumption and high spectral performance. This work presents a current-starved VCO (CS-VCO) that incorporates the LECTOR (LEakage ConTrol TransistOR) technique to suppress subthreshold leakage in deep-submicron CMOS. Implemented in 90 nm technology, the design embeds leakage-control transistors within each delay cell, enabling strong leakage reduction while maintaining full-swing oscillation. The proposed architecture achieves over 90% reduction in static power compared with conventional CS-VCOs, consuming only 95.472 µW. Simulated results demonstrate a wide tuning range of 3.873–5.526 GHz, phase noise of −74.2267 dBc/Hz at 1 MHz offset, and a figure of merit (FoM) of −159.19 dBc/Hz. DRC and LVS validation confirm manufacturability and electrical correctness. With an order-of-magnitude lower power than prior ring and PMOS-load VCOs while maintaining competitive spectral purity, the proposed design is well suited for battery-operated IoT devices targeting Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and sub-6 GHz 5G applications.

title: Modeling and Simulation of an Adaptive Cruise Control System for Enhanced Driving Safety and Comfort
authors: Akash Potdar, Ayan Kudachi, Vaibhavi Patil, Diksha Nalawade, Gujanatti Rudrappa, Arun Tigadi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) include Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) as a fundamental safety feature which keeps vehicles at safe distances while adjusting their speed based on road conditions. The research presents a simulation-based development process which incorporates hardware elements to build an affordable ACC prototype through MATLAB/Simulink and Raspberry Pi integration. The system operates with ultrasonic sensors to detect obstacles in real time while using a DC motor to simulate vehicle movement and a motor driver to control its precise operation. The control system uses pulse-width modulation (PWM) to adjust speed based on distance thresholds which results in maintaining speed on open roads and slowing down for obstacles and stopping at close distances. The control algorithms underwent simulation testing through MATLAB/Simulink before hardware implementation validated their stable and reliable operation throughout different testing conditions. The system achieved smooth speed changes and precise obstacle detection and dependable braking and acceleration performance which proves that simulated results match actual performance. The ACC model would enable cost-effective intelligent driving systems to serve as educational research foundations. The future research will develop computer-vision-based object detection systems and lane recognition capabilities which will integrate with PID and fuzzy logic control methods to create more adaptable autonomous systems.

title: DCNMNet: A Dynamic Contextual Neural Memory Network for Robust Spatio-Temporal Human Action Recognition
authors: Salma Tayeb, Soufiana Mekouar, Mohammed Majid Himmi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Human action recognition (HAR) in video remains challenging due to complex motion dynamics, long-range temporal dependencies, and significant environmental variability. This paper presents DCNMNet (Dynamic Contextual Neural Memory Network), a lightweight deep learning framework designed to jointly model short-term motion cues and longterm contextual dependencies for robust video understanding. DCNMNet employs a ResNet-18 backbone with frame differencing to extract spatial-motion representations, followed by a learnable neural memory module that preserves temporal history across frames. A gated context modulation unit adaptively fuses motion and memory features, while a temporal attention mechanism emphasizes discriminative temporal segments. In addition, an auxiliary gradient-reversal branch encourages environment-invariant feature learning to improve cross-scene generalization. Experimental results on standard HAR benchmarks demonstrate that DCNMNet achieves 99.04% accuracy on UCF50 and 89.68% on HMDB51, while maintaining real-time inference at approximately 112-118 FPS on CPU-only hardware. Ablation and error analyses further validate the complementary roles of neural memory, contextual modulation, and temporal attention. These results indicate that DCNMNet provides an accurate, efficient, and practically deployable solution for realworld video-based HAR applications.

title: When AI Works in Indonesian Workplace: How Technological Self-Efficacy Translates AI Usage into Outcomes under Digital Leadership
authors: Alysia Kharlotta, Maria Grace Herlina
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This research examines the impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) utilization and digital leadership on employees’ technological self-efficacy within Indonesian workplaces, utilizing Socio-Technical Systems (STS) theory as a framework. The study utilizes survey data from 267 employees across various industries in Indonesia, employing Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) to evaluate the proposed relationships. The results show that using AI tools has a significant beneficial effect on technological self-efficacy. This means that using AI tools directly makes employees more confident in their ability to use technology. Digital leadership has a strong positive direct effect on technological self-efficacy. This shows how important it is for leaders to support, have a vision, and be a role model in digital work settings. The results show that digital leadership has a big negative effect on the link between using AI and having confidence in technology. This implies a substitution mechanism in which robust digital leadership diminishes employees’ dependence on AI usage as their sole source of confidence, given that leadership guidance and sociocognitive support inherently enhance technological self-efficacy. The research enhances the literature by establishing technological self-efficacy as a significant outcome of AI implementation, advancing digital leadership theory, and augmenting STS perspectives through the illustration of non-linear leadershiptechnology interactions. In practice, the results underscore the necessity for a balanced socio-technical approach to digital transformation.

title: Economic Load Dispatch with Comprehensive Constraints Using the Vortex Search Algorithm
authors: Prajnadipta Sahoo, Santi Behera, Soumya Ranjan Das, Devi Prasad Acharya, Ajit Kumar Barisal
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Economic Load Dispatch (ELD) remains a core operational problem in modern power systems due to its direct impact on fuel cost and system efficiency. The problem becomes highly non-linear and non-convex when realistic operating constraints such as valve-point loading effects(VPLE), ramprate limits(RRL), prohibited operating zones, and transmission losses(TL) are considered. This work applies the Vortex Search Algorithm (VSA), a population-based metaheuristic inspired by vortex motion in fluids, to address these challenges effectively. A comprehensive ELD formulation is developed to closely reflect real-world operating conditions. The proposed approach is evaluated on a six-unit thermal power system under multiple constraint scenarios. Simulation results are compared with several established optimization techniques reported in the literature, including PSO-based and evolutionary algorithms.
