The comparative analysis demonstrates that VSA consistently achieves lower generation cost and stable convergence, confirming its suitability for solving highly constrained and nonsmooth ELD problems.

title: Quantitative Cost Analysis of Multi-stage Power Dispatch Models Under Prediction Errors
authors: Diqi Liu, Junshuo Chen
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: High-proportion renewable energy integration significantly increases power system uncertainty. To address the limitations of traditional single-stage scheduling, this paper establishes a stochastic multi-stage framework to quantitatively compare myopic and look-ahead dispatch models. Based on full-year backtesting with 2025-2026 AEMO data, the look-ahead model reduces average daily costs by 99.2% and effectively mitigates error cascades. Sensitivity analysis reveals a U-shaped relationship between the look-ahead horizon and dispatch cost, identifying $h=24$ as the optimal balance between foresight and error accumulation. Furthermore, while incorporating energy storage and demand response narrows the inter-model cost gap, the look-ahead approach retains absolute economic superiority by optimizing cross-temporal resource allocation.

title: Automated Noise Source Identification and Screening Method Based on Frequency-Wavenumber Transform and its Application in Surface Wave Imaging
authors: Lina Ye, Kaiguo Jia, Yuguo Cai, Zheng Zhang, Lei Fu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: In microtremor exploration of urban underground spaces, interference from complex noise sources severely constrains the quality of surface wave dispersion imaging. To address this issue, this paper proposes an automated noise source identification and screening method based on frequency-wavenumber (FK) transform. This method attenuates aliasing generated in different surface wave imaging methods through frequency-wavenumber transform, corrects surface wave propagation direction, and defines the signal-to-noise ratio R/L to achieve quantitative screening of dominant noise sources. Theoretical analysis and actual data testing demonstrate that this method effectively suppresses aliasing interference in multiple imaging methods such as ReMi, PMASW, FK, and MAPS, significantly improving the continuity and identifiability of dispersion energy. Field tests at Huzhou High-Speed Railway Station and the Sanhe section of Beijing Metro Line 22 verify that the screened dispersion energy images show marked improvement in clarity and continuity of the fundamental mode, providing an effective preprocessing approach for high-precision microtremor exploration in complex urban environments.

title: Contactless Detection of Atrial Fibrillation in Stroke Patients Based on Bayes Probability Model with Microwave Biomedical Radar
authors: Jie Cao, Li Wen, Liling Wang, Changzhan Gu
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: In this paper, a contactless atrial fibrillation (AF) detection technique is proposed with microwave biomedical radar, which can provide practical benefits for patients requiring longterm daily monitoring. Spectral entropy is proposed to characterize the disorder observed in the heartbeat spectrum of AF patients. These entropy features are then input to a designed naive Bayes model to estimate the probability of AF. In a 3-hour data analysis of a paroxysmal AF patient, the model achieves an accuracy of 93.88% and an F1-score of 88.00% for the labelled segments spanning 52.5 minutes. The trained model can subsequently be applied to unlabeled segments to indicate the risk of AF, thereby providing alerts.

title: Application of cascade control technology in constant power control of thermal equipment
authors: Hao Meng, Tianshun Zhu, Yuhui Dong, Guangming Yang, Ma Jie, Panpeng Sheng
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: During the heating process of thermal equipment, the resistivity of graphite heating load decreases as the temperature rises. Traditional thyristor AC voltage regulation power supplies change the voltage across the load terminals by phase shift triggering, thereby maintaining a constant heating power of the load. However, as the temperature increases, the voltage required by the load decreases, and the triggering angle of the thyristor AC voltage regulation power supply needs to be large, leading to significant deterioration in the power quality at the input terminal of the power supply. This paper proposes a layered control technology to improve power quality and increase power factor. The principle of the layered control technology is analyzed, and the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through simulation .

title: Dynamic Pricing Based Home Energy Management for Islanded Operation with Integrated Distributed Energy Resources
authors: Challa Krishna Rao, Sarat Kumar Sahoo, Tamminaina Lokanadha Rao, Irugatla Swetha, Pappu Baladhitya, Kuchetti Krishna Rao, Vanjarapu Venkata Rao, Boddepalli Manmadha Kumar, B. Srinivasarao
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: An innovative technique for optimizing residential energy consumption using home energy management systems (HEMS) is introduced with a focus on reducing electricity bills and improving energy efficiency. The technique describes a dynamic pricing technique for load optimization in an islanded mode operation of HEMS, especially during situations when grid connection is not feasible. The proposed technique considers an integrated operation of major DER such as photovoltaic systems, electric vehicles, and battery energy storage systems, including situations when grid connection is not feasible. The technique demonstrates real-time control using dynamic pricing signals to maximize energy efficiency. Results have been obtained for two different outage situations: a short-term schedule and a continuous <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex>-hour islanding mode. The results obtained for different seasons, including spring, summer, and winter, indicate a reduction in grid dependency to 10 % for spring months and a subsequent amplification to 50 % in both summer and winter months for short-term outages. As per latest studies, a complete dependency on local resources can be achieved for full-day islanding situations. The latest research shows an impact of up to 60 % in lessening grid dependency using such integrated HEMS systems under increasing demand due to escalating energy prices.

title: Privacy Risks in Metaverse and NFTs Ecosystems
authors: Zacharoula Sereti, Emmanouil Mavrikos, Stamatis Chatzistamatis, George E. Tsekouras
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The convergence of Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs) and the metaverse is reshaping the digital landscape by introducing novel models of ownership, identity, and interaction. NFTs enable verifiable digital scarcity and authenticity, while the metaverse offers immersive, persistent, and socially rich environments. Yet this integration raises significant privacy challenges, as vast amounts of personal, behavioral, and biometric data are continuously collected, processed, and stored. In addition, blockchain interoperability across platforms and the widespread use of avatars and digital identities create new vulnerabilities that extend beyond those found in traditional digital ecosystems. This paper examines the privacy implications of NFTs and the metaverse by mapping key threats, evaluating privacy-preserving technologies, and discussing regulatory, ethical, and societal perspectives. Moreover, by analyzing and synthesizing recent research, it identifies gaps in current methodological frameworks and proposes directions for future research and innovation. It argues that privacy should not be regarded as a barrier to innovation, but rather as a catalyst for building trust and enabling sustainable convergence between metaverse and NFT ecosystems.

title: Digitization of Old Indonesian Documents using YOLO and OCR
authors: Pradah Widjaksono Suherli, Kennard Norbert Sudiardjo, Kevin Tjang, Anderies
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study exhibits the work done so far on the fully integrated framework for the digital preservation of historical Javanese manuscripts that combine state-of-the-art real-time object detection capabilities of YOLOv8 with application-specific Transformer-based OCR (TrOCR) for real-time digitization of documents. The proposed pipeline is the first to focus on the literary and visual attributes of digitization. It moves through the complex and iterative steps of digitization using diverse pre-trained Transformer models for supervised auto-document synthesis through elementary surface automation. The system is configured to efficiently process segment transcription through fully editable digitized documents and produce searchable documents in structured PDFs. The system is able to track and transcribe manuscripts with a character-level accuracy of 93 to 100% on the NusaAksara dataset. However, the system sees a decline in accuracy to $\mathbf{7 0}-\mathbf{8 6 \%}$ on more diverse historical samples from the Endangered Archives Programme, primarily due to a lack of samples within the document and divergence in the data. These results show how important it is to have more diverse training, better tokenizer coverage, and more flexible preprocessing methods to make archival systems more robust in the real world. This research, despite its current limitations, substantiates the viability of an end-to-end digital preservation system and significantly enhances the protection of Indonesian cultural heritage, while also establishing a scalable framework for future research in low-resource script analysis.

title: AI-Optimised Coordinated Control of Ultracapacitors and SSSC for Frequency Stability Enhancement in Renewable-Integrated Interconnected Power Systems
authors: Ch Naga Sai Kalyan, Subhashree Choudhury, CH Hussaian Basha, Taraprasanna Dash, Palanisamy R, R. Murali, Rekha Rangam
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-driven coordinated control strategy to enhance frequency stability in a multi-area, renewable-integrated power system (MARIPS). A tilt-integral-derivative controller with filter (TIDF) is adopted as the secondary frequency regulator, and its parameters are optimally tuned using the Fruit Fly Algorithm (FFA), enabling intelligent adaptation to nonlinearities and disturbances. To further reinforce system resilience, ultracapacitors (UCs) are integrated to provide rapid power support. At the same time, a Static Synchronous Series Compensator (SSSC) is coordinated at the tie-line to improve inter-area damping. The proposed AI-optimised TIDF and UCs-SSSC framework is evaluated under practical constraints, including generation-rate limits, governor dead-band nonlinearity, and communication time delays (CTDs), ensuring realistic operational modelling. Simulation studies with 10% step-load disturbances demonstrate that the FFA-tuned TIDF significantly outperforms conventional PI and PID controllers, reducing oscillations, undershoot, and settling time. The coordinated UCs-SSSC strategy further strengthens dynamic performance, achieving superior stability margins across all operating scenarios. Sensitivity analysis with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 25 \%$</tex> load variations confirms the robustness of the proposed AI-enabled control architecture. The results highlight the effectiveness of AI-assisted optimisation and coordinated device control in achieving reliable frequency regulation in renewable rich innovative grid architectures.

title: CNN2D-Mamba: A Hybrid Deep Learning Architecture for Multi-Temporal SAR Crop Classification in Semi-Arid Regions
authors: Mohamed El Asri, Sara Zada, Imad Hamdi, Hamid Nebdi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Accurate agricultural crop classification in semi-arid regions is essential for food security monitoring and sustainable water resource management. This study introduces CNN2DMamba, a novel hybrid deep learning architecture combining 2D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for spatial feature extraction with State Space Models (SSM) for temporal sequence modeling. The proposed method was evaluated on multi-temporal Sentinel-1A/B SAR imagery over the Tadla plain in Morocco, one of the most strategic agricultural regions in North Africa. Six SAR-derived features (VH, VV, Cross-Ratio, NDRatio, RVI, and VH/VV ratio) were computed from 10 temporal acquisitions covering the 2023-2024 agricultural season. The dataset comprised 2,192 image patches ($32 \times 32$ pixels) representing two major crop types: wheat and sugar beet. Model robustness was rigorously assessed using stratified 5-fold cross-validation repeated across 5 random seeds (25 total runs). Experimental results demonstrate that CNN2D-Mamba achieves an Overall Accuracy of $\mathbf{9 6. 8 5 \%} \pm \mathbf{0. 9 8 \%}$ with a Cohen’s Kappa of $\mathbf{0. 9 3 7} \pm$ 0.020 and a weighted F1-score of $0.969 \pm 0.010$. The per-class analysis reveals recall rates of 98.3% for wheat and 95.4% for sugar beet. The proposed architecture outperforms conventional approaches including CNN-LSTM, CNN-BiLSTM, and CNNTransformer while maintaining computational efficiency through Mamba’s linear-time complexity. These findings highlight the potential of combining spatial CNN features with SSM-based temporal modeling for operational crop monitoring in waterstressed agricultural environments.

title: Leveraging Influencer Marketing and Online Reviews to Enhance Trust in E-Commerce Purchase and Word of Mouth Intentions
authors: Muhammad Bintang Dewantara, Anindya Elia Sari, Shofa Nur Zahwa, Stefanus Rumangkit, Christopher Joshua Leksana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study aims to investigate the influence of influencer marketing and online Customer reviews on Purchase Intention and Intention through word of mouth, with trust as a mediating factor. The population in this study were consumers who had shopped online, with a sample of 280 respondents. The sampling technique used was non-probability sampling with a purposive sampling method. This study used the Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) approach to analyze data collected from consumers who actively use social media and online platforms in making purchasing decisions. The results showed that influencer marketing and online Customer reviews did not positively affect purchase intention. While influencer marketing and online Customer reviews positively impact purchase intention, they affect intention through word of mouth. Trust plays an important mediating role, strengthening the impact of influencer marketing and online Customer reviews on purchase intention.

title: Federated Learning Driven Liver Cancer Detection in IoMT Networks
authors: Sourav Kumar Bhoi, Kalyan Kumar Jena, Rakesh Kumar Panda, Subrat Panda, Tusar Khatoi, Roshan Sekhar Mallick, Sritam Nahak
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Liver cancer is disease that is difficult to identify at an early stage. Now a days machine learning (ML) is widely used for detecting the liver cancer at an early stage. Also, the hospitals are using Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) technology to connect with the server for detection of different diseases. In this work, we identified the liver cancer using IoMT architecture. Here, federated learning is implemented, where instead of transmitting patient data (liver cancer) to the edge server, the model is trained locally at the hospitals that reduces the computation load over the edge server and also it reduces the data transfer mechanism. We used a weighted stacking ensemble machine learning (EML) approach using combination of AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, and SVM. This allows the ensemble approach to achieve high performance. The simulations are conducted in Python environment by comparing weighted stacking EML approach with models such as Logistic Regression (LogReg), Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest(RF), Gradient Boosting (GBDT), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Naive Bayes (NB), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and AdaBoost (AB) using performance metrics like accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC, and ROC. From the results, it is seen that EML achieves the highest accuracy of 95.8%. Also, it shows precision of 95.9%, recall of 95.8%, F1 of 95.6% and AUC of 99.7%. Also, EML shows lower energy consumption with federated learning framework as compared to centralized framework that makes the system energy efficient.

title: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Geospatial Applications: From Foundation Models to Physics-Informed Systems
authors: Edy Irwansyah, Hady Pranoto, Muhamad Iqbal Januadi Putra
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen-AI) has emerged as a transformative approach in geospatial analytics, addressing structural limitations of traditional discriminative models such as label scarcity, spatial heterogeneity, and crossdomain generalization. By learning data distributions, generative models synthesize realistic and semantically consistent geospatial data, enabling advancements in Earth observation, environmental monitoring, and spatial prediction. This study provides a comprehensive review of foundational concepts, technical approaches, and application domains of Gen-AI in geospatial contexts, highlighting recent breakthroughs in multimodal foundation models and physicsinformed architectures.Specifically, the paper examines TerraMind, a foundation model jointly developed by IBM and the European Space Agency, recognized as the first any-to-any multimodal generative model for Earth observation. TerraMind can process nine distinct modalities through a unified architecture and achieves superior performance, surpassing 12 competing models while requiring 10 times less computational cost. Similarly, the DiffusionSat model leverages diffusion mechanisms combined with geolocation-conditioned metadata to achieve breakthroughs in super-resolution, inpainting, and temporal image generation.A key research frontier explored is the integration of domain knowledge through physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), including Spherical DYffusion and AQ-PINNs, which embed conservation laws into deep learning architectures. These advances enhance the physical plausibility of generated outputs and substantially reduce computational overhead, with documented model speedups up to $25 \times$ and parameter reductions of over 50%. However, challenges remain in deploying Gen-AI models at scale, particularly concerning physical consistency, real-time inference, energy efficiency, and multi-sensor interoperability. This paper concludes by outlining future directions for sustainable, physically grounded generative models in geospatial science.

title: Energy-Efficient Protocols and AI-Driven Optimization for Sustainable Green Networking in 6G Communication Systems
authors: Vikas Sharma, Tarun Kumar Vashishth, Shashiraj Teotia, Barkha Samania, Manoj Kumar, Sachin Kaushik
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Rapid advancement of wireless communication technologies, as transition from 5 G to 6 G, is ushering in new and distinct opportunities and challenges to advance energy efficiency and environmental sustainability. This paper attempts to advance the proposed energy efficient protocols and application of AI-based optimization techniques to promote the design of environmentally sustainable green networking architectures in 6 G communication systems. The major contribution of the research was to create a framework, from the energy efficient model, in the context of current energy efficient protocols, where AI-based machine learning algorithms were utilized to allocate dynamic resources, predict traffic and provide energy aware routing considerations. Simulation experiments subsequently demonstrated significant improvement in energy efficiency the network while meeting other Quality of Service (QoS) and latency requirements.

title: Linking Physical Models and Immersive Visualization: A Virtual Reality Approach to Projectile Motion
authors: Zakia Hammouch, Mohammed El Hadi, Abdelkader Ettauri, Mohammed Hbibi, Reda Boussetta, Ali Hachmi, Omar Mommadi, Abdelaziz El Moussaouy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) offers promising opportunities for physics education, enabling the immersive visualization of abstract and spatially complex phenomena. Traditional instructional approaches to projectile motion primarily use twodimensional representations, which can hinder students understanding of motion in three-dimensional space. This paper presents the design and development of a VR-based projectile motion simulation implemented using the Unity game engine. The proposed environment enables learners to manipulate initial conditions and observe projectile trajectories in real time, while displaying the associated analytical equations. Adopting a design-oriented approach, this work aims to support conceptual understanding of projectile motion by explicitly linking mathematical models with immersive visual representations. The simulation is intended as a pedagogical tool with the potential to enhance student engagement and facilitate learning in secondary and early undergraduate physics education.

title: Systematic Investigation of Core Insert Breakage in Plastic Injection Molds: A Case Study
authors: Hamza El Fahime, Mohammed Radouani, Benaissa El Fahime
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In plastic injection molding, core inserts operate under coupled thermal and mechanical loading. High-paced production where repeated heating-cooling cycles and tight dimensional constraints create failure conditions governed by interacting effects rather than a single isolated cause. Early breakage of core inserts therefore remains a recurrent issue associated with unplanned interruptions and tooling losses. This work presents a systematic failure-analysis case study aimed at identifying the dominant contributors to core insert breakage using experimental measurements supported by finite element analysis. The investigation follows a staged protocol: general inspection, material characterization, theoretical load assessment complemented by numerical simulation, and geometrical verification through tolerance and design checks. The proposed framework provides a transferable, physicsgrounded basis for mechanistic interpretation of insert failure and for defining testable routes to verify corrective interventions in injection-mold tooling systems.

title: Sliding K-Means and Progressive Deep Learning for Dynamic Hand Gesture Recognition in Human Computer Interaction
authors: Arpneek Kaur, Sandhya Bansal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The booming area of research on Automated Dynamic Hand Gesture Recognition (DHGR) in AI serves its function well, not only for the speech and hearing-impaired community but also finding its applications in gesture recognition in electronics, robots, drones, vehicles, AR and VR etc., which necessitate gesture input from the user for performance of certain actions, factors for large demands of the research area to vibrancy. Normally the steps in DHGR with deep learning are the extraction of suitable key frames from the input video gesture and training the deep learning model with the key frames that are then acquired. The present paper proposes a DHGR model using a sliding window k-means clustering method for key frames extraction and progressive learning in 3DCNN. The developed model was trained and tested on the IPN hand dataset and ISL Emergency dataset and that by ASL J & Z dataset attaining an accuracy of 89%, 99% and 98.85% respectively. State-of-the-art comparisons show the advantages of the method for key frame extraction and the training method in the suggested model being used.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning–based Robot Path Planning using PPO in 2D Dynamic Environments
authors: Bharani Sethupandian S, Gottipati Ashokarao, Gangireddy Prabhakar Reddy, Md Keramot Hossain Mondal, P Sudheer, S. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Autonomous navigation for robots in dynamic environments is an important but challenging task due to the presence of dynamic obstacles and uncertainties in the environment. This paper proposes a deep reinforcement learning framework based on a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm for efficient path planning in dynamic environments represented as 2D occupancy grids. The CNN is used for efficient feature learning in the environment, and the PPO algorithm is used for efficient learning through a clipped objective function. A structured reward function is also designed for efficient path planning. The framework is trained and tested using a large-scale dataset for occupancy grid maps. The experimental results have shown a high success rate of 97%, an Intersection over Union value of 0.89, a high Dice score value of 0.93, and a low rate of collisions at 2.1%. The proposed framework is efficient and robust for dynamic path planning tasks and future robotic applications.

title: Artificial Intelligence Visual Studio Code Reviewer
authors: Adnan Shaout, Akshat Sikligar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This paper introduces an automated code-analysis and unit-test generation system developed using the Waterfall software engineering methodology. The system is implemented as a full-stack application with an Angular frontend, Java/SpringBoot backend, and an integrated Visual Studio (VS) Code extension. Using the OpenAI API, the system analyzes source code, detects potential code smells, and generates unit test templates to assist developers in improving software quality. The use of a software process ensured a structured and traceable development workflow was in place. The AI Visual Studio code review extension introduced in this paper is able to automate unit test generation and detect code smells. This tool provides an integrated system between a frontend application, backend services, and OpenAI API integration. Experimental results show that the system can reliably detect common code issues and produce meaningful unit tests that aligns with standard software testing practices. The paper contributes to ongoing research and industry efforts aimed at improving software quality assurance and showcases how modern AI-assisted tooling can enhance developer productivity.

title: XAI-Driven Sleep Apnea Detection: Enhancing Transparency in ECG-Based AI Models
authors: Kuldeep Kumar Mishra, Anubhav Sharma, Manoj Chopra, Nikhat Raza, Shivani Tiwari, Nishesh Nigam
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The present paper suggests a new Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI)-based framework of ECG signal-based sleep apnea detection with a focus on improving the model transparency, interpretability, and diagnostic reliability. In contrast to the traditional concept of the black-box deep learning method, the developed system will unite Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) with attention-based feature allocation schemes and, thus, will be able to identify cardiac abnormalities associated with apnea precisely. An adapted ECG signal preprocessing pipeline with adaptive filtering and morphological segmentation guarantees the highly effective features extraction with minimal interference of noise. Decisions of the model are explained by using the Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) and SHAP-based explainability modules, which provide clinicians with clear sight on the physiological patterns that impact the predictions. Moreover, a model trust evaluation metric is presented to be able to measure interpretability and performance accuracy. This technical proposal will bring the gap between the state-of-the-art AI classifiers and clinical disclosures, establishing a basis to achieve safer, explainable systems of diagnostics in sleep medicine. The methodology provides the means of a regulatory compliant, explainable AI solution in the biomedical signal analysis.

title: Gender Bias in Resume Screening using SHAP and LIME
authors: Namrata Khade, Bhakti Deshpande, Samarth Bondarde, Shruti Kamble, Shravani Kadu, Prema Kadam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Online hiring platforms that use Artificial Intelligence to screen resumes are becoming more common because they save time and make the hiring process more efficient. The Artificial Intelligence in these hiring platforms might learn the wrong things from old hiring data and so the online hiring platforms can be unfair to people because of their gender. The online hiring platforms that use Artificial Intelligence are supposed to make things easier. The Artificial Intelligence in these online hiring platforms can make mistakes and that is not good, for people who are looking for a job. The paper describes an explainable solution FairHireXAI which uses Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods to find and explain gender bias in automatic resume screening. The method marries a Random Forest decision-making model with SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations) to articulate the reasoning behind model decisions and locate possible bias. A fabricated set of 10,000 resume profiles featuring equal numbers of males and females was used to portray actual hiring situations Tests demonstrate that the main technical elements such as duration of work, number of skills, and schooling level are the ones really affecting the decision to hire; however minor gender-based differences in treatment can still be detected. The fairness indicators like Demographic Parity Difference (DPD) and Equal Opportunity Difference (EOD) serve to measure these differences. This paper has been accepted for publication in IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence.

title: FraudPredictor: A Real-Time Transaction Analysis Machine Learning Model
authors: Soma Vaishnavi, B.K. Aishwarya, K. Vamshi, M.Sunil Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The main issue facing the digital banking sector at present is the double-edged sword of using transactions that can be redeemed and credit cards that are fraudulent. In order to be fraud-free, this paper suggests a two-tier AI system, which uses the Random Forest algorithm and is able to give very quick feedback on suspicious transactions. The system checks user habits, transaction types, and amounts that are off the norm to make a decision whether a transaction is legit or a hoax. It has a modular structure, which includes among others, the steps of data preparation, construction of relevant features and execution of the model learning phase, followed by result validation and final system deployment, all using cost- efficient tools like Python and Flask. The system also offers very clear visualizations of the results that enhance comprehension of the findings. The study addresses the issues of online detection and proposes the development of adaptive learning and hybrid methods as the next step during evolution of fraud discovery.

title: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Autoencoder for Unsupervised IoT Network Traffic Representation Learning
authors: Shikha Khullar, Chennaiah Kate, Kantimahanthi Padmaja, Prashanth Kumar Ravula, A.Hyils Sharon Magdalene, S. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) networks has led to the generation of large-scale distributed traffic data, raising significant privacy and security concerns in centralized learning frameworks. In order to resolve the issues faced in the centralized learning approach, this paper proposes a privacy-preserving federated autoencoder for unsupervised IoT network traffic representation learning. The proposed architecture is tested using the CYBRIA IoT data set, with the metrics used including convergence of loss, stability of the client, and communication efficiency metrics. The proposed architecture shows the convergence of the loss function in just 15 communication rounds, with the convergence loss being 0.0305, while the inter-client variance is minimal. The proposed architecture is validated using the capability of the learned representations in detecting anomalies in the data, thus showing the efficacy of the proposed architecture in real-world IoT systems, especially in the absence of data labels.

title: EEG-Based Harmful Brain Activity Classification with Hybrid 2D and 3D CNN Ensemble Architecture
authors: Onat Ender, Ömer Faruk Alkan, Enes Ayan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) remains the primary clinical tool for identifying epileptic and other harmful neurological activities, yet interpreting long recordings by hand is slow and prone to disagreement between clinicians. This motivates automated systems that can support diagnosis without replacing expert judgment. Current deep learning studies mostly rely on 2D convolutional neural networks (CNNs) applied to spectrograms, which capture frequency information but discard spatial relations between channels. As a result, they struggle to model how pathological activity evolves across both time and electrode placements. This study introduces a hybrid ensemble architecture that combines transfer-learning-based 2D CNNs with a custom 3D CNN to jointly leverage spectral power and spatial depth. EEG signals are arranged using the Longitudinal Bipolar (Double Banana) montage and processed with Wavelet Transform-based denoising to enhance signal quality. Five pre-trained models (DenseNet121, Xception, MobileNet, InceptionV3, NASNetLarge) form the 2D branch, while the 3D branch learns volumetric representations from stacked spectrograms. Their predictions are fused using a weighted ensemble strategy to balance sensitivity and robustness. Experiments on a large and imbalanced labeled EEG dataset show that this hybrid ensemble architecture achieves an accuracy above 0.73, outperforming individual models. The results suggest that incorporating spatial depth alongside spectral features strengthens automated detection of harmful EEG patterns and may support more consistent clinical decision-making.

title: Kerberos-Blockchain Cross-Authentication: A Smart-Contract Framework for Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs)
authors: Mohammad Alauthman, Amjad Aldweesh, Ahmad Al-Qerem, Ghadeer Albesani, Akram Mustafa, Mouhammd Alkasassbeh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Vehicular ad hoc networks need rapid authentication under short contacts, intermittent backhaul, and roaming across operators. Kerberos provides efficient ticket-based session keys, but it assumes reachable domain KDCs and offers limited cross-operator audit and revocation. KBXAuth is a hybrid design that keeps Kerberos as the fast admission path and uses a permissioned blockchain only for cross-domain consistency. The ledger stores compact attestations as constant-size commitments with counters and validity windows; it never stores tickets, session keys, or clear vehicle identities. RSUs validate Kerberos tickets locally and consult the ledger only when roaming or risk policy requires it. We specify the protocol workflow, RSU decision logic, and a minimal smart contract for registration, verification, and revocation. We also outline an evaluation plan and practical feasibility considerations.

title: Survey of AI and Cytological Techniques for Cancer Subclassification and Diagnosis
authors: Saurabh Gupta, Vijayshri Chaurasia, Sunil Kureel, Mamta Patankar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: In this literature review will done on cyto-pathology based cancer detection and classification using Advance Ai machine learning and deep learning model in different parts of human bodies. In addition to new non-invasive and invasive diagnostic procedures, it emphasizes conventional staining methods. The accuracy of cancer classification and segmentation has been greatly improved by recent developments in artificial intelligence, especially deep learning models like PCMCT, HiAt-Net, and PyMLViT. Despite these achievements, challenges persist in areas such as preprocessing standardization, biologically informed feature extraction, and model generalization across domains. Future research directions emphasize developing interpretable AI frameworks, creating large annotated datasets, and designing robust, real-time diagnostic systems for effective clinical deployment.

title: Multimodal ResNet-based CNN for Early Autism Spectrum Disorder Detection and Severity Stratification
authors: J. Suji Priya, R. Karolin, T M. Harini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder is a developmental disorder that has an effect on a child's ability to communicate, behave, and interact socially. Early detection enables timely intervention and improved outcomes. This work proposes a novel AI-based multimodal diagnostic system for identifying individuals with autism by integrating facial image analysis with behavioral questionnaire data as key indicators. The system extracts visual features through convolutional neural networks, while the behavioral questionnaire provides the necessary measurements for screening purposes. All feature sets obtained from both modalities are combined through a multimodal fusion process to enhance the overall accuracy of prediction. The proposed multi modal autism detection system was evaluated using standard evaluation metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The evaluation results reveal that the proposed model improves upon existing single modal detection systems. This study concludes that incorporating multimodal data for the identification and evaluation of autism will aid health care providers and caregivers in obtaining an early diagnosis as well as an initial estimate of severity.

title: Hybrid Artificial Intelligence Internet of Things Platform for Personalized Healthcare Recommendations and Smart Patient Care Services
authors: B.Girirajan, R. Ashok Kumar, Anita Sofia Liz.D. R, Muthukumaran K, J L Aldo Stalin, Richa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) has transformed healthcare by enabling intelligent, data-driven, and patient-centric solutions. Traditional systems often lack real-time monitoring and personalization, resulting in delayed diagnosis and inefficient care. This study presents a hybrid AI–IoT platform that combines machine learning, wearable IoT sensors, and cloud analytics to provide personalized healthcare recommendations and smart patient management. Physiological data such as heart rate, temperature, oxygen level, and activity are analyzed using a hybrid deep learning model integrating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for health prediction and anomaly detection. The system achieved 96.7% accuracy, with 95.4% precision and 94.8% recall. Real-time monitoring reduced emergency response time by 32% and improved diagnostic reliability by 28%. Overall, the hybrid AI–IoT framework demonstrates strong potential to enhance personalized care, optimize clinical decisions, and promote proactive healthcare delivery.

title: The influence of EEG noise and filtering strategies on feature extraction for neurological applications
authors: Maryem Berradi, Youssef Ben Youssef, Hamid El Malali, Azeddine Mouhsen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) is among the essential tools for the analysis of brain dynamics and the diagnosis of neurological disorders such as epilepsy, sleep disturbances, and cognitive dysfunctions, but its use poses a big problem because of the difficulty of its treatment. The challenge of EEG signal processing is great due to its low amplitude, high non-stationarity, and high sensitivity to physiological and environmental artifacts. The signal quality is impacted by these disturbances, which also compromises the reliability of the extracted characteristics“ including temporal, spectral, nonlinear, and connectivity metrics”. The influence of common EEG artifacts is examined in this article, such as ocular movements (EOG), muscle activity (EMG), cardiac interference (ECG), electrode motion, and power-line noise, on both the neurophysiological information contained in EEG recordings and the derived quantitative features. We will review conventional and advanced filtering methods, including FIR and IIR filters, notch and band-pass filtering, Independent Component Analysis (ICA), Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD), wavelet-based denoising, and Singular Spectrum Analysis (SSA). We present a methodological framework to select the best filtering strategies based on the application domain “epileptic seizure detection, sleep scoring, brain-computer interfaces (BCI), or deep-learning-based classification ”. The results indicate that a rigorous preprocessing pipeline is required to guarantee signal integrity and improve the strength of analytical results and machine-learning models used in clinical neurophysiology.

title: Performance Evaluation of a Smart Wearable Tumor Monitoring System for Predictive Chemotherapy Dosing
authors: Aya El Bouzid, Yassine Salih-Alj
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper assesses the performance of a smart chemotherapy dosing framework for tumor monitoring and adaptive drug delivery. Building on prior wearable and closedloop drug delivery concepts, the proposed approach integrates tumor growth modeling and pharmacokineticpharmacodynamic (PK-PD) dynamics with machine learningbased predictive dosing. Simulated tumor volume and drug concentration time-series data are generated to emulate realtime monitoring conditions. Regression and time-series artificial intelligence models, including Random Forests and recurrent neural networks (LSTM), are developed to predict optimal chemotherapy dosing sequences. These ML methods are then evaluated against baseline dosing strategies using quantitative metrics such as RMSE, R-square, MAE, and therapeutic window compliance. Results demonstrate high predictive accuracy (0.99) and improved dosing precision and adaptability, providing a validated foundation for future integration with wearable tumor monitoring and drug delivery systems.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Chopper-Stabilized Current-Feedback Instrumentation Amplifier for EEG and Biopotential Acquisition
authors: Younes Laababid, Mohammed Habib, Mohammed Chaman, Zghaid Mustapha, Karim El Khadiri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents a low-power chopperstabilized CMOS amplifier for EEG signal acquisition related to voluntary motor control in the primary motor cortex. Designed in a $0.18 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ CMOS process, the amplifier employs an ACcoupled chopper core to suppress 1/f noise, with tunable MOS capacitors and pseudo-resistors enabling programmable bandwidth. A positive-feedback impedance boosting loop (IBL) enhances input impedance and CMRR, ensuring accurate recording of low-amplitude neural signals. The circuit includes input protection and a band-pass filter tuned to the $\mathbf{1 4 - 3 0 ~ H z}$ beta rhythm. Simulation results demonstrate a mid-band gain of $\mathbf{7 2 ~ d B}$, a CMRR of $\mathbf{1 1 8 ~ d B}$, and an input-referred noise of $\mathbf{2 0 0} \mathrm{nV} / \sqrt{ } \mathrm{Hz}$ at $\mathbf{3 0 ~ H z}$, while consuming only $0.95 \mu \mathrm{~W}$ from a $\pm 0.9 \mathrm{~V}$ supply. With a noise-efficiency factor of 2.1, the design achieves an excellent balance between low noise, power, and area, making it suitable for compact, high-density EEG and neural acquisition systems.

title: Enhancing Pathology Diagnostics with Deep Learning and Feature Attribution
authors: Dayanshi Jain, Anjal Bijghavane, Aman Dubey, Vijayshri Chaurasia, J. S. Yadav
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Breast cancer is the most commonly diagnosed cancer among women, with an estimate of 2.3 million cases in the past year. Limited access to pathologists in many regions contributes to delays in breast cancer diagnosis, exacerbating mortality rates. To address these challenges, AI and advanced deep learning architectures are integrated into computer-aided diagnostic (CAD) systems to streamline & enhance diagnostic workflows. In this work, potential of employing a fine-tuned ResNet50 for binary classification of breast cancer images is demonstrated. The performance was impressive with a classification accuracy of 97.47%. Techniques such as Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations were used to aid in understanding visualization of features classifying images to the clinicians. The results provide evidence for the effectiveness of using the resnet50 based model for assisting pathologists in achieving accurate and timely diagnosis, thereby improving the diagnostics process.

title: Real-Time Identity Verification for 6G Network Slicing Using Blockchain and Machine Learning
authors: Omer Waseem AL-Zaidi, Mohammed Al-Hubaishi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: 6G network slicing expands the identity and access-management attack surface by introducing highly dynamic, multi-tenant slice lifecycles with heterogeneous security requirements. Existing approaches often treat blockchain-based trust and machine-learning (ML) analytics as separate components, leaving a gap in end-to-end, slice-aware identity verification that is auditable across slices while remaining operationally deployable. This paper proposes a pipeline-level framework that (i) uses smart contracts to anchor identity claims, verification decisions, and cross-slice consistency checks on-chain, and (ii) applies off-chain ML to classify slice/service profiles from operational features so that appropriate slice-specific security policies can be selected and enforced. We evaluate multiple classifiers on a simulated 6G slicing benchmark built from QoS requirements, transaction latency, and resource-allocation indicators. On this benchmark, Random Forest and XGBoost achieved 100% test accuracy for the constructed slice-type label, and feature-importance analysis highlights QoS and resource-allocation patterns as the dominant signals. To avoid overclaiming, we note that the current label construction can partially overlap with input features (risking inflated performance), and we outline validation on independently labeled and real-world traces as future work.

title: Smart Portable Chest Worn Respiratory Device for Continuous Health Monitoring
authors: Tryphena Prishiba P, Harish I, Manishdharshini K, Monika S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Real-time breathing monitoring is indispensable for the Preliminary detection and management of pulmonary disorders such as asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and Sleep-associated breathing problems. This paper presents the design and Construction of a portable chest-worn respiratory monitoring device for Regular health parameter tracking in everyday activities. The proposed system Adopts a Non-intrusive chest-worn sensor to Collect respiratory signals associated with thoracic movement during inhalation and exhalation. An embedded microcontroller processes the Captured signals filtered using signal-processing methods to extract key respiratory parameters, including respiratory rate, breathing pattern, and Irregularity markers. The compact and Compact and light design Enhances user comfort and suitability for long-term wear. Live data Illustration is provided through a local display, Allowing instant feedback to the user. The system is designed to be low-cost, energy-efficient, and suitable for home-based healthcare applications. The proposed device Illustrates ability for continuous respiratory evaluation, early detection of unusual conditions, and effective monitoring in both clinical and Home-based healthcare settings.

title: Deep Adaptive Perceptual Generative Adversarial Network for Secure Cloud Computing and Big Data Protection to Enable Cyber Threat Prevention
authors: Ruba A, Monisha Priya R, Bangalore Lakshmi Roopa, Naveen G, N. Ramshankar, Kamalarajan P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The spread of cloud-based infrastructures and big data-driven environments has also heightened vulnerability to highly complex cyber threats, and this requires more sophisticated security systems that can be implemented in dynamically and massively scaled environments. The paper suggests a Deep Adaptive Perceptual Generative Adversarial Network (DAP-GAN) proposal of securing cloud computing and big data protection with the intent of preventing cyber threats proactively. This system combines the deep perceptual feature extraction with the adversarial representation learning in order to capture the intricate patterns of behavior and increase the visibility of anomalies in the context of heterogeneous clouds. The framework creates benign data distributions through a generator-discriminator adversarial loop and increases malicious transaction deviations to allow better threat discrimination and lower false negative rates. In addition, adaptive threat analytics and predictive risk scoring enable the current hostile actions like data exfiltration, privilege elevation, and distributed denial-of-service to be identified at an early stage. Security handling systems such as encryption, access regulations and policy-based countermeasures are implemented to protect sensitive resources and ensure compliance in distributed cloud systems. Compared to alternative architectures, experimental assessments prove that the introduced architecture facilitates scalable, intelligent, and resilient security of the current cloud systems, which makes this approach viable as a proactive paradigm of cyber defense in the context of the new big data surroundings.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Crop Yield Prediction Using Agro-Climatic Data
authors: Gurmeet Kaur, Ashanand, Rupinder Singh, Preet Kamal, Rakhi Dua, Kamlesh Kukreti
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Proper forecasting of crop yield is a requirement of proper agricultural planning and protection of food security. The current work presents a broad machine-learning-driven model of the prediction of crop yield, combining the range of regression models with strict evaluation criteria and elucidable artificial intelligence (XAI) methodologies. Agro-climatic, edaphic and nutrient related predictors were trained in decision tree, random forest, gradient-boosting, k nearest neighbors, support vector regression and deep neural networks. The evaluation of performance was conducted with the help of R2 and Root-Mean-Square-Error (RMSE), which showed that ensemble and deep -learning methods provide the best predictive values. Specifically, the highest accuracy was achieved with the Random Forest and Deep Neural Networks, which underscore their ability to estimate complex non-linear relationships among the dataset. Analysis of feature-importance identified temperature, humidity and soil quality as the most influential predictors of yield. Besides, lack of systematic bias and high generalisation was supported by error residual diagnostics. The explanations provided with SHAP also provided global and local answers to the decisions made by the model, thus increasing transparency and interpretability. Together, the proposed framework provides accurate, consistent and interpretable crop-yield predictions, providing priceless insights into data-driven agronomic decision-making and the promotion of sustainable farming systems.

title: Fatigue Life Assessment of Monobloc Railway Wheels Using S–N (Basquin) Method and Hertzian Contact Analysis
authors: Sabah Louragli, Bouchra Abouelanouar, Abdeslam Lachhab
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The fatigue resistance of a monobloc ER8 steel wheel is evaluated using a stress-life ($\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{N}$) approach based on Basquin’s law, with local stresses calculated through Hertzian contact theory. A fatigue safety factor accounts for uncertainties related to loading conditions and material properties. The predicted number of cycles is converted into equivalent service distances. A wheel with a 445 mm radius and 135 mm tread width is analyzed under representative loads. The tread center is safe up to $5.1 \times 10^{6}$ cycles $(\approx 7120 \mathrm{~km})$, while the edge exhibits the shortest lifespan, approximately 2510 km. The combined approach of Hertzian contact analysis, $\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{N}$ modeling, and a fatigue safety factor provides a reliable methodology for predicting wheel fatigue life and planning maintenance operations.

title: Federated Learning with Bayesian Optimization for Lightweight IoT DDoS Detection
authors: Mahmoud Aljamal, Mohammad Aljaidi, Wael Hadi, Zaid Jawasreh, Ayoub Alsarhan, Rejwan Bin Sulaiman, Ahmad Abdullah Alshammari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks are among the most devastating attacks on the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem, in which a plethora of millions of less powerful devices can be easily recruited to botnets such as Mirai to saturate the target to affect service. Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) of DDoS are founded on the centralized training that is implemented based on the raw traffic data, which implies the privacy concerns, and fail to represent the variety of traffic patterns of various IoT fields. To address these issues, we suggest the federated learning-based detection framework that will enable IoT gateways to collectively train a global model without sharing raw data. At local, all clients obstruct flow-level statistical properties of traffic and feed them into a simple deep neural network, and only model updates are swapped with the central aggregator without compromising either privateness or generalization tenacity. The framework combines the following two to address the data imbalance and heterogeneity: the class-weighted loss functions and adaptive aggregation strategies. Top IoT dataset CICIoT2023 evaluations are run, which report accuracy of 99.3%. Macro-F1 of 0.989 and ROC-AUC 0.996, which indicates a maximum of 3.7 percentage points over top of centralized baselines in macro f1. The last model (2.1 MB, 0.85 M parameters) spends 12.4 ms on inference latency on average in scenarios where a Raspberry Pi 4B is used and demonstrates that the model is viable to run in real time to offer a privacy-preserving DDoS benevolent detector in a heterogeneous IoT network.

title: A Piecewise Fuzzy SIER Model for Alzheimer Disease Progression
authors: K. Mythili Gnanapriya, T. Chandrapushpam, M. Gowri Manohari, S. Ruth Kethsial, M. Dhanalakshmi, R. Naveenkumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by slow, uncertain, and stage-wise deterioration of cognitive function. Classical deterministic models are often inadequate for representing the inherent ambiguity in disease progression rates and therapeutic responses. To address this limitation, this paper developed a piecewise fuzzy Susceptible–Exposed–Infected–Removed (SIER) model in which the transition parameters for susceptibility, exposure, symptomatic progression, and removal are formulated as triangular fuzzy numbers to capture neurological and biological uncertainty. The model further incorporates piecewise-defined time intervals to represent changes in dynamical behavior before and after medical interventions such as drug initiation, cognitive therapy, or lifestyle modification. Analytical expressions for the fuzzy progression index, fuzzy equilibrium states, and piecewise stability conditions are derived using fuzzy differential calculus. Numerical simulations illustrate how parameter fuzziness and regime switching influence the temporal evolution of disease stages. The results demonstrate that the proposed model more accurately represents real-world variability and uncertainty compared to classical deterministic SIER frameworks, making it a promising mathematical tool for studying Alzheimer’s disease dynamics.

title: ZIF-8 Deposition on Optical Fibers and Silicon Wafers for Methane Sensing
authors: Artur Samp, Valerius Abb, Alex Wolfgramm, Tobias Reichenstein, Jörg Franke, Martin Kammler
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), such as Zeolitic Imidazolate Framework-8 (ZIF-8), are increasingly explored for sensing applications. In this field, the formation of uniform thin films is often essential. This study details the deposition and stepwise characterization of covalently bonded ZIF-8 thin films for methane sensing on two types of silica-based substrates: silicon wafers with native oxide and optical fibers. We emphasize practical and accessible methods to verify each step of the deposition process. Surface preparation was tailored to each substrate. Silicon wafer substrates were cleaned using Caro’s acid, while optical fibers were activated via oxygen plasma to account for handling constraints. Both substrate types were subsequently functionalized with a monolayer of isopropyltrimethoxysilane (IPTES) to promote covalent bonding of ZIF-8. Particles of ZIF-8 were deposited using a seeded growth approach. Solvothermal synthesis of ZIF-8 was performed, while simultaneously immersing the substrates in the synthesis solution, promoting the formation of a continuous thin film. The deposition process was characterized at each stage. Free ZIF-8 powder obtained from the synthesis was centrifuged, washed with ethanol and analyzed by attenuated total reflection infrared (ATR-IR) spectroscopy. Silicon wafers enabled contact angle measurements to assess IPTES functionalization, as well as spectroscopic ellipsometry for analysis of the formed ZIF-8 thin film. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) was used to examine film morphology on both substrate types. To evaluate methane sensitivity, optical fibers coated with ZIF-8 were exposed to vacuum and methane atmospheres, while changes in transmitted optical power were monitored to probe refractive index variations.

title: Model of Digital Transformation Resistance in the Auditor Profession
authors: Sarah Johana Magdalena Siregar, Bambang Leo Handoko
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Although the emergence of audit technology has clearly provided many benefits, it turns out that many auditors still refuse to adopt its use. This study aims to address this issue by developing a research model based on Innovation Resistance Theory, investigating the reasons why auditors resist digital transformation, and understanding the existing barriers: usage, value, risk, tradition, and image. We conducted quantitative research by distributing a survey to auditors in Indonesia. This survey involved 163 auditors, and the results were processed using partial least squares structural equation modeling (SEMPLS) for data analysis. The data showed that usage barriers (difficulties in adding new technology to natural routines) and image barriers (negative thoughts or fears about technology) were significant predictors of auditor resistance. Meanwhile, value, risk, and tradition barriers did not have a significant effect on resistance. These findings indicate that public accounting firms should prioritize the development of technology systems in terms of usability and assist auditors in perceiving the latest technology to increase the need for digital transformation.

title: Prediction of Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone Control Status Using Interpretable Ensemble Learning
authors: Rowan AlEjielat, Mahmoud E. Farfoura, Janthima Methaneethorn, Didi Rosiyadi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Accurate prediction of thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH) control remains challenging in hypothyroid patients in real-world settings due to complex clinical, metabolic, and behavioral factors. In this study we present an interpretable machine learning framework for predicting TSH control status using multimodal clinical data from treated hypothyroid patients. Sixteen features were engineered from biochemical markers, anthropometrics, treatment characteristics, and lifestyle variables, with class imbalance addressed using synthetic minority oversampling. Multiple models were evaluated, including logistic regression, gradient boosting, random forest, and a soft voting ensemble. The ensemble model achieved the best overall performance on a held-out test set (F1 = 0.89, accuracy = 0.83), while the random forest demonstrated the strongest discrimination (AUC = 0.81). Personalized dosing metrics, body mass index, hormonal markers, and disease duration were among the most influential predictors. These findings support the feasibility of transparent, machine learning–driven decision support for TSH control management. However, findings should be interpreted as preliminary, locally generated evidence. External validation on independent multi-center cohorts is required to confirm robustness, generalizability, and clinical applicability.

title: Method for Automating the Layout of Wireless Charging Infrastructure for Public Transport Vehicles
authors: Patrick Ziesel, Fabian Neff, Konstantin Kuch, Martin Cichon
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Transforming mobility to a shared, public, and decarbonised system is a key challenge of our time. Battery electric buses are a promising option for cities to reduce local greenhouse gas and noise emissions in public transport. The dominant charging strategy to date is concentrated during breaks but mainly overnight in depots, which leads to several disadvantages.The sustainable energy mix in Germany peaks during the daytime. Demand-oriented charging strategies hold a massive potential in increasing the overall efficiency due to smaller and lighter battery packs and shorter downtimes. Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer (DWPT) is considered an economic solution approach. This paper presents a method for automating the planning of the wireless charging systems for public transport. The output is an organized map indicating the optimized positioning and sizing of the inductive coils.Beginning with a demand-oriented energy estimation, the method uses operational, vehicle and infrastructure data to derive an optimized coil positioning within the serviced road network. Therefore, single route sections are evaluated in terms of their characteristics such as maximum permitted speed, route utilisation, stops, traffic lights and pedestrian crossings. Furthermore, the study addresses the challenges posed by the involvement of multiple stakeholders and the identification of barriers to integration with existing transport infrastructure. The study also considers the fusion of meta data from relevant sources such as the charging infrastructure and vehicle specifications to gain additional information and improve the planning process. The complex set of variable input parameters are sampled within an optimization loop to derive the first data based optimum. A posteriori gained restrictions due to local, urban-structural conditions such as telecommunication, energy and other lines narrow down the scope for a new iteration. The paper contributes to the development of sustainable urban mobility solutions by providing coherent approaches to an efficient design of sustainable public transport.

title: A Low-EMI Spread-Spectrum Clocking Circuit with Digital Calibration for High-Precision MCU On-Chip Oscillators
authors: Tianyuan Liu, Xu Meng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: The on-chip clock oscillator is fundamental to the performance and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) of microcontroller units (MCUs). This paper presents the design and simulation of a high-precision RC oscillator integrating an active spread-spectrum clocking (SSC) circuit for substantial electromagnetic interference (EMI) reduction. The design tackles the co-design challenge of maintaining frequency accuracy under modulation by employing a two-step digital calibration system (current-mirror coarse-tuning and resistor-network fine-tuning) in tandem with a triangular-wave-based SSC modulator. Implemented in a $\mathbf{1 1 0 n m}$ CMOS process, the oscillator supports a configurable center frequency $\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{~} \mathbf{~} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{1 6 ~ M H z} \boldsymbol{)}$. Post-layout simulation results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves a peak EMI suppression exceeding $\mathbf{1 0 ~ d B}$ while maintaining a calibrated frequency stability of better than $\pm 1.2 \%$ over the industrial temperature range ($-40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $105^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$), supply voltage variations, and all process corners. This work introduces a coordinated system-level approach to balance frequency precision and EMI mitigation for high-reliability MCU environments.

title: MASDA: A Multi-Agent System with Dynamic Planning and Adaptive Verification for Automated Data Analysis
authors: Achraf Ballari, Mohamed Assellaou, Omar Souissi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The use of Large Language Models for automated data analysis has become a transformative advance in Business Intelligence and decision-making. However, current single-agent approaches struggle with complex, multi-step analytical tasks that require iterative refinement, robust error correction and domain expertise. We propose MASDA-Multi-Agent System for Data Analysis-a framework that integrates six specialized agents operating through hierarchical, graph-based planning, collaborative generation, verification, reflection and experience-informed reasoning. Our design includes a hierarchical, dynamic planning system that decomposes complex analytical goals into task graphs with dependency handling; a Collaborative Verification Protocol (CVP) that uses a separate verifier agent to reduce selfverification bias; and a Case-Based Experience Memory (CBEM) that captures error-resolution patterns for crosstask learning. Evaluations on the InsightBench dataset show MASDA achieving insight and summary scores of 60% and 57%, respectively, matching the best baseline for insights and improving summary quality by 13 percentage points. On InfiAgent-DABench, MASDA achieves 84.05% accuracy with Claude Sonnet 4 and 80.16% with GPT-4o, both surpassing the previous best single-agent result of 78.99% achieved by GPT-4 across 257 questions of varying difficulty. Ablation experiments confirm CVP’s critical role, with a 7.95 percentage-point accuracy drop on hard questions when removed.

title: Machine Learning based Hybrid Technique for Cyber Attack Prediction in VANET
authors: Sumana Achar, M D Nandeesh, Tejaswini S, Bijendra Singh, Sadia Khan, Rovin Tiwari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: This paper introduces a hybrid machine learning-based framework that combines the Random Forest and the XGBoost machine learning algorithms for effective prediction of cyber-attacks in Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs). These networks are especially sensitive to various cyber-attacks, such as denial-of-service attacks, spoofing attacks, and the Sybil attacks, due to a very dynamic topology, decentralization architecture, and real-time communication needs in VANET environments. To overcome them, the hybrid approach is suggested, as the random forest feature selection power and preliminary classification are enabled to enable the principle of a strong feature selection with the help of random forest, after which the optimization power of the XGBoost is suggested to increase the predictive ability and reduce the level of misclassification through boosting. Its methodology includes preprocessing systematic data, feature engineering, normalizing, and class imbalance to attain trustworthy model training. The experimental outcomes suggest that the combined model is better in accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score compared to its individual counterparts, which allows the proposed integrated model to offer a scalable and efficient system of detecting and predicting cyber-attacks in real-time in intelligent transportation systems.

title: Comparison between the Mean-Shift and Spectral Clustering Algorithms for the Classification of Earthen Construction Materials in Essaouira
authors: Marouane Belhaouate, Mouna El Mkhalet, Nouzha Lamdouar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper compares Mean-Shift and Spectral Clustering for classifying earthen construction materials in Essaouira (Morocco). Mean-Shift identifies fine local modes based on density peaks, revealing micro-variations in clay, density, and swelling, while Spectral Clustering forms globally consistent families through graph-based connectivity. Using the same dataset, Mean-Shift produced five compact clusters versus three broader spectral groups. The study highlights the importance of validation metrics Silhouette, Davies-Bouldin, and CalinskiHarabasz for objective evaluation. Overall, Mean-Shift suits heterogeneity detection, whereas Spectral Clustering yields interpretable engineering families supporting sustainable earthen construction.

title: A 78–109-GHz Octupler Using Injection-Locked Phase-Difference Control and a Gilbert Cell With >50-dBc Harmonic Rejection
authors: Zhaoxiang Ren, Dongxin Ni, Chengrui Zhang, Liang Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents the analysis, design, and implementation of a wideband, high-efficiency, and high-harmonic-rejection (HR) octupler in 65-nm CMOS. A harmonic-recycling quadrupler generates a broadband excitation, while an injection-locked structure suppresses unwanted harmonics and controls phase relationships in the quadrupled output. A Gilbert cell further cancels odd- and even-mode components during frequency doubling, which reduces harmonic products and improves HR performance. An output-stage amplifier and balun enhance spectral purity by exploiting the intrinsic filtering and even-mode suppression. The proposed architecture is verified through even–odd mode theoretical analysis and circuit-level simulation. The design achieves an output frequency of 78–109 GHz (33.16% fractional bandwidth) with an input range of 9.75–13.625 GHz, delivering 8.01-dBm peak power with 5.75% conversion efficiency. The measured HR-ratio (HRR) exceeds 86.54 (1st), 68.17 (2nd), 83.44 (3rd), 59.32 (4th), 74.20 (5th), 58.32 (6th), 50.12 (7th), 50.98 (9th), 56.51 (10th), and 61.17 dBc (11th). The core occupies only 0.265 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Optimizing Campus Security Systems: Leveraging Ontologies and Large Language Models to Enhance Detection Accuracy and System Reliability
authors: Yangyi Xia, Shiyu Liang, Fuhong Jiang, Xian Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Ensuring campus security requires AI-driven anomaly detection systems that are not only accurate, but also interpretable and responsive. This study presents a decisionsupport framework that combines retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with ontology-driven reasoning and large language models (LLMs) to enhance security monitoring. A controlled experiment involving 30 professional security personnel and 50 simulated scenarios evaluated the system’s effectiveness. The results indicate a significant improvement in anomaly detection accuracy, increasing from 65% (baseline) to $85 {\%}(p=0.027)$, while reducing false alarms. Additionally, response times decreased by $\mathbf{5 2} {\%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{p}=$ 0.001) compared to conventional keyword-based retrieval systems. By integrating structured domain knowledge with adaptive AI reasoning, this approach facilitates precise threat identification and enables faster, well-informed security decisions. These findings demonstrate that ontology-enhanced AI can effectively address key limitations in security monitoring, offering a scalable and interpretable solution for real-time campus safety management.

title: On the Use of CTGAN Data Augmentation for Enhancing MLP-based Software Development Effort Estimation using small datasets
authors: Halima Bouaalal, Fatima Azzahra Amazal, Fatima Ezzahra Boujida
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Accurate effort estimation is vital for successful software project management, yet limited historical data often hinders the performance of estimation models. This study investigates the impact of CTGAN-based data augmentation on a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) architecture for Software Development Effort Estimation (SDEE). We assess the performance of an MLP model augmented with synthetic data generated by CTGAN using five benchmark datasets, namely ISBSG, COCOMO, Albrecht, Desharnais, and China. The performance of the CTGANaugmented MLP is compared to a baseline MLP trained without augmentation using key metrics like Pred(25), MAE, MMRE and MSE. According to the experimental finds, CTGAN augmentation improves estimation accuracy substantially on most of the datasets. In this context, Pred(25) scored $\mathbf{9 8 \%}$ on ISBSG and $\mathbf{9 6 \%}$ on COCOMO. However, the performance drops of the China dataset demonstrate how the characteristics of a dataset affect the efficacy of augmentation. The research findings indicate that the use of CTGAN-based data augmentation is a viable approach for advancing the performance of effort estimation models in the presence of scarce historical data.

title: Toward a Soft Robotic Gripper Integrating High Force Grasping and Dexterous Manipulation: The SOFRo Gripper_supp6-3693142.mp4
authors: Dongjun Shin
publish: N/A
abstract: Developing a robotic hand that integrates high fingertip force, rapid response, and multi-degree-of-freedom (DoF) motion to achieve human hand-like capabilities remains a challenge in the field of robotic hands. This study presents the SOFRo Gripper, a soft, high-force robotic gripper designed to combine these capabilities within a compact pneumatic architecture. The finger module of the SOFRo gripper incorporates synergistically arranged chambers together with a multinode tendon routing strategy that distributes actuation forces, enabling both flexion and abduction/adduction motions while enhancing fingertip force. Furthermore, a Force-Enhanced Pleated (FEP) mechanism was applied to the chambers to increase fingertip force, resulting in a 32.41% improvement over conventional chamber designs. The proposed SOFRo gripper achieves a high fingertip force of 68.76 N and dexterous motion capabilities, enabling a maximum lifting force of 400.0 N and in-hand manipulation. To validate its versatility, extensive experiments were conducted across a wide range of tasks, including object grasping, high-force manipulation (e.g., lifting heavy objects, closing a valve), delicate handling (e.g., grasping tofu, inserting a light bulb), and high-speed manipulation (e.g., spinning a top, catching a ball). These results demonstrate the SOFRo Gripper’s ability to combine high force with versatile soft robotic manipulation.

title: Defect Redistribution-Induced Leakage Degradation in β -Ga 2 O 3 SBDs Under 300 MeV Proton Irradiation
authors: Xiaoyu Sun, Bo Mei, Peng Ding, Chao Zhang, Yahan Zhang, Yingying Sun, Yinghui Zhong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
