abstract: This study investigates the effects of 300MeV proton irradiation on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> Schottky barrier diodes (SBDs) under an applied reverse bias of −120V, focusing on leakage mechanisms and defect evolution. While the unbiased device showed minor degradation, the biased device suffered severe degradation: a rightward shift in the forward current curve and a four-order-of-magnitude increase in reverse leakage current with a distinctly altered shape. Deep-level transient spectroscopy (DLTS) analysis revealed that the electric field actively promotes the generation of bulk gallium vacancies (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ga</sub>, Ec-0.8eV), leading to an increase of about 51% in their concentration. Moreover, the strong electric field drives ionized holes toward the Schottky interface, triggering the migration and aggregation of V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ga</sub> near the interface. This irradiation-induced redistribution of defects is identified as the direct cause for a fundamental transition in the dominant leakage mechanism. The pre-irradiation regime, marked by the coexistence of intrinsic Schottky transport (TE and TFE) and defect-mediated conduction (VRH and PFE), evolves into a post-irradiation state dominated by trap-assisted tunneling (TAT) alongside residual TE. The findings offer a useful understanding for improving the reliability of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> SBDs in practical aerospace applications.

title: SNR-Based Detection of Static and Dynamic Eccentricity in Resolvers
authors: Ali Namdar, Zahra Nasiri-Gheidari, Farid Tootoonchian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Accurate position estimation is essential for electrical machine control and commutation, making resolvers critical components. Due to low manufacturing cost and robustness in harsh environments, resolvers are used but remain vulnerable to mechanical faults—especially static eccentricity (SE) and dynamic eccentricity (DE)—which degrade accuracy. Hence resolver health monitoring is vital. This study presents a robust signal-to-noise ratio (SNR)-based algorithm for detecting mechanical faults under noisy conditions. Applicable to various resolver types (wound-rotor, WR; variable reluctance, VR) and winding schemes (overlapping and non-overlapping), the method uses the same signals employed for position estimation, removing the need for extra sensing channels. Fault detection analyzes deviations of the signal envelope from a healthy baseline by subtracting resized data windows aligned via maximum sample cross-correlation (SCC). The SNR, defined as the ratio of fundamental signal power to fault-induced components, serves as the evaluation metric. Validation through finite element analysis (FEA) and experiments demonstrates 99.5% detection accuracy with suitable computational burden.

title: Crafting Your Evolving Dreams: Concept-Incremental Versatile Customization
authors: Jiahua Dong, Wenqi Liang, Hongliu Li, Yang Cong, Duzhen Zhang, Hanbin Zhao, Henghui Ding, Yulun Zhang, Salman Khan, Fahad Shahbaz Khan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Custom diffusion models (CDMs) have garnered significant interest owing to their remarkable capacity for generating personalized concepts. However, the majority of CDMs unrealistically presume that the user's collection of personalized concepts is static and incapable of incremental growth over time. Furthermore, they exhibit significant catastrophic forgetting and concept neglect of previously learned concepts when incrementally learning a sequence of new ones. To resolve the above challenges, we develop a novel <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</u>ontinually <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</u>ustomizable <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</u>iffusion <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</u>odel (CCDM), enabling users to perform concept-incremental versatile customization. Specifically, we design an attribute-decoupled LoRA (AD-LoRA) module and a relevance-guided AD-LoRA aggregation strategy to mitigate catastrophic forgetting. They can preserve concept-specific attributes of each task and leverage beneficial inter-task correlations to enhance the continual learning of new customization tasks. Additionally, to address the challenge of concept neglect, we propose a controllable regional context synthesis strategy that performs multi-concept composition in alignment with user-provided conditions. This strategy enhances the overall consistency in multi-concept synthesis by guaranteeing semantic independence between user-defined regions and their smooth boundary transitions. Experiments show our CCDM exhibits significant improvements over baseline methods.

title: Optimum Design of Multisegment Compliant Finger via Fractional Kolmogorov-Arnold Network-Based Parameterization Framework
authors: Tianshu Liu, Feiyang Yu, Qinglei Li, Xiao-Jin Wan, Zixun Han, Siyuan Liu
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Neural network-based topology optimization has become a research hotspot in structural design, yet current algorithms do not demonstrate outstanding performance advantages and have not been applied in engineering practice. This article proposes a novel method to design multifinger segment-compliant gripper fingers via topology optimization. The method uses a reparameterization of fractional Kolmogorov–Arnold Network (fKAN). Each finger is divided into a compliance-driven mechanism and three finger segments, with separate topology optimizations maximizing mutual strain energy under volume constraints. The Jacobi polynomial is used to construct the basis functions of the fKAN activation function, using their learnable parameters to represent element densities. An external finite element solver calculates structural responses. Backpropagation is employed instead of manual sensitivity analysis of the topology optimization model. Adam with Decoupled Weight Decay is used to update the learnable parameters in the activation function. The proposed method proves effective compared to both a fully connected feed-forward neural network-based approach and a classical density-based approach. The adaptability of the developed fingers to various shapes of objects was evaluated via grasping experiments. The advantages of the segmented finger design were demonstrated by comparison with the monolithic finger design.

title: STGBD-Net: Spatio-temporal Gradient Basis Decomposition Network for Infrared Small Target Detection
authors: Chen Hu, Mingyu Zhou, Shuai Yuan, Hongbo Hu, Zhenming Peng, Tian Pu, Xiying Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: A key challenge in infrared small target detection (IRSTD) is that weak target signal responses are easily obscured by strong background clutter, frequently resulting in missed detections. While traditional gradient-based methods attempt to capture fine details, their robustness is limited by the static fusion of multi-directional gradient features. In this paper, we rethink feature fusion from the perspective of Basis Decomposition Theory and propose a novel framework that reformulates the process into an explicit and adaptive decomposition-and-reconstruction paradigm. Specifically, we introduce the Basis Decomposition Module (BDM) and its specialized variant, the Gradient Decomposition Module (GDM) for IRSTD. GDMs treat the normalized gradient features as basis vectors to reconstruct a new feature, thereby maintaining detailed structures and highlighting infrared small targets. By integrating GDMs into a lightweight three-stage U-Net, we develop two unified architectures: the Spatial Gradient Basis Decomposition Network for single-frame detection and the Spatio-temporal Gradient Basis Decomposition Network for multi-frame scenarios. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our networks achieve state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance across multiple benchmarks, offering a superior balance between detection accuracy and computational efficiency. Our codes will be made public at: https://github.com/greekinRoma/IRSTD HC Platform.

title: CHA: Physics-Based Dynamics Modeling for Clothing-Editable Human Avatars
authors: Yong Deng, Baoxing Li, Xu Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: This paper proposes a novel framework for generating human avatars from monocular videos. Existing methods struggle to balance dynamic effects and modeling efficiency due to the need for per-subject retraining. To address this, we introduce CHA, a physics-based approach for Clothing-editable Human Avatars, which decouples reconstruction and driving, thereby eliminating the need for per-subject fitting, and utilizes physics-based cloth dynamics modeling to generate realistic clothing dynamics. Our framework comprises three core components: First, an avatar reconstruction module employs an attention mechanism to fuse multi-frame normal information, ensuring complete human surface recovery. Second, a cloth recovery module exploits the static equilibrium state to restore standard clothing templates from observed garments, facilitating downstream dynamics modeling. Third, we propose a new physics-based cloth dynamics module to generate realistic cloth-aware avatar animations. The module generalizes well across different clothing sizes and styles by employing dual-layer graph networks with local predictions and a dynamic KNN-based graph construction that is not limited by mesh topology. Furthermore, we employ a multi-frame joint semantic segmentation algorithm to separate clothing templates, making our method applicable to clothing editing applications, such as virtual try-on. Extensive quantitative and qualitative experiments demonstrate that our avatars exhibit enhanced physical accuracy in clothing dynamics, well-preserved individual body characteristics, and distinct clothing layers.

title: A Data-Centric SPGT-ConvTransformer Framework for UAV-Based Soybean Disease Classification
authors: Sayali Shinde, Vahida Attar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Soybean cultivation is economically important across global agricultural sectors; however, plant diseases and pest attacks pose significant threats to crop yield and productivity. Rapid and accurate identification of diseases over large agricultural areas is therefore essential for timely intervention. Despite recent progress, developing reliable automated disease classification systems using UAV imagery for large-scale monitoring remains challenging due to complex canopy structures, partial occlusion, illumination variability, and strong visual similarity among disease symptoms. These challenges primarily arise from limitations in visual representation and data utilization rather than from model capacity alone. In order to address these issues, this study introduces a data-centric SPGT-ConvTransformer framework for UAV-based soybean disease classification. The proposed approach employs Structural Prior-Guided Tokenization (SPGT), which integrates superpixel-derived structural priors to preserve region-level coherence and reduce the fragmentation inherent in conventional patch-based tokenization. Furthermore, the framework combines convolutional neural networks for local feature extraction with Transformer self-attention for global contextual modeling. The proposed method is evaluated on the MH-SoyaHealthVision dataset, a self-curated Indian UAV benchmark comprising four classes (Healthy, Mosaic, Rust, and Semilooper pest attack). Performance evaluation demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves 99.3% overall classification accuracy and consistently outperforms CNN and Transformer baselines under identical experimental conditions. Moreover, the inference design supports practical deployment in precision agriculture applications such as early disease diagnosis and targeted intervention. Although this study focuses on soybean, the proposed SPGT-based ConvTransformer framework is crop-agnostic and can be extended to other UAV-based disease classification tasks in precision agriculture.

title: Detection of Heart Rate and Respiratory Rate from Facial Video Using Eulerian Motion Analysis and Kalman Filtering
authors: Luis Alberto Chavarría Zamora, Luis Felipe Jiménez Ulate, Carlos Eduardo Rodríguez Segura, Pablo Soto-Quiros
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents a preliminary study on a contactless system for estimating heart rate (BPM) and respiratory rate (RPM) using standard RGB video. Eulerian motion magnification detects subtle facial color changes for heart rate, while motion analysis with Kalman filtering enables respiratory rate detection. Validated on three subjects (ages 1824), the system achieves BPM error rates of 5.14-12.13% (mean absolute error $\mathbf{5. 6 B P M}$) and RPM error rates of $\mathbf{2. 7 0}-\mathbf{9. 6 8} \boldsymbol{\%}$ (MAE $\leq 2$ RPM) compared to reference measurements. This low-cost, non-invasive approach shows potential for remote vital sign screening applications.

title: KINDSHARE: A Smart Financial Budgeting and Resources Monitoring System
authors: Merriam Mae A. Salas, Drusila G. Otaza, Frietzzylyn Mhay T. Salarda, Jenelyn R. Asis, Jayrhom R. Almonteros, Jenie L. Plender-Nabas, Jane Francis P. Jaictin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Kingdom Life Butuan (KLB) is a church-based organization actively involved in charitable programs such as feeding missions, school supply drives, disaster relief, and weekly ministry outreach. Currently, the management of donations and budget in KLB is done manually. It is done through the pen and paper method of entries. This study seeks to eliminate possible cases of misreporting or overstretching beyond the budget, making the whole donation procedure much easier, ensuring transparency, better application, and efficiency of resources. A web-based system was developed using the Agile Methodology. The system features modules for tracking monetary, in-kind, and food donations, with rolebased access, Budget Admin, and Financial Admin. Key functionalities include dashboards visualizing the donation entry, resource availability, food expiration alerts, and donation notifications. The platform suggests available resources when plotting an event, prioritizing based on expiration and the life span of the resources. The system was evaluated by KLB’s admins and stakeholders, achieving a System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 74.25%, indicating high usability and acceptance. The system provides smart assistance to the proper use of available resources, preventing expiration or possible mishandling. Thus, supports KLB’s long-term goal of establishing a foundation for orphans and expands their capacity to manage charitable resources effectively.

title: A Hybrid Framework for Multi-LOD Building Model Reconstruction from Urban Point Clouds
authors: Zexin Yang, Ming Zhang, Yang Liu, Dong Xu, Zequan Chen, Keke Liu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Large-scale polygonal building models are fundamental for digital twins, yet reconstructing regularized structures from noisy airborne LiDAR remains a challenge. We propose an automated hybrid pipeline for multi-LOD (0-2) building reconstruction. Building instances are extracted via an improved Superpoint Graph (SPG) framework featuring geometry-aware merging and topology-preserving sampling. For reconstruction, a bottom-up strategy is employed: LOD 0 footprints are regularized via Quadratic Programming to enforce orthogonality, while watertight LOD 2 roof models are generated through Markov Random Field optimization on spatial partitions. Experimental results on large-scale datasets from Rotterdam and Shenzhen validate our framework's robustness across diverse urban morphologies and showcase its scalability for city-level structured modeling.

title: Comparative Study of Deep Learning Architectures for Brain Tumor Segmentation
authors: Hatim El-Amarty, Abdelghafour Abbad, Mohammed Oulhadj, Khalid Zenkouar, Khalid Abbad
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Brain tumor segmentation using magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) plays a critical role in clinical diagnosis, treatment planning, and disease monitoring. In recent years, deep learning methods have demonstrated impressive improvements in medical image segmentation. However, choosing the appropriate model is still a challenging task because of variations in accuracy, computational complexity, and robustness. This paper presents a comparative study of four deep learning models for brain tumor segmentation: U-Net, Attention U-Net, DeepLabV3+, and TransUNet. These models represent different architectural paradigms, such as convolutional neural networks (CNN), attention-based mechanisms, and hybrid CNN-Transformers approaches. All models are trained and tested under the same experimental conditions to ensure a complete and objective comparison. We performed the evaluation using standard segmentation metrics, such as Dice Similarity Coefficient and Intersection over Union. We present the results using both quantitative and qualitative analyses to demonstrate the accuracy and efficiency of the segmentation models. The results highlight the strengths and limitations of each approach. This study aims to assist clinicians and researchers in choosing effective models for brain tumor segmentation in real-world medical applications and to serve as a foundation for developing more advanced segmentation approaches.

title: Performance Assessment of an Advanced MPPT Technique for Grid-Connected Photovoltaic Systems Using Real Irradiance Data
authors: Oumaima Echab, Noureddine Ech-Cherki, Tourıa Gueddouch, Omaima El Alani, Abdellatif Obbadi, Youssef Errami, Smail Sahnoun
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper proposes a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) based on an integrated Incremental Conductance-Backstepping Control (IC-BC) technique to enhance the robustness and performance of grid-connected photovoltaic systems (GCPVS) under realistic operating conditions. The proposed method is evaluated using two irradiance profiles; the first one presents a typical clear-sky day, and the second displays rapid and uncertain changes. The feasibility of the technique is validated by Arduino Due through Processor-In-the-Loop (PIL). The results demonstrate the high stability of the technique, superior efficiency, and a diminished Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) with an average value of $\mathbf{1. 0 3 \%}$ with fewer fluctuations. The IC-BC outperforms other benchmarked techniques, proving its ability for real-time practical applications.

title: Machine Learning Models for SQL Injection Detection: A Comparative Analysis and Hybrid Framework Proposal
authors: Mahmoud Baklizi, Mohammad Alkhazaleh, Loui Al Sardy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: SQL Injection (SQLi) has continued to be among the most severe threats to web application security and has always been featured in the OWASP Top Ten. Conventional signature-based and static analysis can not cope with new attack vectors and obfuscation. In this paper, the authors provide an in-depth comparison of machine learning (ML)-based SQLi detectors, comparing the performance of traditional and deep learning architectures and ensemble-based approaches on a curated and modern dataset. We prove that our hybrid models that combine natural language processing (NLP) with ensemble learning outperform better, and the F1-scores are more than 99.2. In addition, we introduce SQLiDetect-Hybrid, a new framework that consists of syntactic features extraction, contextual embedding with language models and a stacking ensemble. The framework has a false positive rate (FPR) of 0.42% and real-time (<15ms) queries. We find empirical support to integrate adaptive ML-based detection in the next generation web application firewalls (WAFs) and intrusion prevention system (IPS).

title: IoT-Enabled Multi-Sensor Patient Health Monitoring System for Real-Time Hospital Tracking and Display
authors: Dhaarani T G, Dharani T, Divyadharsan S, Kavinila C
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Traditional health care systems rely on observation and regular examinations in order to check on patients’ health, but this approach could allow for a major health condition to go undetected. Health monitoring is an essential component in diagnosing and treating diseases in a timely manner. Patients at the greatest risk are older individuals, those with chronic health conditions, and those who live in rural areas, because they may not see a rapid change in their vital signs until something has already gone wrong. The lack of continuous health monitoring and predictive analytics through traditional health care methods limits the ability to intervene in a timely way and increases the chances that a patient will experience a medical emergency. To improve this situation, the use of advanced technologies for real-time monitoring of a patient’s major health indicators (temperature, blood pressure, heart rate and oxygen saturation) is recommended. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) powered Algorithms will provide health care professionals with the ability to access data accumulated from many different health care providers and use this data in order to identify potential health issues much earlier through analysis of trends and patterns.

title: Cross-Attention Fusion of Molecular Fingerprints and Graph Transformers for hERG Cardiotoxicity Prediction
authors: Cheikh Beidja, Abdelmajid Bousselham, Zakeriae En-Naimani, Mohammed Mestari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The human ether-a-go-go-related gene (hERG) potassium channel represents a critical safety liability in drug discovery, since unintended channel blockade can lead to QT interval prolongation and life-threatening cardiac arrhythmias. Consequently, reliable early-stage identification of hERG-related cardiotoxicity is of major importance in pharmaceutical research. In this work, we introduce GT-MFCA, a multimodal deep learning approach for hERG cardiotoxicity prediction that integrates graph transformer-based molecular representations with multiple molecular fingerprint descriptors using a cross-attention fusion strategy. Molecular structures are encoded as graphs and processed by a graph transformer encoder to model atom-level interactions and long-range structural relationships. In parallel, global chemical characteristics are captured through Morgan, MACCS, RDKit, and atom-pair fingerprints. The proposed crossattention module enables molecule-specific feature fusion by allowing the graph representation to selectively weight fingerprint information. GT-MFCA was evaluated on a publicly available hERG benchmark dataset comprising 23,381 compounds. On an independent test set, the model achieves an accuracy of 0.882, an AUROC of 0.930, an AUPR of 0.921, and an F1-score of 0.904, outperforming several established baseline methods. In addition, an attention-based interpretability analysis provides a first transparent view of fingerprint-level and atom-level importance patterns learned by the model. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining structural graph information with complementary fingerprint-based descriptors for robust and more interpretable hERG cardiotoxicity prediction.

title: Learning Analytics for Dual Education: A Privacy-Preserving Framework Bridging Campus and Workplace Competencies
authors: Mohammad H. Abu-Arqoub, Amal Shafiq Abdulla, Ali A. Titinchi, Rashiq Rafiq Marie
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Dual education programs struggle with a fundamental challenge: demonstrating empirically how campus learning activities translate into workplace performance. This paper presents a privacy-preserving analytics framework that integrates learning management system behavioural patterns with supervisor-assessed workplace competencies and an institutional outcomes assessment infrastructure. The architecture introduces three components: a behavioural indicator extraction engine operating on Moodle’s event logs, a cross-context competency mapping protocol validated through expert consensus and aligned with institutional rubrics, and an uncertainty-aware prediction pipeline with automated quality controls. Deployed across 47 students in computer science and software engineering programmes at the University of Petra over 12 weeks, the framework revealed strong associations (Spearman ρ = 0.61-0.76) between LMS engagement patterns and structured workplace evaluation rubrics measuring ten performance criteria. The system requires 15-20 hours of initial setup, operates under institutional review board-approved governance protocols, and integrates with existing program learning outcomes assessment systems to provide comprehensive evidence for accreditation and quality assurance.

title: Automated Nursing Care Plan Creation Based on NANDA-I, NIC, and NOC Standards Using Pre-Trained Transformer Models
authors: Allen Bernard M. Tan, Herwayne John H. Cawili, Japeth A. Gomez, Salimar B. Tahil
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study developed a domain-specific constrained generation workflow for Nursing Care Plan (NCP) generation grounded in the standardized NANDA-I, NIC, NOC (NNN) frameworks and powered by pre-trained transformer models. The goal was to support nursing education by enhancing the accuracy, consistency, and efficiency of NCP creation while evaluating the capability of modern large language models (LLMs) to generate clinically aligned nursing documentation. The workflow benchmarked three large language models (LLMs): Claude Sonnet 4.5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and GPT-5 across 20 expert-curated cases (derived from 80 student NCPs) to rank suitability for educational NCP support, prioritizing NNN adherence as the primary criterion. A model selection framework was applied to identify the most suitable for clinical educational use, prioritizing NNN adherence as the primary criterion. Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieved the highest adherence score (77.93%), outperforming Gemini 2.5 Pro (74.62%) and GPT 5 (62.20%), resulting in its selection as the system’s core model establishing it as the optimal pilot model. These pilot-scale metrics provided functional guidance for educational deployment rather than clinical perfection benchmarks. The developed system was subsequently evaluated through expert validation by nursing faculty and usability testing with nursing students. Expert reviewers found the AI generated NCPs to be clinically acceptable and strongly aligned with NNN standards, while student respondents reported high usability, clarity, and perceived educational value of the system. Overall, the study demonstrates the feasibility of integrating transformer based LLMs into nursing education to support standardized NCP development. The findings highlight the potential of AI assisted tools to enhance learning, promote documentation accuracy, and streamline academic workflows in nursing programs.

title: Experimental Analysis of the application of freezing technologies in desalination: An Overview
authors: Oumaima Rhandari, Soukaina Bakkass, Naoual Semlali Aouragh Hassani, Driss Khomsi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The growing global demand for freshwater, coupled with the environmental impacts of climate change, has resulted in the expansion of desalination as a viable solution to freshwater scarcity. Conventional technologies present major limits, especially regarding significant inputs of heat, pressure, or electrical potential. When these processes are driven by fossil fuels, they are often associated with high operational costs as well as significant emissions of air pollutants. In contrast, freezing desalination (FD) Desalination by freeze crystallization is a key alternative based on the freezing-melting process in which consists of water crystallization to form ice that is separated from the concentrated saline solution. In this work, a systematic review on freezing desalination is conducted as the field is experiencing renewed research interest. Results show that with optimized methods and specific parameter control, a promising efficiency can be accomplished, which demonstrates the good feasibility of freezing desalination

title: Federated Learning as a Governance-Aligned Intelligence Paradigm for Pharmaceutical Manufacturing and Research
authors: Faisal Aburub, Mayyas Al-Remawi, Rami Abdel-Rahem, Faisal Al-Akayleh, Ahmed S.A. Ali Agha
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The pharmaceutical sector is experiencing rapid growth in data generation across discovery, development, manufacturing, and post-marketing activities. Despite this expansion, the adoption of advanced machine learning remains constrained by structural data fragmentation, stringent regulatory oversight, and the need to protect intellectual property and sensitive operational knowledge. Centralized learning paradigms are therefore often incompatible with pharmaceutical data governance requirements. This article advances a conceptual reframing of pharmaceutical artificial intelligence (AI) through federated learning, a distributed machine learning paradigm that enables collaborative model development without centralized data aggregation. Federated learning is examined as an enabling infrastructure for cross-site manufacturing intelligence, multi-institutional pharmaceutical research, and lifecycle process optimization. Drawing on documented industrial and healthcare-adjacent case studies from the literature, the manuscript demonstrates how federated learning reconciles advanced analytics with regulatory compliance, validation integrity, and organizational autonomy. The central contribution of this work lies in positioning federated learning not merely as a privacy-preserving technique, but as a governance-aligned intelligence paradigm for regulated pharmaceutical systems.

title: Deep Learning–based Detection of Mango Leaf Diseases and Nutrient Deficiencies using DenseNet-121
authors: Kiran Kumar Jajala, Suresh Babu Jugunta, S Girinath, Gurajala Laasyapriya, Katta Teena Reddy, Kovuru Srivalli
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Mango (Mangifera indica L) is considered to be one of the most useful fruits in the world and plays a major role in terms of nutrition and revenues more so among farmers in tropical and subtropical areas. Nonetheless, major issues caused by pests and deficiency of nutrients are posing threats to the production of mangoes. These have been leaf diseases, nutrient deficiencies which may cause massive losses in production. The conventional methods of inspecting these issues rely mostly on the eye of the farmer, are time consuming and are not easy to be used by the small farmers. The paper will consider this critical concern but the automated tool will be presented that applies deep learning to study mango leaf health. The suggested system is based on DenseNet-121 convolutional neural network and transfer learning. It is capable of categorizing the mango leaf condition into eight groups; seven diseases and one healthy group. These diseases are Bacterial Canker, Powdery Mildew, anthracnose, Gall Midge, Sooty Mould, Die Back and Cutting weevil. Since certain diseases are similar to the lack of specific nutrients, it is also possible to detect potential problems with nutrients with the help of the system. The model was trained using an equal number of high-resolution leaf images of 4,000 balancing high and low and achieved a 98.75 percent accuracy on validation. Its computational requirements are ideal in the use on a mobile device. This research will make precision agriculture successful as it will offer rapid, precise, and viable diagnostic tests, thereby linking innovated artificial intelligence and the actual existence of farming.

title: Hybrid Multi-Scale CNN and Vision Transformer Model for Accurate Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Ramanujam E, Sekar K, Sangeetha K, Niranjan S, Madhu Mitha M, Nithya Shree A K
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A significant health issue of concern in the world today is skin cancer especially melanoma because it has been steadily growing and its mortality rate is high when it is not detected at an early stage. Traditional methods of diagnostics are based on dermoscopic imaging and expert visual examination, which tend to be subjective and vulnerable to discrepancy. Recent progress in deep learning, particularly, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have demonstrated encouraging outcomes in automated skin lesion classification by discovering local spatial patterns of features like texture and pigmentation patterns. CNN-based models are however limited in the sense that they fail to capture long-range contextual dependencies within lesions. Vision Transformers (ViTs) eliminate this drawback using self-attention but typically demand large-scale data and can frequently perform poorly with lesions of different sizes. To address these difficulties, this paper presents a new Multi-Scale Attention Vision Transformer- CNN (MSA-ViTCNN) framework that combines the multi-scale convolutional feature detection with the attention-based global context-representation. Compared to traditional CNNs and current ViT-based models that can only train at one scale or use tokenized features, the model proposed combines the fine-grained local lesion features and long-range relationships, resulting in enhanced resilience to the lesion with different sizes. The dataset made up of the images of the HAM10000, ISIC 2018 and 2019 repositories is balanced to facilitate generalization. The experimental findings prove that the suggested model is more effective than standalone CNNs, ViTs, and other hybrid models in regard to classification accuracy and stability. Moreover, visual explanations in the Grad-CAM paradigm are easier to interpret as they prioritize clinically meaningful areas, which can support the trustworthy clinical decision-making of the suggested framework.

title: AI-Enhanced BIM for Smart City SDGs: Governance and Environment Pillars
authors: Sadeen Alquran, Mohammad Alauthman, Amer Al-Jokhadar, Ali Al-Maqusi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Urban digital twins (UDTs) increasingly combine building information modeling (BIM), geospatial information systems (GIS), and Internet-of-Things (IoT) sensing to support evidence-based city governance and sustainability reporting. In parallel, cities are adopting machine learning (ML) and foundation models (including large language models, LLMs) to automate indicator computation, forecasting, and decision support. Yet integrating AI into BIM-enabled UDT pipelines remains difficult due to fragmented data ecosystems, interoperability constraints, evolving regulations, and emerging security and safety risks. This paper reframes SDG-aligned UDT development as an AI integration problem. We propose a layered reference architecture for AI-enabled BIM-to-city digital twins and an end-to-end AI integration lifecycle that unifies MLOps/LLMOps practices with public-sector governance constraints. We synthesize current integration challenges in a lifecycle-oriented matrix and derive a risk–mitigation mapping grounded in recent standards and security guidance. Finally, we outline future trends agentic systems, multimodal AI, edge/on-device AI, retrieval and tool use, regulation, trustworthy AI, and evaluation/observability and discuss implications for municipal governance, sustainability decisionmaking, and building/urban-quality evaluation (e.g., daylighting, acoustics, spatial connectivity, and biophilic performance) beyond energy and carbon reporting.

title: Multi-Model Time-Series Forecasting Framework for Stock Price Prediction Using Statistical and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Shekar Vollem, Faiz Mohiuddin Mulla, Anuj Kumar Shah, Saiprakash Kodela, Mandeep Kaur, Vivek Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Predictions unfold through three methods: AutoRegressive, ARIMA, and LSTM. Stock prices take shape using numbers pulled from Yahoo Finance between 2015 and 2020. Instead of one big test, each model faces fresh challenges every five days. Training fills 80 percent of the data window - testing claims the rest. Rolling forward like waves, results emerge step by step. Models do not guess once; they repeat, adapt, measure again. The numbers show ARIMA(1,1,1) hits the closest mark, landing an RMSE of 51.49 - down by 11.4% compared to the basic AR method. On another path entirely, the double-layer LSTM cracks nearly all variation with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9786, plus it tracks direction better, though demanding hundreds of times more computing muscle. Checks on leftover errors back up steady performance across models. Looking ahead three months, every forecast bends toward the average again, hinting predictions alone may only hold trust within about ten days. One way to look at it - ARIMA works best when speed matters and resources are tight. Instead of chasing complexity, sometimes simpler models deliver what’s needed. On infrastructure that can handle heavy loads, LSTM takes the lead by spotting patterns others miss. Performance alone isn’t enough if the system can’t run it smoothly. What changes here is how we weigh accuracy against real-world limits. Matching method to environment becomes the quiet deciding factor. Not every solution fits every setting, even if one seems stronger on paper.

title: Adaptive Channel Learning for Early ADHD Prediction using Attention-based EEGNet
authors: Krithiga R, T. Thilagam, Siva Rama Lingham N
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) has been one of the most significant clinical issues because of the difficulty of diagnosing the disorder at an early childhood developmental stage, where normal behavioral tests tend to be subjective and unreliable. The article proposes an EEG-based automated model of predicting ADHD at an early stage through an Attention-Enhanced EEGNet (AE-EEGNet) architecture. One of the proposed models incorporates an adaptive channel attention process to highlight the most informative EEG channels and suppress those that are redundant and noisy. Multi-channel EEG data are subject to extensive preprocessing such as missing values, standardisation, sliding window time segmentation, bandpass filters and more sophisticated data augmentation techniques such as time shifting, amplitude scaling, Gaussian noise injection and channel dropout to enhance model robustness. The AE-EEGNet structure is a hybrid of time-based convolution, depthwise separable convolution and attention guided feature learning, with the subsequent optimization of classification layers. The rigorous 5-fold cross-validation protocol is used as a model training and evaluation protocol to guarantee the statistical reliability and generalization. Based on the experimental data, it is clear that the suggested framework is always superior to the baseline EEGNet model in terms of classification accuracy and also high levels of precision, recall, F1-score, and area under the ROC curve. The results have shown that attention directed deep learning of EEG data has a potent and dependable solution to early ADHD detection and has a high chance of objective clinical decision support.

title: Multi-Task Learning Framework for Simultaneous Detection of Crop Disease, Yield Estimation, and Climatic Risk Classification Using Multisource Agricultural Big Data
authors: Rashi Saxena, Atul Singla, Yogesh Lohumi, Mina Kumari, Orchu Venkata Koteswararoa, Ashish Kumar Singhal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Combination of multisource agricultural big data and multi-task learning has created new horizons in smart farming, as the concurrence and precise forecasts of crop diseases, crop yields, and the climatic risks can be made. To do this, the smart AI architectures will use heterogeneous data, including satellite imagery, soil measures, weather trends, or historical yields. The proposed framework will use deep neural networks with attention mechanism and shared feature extraction layers to facilitate consolidating learning tasks and hence reduce the computational redundancy and enhance generalisation. It is through this joint modelling scheme that synergistic learning is possible and benefit of the knowledge gained on one task is applied to the other. This means that the system is both highly scalable and adaptable in both crop variety and location by addressing task-specific issues like data-imbalance, noisy inputs and temporal shifts. Assessment on large scale data demonstrates an improvement by high margins of better accuracy, robustness, and interpretability relative to single task and classical assembling models. The capacity of the architecture to unite prediction objectives without trade-offs to performance potentials depicts the possibility of the architecture in assisting data-driven policy-making and farm-level decision makers in agricultural sciences cultivating stability and sustainability in contemporary agronomy. The suggested system had a highest 96.2% accuracy rate and a 95.1% Macro F1-Score beating other approaches.

title: A Review of Reinforcement Learning Frameworks for Adaptive and Privacy-Preserving PDAs
authors: Abhishek N Biradar, Pooja S Kiragi, Veeresh Angadi, Chiranth P, Achyuth Kasyap P, Sandhya N
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Personal Digital Assistants (PDAs) have evolved beyond static, rule-based systems into adaptive and context-aware agents, largely due to advances in Reinforcement Learning (RL). RL enables PDAs to learn directly from user interactions and deliver personalized assistance over time. However, the rapid development of diverse RL techniques has led to a fragmented research landscape, making it difficult to systematically compare existing approaches. This paper presents a structured review of modern RL-based PDA frameworks. The literature is organized along key dimensions, including the types of RL algorithms employed (ranging from value-based methods to hierarchical and multi-agent RL), privacy-preserving strategies such as federated and offline learning, and deployment paradigms spanning cloud-based and edge-based systems. The survey also examines major application domains, including task management, ergonomic monitoring, and dialogue systems. By synthesizing existing work, this paper identifies key research gaps, open challenges, and fundamental trade-offs, particularly between model complexity, real-time performance, and personalization. Overall, this survey provides a consolidated view of the current state of RL-driven PDAs and outlines promising directions for future research on adaptive personal assistants.

title: Hybrid Symbolic-Neural Framework for Predicting Zero-Divisor Geodetic Indices in Idealization Rings
authors: Manal Al-Labadi, Wasim Audeh, Enoch Suleiman
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The Zero-Divisor Geodetic Index (ZDGI) is an index of overall efficiency of Commutative Rings. It combines two aspects of the ring’s structural behavior, namely its "geodetic" and "domination" characteristics. For many types of Commutative Rings, there are closed form expressions for ZDGI. However, developing algorithms to compute this index for arbitrary Idealization Rings is still a difficult computational problem. In this paper we present a Hybrid Symbolic Neural Network Architecture which combines the advantages of closed form symbolic computation with the power of deep learning predictions. The system will determine when it should use symbolic computation (for cases where we know the closed form solution), and when it should use neural networks to make predictions for more complex cases where no closed form expression exists. We train a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) based neural network model on 15,000 different structural features of Idealization Rings, resulting in a prediction accuracy of a high percent and a mean absolute error of 0.018. The hybrid system has shown a 340× increase in speed over traditional graph theoretic computations while ensuring that all results have been mathematically proven through the use of symbolic validation. Our results show that the most important features used to predict the value of the Zero Divisor Geodetic Index are the number of annihilators (the cardinality of the annihilators) and the module order, however, the prime factorization structure of the ring also contributes significantly to the variance in the value of the ZDGI. Thus our work presents a new paradigm.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (ML) Models for Epileptic Seizure Detection from electroencephalogram (EEG) Signals
authors: Nirav Narendrakumar Modh, Jarnail Singh, Rupinder Singh, Sachin Kumar, Rahul Chauhan, Harvinder Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Epilepsy is a neurological disease that is long-term and affects millions of people. The detection of epileptic seizures accurately and in a timely manner is a key to its appropriate diagnosis and clinical treatment. The most common modality used to monitor brain activity is electroencephalogram (EEG) signals, though these have to be manually interpreted, and such interpretation is very labour-intensive, and needs a lot of clinical expertise.In order to detect epileptic seizures from EEG signals, the research presents a comparative analysis of complex deep learning (DL) models and traditional machine learning (ML) models. 11,500 segmented EEG recordings from publicly accessible data are used in the study. The state-of-the-art DLs are tested alongside conventional ML classifiers like Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression. The conventional evaluation measuring techniques—precision, recall, accuracy, F1-score, and area under the receiver operating characteristics curve (ROC-AUC)—are the foundation for the model performance assessments.With the greatest precision of 99.1% and F1-score of 0.977, the experimental results show that the single-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) performs significantly better than the traditional ML models. According to the study's findings, 1D-CNNs may be a very useful and promising way to develop robust, computerized EEG-based epileptic seizure detection systems which will enhance patient care and clinical decision-making.

title: Continuous Reformulation of Boltzmann Statistics and Geometric Partition Functions
authors: Jaafar Gaber
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: We introduce in this paper a spectral parametrization of Boltzmann-Gibbs statistical mechanics, in which the canonical weighting is preserved while the energy spectrum is described by a functional law $E_{n}=E_{0} f(n)$. This framework provides a scale-organized representation of canonical ensembles and enables the analysis of thermodynamic properties under nonuniform spectral structures. Rather than modifying the entropy or probability law, the approach presented promotes the spectral organization itself as a geometric degree of freedom. This leads to a unified description of additive, multiplicative, and power-law spectra within the standard canonical formalism.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Predicting Chess Outcomes Across Skill Strata
authors: Sumanyu Singh, Gaurav Yadav, Sidharth Kumar, Geet Kiran Kaur, Aniket Dwivedi, Malti Rani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Predicting the result of chess game is an interesting problem. In chess, players rating shows their skill level in our study, we compare the two-machine learning outcome to predict the result of chess matches. in this study we have use the datasets form Kaggle names as "60000+ games form chess.com", then we have divided player from beginner (below 1200), intermediate (1200-2000) and advanced (above 2000). We have trained two model logistic Regression and gradient boosting for each group, the model was tested using accuracy, recall, and f1 score. Both model accuracy lies between 62 and 72 %, however linear regression performed better for the advanced players and for beginner player Gradient boosting performed better. This study endorses the utilization of smart systems for the analysis of player performance and learning. This study enhances intelligent decision-support systems for skill-aware performance prediction, in accordance with Sustainable Development Goals SDG-9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) and SDG-4 (Quality Education). In contrast to previous studies, we examine model behaviour independently across player skill tiers, uncovering a reversal in model efficacy at elite levels.

title: A Real-Time Smart Learning Management System with Automated Performance Analytics and Adaptive Content Recommendation
authors: Sai Vamsi Chennupati, Swetha B, Pawan Kumar, Ginni Nijhawan, Darshan Singh, D Sai Niharika
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The online learning issue emergence needs clever systems that will permit delivery of individual, adaptive, and data-driven learning. The main types of traditional Learning Management Systems (LMS) are content storage tools that do not offer much ability in the performance monitoring and dynamically adjusting the learning content, limiting their usefulness in improving student results. The present study suggests a Real-Time smart learning management system (RT-SLMS) combining automated performance analytics and dynamic content recommendation. The system uses machine learning models to track the interest of learners, make assumptions about their academic performance, and dynamically respond to their unique learning styles. Real-time analytics dashboard offers an opportunity to the instructors in order to have actionable insights and identify struggling students early on in order to develop interventions targeted. The adaptive recommendation engine matches the educational contents to both individual strengths and weaknesses, and preferred learning styles of students, which guarantee a higher rate of knowledge retention and engagement. This has resulted in the suggestions of SLMS being found to perform better than the available means of operation, with Content Recommendation Accuracy at 94% and Efficiency at 93% indicating a high-level relevance in the content being presented and the subsequent learning achievements. Therefore, the suggested framework is more adaptable, scalable, and intelligent and is a major improvement in comparison with traditional systems.

title: Graph Attention Networks Enhance Fine-Grained Academic Performance Prediction in MOOCs by Modelling Structural Relationships
authors: Huang Shiqin, Zhou Jiaying, Xiao Hongyun, Hai Tao, Yang Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Predicting student performance in massive open online courses (MOOCs) is critical for personalizing education and preventing dropouts, but existing methods are limited by their coarse granularity and neglect of complex structural relationships within educational data. Here, we introduce a graph-based approach that significantly improves the accuracy of fine-grained, challenge-level score prediction. We first present MOOPer, a large-scale dataset from a major Chinese MOOC platform comprising 2.53 million practice records and a rich knowledge graph of 11 entity types. We model student-challenge interactions as a bipartite graph and employ a graph attention network (GAT) to learn structural embeddings, which encode the importance of relationships without pre-training. Integrating these graph-derived features with standard student and challenge data, we achieve state-of-the-art prediction performance. Our GAT-enhanced gradient boosting model attained an accuracy of 0.89 and an F1-score of 0.92, substantially outperforming baseline models. Feature importance analysis confirms that structural metrics—such as node centrality and interaction frequency—are critical predictors. This work demonstrates that explicitly modelling the educational graph structure is essential for accurate fine-grained prediction, providing a foundation for intelligent intervention systems in online learning.

title: Intelligent Dynamic Positioning Strategy for Sensor Networks subject to Information Dropout during Handover Using Wavelet Neural Network
authors: Vishwalata Bagal, A.V.Patil
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The unreliable target tracking process in wireless sensor networks (WSNs) faces major difficulties because of two factors, which include data dropout, and uncertainty that occurs during node handovers between different sensing regions. The Intelligent Dynamic placement Strategy that combines multi-model fusion with adaptive uncertainty learning serves as the solution to achieving stable and precise placement requirements for this specific situation. The system uses the Interactive Multiple Model (IMM) method as its first step to transform model probabilities into normalized values. The system first establishes accuracy weights according to the sensor error covariance values and then it establishes stability weights according to the principles of relay-based navigation. A Wavelet Neural Network (WNN) system uses wavelet-based activation functions to capture localized time-frequency variations, which the system uses to perform corrections for functional and parametric errors that occur due to disturbances and reflections and multipath fading. The system establishes adaptive tuning methods, which maintain tracking stability and limit parameter estimation errors despite the occurrence of random packet loss. The simulation studies demonstrate that the proposed method achieves real-time WSN deployment through its efficient computational performance, which simultaneously provides better positioning accuracy and handover continuity and system robustness than existing IMM-based filters and particle filters and deep learning methods.

title: Optimization Accuracy of Crime Rate Prediction using K-Mean and Random Forest based Machine Learning
authors: Nikita Namdev, Ritu Prasad, Arjun Rajput, Saurabh Karsoliya
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: In smart cities, proper prediction of crime rates is critical in improving security of the citizens, strategic policing, and efficient management of available resources. Conventional crime analysis tools have difficulties with complex spatial-temporal patterns and latent relationships that occur in large and heterogeneous crime data. The study hypothesizes a hybrid ML model that will combine K-Means clustering with the RF classifier to maximize the accuracy of prediction. The strategy starts with the preprocessing of historical crime data, which includes the extraction of the information as the type of crime, the patterns of location, frequency of incidences, and time aspects. The clustering method used is K-Means, which is an unsupervised data clustering method where similar incidences of crime are grouped into meaningful clusters to reduce noise in the data and achieve better features separation. These clusters are further fed into the Random Forest model as supplementary inputs and thus allow the model to acquire more elegant and organized patterns to learn. Random Forest, which was characterized by its stability and use of the ensemble method to make decisions, predicts more successfully and with high stability the types and frequencies of crimes. Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score and ROC analysis are the evaluation metrics that show that the hybrid model is much better than the traditional standalone techniques. The findings indicate that feature enhancement through clustering results in a superior way of detecting the existence of crime hotspots and more dependable prediction of possible future crime incidences. It is concluded the paper, the hybrid K-Means-RF system provides a viable, scalable, and efficient way to estimate intelligent crime rates in high-tech urban environments.

title: Automating Goal-Oriented Modeling with NLP: Extracting Actors, Roles, and Dependencies from Functional Requirements
authors: Touseef Tahir, Maham Shoaib, Mohammad Noman, Shakeel Ahmad, Shahan Ahmad Chowdhury, Mohammad Ahmad
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Conceptual modeling plays a central role in bridging functional requirements and system design, supporting clear and effective communication among developers, analysts, and stakeholders. This paper proposes an automated approach for generating goal models directly from functional requirements using natural language processing techniques. Goal models capture and structure the high level objectives, intentions, and responsibilities that guide a system or product, thereby strengthening alignment between stakeholder needs and system behavior. By improving clarity, traceability, and decision making during early development phases, goal oriented modeling contributes to systems that better satisfy business objectives and deliver intended value. The proposed method combines NLP preprocessing techniques such as tokenization, part of speech tagging, lemmatization, and stop word removal with rule based extraction of actors, agents, roles, goals, and actor relationships, following the iStar 2.0 framework. The approach is evaluated on more than 500 functional requirements collected from software projects in four different application domains. Experimental results show 94 percent precision, 86 percent recall, and an F1 score of 86 percent for goal model components, demonstrating the accuracy, scalability, and domain independence of the proposed approach.

title: A Blockchain and IoT-Based Framework for Transparent and Traceable Smart Agriculture: AlgroTech
authors: Lamis Zaaboub, Soundes Belaib, Sarra Cherbal, Ashraf Mashaleh, Amjad Gawanmeh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Agriculture is a key pillar of Algeria’s economy and food security; however, the sector faces persistent challenges related to inefficient resource management, limited adoption of digital technologies, and the lack of transparent and reliable traceability mechanisms. These issues negatively impact agricultural productivity and reduce the competitiveness of Algerian products in international markets that increasingly require verifiable and tamper-resistant data. This paper proposes AlgroTech, an integrated IoT–Blockchain framework designed to enable smart and transparent agriculture. The system combines real-time soil moisture monitoring with automated irrigation control to optimize water usage. Agricultural data are securely recorded on the Ethereum blockchain through smart contracts, ensuring data integrity, immutability, and transparency. A QR code–based traceability mechanism allows consumers and stakeholders to verify crop information directly from the blockchain. A functional prototype was implemented and experimentally evaluated, demonstrating reliable real-time sensing, fast irrigation response, successful blockchain data registration, and effective QR code–based verification. AlgroTech demonstrates strong potential to support export compliance, enhance transparency, and promote sustainable smart agriculture in Algeria.

title: Improved Cloud-based Task Scheduling Using Hybrid MCTFFA Model for Enhanced Performance Metrics
authors: Rashmi Makkar, Neeraj Mangla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Cloud Computing has developed as a basis for a new Information Technology (IT) organization, providing on-demand and commercial access to computing assets. Efficient Task Scheduling (TS) is pivotal to increasing Resource Utilization (RU), reducing Energy Consumption (EC), and ensuring high system performance in dynamic cloud environments. Existing techniques, including heuristic, meta-heuristic, hybrid, and intelligent algorithms, address several aspects of task allocation, yet challenges persist due to heterogeneous resources, dynamic workloads, and the need for SLA compliant energy-efficient operations. This research article introduces a hybrid approach using minimum completion time with firefly algorithm (MCTFFA) that integrates task scheduling plans with an advanced meta-heuristic optimization method. The hybrid form of proposed architecture provides high-quality execution patterns with the help of analyzing execution time, available resources, and a sanitized schedule to check the availability and executions. The proposed architecture initializes the virtual machines in a cloud environment and then pushes the optimized task patterns given by the hybrid approach to find high resource utilization and less waiting time. The execution process finds the best fit until the maximum iterations are met. Once it gets the best fit from the deployed solution generated from the MCT/random deployment, it starts the execution on the network. The proposed approach, MCTFFA, has higher resource utilization as 87.22%, 17.26 seconds average waiting time, and MCTFFA shows high performance with 1.57 tasks per second, as compared to the existing HFFSSA method. Experimental evaluation using MATLAB simulations demonstrates that the planned method outdoes existing meta-heuristic and hybrid approaches in RU, Avg waiting time, and throughput.

title: Spectral Energy Thermodynamics: the exponential spectral case
authors: Jaafar Gaber
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In this paper, we present Spectral Energy Thermodynamics (SET) as a thermodynamic framework for exponentially structured energy spectra, building on the spectral parametrization $E_{n}=E_{0} f(n)$ introduced in [13]. Focusing on the exponential case $f(n)=e^{\omega n}$, we analyze the resulting partition function and show that it admits a natural representation in terms of exponential integral functions. This formulation provides a scale-dependent description of thermal equilibrium, in which energy levels are organized multiplicatively rather than through additive spacing. The corresponding continuous representation is expressed in logarithmic coordinates, where the spectral structure becomes uniform. Within this class of spectra, SET yields a consistent thermodynamic description and clarifies the Boltzmann limit as the regime $\omega \rightarrow 0$ with $E_{0} \omega$ fixed, recovering the standard linear energy ladder up to an additive constant.

title: Parallel Genetic Algorithm on Graphics Processing Unit for Distribution Network Planning Considering Harmonics
authors: Vincent Roberge, Mohammed Tarbouchi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents a parallel genetic algorithm (GA) on graphics processing unit (GPU) for the planning of power distribution networks with harmonic considerations. Distribution systems are typically operated radially and may include distributed generation units (DGs) and capacitor banks (CBs) to reduce losses and improve voltage profiles. Nonlinear loads and DGs introduce harmonics that degrade power quality and increase losses. The proposed GA simultaneously optimizes network topology, DGs and CBs placement and sizing to minimize real power losses while satisfying network constraints. Radiality is ensured using a minimum spanning tree-based encoding. Fundamental voltages are determined by the backward-forward sweep method, and harmonic behavior is evaluated using a decoupled harmonic power flow based on the same approach. The algorithm is parallelized on GPU and validated on systems ranging from 16 to 880 buses. Results demonstrate that simultaneous optimization significantly reduces power losses and improves overall system efficiency. They also show that the parallel implementation on GPU reduces the execution time by more than 2 orders of magnitude allowing for a fast solution.

title: SLRD-IMC: Structured Low-Rank Matrix Decomposition for Magnetic Anomaly Detection with Iteratively Merging Clusters
authors: Huan Liu, Hairu Wang, Sheng Du, Haobin Dong, Zheng Liu, Xiangyun Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Magnetic anomaly detection (MAD) is pivotal for geophysical prospecting, particularly in defense and resource exploration. However, weak signals are often corrupted by electromagnetic interference and industrial noise, while conventional methods excessively focus on signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) improvement at the expense of structural fidelity. This study introduces a novel MAD framework integrating structured low-rank matrix decomposition with iteratively merging clusters (SLRD-IMC). First, implementing a frequency-domain structured Hankel low-rank transformation coupled with sparse structuring to amplify the intrinsic low-rank characteristics of magnetic signals. Second, incorporating a nonlocal similarity-based iterative clustering strategy that synergizes with adaptive singular value thresholding to achieve simultaneous noise suppression and anomaly detail retention. Third, developing a prototype detection system for comparative validation against four state-of-the-art techniques. Experimental results demonstrate SLRD-IMC’s superior performance across SNR from -5 dB to 20 dB, exhibiting average improvements of 32% in SNR, 100% in structural similarity (SSIM), and 42% reduction in mean square error (MSE) versus benchmark methods. In particular, when the initial SNR plunges to -10 dB, a scenario in which conventional methods fail, SLRD-IMC maintains robust detection capability. These findings conclusively validate the method’s exceptional noise resilience and precision in extreme conditions.

title: Fréchet Power-Scenario Distance: A Measure for Evaluating Synthetic Data Across Multiple Time-Scales in Smart Grids
authors: Yuting Cai, Shaohuai Liu, Chao Tian, Le Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence models for smart grids have advanced rapidly in recent years, and the synthetic data they produce can help mitigate limited data access caused by strict confidentiality constraints in power systems. A central challenge, however, is how to rigorously evaluate the quality of both the generative models and the synthetic time series they produce. Generic evaluation metrics are often not tailored to grid-specific requirements—such as multi-timescale temporal structure, cross-resolution consistency, and physically meaningful operating regimes—and therefore can yield misleading assessments. In this work, we propose a novel measure based on the Fréchet Distance (FD) estimated between two datasets in a power-centric learned feature space. Our approach embeds smart-grid time series using a hierarchical multi-resolution encoder trained on real grid data that encourages sensitivity to multi-timescale dynamics and resolution-consistent operational signatures. Across multiple use cases, the resulting Fr’echet Power-Scenario Distance (FPD) exposes temporal and physical mismatches more reliably and correlates with downstream task performance more strongly than conventional baselines, enabling more robust benchmarking of generative models for smart-grid data.

title: Adaptive Duty Cycle Calculation for SEPIC Converter Using Bisection Method Under Fluctuating Solar Input Conditions
authors: Md Imran Hasan Emon, Md Alimul Hossain, Shafiullah Lami, Hamim, Shahriar Mahmud Kabir
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents an adaptive algorithm for computing the duty cycle of a Single-Ended Primary Inductor Converter (SEPIC) used in photovoltaic (PV) battery-charging systems. Due to fluctuating irradiance and temperature, the solar input voltage varies significantly, whereas the battery requires a constant charging voltage. To address this, a numerical method based on the bisection algorithm is implemented to dynamically compute the appropriate duty cycle that regulates the output at 48 V regardless of input variations. The SEPIC converter circuit was modeled and simulated in OrCAD PSpice, while MATLAB was used to execute the adaptive duty cycle calculation. Comparative analysis between MATLAB-computed duty cycles and PSpice simulations confirmed close agreement, with a deviation of less than 1.2%. The method consistently converged within 22–27 iterations and provided stable voltage regulation, demonstrating robustness under rapidly varying solar input. Results highlight that the proposed approach is computationally efficient, accurate, and suitable for real-time embedded control in renewable energy applications.

title: Next-Gen AI-Driven Cybersecurity Framework for Flying Taxis with Intrusion Detection and Secure Command Validation
authors: Kalaivani C T, Tamil Selvi S K, Vidhyashree M
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The rapid development of autonomous flying taxis presents new opportunities for urban transportation, but also introduces significant cybersecurity risks. This project, Futuristic AI in Cybersecurity for Flying Taxis, aims to design an adaptive, AI-driven cybersecurity framework to protect these next-generation vehicles from evolving cyber threats. The proposed system integrates machine learning, deep learning, and reinforcement learning for real-time threat detection and autonomous response. It leverages blockchain for secure communication, federated learning for decentralized data protection, and digital twin simulations for vulnerability forecasting and resilience testing. This AI-enhanced framework ensures continuous surveillance, system adaptability, and proactive threat mitigation across critical components such as navigation, flight controls, and passenger interfaces. The project contributes to the safe and secure integration of flying taxis into smart cities by addressing emerging cybersecurity challenges in the rapidly evolving urban air mobility landscape.

title: A Confidence-Inference-Based Dual-Layer Robust Elastic Factor Graph Method for Multi-Sensor Fusion Navigation
authors: Haoran Zheng, Binghua Xu, Xudong Zhao, Zexu Wang, Xin Wang, Shizu Wei
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: To address the vulnerability of multi-sensor GNSS/INS/OD/MAG fusion navigation systems to anomalous observations, sensor failures, and environmental interferenceissues that limit the robustness and adaptability of conventional factor graph methods-this paper proposes a confidence-driven, two-layer robust elastic factor graph fusion navigation approach. The method employs a sliding-window residual Mahalanobis distance to perform online evaluation of sensor observation reliability and designs a confidence-driven factor sleep-activation mechanism to construct a dynamically reconfigurable elastic fusion architecture. Simultaneously, confidence weights are integrated with an improved Huber robust kernel, forming a twolayer optimization model that combines outer-layer confidencebased decision-making with inner-layer robust suppression. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with traditional factor graph methods, the proposed approach improves positioning accuracy by 71.3 % and 68.7 % in the east and north directions, respectively; compared with adaptive Kalman filtering (AKF), improvements are 57.1 % and 56.2 %; and compared with robust factor graph methods, improvements are 45.7 % and 45.9 %, with a significant reduction in maximum errors. Further analysis of window length indicates that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{L}=20$</tex> achieves a favorable balance between statistical stability and dynamic responsiveness. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in enhancing positioning accuracy and robustness under complex interference conditions.

title: A Touch-Sensing Method Based on Fractal Defected Ground Microstrip Lines and Chaotic Signals
authors: Xingyu Liu, Zhihui Wang, Jingyuan Huang, Jiayu Song, Xiyu Wu, Jiangtao Huangfu
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Touch sensing is essential for smart terminals, human-computer interaction (HCI), and intelligent systems. This paper presents a broadband touch-sensing method using a Hilbert fractal defected ground structure (DGS) microstrip line excited by chaotic signals. Three Hilbert fractal DGS units of different sizes are embedded on the back side of the microstrip line. The disrupted ground-plane current path produces localized LC resonances that generate multiple stopbands, while the fractal geometry enables sensor miniaturization. The broadband spectrum of chaotic signals allows incoherent detection via a radio-frequency (RF) power meter, simplifying system design. Simulations and experiments across five cases confirm reliable discrimination of touch events, indicating suitability for wearable devices and interactive surfaces.

title: Flexible Vivaldi Antennas for Estimating Breast Skin Parameters
authors: Milad Mokhtari, Milica Popović
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: In this work, we present two generations of compact, flexible surface-wave-exciting Vivaldi antennas designed for tissue-adjacent operation and fabricated on Kapton polyimide films. Through accurate modeling of surface-wave propagation along the breast tissue-air interface, we aim to reliably calculate the propagation delay required for confocal microwave breast imaging and to even sense superficial pathologies, such as Paget's disease. The Gen-1 antenna employs a two-layer microstrip feed, while the Gen-2 design adopts a simplified single-layer CPW-fed architecture to improve flexibility. Measured reflection coefficients show acceptable agreement with full-wave simulations, suggesting that the antennas and the surface-wave approach can yield data to help improve the overall performance of the confocal microwave imaging system.

title: A Deep-Clustering Fusion Approach for Plant Leaf Disease Classification Using SqueKM-Net
authors: K. Madhavi Priya, P. Mangayarkarasi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Improving crop yield and lowering financial losses in agriculture depend on the early and precise detection of plant leaf diseases. In order to classify leaf diseases effectively and robustly, this paper suggests a novel lightweight hybrid model called SqueKM-Net, which combines SqueezeNet, KMeans clustering, and a Multiclass Support Vector Machine (MSVM). Binary thresholding is used to highlight diseased regions after image pre-processing, which includes resizing, color space transformation, and median filtering, is completed in the suggested methodology. SqueezeNet, a compact convolutional neural network intended for high accuracy with minimal computational complexity, is used to perform deep feature extraction. The Gray-Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) is used to derive handcrafted texture features, and centroid detection is used to locate objects. A comprehensive feature vector is created by fusing the extracted deep and handcrafted features, and a multiclass SVM is then used for classification [9]. To improve interpretability and confirm class separability, the K-Means algorithm is also used for unsupervised clustering. SqueKM-Net is appropriate for realtime agricultural disease monitoring applications because experimental results show that it achieves high accuracy and sensitivity while maintaining computational efficiency. A comparison of the suggested hybrid approach's performance with that of standalone SVM and K-Means models further demonstrates its superiority

title: SMARTGRID FORECAST: Machine Learningdriven Electricity Price Prediction for Smart Energy Systems
authors: Ch Armaan, Anuj Panda, Tumulu Rajsekhar, Neelamadhab Padhy, Mahavir Ojha, Chinmayee Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
