abstract: Electricity prices are becoming less and less predictable because of changes in demand, the unpredictability of renewable energy sources, and unseen market forces from outside the market. Because both suppliers and consumers are affected by these changes, there is a greater need than ever for precise price forecasting tools. Objective: Our main goal is to create a machine learning model that uses environmental and historical data to predict short-term changes in electricity prices. Energy stakeholders can make better planning and consumption decisions with improved forecasting. Material/Method: This study includes several regression-based machine learning models, including Linear Regression, Ridge, Lasso, Elastic Net, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost. Each model was evaluated on predictive accuracy and robustness using metrics such as RMSE, MAE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>. We used a dataset containing half-hourly records of electricity market variables. It consists of over 35,000 instances with features such as wind production (forecasted and actual), system load, temperature, wind speed, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> intensity, and time-related details. The target variable is SMPEA (System Marginal Price for EA), which represents the electricity price. Result: After evaluating multiple models, we got the maximum accuracy of 83 % only with [35, 000, 22] of data, and then we applied the Advanced Feature Engineering, and we got that The Algorithms like XG Boost, LightGBM, and Cat Boost demonstrated the superior performance compared to other linear Models with a Performance Matrix of 99.08 %.

title: Optimal Energy Optimization of Renewable-Assisted AC Microgrid with Hybrid Energy Storage via Lagrange Multiplier
authors: Khadija Azakaf, Abdelmounime El Magri, Rachid Lajouad, Ilyass El Myasse
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Microgrids are emerging as a promising solution for sustainable energy by integrating renewable energy sources (RES) like solar and wind power. However, the intermittent nature of RES presents significant challenges to system stability and cost-effective operation. To address these issues, hybrid energy storage systems (HESS) are being used to enhance flexibility, reliability, and environmental performance. This paper proposes an optimal energy management strategy for a renewable-assisted AC microgrid that includes RES, a diesel generator and HESS. The optimization problem is formulated using the Lagrange Multiplier method to minimize operating costs, ensure a reliable power supply, and reduce pollutant emissions. Simulation results confirm that the proposed strategy effectively improves energy flow management, mitigates the impact of RES intermittency, reducing pollutant emissions by $\mathbf{7 5 \%}$, and supports the transition toward a more sustainable and resilient microgrid.

title: Security and Privacy in Connected and Autonomous Vehicles: Architecture and Challenges
authors: Mohamed Alhyan, Zineb Nadifi, Mariya Ouaissa, Mariyam Ouaissa, Ali Kartit
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The combination of autonomous vehicles and the internet, along with related modern information and communication technologies, has created the concept of the Connected and Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs). CAVs are a promising direction for the development of the intelligent transportation system. However, CAVs are also an open system with a huge number of attack surfaces and vulnerabilities, which could lead to considerable cybersecurity risks. Although considerable research has been dedicated to addressing such risks, there is a lack of an overall assessment framework for CAVs’ cybersecurity. This paper proposes a comprehensive cybersecurity assessment framework for CAVs that combines architecture analysis, attack taxonomy, and a multi-factor risk model.

title: Inversion of Iron Oxide Abundance Using Hyperspectral Data Based on Wavelet Packet Analysis
authors: Changbao Yang, Yu He, Mengyao Zhu, Liguo Han
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Accurate inversion of iron oxide abundance is crucial for mineral exploration and geological assessment. However, traditional spectral inversion methods are susceptible to interferences from factors such as particle size, topography, and mixed pixels, which limit prediction accuracy. To address this issue, this paper proposes a quantitative inversion method for iron oxide abundance using hyperspectral remote sensing, combining Wavelet Packet Analysis (WPA) and Partial Least Squares Regression (PLSR). This method utilizes the optimal wavelet packet basis function to perform multi-scale decomposition on laboratory mineral spectra, separating approximation signals and detail signals, and combining with PLSR to establish a high-precision abundance prediction model. Subsequently, the optimal model is applied to GaoFen-5 (GF-5) AHSI hyperspectral satellite data. The results show that the spectral detail signals can not only effectively amplify the absorption features of iron oxide (sensitive bands at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400-1200 \text{nm}$</tex>), significantly improving the fitting accuracy, but also effectively separate and remove the systematic stripe noise in the hyperspectral images during the image reconstruction phase. By effectively isolating high-frequency spectral features, this combined model successfully mitigates the interferences of topography and particle size effects, achieves high-precision spatial distribution mapping of mineralization zones in the study area, and provides reliable technical support for the delineation of prospecting targets.

title: Spatiotemporal Distribution Prediction of Charging Load at Highway Service Areas Based on Traffic Flow Simulation
authors: Yachao Wang, Jie Wang, Dongxiang Jiao, Bozhi Zhang, Yanjin Wang, Xuemei Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: To address the difficulty in predicting the spatiotemporal distribution of charging load caused by the sharp increase in the number of electric vehicles on highways, this paper proposes a prediction method for the spatiotemporal distribution of charging load at highway service areas based on traffic flow simulation. First, a chain-type highway model is constructed based on the actual highway topology. The initial distribution of vehicle state of charge is simulated using the Monte Carlo method, and parameters such as the total number of vehicles and the electric vehicle penetration rate are set to initialize the simulation. Second, the Cell Transmission Model is introduced to simulate highway traffic flow, obtaining real-time traffic flow data for each road segment. Third, the electricity consumption of electric vehicle s during driving is calculated, their state of charge is dynamically updated, and the charging demand is determined accordingly. Finally, a case study is conducted on the "Qinghe Toll Station to Zhangjiakou West Toll Station" section of the highway from Beijing to Zhangjiakou City. The results show that the proposed method can effectively address the problem of spatiotemporal distribution prediction of charging load at highway service areas.

title: An Intelligent Hybrid Model of CNN-BiLSTM for Fault Classification in AC Microgrid
authors: Rakesh Sahu, Pratap Kumar Panigrahi, Deepak Kumar Lal, Rudranarayan Pradhan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The reliable and fast fault classification is paramount for the stability and resilience of your modern power systems as the networks are increasingly dynamic through adopting renewables. This paper proposed a hybrid Deep Learning model employing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks for the intelligent classification of three main transmission line faults; Line-to-Ground (LG), Line-to-Line (LL), and Double Line-to-Ground (LLG). In the proposed model, CNN layers were used to extract local spatial and frequency-domain features during faults occurring in threewaveforms of current signals, while the bi-directional layer is effective in identifying long-range temporal dependencies during transient fault conditions. An attention mechanism was used to indicate the relevant parts of the temporal signal window during the classification process, providing interpretation of the signal focus. In order to improve the accuracy, especially for LL faults, hyperparameter optimization with Optuna was used to tune important model hyperparameters including the learning rate, LSTM size, and dropout rate. The model was trained using synthetically produced, Z-score normalized datasets with data augmentation to mimic realistic operating conditions. When compared to traditional classifiers including Decision Tree, SVM, Naïve Bayes, and MLP, the CNN-BiLSTM architecture demonstrated superior performance with accuracy reaching up to 99.9 % for each fault type. The model also showed good generalization and robustness, as evidenced by confusion matrices, ROC curves, and visualizations of attention-weighs. The findings reinforce that the suggested CNN-BiLSTM approach provides an effective and interpretable option for performing fault classifications in real-time, paving the way for intelligent protection schemes for future smart grids.

title: LVRT Control Strategy for a Grid-Forming Wind-Storage System Based on DC-Link Power-Energy Coordination
authors: Qing Dong, Shaoze Tian, Xiangwu Yan, Fan Zhou, Peisheng Shi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: To address the transient energy imbalance of the DC-link and the sluggish response of traditional energy storage control based on DC-link voltage feedback during the low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) process of grid-forming doubly-fed wind-storage integrated systems under weak grid conditions, this paper investigates a grid-forming doubly-fed wind-storage system equipped with a supercapacitor energy storage unit. The DC-link energy coupling mechanism among the machine-side converter, grid-side converter, and energy storage branch during LVRT is analyzed. On this basis, an LVRT control strategy based on DC-link power-energy coordination is proposed. A simulation model of the grid-forming doubly-fed wind-storage system is established in MATLAB/Simulink, and comparative analysis is carried out against the conventional DC voltage regulation-based energy storage control strategy. Simulation results show that the proposed strategy can significantly reduce the DC-link voltage sag and recovery overshoot during LVRT, shorten the system recovery time, suppress post-fault oscillations, and enable the energy storage unit to participate in DC-link transient energy regulation more rapidly and effectively.

title: An AI–IoT Integrated Multi-Modal Deep Learning Framework for Crop Submergence Prediction and Mitigation
authors: K. Sudharson, Narukula Chiru Venkata Mohan, Tarra Purna Chandhu, R.R. Ramya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The productivity of crops has come under severe threat due to increased flooding of farmland, which in turn has also threatened food security due to increased climate variance. To combat this issue, the authors have developed DeepHydro which uses artificial intelligence, integrated with the internet of things, to mitigate the risk of crop flooding at the field level through advanced multi-modal deep learning. The system uses satellite images along with weather data, GIS topography, IoT developed sensors and meteorological data to assess the risk using a hybrid CNN-LSTM model. The system uses IoT sensors and satellite images to devise an adaptive decision-support module which estimates the risk of water over-stress and recommends appropriate irrigation and drainage actions. The system has been validated in three differnet agro-climatic zones which shows lower RMSE and a prediction accuracy of over 94.2%, at 12-18% greater water-use efficiency and 9-14% yield improvement in comparison to baseline models. The findings demonstrate DeepHydro as an intelligent, climate-resilient, and straightforward solution for adaptable agro fields.

title: Adaptive SOC-based equalization charging with flyback converter for battery packs
authors: Xi Chen, Zhijie He, Dui Liu, Liwei Guan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Battery inconsistency is a critical issue affecting the performance, safety, and lifespan of electric vehicle battery packs. To address this problem, this study proposes an adaptive state of charge (SOC)-based equalization charging method combined with a flyback converter topology. The approach employs independent charging modules for each battery cell and utilizes real-time SOC estimation to dynamically adjust the charging current, enabling adaptive energy distribution for individual cells without relying on inter-cell energy transfer structures. A flyback-based equalization charging system is developed, and key parameters of the converter including transformer design and core selection are systematically derived. Meanwhile, an SOC estimation method integrating open circuit voltage, Coulomb counting, and Kalman filtering is adopted to improve estimation accuracy and robustness. Experimental results on a series connected lithium-ion battery pack demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves cell consistency. The maximum voltage difference among cells is reduced from 220 mV to 50 mV, effectively mitigating imbalance-induced performance degradation. In addition, the proposed system exhibits stable operation and satisfactory thermal performance under practical conditions. The proposed method achieves a favorable balance between system simplicity, scalability, and equalization efficiency, demonstrating strong potential for practical battery management system applications.

title: Machine Learning-Based Prediction Modelling of EDM Application on Ti6Al4V Alloy Using 3D-Printed SS316L Electrodes
authors: Satyabrata Barik, Basanta Kumar Nanda, B. Surekha, Swayam Bikash Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: This study explores the application of 3D-printed SS316L electrodes in the Electrical Discharge Machining (EDM) of Ti6Al4V alloy and employs machine learning (ML) models to predict and optimize key performance metrics such as Material Removal Rate (MRR), Tool Wear Rate (TWR), surface roughness, and hardness. EDM experiments were conducted using a BoxBehnken L27 orthogonal array design, and data-driven models were trained using four regression algorithms. Initial experiments with 27 data points showed limited model accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{5 6. 2 \%}$</tex> for MRR using Random Forest). To enhance prediction capability, the dataset was expanded to 200 samples using polynomial interpolation. After expansion, Random Forest achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 6} \boldsymbol{\%} \mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> in predicting MRR and TWR, while XGBoost reached <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98.6 \% \mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> for surface roughness and hardness. These findings demonstrate that integrating 3D-printed electrodes with AI-driven modelling and improving the data set significantly improves the predictive control.

title: A Method for Inverting Pollutant Concentration in Atmospheric Remote Sensing Images Based on an Improved U-Net Network
authors: Shijie Fu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: A multi-scale gated fusion boundary enhancement UNet (MGFBE-U-Net) algorithm is proposed for inverting pollutant concentration in atmospheric remote sensing images. Based on the U-Net encoding and decoding architecture, the algorithm designs a multi-scale residual dense coding module to extract fine-grained spectral features, a cross-modal gated fusion module to achieve adaptive fusion of remote sensing spectral features with meteorological and topographic auxiliary features, boundary enhancement attention skip connections to strengthen the transmission of pollution boundary details, and a composite loss function to optimize regression accuracy. Using PM2.5 as the inversion target, experiments were conducted using Sentinel-5P TROPOMI imagery, measured data from national monitoring stations, and multi-source auxiliary data. The results show that the algorithm has a MAE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.12 \mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, an RMSE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.58 \mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 2 6}$</tex>, significantly improving accuracy compared to the original U-Net and traditional methods. It also exhibits good generalization and resistance to cloud interference, accurately reconstructing the boundaries of highly polluted areas, providing technical support for refined inversion of atmospheric pollutant concentrations.

title: A Multilayer Perceptron Fitted Simulation Model for 2D Sea Surface Scattering and Doppler Velocity Based on M4S
authors: Yingrui Zhang, Rui Qi, Xiaonan Yao, Xiaoqing Wang, Zhibin Wang, Xinzhe Yuan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The normalized radar cross-section (NRCS) and Doppler velocity are critical parameters in ocean microwave remote sensing, playing a key role in studying various oceanic phenomena, such as wind, currents, internal waves, and shallow topography. Simulating NRCS and Doppler velocity textures under complex current modulation effects is essential for understanding these phenomena. The widely used M4S simulation model provides essential information but suffers from high computational costs and a limited number of pixels. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a multilayer perceptron based fitted model for 2D sea surface scattering and Doppler velocity, using M4S as the reference. The proposed model establishes a mapping from sea surface current fields, wind fields, and radar parameters to the resulting NRCS and Doppler velocity. Compared to M4S, the prediction time of the proposed model is reduced by four orders of magnitude. Moreover, the correlation coefficients between the model predictions and M4S results are 0.89 for NRCS and 0.99 for Doppler velocity.

title: GUIDE: Generalizable Trajectory Prediction Using Intention-Aware Diffusion With Efficiency
authors: Yuanbo Yang, Sheng Jin, Claudio Roncoli, Panagiotis Angeloudis, Simon Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The ability to predict the future trajectories of surrounding vehicles is crucial for the safe and efficient operation of autonomous vehicles. However, most existing data-driven trajectory prediction models lack a deep understanding of driving intentions, which complicates the prediction task and often leads to suboptimal results. In this paper, we introduce <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GUIDE</monospace>, a framework that decomposes driving intention into driver personality traits and driving maneuvers. This approach incorporates these inferred intentions into a diffusion-based trajectory prediction model, which better models the probability distribution of future trajectories in a generative manner. Furthermore, to address the limited generalization ability of existing models in unseen scenarios, we focus on increasing the data scale and diversity during training. To this end, we have designed a unified data processing framework and evaluation benchmark that integrates multiple autonomous driving datasets. Upon this foundation, we build our diffusion-based, high-generalization trajectory prediction system. Experimental results demonstrate that by training on this unified dataset, our model learns more robust scene representations, leading to significant improvements in both prediction performance and generalization ability.

title: Recent Advances in Intelligent Energy Management for Design and Operation of Green Hospitals
authors: Qing Wang, Leqi Weng, Taojun Wang, Jingshan Li
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Reducing energy consumption and adopting alternative or renewable energy in hospitals are of significant importance. In this paper, a review of recent advances in intelligent energy management for design and operation of green hospitals is presented from the perspectives of energy efficiency, renewable energy, building design and logistics, particularly in terms of optimization and AI applications.

title: Bearing Fault Diagnosis Using Teager Energy Concentrated Time-Frequency Transforms
authors: K. Krishnendu, G. L. Priya, Pyari Mohan Pradhan
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Bearing fault diagnosis using vibration signals collected using strategically located accelerometer and vibration sensors is critical for predictive maintenance of industrial rotating machinery. While time-frequency analysis (TFA) techniques are well established diagnostic tools for identifying the fault features, conventional TFA methods result in poor energy concentrated time-frequency representation. In this context, this study uses the Teager energy operator (TEO) as a potential energy estimation tool to develop an optimum window function that jointly maximizes the product of time-domain (TD) and frequency-domain (FD) Teager energy concentration (TEC), termed temporal spectral TEC-product optimized window (TSTPOW) . Theoretical bounds of attainable TEC and practical guidelines for parameter selection are presented. The proposed approach is compared with the state-of-the-art techniques for bearing fault detection using the Case Western Reserve University and Paderborn University bearing datasets. The performance analysis shows that the proposed approach is able to detect the inner and outer race bearing faults and ball-fault more accurately in terms of fault occurrence time, fault frequency and the separability of fault features. The results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed approach for enhanced bearing fault diagnosis in industrial condition monitoring.

title: VIRTUS-FPP: Virtual Sensor Modeling for Fringe Projection Profilometry in NVIDIA Isaac Sim
authors: Adam Haroon, Anush Lakshman, Badrinath Balasubramaniam, Beiwen Li
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Fringe projection profilometry (FPP) is a high-precision structured-light sensing technique for 3D surface reconstruction, yet its practical deployment is often constrained by complex calibration procedures, sensitivity to environmental conditions, and the high cost of physical experimentation. At the same time, robotics research increasingly relies on simulation platforms such as NVIDIA Isaac Sim for scalable development and validation, but accurate virtual representations of optical metrology sensors such as FPP are not currently available. In this work, we present VIRTUS-FPP, the first end-to-end virtual sensor modeling framework for fringe projection profilometry implemented in NVIDIA Isaac Sim, enabling physically grounded simulation of the complete FPP pipeline, including structured light projection, image formation, calibration, and 3D reconstruction, without dependence on precalibrated physical systems. The framework leverages an inverse camera model for projector representation, ensuring geometric and photometric fidelity consistent with structured-light principles. By bridging optical metrology and robotics simulation, VIRTUS-FPP enables high-fidelity synthetic data generation, systematic evaluation of sensing pipelines, and digital twin replication of real-world FPP systems. Experimental results demonstrate sub-millimeter reconstruction accuracy and strong correspondence between simulated and physical measurements, highlighting the framework’s effectiveness and its potential to advance perception-driven robotics, simulation-to-reality transfer, and scalable optical sensor design.

title: Strain Response Model of CORC Cable Under Transverse Compression and Axial Tension Load
authors: Yangyang Shi, Yuxi Liu, Weimeng Yuan, Shaotao Dai, Tao Ma, Junfeng Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The CORC cable possess the characteristics of high current-carrying density and high flexibility, and are considered one of the most promising candidates for fusion devices. When used in fusion devices, CORC cables are subjected to significant electromagnetic forces, primarily manifesting as transverse compression and axial tensile loads. Excessive electromagnetic loads can cause irreversible degradation in the current-carrying capacity of CORC cables. Currently, research on the current-carrying characteristics of CORC cables under transverse and axial mechanical loads is mostly conducted independently. However, in practical applications, CORC cables experience combined transverse compression and axial tension load. Therefore, it is necessary to study the current-carrying characteristics of CORC cables under the combined action of these two load types. This paper establishes a strain response model based on the maximum strain component for CORC cable under combined loads. However, the computational complexity is high when both loads are applied simultaneously. Applying the loads sequentially can effectively mitigate this issue. Thus, this study investigates two loading sequences: applying tension first followed by compression, and applying compression first followed by tension. The results indicate that the tension-first followed by compression sequence more closely approximates the real condition. In addition, the impact of changing a single load on the strain response of CORC cable under comprehensive loads is compared. The findings of this study can provide theoretical guidance for the simulation of CORC cables under combined loads.

title: Visibility-Constrained Vision-Based Air-to-Air Tracking and Control for Non-cooperative UAVs with Unknown Maneuvers
authors: Bingjin Liu, Zhuanghua Liu, Yi Huang, Jian Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a visibility-constrained vision based air-to-air tracking system for the pursuit of non cooperative Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) with complex and unknown maneuvers, which comprises a target motion estimator and a Position-Based Visual Servo (PBVS) controller. Firstly, the target motion estimator integrates Pseudo Linear Kalman Filter (PLKF) with Interactive Multi Model (IMM) filters, which can effectively cope with the high uncertainty of target motion in air-to-air scenarios, overcoming the limitations of traditional single-model estimation in complex maneuvering trajectories. Moreover, the proposed estimator does not require the observer to perform higher-order motion than the target to recover the target state. Secondly, the designed PBVS controller is based on virtual coordinate frame to decouple the translation and rotation control, thereby simplifying the controller design. In order to ensure the target visibility throughout the entire tracking process, the Control Barrier Function (CBF) is incorporated to dynamically adjust the controller input by imposing constraints on the actual image plane, which enhances the reliability and safety of the system. Finally, simulation and real-world experiment results have verified the effectiveness of the proposed method.

authors: Dongjae Lee, Kihong Heo

title: A novel asynchronous intermittent communication methodology for multi-agent systems with unmodeled disturbances
authors: Jian Sun, Ruotong Wang, Lei Liu, Yang Yang, Qihe Shan, Jianxin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper investigates the consensus control problem of multi-agent systems under intermittent communication and unmodeled external disturbances. The main contribution is to overcome the limitation of current synchronous intermittent framework and propose a novel asynchronous intermittent communication methodology on multi-agent systems. In this intermittent methodology, the state space is divided into three distinct regions by introducing both safety and intermittent boundaries, which enables effective monitoring of agent error dynamics.Furthermore, an asynchronous intermittent communication protocol is designed, where the activation and rest intervals are adjusted based on the real-time error states of the agents.By utilizing the distributed extended observer to observe the relative output information and unmodeled disturbances, the novel asynchronous intermittent consensus protocol with disturbance rejection is designed to realize the overall consensus of the multi-agent systems. The proposed spatial-segmentation-dependent intermittent communication methodology can adjust communication and non-communication time of each agent asynchronously according to the communication requirements, under which the multi-agent systems can tolerate more non-communication time and reduce the communication frequency. Finally, numerical simulations are performed to verify our results.
