title: ASIC Implementation of a Multiplier-Less CNN Accelerator
authors: Sasmita Padhy, B Rahul, Subham Kumar Mishra, Deepak Gouda, Poonam Panigrahy, Swayam Suvam Nayak, Raghunandan Swain, Sandipan Mallik
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The design and implementation of an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) architecture of a Multiplier Accumulator (MAC) unit for convolution layers suitable for Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) based applications, is presented in this work. The proposed architecture emphasizes low power consumption and reduced hardware complexity. A 4-bit Signed-Digit (SD4) quantization scheme replaces conventional multipliers with bit-shift operations, resulting in significant reductions in power, area, and latency. The core computational engine is organized as a systolic array to enhance data reuse and parallelism. Designed and implemented in 180 nm CMOS technology, the MAC unit occupies 72 µm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> area and 1.85µW power consumption, making the MAC unit well-suited for edge artificial intelligence (AI) and embedded vision systems. Comprehensive simulation and synthesis results validate the effectiveness of the proposed design, showing optimized power and area utilization. This approach highlights the potential of ASIC-based accelerators as a viable alternative to GPU-based solutions suitable for face recognition and similar AI-driven image processing tasks.

title: Sensorless nonlinear control of flying capacitor multilevel inverter based grid-tied Photovoltaic system with an LCL filter
authors: Siham Chakiri, Younes Abouelmahjoub, Youssef Mchaouar, Hassan Abouobaida, Fouad Giri, Fatima Zahra Chaoui, Oussama Khentaoui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents a control strategy for a single stage grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) system based on a flying capacitor multicellular inverter (FCMI) and an LCL filter. The proposed controller is designed to deal with two main objectives: (i) ensure power factor correction (PFC) and guarantee a balanced input DC voltage distribution; (ii) ensure maximum power extraction from PV panels despite climate changes and control the DC bus voltage. The proposed sensorless nonlinear controller consists of two cascaded loops: (i) an outer loop is based on a filtered proportional integral regulator for the PV panels voltage regulation, and (ii) an inner loop is developed using the Lyapunov and integral backstepping approaches for the PFC and flying capacitors voltage regulation. Additionally, a state observer is combined with the regulator to achieve the grid voltage estimation. The performances of the advanced power system and control approach are validated through numerical simulations, using MATLAB/Simulink environment under varying climatic conditions.

title: Nutriscan: An Arduino-Based Food Health Classification System
authors: Anusha Sridhar, Munipalle Bhavani, Sala Pavani, Parvathi Muddapu
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: In today's fast-paced lifestyle, diet-related health issues such as hypertension, obesity, and diabetes are increasingly prevalent, often due to unregulated consumption of salt and fat. While digital health devices and nutrition apps are available, they largely depend on manual data entry or packaged food labels, leaving a significant gap in assessing the nutritional value of home-cooked or unpackaged meals. To bridge this gap, NutriScan–Smart Sensing for Safer Eating is introduced as an innovative, Arduino-based IoT system designed to analyze and classify food samples based on their salt and fat content. Utilizing a TDS sensor and a capacitive moisture sensor, NutriScan measures the levels of salt and total fat in a 100g sample and classifies the food into three health categories: Healthy (Green), Moderately Healthy (White), and Unhealthy (Red). Results are displayed on an LCD with corresponding LED alerts for intuitive user feedback. NutriScan offers a low-cost, real-time, and user-friendly solution to enhance dietary awareness and encourage informed food choices. By integrating embedded sensing technology into daily nutrition monitoring, this system promotes healthier lifestyles and supports public health initiatives aimed at reducing the risks of diet-related diseases.

title: MAMNet: A State Space Attention Collaborative Network for Photovoltaic Change Detection
authors: Jinyuan Miao, Hongli Li, Wenjie Hu, Xiaoyu Zhang, Yuxi Sun, Sen Jia
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: To address the challenges of small-scale change regions, densely distributed structures, and susceptibility to illumination variations and background interference in rooftop photovoltaic (PV) facilities from high-resolution remote sensing imagery, this paper proposes a photovoltaic change detection network named MAMNet, which integrates state space modeling with attention mechanisms. Based on an encoder-decoder framework, the method employs a dual-temporal feature encoder with shared weights to maintain cross-temporal semantic consistency, and enhances the representation capability for finegrained changes through multi-scale feature fusion and global dependency modeling. Meanwhile, in the decoding process, it jointly utilizes differential information and semantic discriminative information to improve the integrity of change region reconstruction and suppress pseudo-changes. Extensive experiments conducted on the SP-CD photovoltaic change detection dataset demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms several state-of-the-art approaches in terms of IoU, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{1}$</tex> score, and recall.

title: Image Dehazing Algorithm Based on Improved AOD-Net
authors: Zhong Zheng, Jin Ma, Kun Yang, Haichen Sui
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: In response to the issues of insufficient clarity and loss of detail information encountered by the AOD-Net (All in One Dehazing Network) model when processing hazy images, this paper proposes an improved dehazing algorithm based on the AOD-Net model. The main contributions of the proposed algorithm include the following aspects: To address the problem of blurred edges and textures caused by the use of large convolutional kernels in the AOD-Net model, we introduce a max-pooling layer into the network architecture. This modification allows the model to more effectively focus on global features, thereby preserving important information in the feature maps and enhancing the fine details and realism of the dehazed images. Furthermore, to better restore image detail, we employ the Smooth L1 function. This loss function facilitates smooth optimization of the model during the removal of haze, enabling the retention of more image details. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to the original model, the improved model achieves a peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) increase of 12.96%, a structural similarity index (SSIM) improvement of 3.54%, and an increase in image entropy of 2.37%. The enhanced dehazing algorithm exhibits superior performance in image dehazing, effectively improving the realism and detail preservation capabilities of the images. Notably, this method shows significant advantages when processing images for slope measurement based on binocular vision compared to the original model.

title: Two Types of Errors in the Assessment of Spatial Data Positional Accuracy
authors: Fujun Luo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Positional information is the core element of spatial data, with its accuracy directly determining the credibility and usability of the products. Currently, there is no academic research analyzing the credibility of positional accuracy assessment. This paper focuses on two types of risks in the positional accuracy assessment of spatial data, systematically analyzing the Type I error (false positive risk) and Type II error (false negative risk) present in the accuracy assessment process. The study, based on probabilistic statistical models, derives rigorous mathematical relationships between the accuracy of reference data, the precision of test results, and the designed accuracy of products, and calculates the corresponding risk probabilities. The results show that the false negative risk significantly increases with a decrease in the accuracy of reference data or an increase in measurement error during testing. When using high-precision testing, if the measurement error exceeds 0.7 times the designed measurement error, there exists a false negative risk; notably, when the measurement error equals the designed measurement error, the false negative risk reaches as high as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 4. 8 \%}$</tex>. False positive risk occurs when the measurement error surpasses the designed measurement error. Similarly, in high-precision testing, when the measurement error is 1.2 times the designed measurement error, it can lead to a false positive risk of 23.7 %. Meanwhile, both types of risks are significantly higher in same-precision testing compared to high-precision testing and should be avoided unless necessary.

title: IRASET 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)

title: AquaXplain: Interpretable Ensemble Learning for Environmental Engineering Aquatic Ecosystem Health Assessment via SHAP Attribution and Physicochemical Correlation Networks
authors: Jinrui Yao, Siying Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Accurate and interpretable assessment of aquatic ecological status is a critical challenge at the intersection of environmental engineering and computational intelligence, with direct implications for drinking water safety, ecosystem health monitoring, and sustainable water resource management. Existing approaches suffer from three key limitations: (1) conventional water quality classification methods lack predictive robustness when confronted with high-dimensional, noisy physicochemical measurements from real-world aquatic monitoring stations; (2) black-box machine learning models, despite their strong predictive performance, fail to provide the mechanistic interpretability required by environmental engineers for evidencebased ecological intervention; (3) the complex synergistic and antagonistic interactions among hydrochemical driving factors remain poorly characterized, hindering a holistic understanding of the physicochemical determinants of water potability. To address these challenges, we propose AquaXplain, an integrated computational framework that couples ensemble machine learning with multi-level SHAP-based interpretability analysis and correlation network construction. Specifically, AquaXplain introduces: (1) a cross-validated ensemble classifier with KNNimputed preprocessing for robust binary potability prediction; (2) a hierarchical SHAP attribution pipeline that jointly delivers global feature importance ranking, local sample-level explanations, and pairwise interaction value quantification; (3) a Pearson-threshold correlation network that topologically encodes statistically significant inter-feature relationships for ecologically grounded interpretation. Extensive experiments on the Water Potability benchmark (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=3,276$</tex>) demonstrate that AquaXplain achieves superior classification performance (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.98$</tex>) while revealing that Organic_carbon, Turbidity, and Conductivity are the dominant physicochemical drivers of aquatic ecological status, providing actionable and interpretable insights for environmental engineering practice.

title: Architectural Impact on IVIF: Evaluating the Performance of CNNs vs. Transformers in Infrared-Visible Image Fusion
authors: Puneet Kapoor, Maya Thapa, Pankaj Vaidya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Infrared and visible image fusion (IVIF) is used when a single sensor cannot capture all the useful information in a scene. The goal is to combine the thermal cues present in infrared images with the fine spatial details available in visible images so that the final image is informative under day, night, and adverse-weather conditions. A variety of IVIF approaches have been reported in the literature, but in recent years deep learning methods based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and, more recently, Transformers have become the two main directions. CNN-based fusion models learn strong local representations and therefore capture edges, textures, and other small-scale details effectively. Transformer-based models, in contrast, model global dependencies in the image and tend to generate fused outputs with more coherent overall structure. In this paper, we evaluate representative models from both families on three widely used IVIF benchmarks, namely MSRS, M3FD, and RoadScene. Our quantitative results show that the detail-oriented CNN model produces fused images with sharper boundaries and richer local information, which is reflected in higher Entropy, Average Gradient, and Spatial Frequency scores. The Transformer-based model, on the other hand, keeps the scene layout closer to the original sources and achieves higher MSSSIM and lower Mean Squared Error values. These findings suggest that an effective fusion network should account for both local-detail preservation and global-structure consistency, and the observations reported here can guide the design of future hybrid IVIF architectures.

title: XGBoost-SHAP: Gradient-Boosted Point Cloud Processing with Explainability-Driven Feature Analysis for Urban Road Semantic Segmentation in Surveying Engineering
authors: Yu Gao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Semantic segmentation of large-scale urban point clouds acquired by mobile laser scanning (MLS) systems is a fundamental task in surveying engineering, yet it remains challenged by three persistent limitations: (1) severe class imbalance suppresses recognition of safety-critical minority objects such as Pole and Fence; (2) existing pipelines demand substantial computational resources and lack transparency for engineering-grade quality control; and (3) the black-box nature of ensemble classifiers obscures which geometric and radiometric cues drive semantic decisions. To address these challenges, we propose XGBoost-SHAP, a tightly integrated framework in which Tree-SHAP is embedded as a native post-hoc explanation layer directly operating on the internal tree structures of the XGBoost ensemble—sharing the same model object and requiring no separate inference pass—comprising (A) a grid-based spatial feature extractor encoding multi-scale elevation, roughness, and density statistics; (B) a gradient-boosted tree classifier with second-order regularisation and histogram-based split search; and (C) a Tree-SHAP module delivering exact, instance-level feature attribution across all nine semantic categories. Experiments on the Toronto-3D benchmark demonstrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{OA}=95.8 \%$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mIoU}=86.0 \%$</tex>, and macro <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AUC}=1.00$</tex>, outperforming six baselines including Random Forest, LightGBM, and Gradient Boosting, while providing fully explainable predictions that expose the discriminative role of height-derived features in urban road scene understanding.

title: A Compact Wideband CPW-Fed and AMC-Backed $2 \times 2$ Planar MIMO Antenna for Sub-6GHz 5G and WLAN Access Points
authors: Niten Kumar Panda, Susanta Kumar Badi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: In this work, a compact and wideband coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> multi-input multi-output (MIMO) antenna is proposed. The antenna employs a layered substrate (FR4-air-FR4) configuration backed by artificial magnetic conductor (AMC). It operates over a wide frequency range from 3.43 to 6.04 GHz with 55% bandwidth, thereby covering multiple 5 G NR bands (n46, n47, n48, n77, n78, and n79) as well as WLAN IEEE 802.11a/h/n/ac/ax/be/y/j/p bands. The proposed antenna achieves a peak realized gain ranging from 2.4 to 4.2 dBi and a radiation efficiency between 81 to 96 %. The overall physical dimensions of the antenna are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$44.5 ~\text{mm} \times 44.5 ~\text{mm}$</tex> with a total height of 5.17 mm. An adequate inter-port isolation of 15 to 32 dB is observed between the MIMO elements. Furthermore, the antenna demonstrates excellent MIMO performance characteristics, including a low envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) of less than 0.056, a total active reflection coefficient (TARC) below -10 dB, and a channel capacity loss (CCL) of less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.25 \text{bps} / \text{Hz}$</tex>. The antenna performance is analyzed through full-wave electromagnetic simulations using CST, and the derived MIMO parameters are evaluated using MATLAB.

title: A Data-Driven Comparison of Classical Statistical and Machine Learning Models for Peatland Fire Prediction
authors: Muhammad Farid, Adilan Widyawan Mahdiyasa, Kurnia Novita Sari, Udjianna Sekteria Pasaribu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Tropical peatland ecosystems in Indonesia are significant global carbon sinks, but the risk of fire on these lands has a widespread impact on the environment and public health. Peatland fires are triggered by complex interactions between meteorological and hydrological variables, which often have non-linear relationships. Although logistic regression is often used in risk modeling, this classical statistical method has limitations in capturing complex interactions between factors and is prone to data imbalance issues. This study aims to empirically compare the performance of peatland fire prediction between logistic regression models and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) as a machine learning algorithm, as well as to identify the most influential variables in predicting fire risk in each model. The test results show that MLP has superior performance with an accuracy of 94% and an AUC value of 0.940, surpassing logistic regression, which produced an accuracy of 92% and an AUC of 0.938. The most striking difference was seen in the ability to detect fire classes, where MLP successfully increased the recall value from 0.06 to 0.42, equivalent to a sevenfold increase in actual fire detection capacity. Based on the logistic regression approach, temperature and water table variables were identified as linearly influential factors. However, through permutation importance analysis on MLP, soil moisture and rainfall variables were found to make an essential contribution through non-linear interaction patterns that were not captured by the linear model.

title: A Pragmatic Analysis of Deepfake Image Authentication Using Fine-Tuned ResNet50 and DenseNet Based DNN Models
authors: Dipu Hota, Ram Chandra Barik, Sampa Sahoo, Saroj Padhan, Niranjan Behera
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The rapid growth of deepfakes, or artificial visual media, poses a serious threat to digital authenticity and trust. This study investigates deepfake image authentication practically and comparatively using two well refined convolutional neural network backbones: ResNet50 and DenseNet. We develop a consistent fine-tuning workflow that employs robustness-aware enhancements, selective layer unfreezing, and feature-space regularization to increase sensitivity to forgery-specific artifacts while decreasing overfitting. Experiments with FaceForensics++, Celeb-DF, and WildDeepfake are planned to evaluate intradataset and cross-dataset generalization. Performance is assessed using metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, ROCAUC, and latency; interpretability is examined using Grad-CAM and embedding visualizations. The targeted comparison aims to provide useful guidance for creating reliable, understandable, and implementable picture authentication systems.

title: Dynamic sliding mode control of Grid Connected PV system via a series active power filter with a 3-level inverter
authors: Oussama Khentaoui, Youssef Mchaouar, Younes Abouelmahjoub, Hassan Abouobaida, Fouad Giri, Fatima Zahra Chaoui, Siham Chakiri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents a study of power quality improvement for a single-phase series active power filter under distorted grid conditions, based on a three-level halfbridge inverter topology powered by two photovoltaic sources. A new non-linear control strategy is proposed to: (i) attenuate grid voltage harmonics, (ii) compensate for sudden voltage sags and swells, and (iii) ensure the voltage balancing of the DC bus capacitor. The proposed non-linear controller is designed based on the non-linear model of the system using a dynamic sliding mode technique and Lyapunov stability. The overall performance of the system is analysed using stability analysis tools and validated by numerical simulations in MATLAB/Simulink. A comparison is also made with a classical backstepping controller and the results highlight the superiority of the proposed controller in terms of stability, signal quality and total harmonic distortion, considering different real operating conditions.

title: Electro-Thermal Synergistic Control Strategy for Grid-Forming ANPC Converters under Extreme Overload based on Digital Twin and Loss Reallocation
authors: Xingyu Pei, Xiaotian Qin, Bin Yi, Yong Chen, Hongyuan Wu, Xu Cheng
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: With the rapid penetration of renewable energy, grid-forming (GFM) converters have become essential for maintaining voltage stability in low-inertia power systems. Recent grid codes require GFM converters to withstand severe short-circuit conditions, delivering up to 3.0 p.u. current for 5 s, which imposes extreme electro-thermal stress on both power semiconductors and passive magnetic components. Conventional solutions mainly rely on hardware redundancy or current-limiting strategies, which either increase system cost or weaken the inherent grid-support capability of GFM converters. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a digital-twin-assisted electro-thermal coordinated control strategy for Active Neutral-Point-Clamped (ANPC) grid-forming converters. A reactor digital-twin thermal model is embedded into the control loop to estimate internal core temperature in real time. When thermal risk is detected, an adaptive switching-frequency derating mechanism is activated to reshape the internal loss distribution. By exploiting the distinct frequency characteristics of copper and iron losses, the strategy forms a loss "scissors effect," where the sharp reduction in iron loss outweighs the slight increase in copper loss. Experimental results show that reducing the switching frequency from 3 kHz to 1.5 kHz decreases the reactor thermal dissipation by approximately 37%, limiting the core temperature below 160 °C and enabling successful 5 s fault ride-through under 3× overload conditions. The proposed approach significantly enhances the thermal survivability of GFM converters without additional hardware, offering a lightweight and cost-effective solution for high-power-density power electronic systems.

title: Brain Tumor Segmentation and Personalized Treatment with SL-UNet
authors: N. Sunil Kumar, Karthik Suruvu, Pedakanti Himasri, Shaik Mahammad Rizwan, Mandli Lahari, Kowshik T H
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The brain tumors nerve disorder is one of the life threatening and therefore must be diagnosed effectively and timely to be treated. Manual segmentation from MRI scans is a very tedious work and prone to error work. This paper proposes a Skip-Lightweight U-Net (SL-UNet) framework for high segmentation accuracy with low computational expenditure to be used in the clinical setting. The model is made up of a combination of an optimum decoder and encoder architecture and attention-based skip connections, hybrid dice focal loss and GradCAM visualization in the form of interpretability. It was trained on braTS dataset and provided a 99.2 % training accuracy, 98.5 % validation accuracy, 0.98 precision and 0.97 recall. Its efficiency and lightweight design makes is compatible with deploying to mobile and edge devices, so as to make a quick diagnosis and to assist in the determination personalized treatment decisions.

title: Comparison of Nonlinear Solution Methods for Seafloor Datum Point Sound Ray Tracing Positioning
authors: Xingguo Gao, Minze Li, Zengliang Chang, Fenglong Wang, Meng Qu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The positioning of marine acoustic datum point suffers from insufficient inversion accuracy of sound ray propagation paths and inadequate correction of sound speed refraction effects under complex dynamic sound speed fields. The bistatic ray-tracing positioning for seafloor acoustic datum point takes the minimization of the sum of squared time-delay observation residuals as the optimization criterion, and achieves accurate estimation of beam incidence angles and seafloor datum point coordinates through solving nonlinear equations. However, different nonlinear equation solution methods differ significantly in theoretical principles, computational efficiency, and other aspects. Therefore, six methods-Newton iteration, Aitken iteration, discrete Newton method, Steffensen iteration, Levenberg Marquardt iteration and fixed-point iteration-are compared and analyzed. Experimental analysis is carried out using deep-sea test data from the South China Sea. The results show that the computational efficiency of the Steffensen iteration method is improved by 27.80 % compared with the Newton iteration method.

title: Up-down Line Current Ratio-based Fault Location Method for Fully Parallel Autotransformer (AT) Traction Power Network
authors: Mekdes Tadese Admas, Guosong Lin
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The fully parallel autotransformer (AT) power supply method is a key approach to enhancing the power supply capacity of traction networks. Fault location is an important guarantee for the reliable operation of the traction power supply system. The up-down line current ratio fault location method is one currently applied in high-speed electrified railway systems. Traditionally, the up-down line current ratio method can be used for the power supply section that is end-parallel. In the AT traction network, the up-down line current ratio method is adopted in the section of the traction substation (SS) to the autotransformer post (ATP). In the second section of the traction network (ATP-SP), direct measurement of line current is impossible, which makes the up-down line current ratio method difficult to implement. In this article, by analyzing the traction network during short-circuit fault occurrences in the second section of a unilateral fully parallel AT supply traction network, the currents in section ATP-SP are calculated. The currents were obtained using measurements at the traction substation (SS) and ATP. However, the current needs to be calculated based on the synchronized data at both stations. A calculation method for the up-down line current ratio and a current wiring method are derived when the fault point is in the ATP-SP section. Then, the relationship between the up-down line current ratio and fault distance from the AT station to the fault position is obtained. Finally, a MATLAB simulation is conducted to verify the proposed method for various fault scenarios.

title: Comparative Analysis of 4:2 Compressor for Lightweight CNN-Based Face Recognition
authors: Rashmita Karna, Raghunandan Swain, Dinesh Kumar Dash
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The objective of an emerging paradigm known as approximation computing is to enhance the power and area of convolutional neural network applications while accepting errors within certain limit. These letters define a novel lightweight CNN model for face recognition applications. The CNN approach uses a variety of pre-existing multipliers to lower the computational complexity during the partial product reduction stage. The result shows the best 8-bit multiplier architecture improve the power and PDP by 56 % and 33 % in compression to precise multiplier design. Furthermore, the proposed CNN model is validated using existing multiplier designs that offer reasonable accuracy.

title: XAI-Driven Deep Neural Network Approach for Synthetic Image Identification
authors: Jayanti Rout, Ashutosh Soni, Diyotima Mondal, Mrutyunjaya Sathua, Surendra Kumar Nanda
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The wide availability of deepfake generation models has led to the amalgamation of fake media with the original ones. It raises questions about the validity of images on the internet. Deepfakes pose threats to society, including financial losses. Traditional and machine learning detection measures are infeasible due to the inability to detect them efficiently. This led to a shift to Deep Learning (DL) approaches. However, the black-box nature of their prediction inhibits their real-life deployment. To address it, this study proposes a custom convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture to detect deepfake images and provide explainability of decisions. Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) is used to visualize the region-of-interest that affects decision-making the most. The classifier is organized into four convolutional blocks of 64,128,256, and 512 filters, respectively. A residual connection is added to the final convolutional block to retain vital feature representations. Three fully connected layers are used for classification in 512, 128, and 2 neurons. Various callbacks and training optimization mechanisms ensure a smooth training process without overfitting. The 140K real and fake faces dataset is used to train and evaluate the model. It achieved a test accuracy and AUC of 98.5% and 0.9987, respectively. Various other near-ideal parameters with minimal error rate demonstrate the efficacy of the model. This model presents a computationally balanced architecture to detect deepfake images efficiently. Its domain-agnostic design ensures easy adoption.

title: Object Detection and Classification in Images Using YOLO Algorithm
authors: K. Shankar, Y. Sunanda, Y. Pavan Kumar Reddy, M. Ravi Kishore, B. GangaDevi, M. Anusha
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Object Detection and classification are aspects that are used in computer vision, a key to making machines comprehend and interpret visual data. This project is aimed at the application of a strong and real time. Object detecting and classification system based on the YOLO algorithm. YOLO is a deep learning that is state-of-the-art framework which is characterized by high speed and sensitivity in identifying several physical objects in one picture. This is approached in the YOLO algorithm problem by splitting up the picture into a grid and at the same time bounding box prediction with corresponding class probabilities with the use of a single neural network pass. This standardized architecture plays an important role as it increases the speed of detection with competitive accuracy. The given system is trained by using pre-existing benchmark datasets like COCO and PASACAL VOC, which contain various object types and complicated scenes. Evaluation metrics including precision, recall and mAP are employed to rigorously assess the performance of the model. This work demonstrates the practical applicability of deep learning-based object detection in domains such as automated surveillance, intelligent transportation systems, robotics and smart environments, offering a foundation for further advancements in real-time visual understanding

title: Study on the Vertical Distribution and Dominant Factors of Atmospheric Absorption Coefficients Based on Ground-Based Microwave Radiometers
authors: Peng Wu, Kairui Xing, Changzhe Wu, Hanze Wu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The microwave radiometer is a passive remote sensing instrument, and this study utilizes the MP-3000A groundbased microwave radiometer as the experimental platform. Atmospheric forward modeling based on the microwave radiometer enables the calibration of brightness temperature accuracy, while the absorption coefficient determines the computational precision of the forward modeling. In actual highaltitude sounding, atmospheric parameters exhibit significant nonlinear fluctuations rather than constant changes, which markedly affect the accuracy of absorption coefficient calculations. Vertical variations in absorption coefficient characteristics and the influence of dominant factors as affected by atmospheric parameters. This paper constructs a physical model that maps atmospheric parameters, frequency, and altitude to the absorption coefficients of water vapor and oxygen. The experiment utilized sounding and microwave radiometer measured data from Taizhou, Zhejiang, between 2011 and 2012. First, a comparative experiment was designed using the controlled variable method to investigate the effects of atmospheric parameters on variations in the absorption coefficient. Second, combined with refined stratification, the study provides an in-depth analysis of the vertical evolution patterns of the absorption coefficient. The experiment concludes that the macroscopic vertical decrease of the oxygen absorption coefficient is dominated by pressure, while sudden temperature fluctuations in the low-to-medium layers directly manifest as nonlinear oscillations on the absorption profile. In contrast, the attenuation of the water vapor absorption coefficient is highly dependent on changes in water vapor density, exhibiting significant seasonal characteristics with a summer peak and a winter trough.

title: Industrial Anomaly Detection Integrating Graph Attention Mechanism with Graph Deviation Network
authors: Ming Wan, Rui Zhu, Taofeng Shi, Mengqi Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: ICSs (Industrial Control Systems) are gradually transforming towards networking and intelligence, which expose them to extremely severe security threats. To effectively identify cyberattacks, this paper proposes a novel industrial anomaly detection approach which organically integrate the graph attention mechanism with GDN (Graph Deviation Network). Furthermore, by incorporating GRU (Gated Recurrent Unit) and multi-head attention mechanism to enhance the robustness of feature representation, a robust feature extraction algorithm based on the improved graph attention mechanism is introduced to accurately depict the dynamic variation patterns of industrial traffics. Additionally, to strengthen the detection capability, an applicable anomaly detection model based on GDN is designed, whose hyperparameters are optimized by the improved Bayesian optimization algorithm. Finally, we utilize the typical SWaT datasets to conduct experimental evaluations, and the comparative results demonstrate that the proposed approach can exhibit competitive detection performance across three evaluation metrics and maintain a relatively stable detection accuracy.

title: AI-based Early Alzheimer's Disease Detection using Deep Learning
authors: Pavithra E, Kathirvel R, Subramani P, Sivapraveen R, Sivasurya S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Alzheimer disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative condition that causes memory impairment, cognitive impairment, and behavioral alterations. Early identification can help in providing better care to the patient and reduce the rate of disease development. The conventional diagnostic techniques rely on manual analyses of MRI and PET images that are time-consuming and prone to human errors. In order to deal with these issues, the proposed project is an Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based Alzheimer Detecting System whereby an advanced form of deep learning algorithms makes the diagnosis automated. Image-based diagnosis relies on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs) whereas the sequential data are processed using the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) networks to resolve the patterns over time. To improve accuracy and decrease computing time, the architecture combines preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification modules with publicly available datasets trained with the help of transfer learning. An interface based on Flask or Django is a secure web-based interface allowing clinicians to upload data, view scans, and diagnostic results. The privacy of the data has been secured by encrypted storage and security systems that comply with the healthcare regulations. Also, there are cognitive training modules that will help the patients and caregivers. Altogether, the AI-based system suggested is an efficient, secure, and intelligent platform to detect and support the patients with early Alzheimer.

title: AI-Driven Economic Benefit Measurement Model of Data Assets in Typical Scenarios of the Power Industry
authors: Yingdong Song, Yufei Li, Xiaojie Ji, Pengcheng Guo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: With artificial intelligence (AI) and data elements recognized as new production factors, the power industry faces the challenge of transforming vast operational data into measurable economic assets. This study develops an AI-driven economic benefit measurement model and a Five-Dimensional Integrated Maturity Evaluation System covering business, compliance, benefit, cost, and data dimensions. Using Anhui Power as a case study, multiple AI application scenarios—such as intelligent voice fee collection, precision maintenance, refined equipment management, and digital line loss control—are empirically analyzed. Results show that AI-enabled data assets generate both internal value (cost reduction, efficiency improvement, and risk control) and external value (data services and algorithmic products). A significant positive correlation is found between application maturity and economic benefit. The proposed model provides a quantitative framework for valuing data assets and supports data capitalization and marketization in the power industry.

title: Securing Critical Infrastructure Networks Using Blockchain-Assisted Artificial Intelligence: A Survey
authors: Nordine Akki, Ait Madi Abdessalam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper surveys the integration of Blockchain and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to enhance the security of critical infrastructure against increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. It demonstrates how Blockchain provides a decentralized and immutable ledger for reliable traceability, while AI enables proactive detection and automated response to anomalies. This synergy is illustrated through architectures such as Blockchainassisted federated learning, which preserves privacy, and hybrid explainable AI systems for transparent intrusion detection. While promising for domains like smart grids and $\mathbf{5 G}$ telecommunications, this approach still faces challenges related to scalability, interoperability, and regulatory frameworks for largescale deployment.

title: A Hybrid Neural Network Model for Short-Term Regional Tropospheric Delay Prediction
authors: Ruishan Zhao, Zeren Gao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid neural network model integrating a temporal convolutional network (TCN), a bidirectional long short-term memory network (BiLSTM), and an attention mechanism to address insufficient local fluctuation feature extraction and inadequate temporal dependency modeling in short-term regional zenith tropospheric delay (ZTD) prediction, which limit prediction accuracy and recursive forecasting stability. Based on GNSS observation data from 18 reference stations of the Hong Kong SatRef network during 2021-2022, a ZTD reference series with a temporal resolution of 1 h was constructed, and historical ZTD, temporal information, and station spatial information were used as input features. One-step prediction and 1-24 h multi-step recursive prediction experiments were conducted on this dataset, and the proposed model was compared with UNB3M, GPT3W, BPNN, and LSTM. The results show that the proposed model achieves an RMSE of 4.793 mm and an MAE of 2.993 mm in one-step prediction, while the RMSE in 24 h recursive prediction is 48.321 mm, outperforming the comparison models overall. The ablation study further shows that each module contributes to reducing the prediction error. These results indicate that, under the dataset and experimental settings of this study, the proposed model improves the accuracy of short-term regional ZTD prediction and shows good recursive forecasting stability.

title: Hybrid PSO–ELM-Based Clinical Decision Support for Heart Disease Diagnosis
authors: Alaa Sheta, Sara Elsir Ahmed, Walaa H. Elashmawi, Abdel Karim M. Baareh, Nastaran D. Pahnehkolaee
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are still one of the foremost causes of death globally. An urgent need for an accurate premature diagnosis is essential for efficient treatment and avoidance. The goal of this research is to introduce our initial idea for developing a hybrid intelligent diagnosis model that integrates the Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) with Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to enhance diagnosis accuracy for heart disease. To address the problems of random initialization, the PSO algorithm is used to optimize the ELM’s input biases and weights. Experiments using the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset evaluated the performance of traditional ELM and the proposed PSO-ELM model. Results indicate that integrating PSO for parameter tuning yields significant improvements across evaluation metrics. The traditional ELM recorded an accuracy of 0.8595 on the training set and 0.8525 on the test set, while the PSO-ELM model achieved up to 0.9174 in training and 0.8852 in testing. Enhancements were also noted in precision, recall, and F1-score, with the F1-score increasing from 0.8768 for traditional ELM to over 0.92 for several PSO configurations.

title: A Security-Aware Graph-Based Learning Framework for Joint Task Scheduling and Resource Allocation in Cloud Computing
authors: Salam Al-E’Mari, Yousef Sanjalawe, Fadi Fataftah
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Cloud infrastructures face the challenge of efficiently managing diverse task workloads while meeting performance, energy, and security constraints concurrently. Task scheduling and resource allocation are core to this problem, and they are intricately coupled, especially when dealing with workflow-based applications that involve complex task dependencies. On the other hand, as cloud data sensitivity increases, there is a need to implement security solutions with non-negligible computational costs, making this problem even more challenging. The current state of the art involves determining task scheduling, resource allocation, and security enforcement as separate optimization processes, making this problem difficult to adapt and globally efficient. The paper introduces a "Security-Aware Graph-Based Learning Framework" to jointly address task scheduling and resource allocation in cloud computing environments. The proposed solution uses a graph-based system model that incorporates task dependencies and resource interactions as an explicit graph structure. The proposed solution uses a centralized decision policy learned through a simultaneous optimization of task mapping, resource allocation, and security-level decisions, with overall efficiency, energy, SLS agreement, and security as core goals. The proposed approach incorporates security awareness into its optimization process, accounting for task sensitivity and virtual machine trust levels.

title: A Voting Classifier Approach to Bloom’s Taxonomy: Integrating BERT and Large Language Models for Questions Automatic Classification
authors: Mohamed Amine Ouassil, Mohammed Jebbari, Rabia Rachidi, Hassan Razouki, Bouchaib Cherradi, Abdelhadi Raihani
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The application of Bloom’s Taxonomy is key to education, especially in developing educational objectives, curricula, and assessment. It helps teachers in formulating the learning outcomes at different levels of cognition and beneath fundamental knowledge retention to advanced thinking skills, such as analysis, evaluation, and creation; it covers everything. The use of Natural Language Processing and Deep Learning algorithms for question classification during exam creation would make the objective clear, minimize the time required, and provide a well-balanced assessment. This research proposes a hybrid model combining Discriminative AI with Generative AI using a voting classifier. This ensemble integrates an advanced Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) model with Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT). For this reason, we finetuned two models based on GPT architecture. The proposed approach showed better performance compared to models in literature and the accuracy reached 89.18%. The proposed method shows the benefits of combining discriminative deep learning architectures with Large Language Models (LLMs) to accurately classify educational texts.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Approaches for Personalized Product Recommendation in Online Retail Systems
authors: Dilip Patel
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The high contribution of customized recommendation systems to the improvement of customer satisfaction and interaction has been identified by the fastexpanding e-commerce platforms. This research paper outlines how a new Product Recommendation System has been developed and evaluated to take into account the latest methods of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to make personal product recommendations. This system uses machine learning (ML) and the use of LLMs to identify the numerical patterns and the meaning of the interactions of the users with the products and the description of the products. The behavioral information coupled with the contextual understanding of the language enables the model to provide the most applicable and valuable recommendations. The experiment’s outcomes are promising, as the Logistic Regression model’s accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score all reach up to 0.9985 on the hold-out test set. The comparison with the already introduced models: Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB), Random Forest (RF), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), RoBERTa, and VGG19 that an F1-score between 57.81 and 87.93, demonstrates consistent performance improvements rather than absolute superiority proving the strength of the proposed system in accuracy and generalization. This observable improvement identifies the strength of the recommendation task combination of semantic similarity and hybrid filtering.

title: Smart Learning or Smart Risks? AI in Indian University: A Systematic Review
authors: Shrikant Upadhyay, Neetu Anand, Kumar Gaurav, Binita Roshima Hinz, Binod Kumar, Sampurna Mandal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: This paper examines both the prospective nature of AI (i.e., smart learning) as well as its retrospective nature (i.e., smart risks) in Indian universities and explores the broader question of whether AI supports smart learning or has created smart risks that negatively affect the overall ecosystem of higher education. In doing so, the paper draws on relevant empirical studies, policy documentation, and academic literature; analyses pertinent pedagogical, ethical, and institutional impacts associated with the implementation of AI specifically in the context of the Indian educational system and the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020; and investigates whether AI will help to address some of the challenges (e.g., the need for larger class sizes and learner diversity) faced by higher education institutions or will instead exacerbate inequities and undermine the level of trust individuals have in higher education organizations if there are no regulations controlling their use or there are inequities in access to AI technologies. As a means of supporting the establishment of sustainable, inclusive, and ethical innovations within the realm of higher education in India, the report points out that the implementation of AI will need to be done responsibly, transparently and in a contextually appropriate manner.

title: Detection Anomaly Attacks For IoT Devices using Machine Learning and Deep Learning
authors: Mohamed Bachar, Azeddine Khiat, Kamal El Guemmat
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: IoT devices are now a natural part of everyday life, supporting a wide range of applications in areas such as healthcare, industry, marketing, medicine, and education. While these technologies make our lives easier in many ways, they also introduce serious security challenges, as many IoT devices handle sensitive data and operate with limited protection mechanisms. As a result, they are increasingly vulnerable to cyber threats such as malware and distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks. In this work, we explore the use of anomaly detection techniques based on machine learning and deep learning as a practical approach to improving IoT security. We evaluate several methods, namely Isolation Forest, Support Vector Machine, Autoencoder, and a hybrid model that combines Isolation Forest with an Autoencoder. The results show that all approaches are effective in detecting anomalous behavior, with Isolation Forest achieving an accuracy of 0.95, and both SVM and Autoencoder reaching 0.98. The best performance is obtained with the hybrid approach, which achieves an accuracy of 0.99, demonstrating its strong potential for reliable and efficient anomaly detection in IoT networks.

title: Real-Time Multimodal Sign Language to Speech Translation System
authors: B. Rama Subba Reddy, D.Ganesh, Gadwala Shaik Mahammad Abdulla, Gollapudi Venkata Nandini, Gantla Ram Satish, Gurram Chenchu Rakesh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Communication barriers between non-signers and those with hearing impairments continue to be a significant social issue. Rich, visual-spatial languages, sign languages rely on intricate body language, facial expressions, and hand gestures. Conventional sign language translation systems frequently lack grammatical fluency, concentrate on discrete gestures, or are unable to function in real time. The Real-Time Multimodal Sign Language to Speech Translation System described in this paper converts continuous sign language gestures into natural-sounding speech and grammatically correct English text. Real-time video capture, multimodal feature extraction using Media Pipe Holistic, temporal gesture modeling using a Transformer Encoder, sequence decoding using Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC), grammatical refinement using a BERT-based language model, and neural speech synthesis using Taco Tron 2 and Wave Glow are all integrated into the suggested system. The system enables seamless communication by displaying refined English text and generating clear audio output, demonstrating high accuracy, fluency, and low latency. The system achieves a Word Error Rate (WER) of 9%, multimodal recognition accuracy of 89%, and average end-to-end latency of 150 ms on a continuous sign language dataset.

title: Secure Software-Defined Networking (SDN) Using ML-Driven Threat Prediction
authors: Balaji Salem Balasundram, Yogesh Ramaswamy, Balpreet Singh, Jaishankar Bhatt, Prabhakar Bhandari, Soumya George
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Our model is a new, technical, approach to securing Software-Defined Networking (SDN) with the help of ML-based threat prediction that goes out of the traditional reactive defenses. Our system is based on an adversarial-sensitive ensemble and small dynamic bottom-up continual learning prediction of an enriched flow-level telemetry and protocol semantics by a spatio-temporal graph neural network (ST-GNN) supplemented by assets, protocol, or the topology akin to trace graphs. Threats are computed through a policy synthesizer on a controller-side that prints minimal but provably consistent flow rule and mitigation staged (rate limit, flow quarantine, selective reroute) requirements as OpenFlow rules and controlled by staged updates (fast-path) delivered by a controller into its path. Our prototype was applied to Open Daylight and tested in Mininet with both combined real and synthetic attack traces. Findings reveal an accuracy of detection of 96.1 percent, 2.5 percent false positive, mean prediction lead time of equality of 120ms and 82 percent reduction on effective attacks with no increase on over 94 percent base-line throughput; the CPU overhead of the controller was less than 7 percent. Outcome value: a high performance, scalable, deployable, low-latency predictive SDN security overlay that can significantly minimize both the mean time to mitigate and the overall attack surface exposure.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Decentralized Recruitment Platform with Verifiable Credentials and Privacy-Preserving Hiring
authors: Vishal Sharma, Akshay Sharma, Ansh Kumar, Abhishek Raj, Harsh Gautam, Saksham Rana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The conventional online recruitment systems depend heavily on centralized systems which often create problems in transparency, resume fraud, data monopoly, and low levels of trust between employers and applicants. As a solution to such shortcomings, this paper considers a blockchain-based decentralized recruitment system, combining verifiable credentials, decentralized identity (DID), and hiring technology that respects privacy. The system suggested uses smart contracts to make the recruitment process a fully automated procedure (job posting, application, credential verification, and hiring decisions) that does not rely on centralized intermediaries. Candidate credentials (including education, certifications, and work experience) are cryptographically signed by authorized issuers, and will be stored as hashes on the blockchain, which cannot be altered, whereas documentation is stored in the decentralized storage (IPFS). To enhance privacy, the platform uses selective disclosure and zero-knowledge proofs (ZKP) features that also allow applicants to reveal their knowledge of skills without revealing sensitive personal data. The architecture also introduces DAO-based governance and score of reputation to increase responsibility of employers, credit issuers and applicants. Experimental testing using synthetic recruitment data proves that it has higher reliability in recruiting, with 91% accuracy in catching resume fraud compared to 78% with conventional applicant tracking system and has reasonable transaction latency and growth capability with a hybrid on-chain off-chain architecture. The findings indicate that the use of blockchain in the recruitment systems can significantly positively contribute to the transparency, authenticity of credentials, and trust in the modern hiring ecosystems.

title: ZoneSep: A Lightweight End-to-End Neural Beamformer with Post-mask Decoder for In-vehicle Multi-zone Speech Separation
authors: Longjie Luo, Junnan Wu, Lichun Fan, Zhenbo Luo, Jian Luan, Qingyang Hong, Lin Li
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Recently, the task of in-vehicle multi-zone speech separation (IMSS) has attracted significant interest. However, a persistent issue in this area is the cross-zone leakage problem. To address this, we present ZoneSep, a lightweight, fully end-to-end neural beamformer equipped with a post-mask decoder to suppress leakage effectively. Additionally, we propose a novel loss function, SC-SI-SDR loss, which enables simultaneous optimization of both non-silent and silent zones. Experiments on the IMSS dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our approaches, showing that ZoneSep significantly outperforms recent advanced methods while requiring only 0.65M parameters and 4.02 GMACs. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/EeLLJ/ZoneSep/</uri>.

title: MobileROS: A Wireless-Native Robot Operating System for Mobile Robotics
authors: Boyi Liu, Qianyi Zhang, Yongguang Lu, Jianhao Jiao, Jagmohan Chauhan, Wen Wu, Jun Zhang, Dimitrios Kanoulas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Robotics
abstract: The increasing deployment of mobile robots in dynamic outdoor environments necessitates robotic systems capable of maintaining reliability amidst fluctuating wireless connectivity. While the Robot Operating System (ROS) has established itself as the de facto standard for such networked robotics, its abstraction of communication as an opaque, besteffort utility creates a critical bottleneck: it fails to leverage physical layer (PHY) information, resulting in degraded performance and unreliable execution in fluctuating networks. To address this, this paper presents MobileROS, a wireless-native robot operating system that transforms wireless communication from an external service into a core system resource. Grounded in the Symbiotic Paradigm, MobileROS establishes a bidirectional exchange where network conditions inform robotic decisions and mission requirements guide network resource allocation. Based on service mesh principles and domain-driven design, our architecture implements a Hub-Engines-Cells (HEC) model. It features a central Hub for global optimization, three specialized engines (the Radio Information Engine, the Cross Domain Engine, and the Physical Adaptive Engine) for crosslayer intelligence, and distributed Cells as functional units. A key mechanism, Application-Driven Bidirectional Dynamic Slicing, allows robots to actively reconfigure network resources based on semantic urgency, transforming the robot from a passive observer into an active network controller. We systematically evaluate MobileROS across three cities (London, Hong Kong, and Shenzhen) in five scenarios: distributed visual SLAM, cross-domain LiDAR perception, V2X autonomous driving, hybrid multi-robot collaboration against WebRTC baselines, and partition recovery validating CAP-theorem-aware failsafe mechanisms. Results demonstrate that MobileROS maintains significantly more stable performance than standard ROS in mobile wireless deployments.We provide implementation details at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/MobileROS</uri>.

title: Uniformity Analysis in the WebGPU Shading Language
authors: James Lee-Jones, John Wickerson, Alastair F. Donaldson
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
