authors: Oussama Bia, Jaouad El Gharfaoui, Yassine El Alami, Fatima Ait Sidihammou, Charaf Hajjaj, Fatima Chanaa, Mohssin Aoutoul, Bachir Benhala, Elhadi Baghaz
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The rapid expansion of photovoltaic (PV) power installations worldwide has made reliable operation and maintenance of these systems critically important. While PV systems offer significant environmental and economic advantages, their long-term performance is continuously threatened by a wide range of defects and faults that can silently reduce power output and shorten system lifetime. This paper presents a comprehensive review of defect detection and fault diagnosis methods for PV power plants. A structured taxonomy of defects is first presented, covering physical defects such as cracks, delamination, and corrosion; environmental defects including partial shading, soiling, and hotspots; and electrical faults on both the DC and AC sides of the system. The main fault detection approaches are then reviewed, encompassing electrical-based methods, visual and thermal inspection techniques, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and machine learning algorithms, and IoT-enabled remote monitoring platforms. The growing role of AI in enabling automated and scalable fault diagnosis is highlighted as a central theme. Failure prognosis strategies and the key challenges facing the field are also discussed. The findings provide a consolidated reference for researchers and practitioners working toward more intelligent and cost-effective PV monitoring solutions. Unlike prior reviews that address imaging, electrical, or deep-learning methods in isolation, this work uniquely integrates all detection families electrical, visual-thermal, AI-based, and IoT-enabled - alongside failure prognosis and a structured defect taxonomy, providing a unified and actionable framework for the field.

title: City Scope A Smart Parking Management System for Real-Time Vehicle Detection and Tracking
authors: Prakhar Kaushik, Kovid Kaushik, Mrinal Kumar Das, Prashant Singh, Himani Garg
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The paper describes a vision-based and low-cost smart parking system that can track vehicles in real-time, identify parking-lot occupancy, and accurately locate vehicles without ground-based sensors. ESP32-CAM is used to offer automatic number plate recognition (ANPR) at the entrance and exit points, and the overhead cameras attached to Raspberry Pi Zero 2W are used to offer 24/7 monitoring. The optimised version of TensorFlow Lite on the YOLOv5-Nano model is used to identify vehicles and slot occupancy by applying a region-of-interest (ROI) mapping strategy. Correlation between vehicle identities is done by time-stamping, when a vehicle enters and leaves, and matching by visual features is done to associate vehicles with the parking slots. Planar homography also transforms the image-plane coordinates into spatial positions in the real world that could be used to estimate the distance correctly and guide the vehicle via a web-based dashboard. Using the SRT protocol, secure and low-latency video streaming is possible. The findings of the experiments indicate that the accuracy of occupancy detection is over 92, vehicle-to-slot is over 95, and the localization error is between 0.25 and 0.65 meters, which proves the validity of low-cost edge-AI systems to intelligent parking systems.

title: An Adaptive Encryption-Inference Two-Layer Security Algorithm Framework for Scientific and Technological Achievement Databases
authors: Wang Jinsong
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive encryption-inference two-layer security algorithm framework. The framework is designed to address the structural contradiction between open sharing and privacy protection in scientific and technological achievement databases, as well as the shortcomings of traditional encrypted query mechanisms in terms of access pattern leakage, dynamic update security, and result verifiability. The framework divides the security mechanism into an encryption protection layer and an inference verification layer at the architectural level. This process achieves unified protection of data confidentiality, query privacy, and result credibility through loose coupling and collaboration. In terms of methodological design, the encryption layer constructs a hybrid encryption model based on the fusion of AES-GCM and Feistel structures. This model achieves differentiated protection for high-frequency index fields and large-scale data volumes. Simultaneously, this research designs a dynamically updatable symmetric searchable encryption (DSSE) index structure. This structure introduces a tag perturbation and state-driven key evolution mechanism, which effectively resists statistical analysis and access pattern inference attacks. Furthermore, a sensitivity-driven adaptive desensitization function is used to achieve dynamic risk control during the data writing stage. The inference verification layer constructs a secure computation pipeline based on a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE). This study integrates query semantic parsing, N-Gram-based behavioral modeling, and anomaly detection mechanisms, and introduces a query consistency verification method based on Authentication Data Structure (ADS). The experiment was conducted using a database of scientific and technological achievements containing 300,000 multi-source heterogeneous data points. The experimental environment was deployed in a hybrid cloud environment supporting Intel SGX. Results show that compared to the DSSE+TEE method, the proposed model only increases query latency by approximately 4.1%–5.2%, while throughput decreases by only 5%–8%. Significantly, in terms of security, the success rates of query replay, frequency analysis, and access pattern inference attacks are lowered to 3.8%, 4.9%, and 6.3%, respectively; these numbers show an average reduction of more than 65% when compared to the baseline method; additionally, the tamper detection rate in result integrity verification reaches 99.3%, with a false positive rate of 1.9%. This represents an improvement of approximately 18% in the detection capability compared to the traditional Merkle method, while simultaneously reducing computational overhead by approximately 38%.

title: SCHEMEFORGE-AI: An Intelligent Framework for Automated Scheme Analysis and Recommendation using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Niventh P, Vijayalakshmi N, Swathi G, Pavithran K, Prasanth B, Jeyaram G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: SCHEMEFORGE-AI is a smart scheme analysis and recommendation system that is aimed at automatically identifying, classifying, and delivering welfare and service schemes to customers in a personalized manner based on the use of artificial intelligence. Most of the traditional scheme dissemination systems use manual rule based filtering and hard disk databases, which usually lead to lack of accessibility, lack of personalization and inefficient decision making. To overcome these shortcomings, the suggested SCHEMEFORGE-AI approach combines natural language processing to extract important attributes of unstructured scheme documents with machine learning models to classify schemes with a hybrid eligibility-matching system that takes learned patterns and rules together with rule-based validation. The scheme attributes are sampled with user profiles in a dynamic manner to produce rank and explainable recommendations. It is experimentally proved that SCHEMEFORGE-AI performs with a classification accuracy of 93.8% and recommendation accuracy of more than 91.7% at large-scale workload, which is significantly better than traditional rule-based systems. The accuracy of the recommendations is determined as the number of the correct matching schemes of the eligible schemes divided by the number of the recommended schemes.

title: PaudhRog: Plant Leaf Disease Detection using Transfer Learning with InceptionResNetV2
authors: Sheenam Middha, Abhay Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Agricultural world is largely worried about reduced crop yields due to infestation by pests and diseases. The challenge that is facing the farmers is that they have had a hard time trying to detect and diagnose plant diseases especially at the early stages on the leaves before they appear in the fruits. To meet this requirement, the new product PaudhRog, relies on artificial intelligence (AI) to recognize the illness of plant leaves at an early age with special attention to crops like wheat, tomato, apple, and several tomato and other plants. The research will be able to surpass the admirable 99.5 percent accuracy rate using the potent InceptionResNetV2 model. To achieve this, such strategic measures as reducing the imbalances of datasets through oversampling and under sampling, integrating transfer learning with InceptionResNetV2, and systematic application of TensorFlow and Flask in Python have been used. It enhances its ability to identify the disease using high quality photos in the huge plant village collection that has a broad selection of plant leaves. Since the contribution of the meteorological conditions to the crop productivity is enormous, the weather widget on the website is not only usable but also provides the farmers with the necessary information about environmental conditions that are critical. PaudhRog aims to become an automated disease detector that seeks to be a proactive partner in safeguarding agricultural output and a major stakeholder in the global food security.

title: Effect of nTiC addition on the performance improvement of pyramid solar still - A Renewable Energy Focus on Sustainability
authors: B. Deepanraj, L. Anantha Raman, Gaydaa AlZohbi, N. Senthilkumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: A square pyramid solar still (PSS) with a single basin was fabricated using galvanized iron absorber coated matte black and insulated with thermocol for desalination of brackish salt water. Experiments were conducted in Chennai $\left(13.0843^{\circ} \mathrm{N}, 80.2705^{\circ} \mathrm{E}\right)$ using basin water depths (WD) of $1-5 \mathrm{~cm}$ dispersed with titanium carbide (TiC) nanoparticles with concentrations of $\mathbf{0 - 1} \mathbf{w t. \%}$. nTiC were dispersed evenly using magnetic stirrer and ultrasonication methods. Adding nTiC consistently enhanced thermal and desalination performance relative to the base case (without nTiC) by improving photothermal absorption and heat distribution. Across all trials, productivity increased from 0.186 to $0.812 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ (4.37 folds higher), while water and glass temperatures increased up to $59.18^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $57.36^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, respectively. Exergy output rose from 30.2 to $158.4 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{h}$ (5.25 folds higher). The best overall performance occurred at $0.5 \mathrm{wt}. \% \mathrm{nTiC}$ and 3 cm water depth, yielding 0.812 $\mathrm{kg} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ productivity and $158.4 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{h}$ exergy, demonstrating an optimal balance between thermophysical gains and high-loading penalties such as agglomeration, viscosity rise, and optical shielding.

title: SecureCloudOpt: Risk-Constrained Hybrid Metaheuristic Optimization for Security-Aware VM Placement with Co-Residency Mitigation
authors: Hasan Kanaker, Hussam N. Fakhouri, Muhannad Alkhalaileh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Consolidating virtual machines (VMs) improves cloud efficiency but increases security exposure in multi-tenant infrastructures by amplifying co-residency risks and inherited host vulnerabilities. This paper studies a security-aware VM placement (S-VMP) optimization problem that jointly minimizes (i) host exposure, (ii) trust-gap between VM sensitivity and host security posture, and (iii) risky co-location among VM pairs, while also considering energy and inter-VM latency. We formulate S-VMP as a risk-constrained mixed-integer quadratic model and adopt a normalized weighted objective to compare solvers. To solve medium-to-large instances, we propose SecureCloudOpt (SCO), a hybrid optimizer that combines Differential Evolution exploration with greedy feasibility repair and placement-specific local search (migration and swap). We ly compare SCO with 11 popular optimizers—GA, discrete PSO, DE, GWO, WOA, Simulated Annealing, Tabu Search, Ant Colony Optimization, Artificial Bee Colony, Firefly Algorithm, and Teaching–Learning-Based Optimization—using instances at three scales. The reported results illustrate that SCO achieves the lowest objective values and the most stable convergence patterns, highlighting the value of repair-based constraint handling and local refinement in security-constrained placements.

title: ML in medical research: A bibliometric analysis of methodological evolution and clinical applications
authors: Duha Alsmadi, Gayle Prybutok
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Machine learning (ML) has increasingly become integral to medical research, yet comprehensive analyses of its evolution, applications, and research maturity remain limited. This study examines the temporal evolution of ML techniques in medical research, identifies dominant clinical application domains, and evaluates the functional maturity of ML implementations between 2019 and 2025. We conducted a bibliometric analysis of 20,000 journal articles retrieved from the Scopus database (2019-2025). Articles were analyzed for ML methodologies, clinical applications, and functional purposes using keyword extraction and topic modeling. Temporal trends were tracked to identify shifts in research focus and methodological approaches. Publications increased 7.4-fold from 663 in 2019 to 4,897 in 2025, demonstrating immense growth. Deep learning emerged as the second most frequent term (2,899 occurrences) after ML (5,448), indicating a substantial shift toward neural network architectures. Diagnosis and detection dominated functional applications (8,440 papers, 42%), followed by prediction and prognosis (5,053 papers, 25%). Medical imaging, particularly through convolutional neural networks (CNNs), represented a major application area (380 occurrences). Topic modeling revealed four dominant topics, while clustering analysis identified four research communities, showing accelerated growth from 2023 onward. Medical ML research has matured significantly, transitioning from classical algorithms to deep learning architectures, with diagnosis and detection remaining the primary focus. The field demonstrates increasing sophistication in methodology while expanding into diverse clinical domains, particularly precision medicine and healthcare analytics.

title: Numerical Analysis of Fin-Enhanced PCM Cooling Using Different Heat Sink Materials for Photovoltaic System
authors: Youness Bannour, Yassine El Alami, Hanane Chetnouf, Hayat Allam, Zineb Hekss, Ali Lamkaddem, Elhadi Baghaz, Bachir Benhala, Ahmed Faize
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This research examines the performance of photovoltaic (PV) phase change material (PCM) systems through numerical analysis of finned heat sink materials. To do this, the proposed model utilized the enthalpy-porosity method in ANSYS Fluent to simulate a multi-layer PV module with a PCM layer and embedded aluminium, copper, and steel heat sinks. Ultimately, the study found that the material of the heat sink dramatically affected the temperature distributions throughout the PV module, how the PCM melted, and the overall operating temperature of the PV module. Of all the materials tested, aluminium exhibited the most effective thermal regulation and resulted in an average PV temperature decrease of 6.62 K (approximately 2.05%) when compared to steel. Even though the steel configuration had the highest melt fraction of PCM due to greater thermal loading, this did not result in improved cooling performance, rather the electrical efficiency of PV modules with aluminium cooling increased from 11.51% to 11.90% (an approximate increase of 3.39% relative). The results of this study indicate that to achieve optimal efficiency in a PV-PCM system, it is critical to optimize thermal regulation of the PVs rather than prioritize maximum PCM melting.

title: Optimization-Enabled Information Management Architecture for Smart Digital Enterprises
authors: Pramod Muppala, Sruthi P, Seema Raj, Lavish Kansal, Neha Tripathi, Ruchita Singhania
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The digital evolution of contemporary businesses has accelerated, which has increased the pressure on smart information management that is able to process huge, heterogeneous, and real-time data. The conventional systems of information management have problems with scalability, ineffective resource deployment, data silos, and weak decision support. To overcome these issues, the current work presents an Optimization-Enabled Information Management Architecture (OE-IMA), which is Smart Digital Enterprise-specific. The suggested architecture would combine the latest optimization algorithms with machine learning-based analytics to coordinate the dynamic resources, improve the quality of information, and control the autonomous workflow. OE-IMA uses a layered architecture that comprises of intelligent data ingestion, federated storage, modules of adaptive optimization and real-time analytics engines so that there can be reliable, secure, and context-aware use of information by distributed enterprise environments. The optimization system constantly analyzes the metrics of performance in the system and reallocates resources to ensure that the latency is minimized, the operational costs are minimized, and the efficiency of extraction of knowledge is maximized. The experimental design has shown that OE-IMA can make a large contribution to the accuracy of the decision-making process, the resilience of the enterprise, and the sustainability of operations, which establishes a scalable basis of next-generation smart enterprise ecosystems.

title: Improving Avocado Detection in Isochromatic Foliage using YOLOv11 and Piecewise Linear Contrast Stretching
authors: Neneng Suyeni, Indrabayu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Automatic avocado fruit counting using UAV image analysis remains challenging due to overlapping conditions and isochromatic similarity between green fruits and surrounding foliage. This study integrates Piecewise Linear Contrast Stretching (PLCS) preprocessing with YOLOv11m to address these challenges. Three domain-specific PLCS presets were designed targeting fruit-leaf color separation, shadow detail recovery, and maximum object-background contrast. The dataset comprises 506 UAV images with 2,919 annotations from tropical Indonesian avocado orchards, divided 80:20 before augmentation. Experimental results demonstrate that the PLCS model achieves the highest mAP50-95 of 0.8970 and recall of 0.9890, while attaining the lowest values across all six \loss metrics compared to both baseline and standard augmentation models. Confusion matrix analysis confirms that PLCS produces only 6 false negatives out of 584 ground truth objects with a 0.51% counting error. Visual validation on unseen drone footage independently confirms the robustness of PLCS under real-world field conditions, supporting its practical applicability for precision agriculture yield estimation.

title: ThermoLine-Net: A Deep Learning–Based Algorithm for Overheating Detection in Transmission Lines
authors: Yizhao Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Overheating detection in transmission lines is essential for ensuring the safe and stable operation of power systems, as overheating may cause equipment damage and power outages. Traditional methods, including manual inspection and infrared imaging, often suffer from limited accuracy and poor adaptability to complex environments. To address these limitations, this paper proposes ThermoLine-Net, an improved YOLOv12-based framework for transmission line overheating detection. The model incorporates three key components: a Multi-Scale Heat Thermal Fusion (MSHTF) module to enhance cross-level feature interaction and improve small-target detection; an Environment-Enhanced Guided Attention (EEGA) mechanism to suppress environmental interference and highlight critical overheating features; and a Remote-Distance Micro-Heat Enhancer (RDMHE) to strengthen recognition of small, low-resolution overheating targets at long distances. Experimental results on a public power system dataset demonstrate that ThermoLine-Net achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 96.5%, validating its effectiveness and robustness in complex transmission scenarios.

title: Performance Simulation and Analysis of Bdsbas for Civil Aircraft Navigation
authors: Haoran Li, Jinyu Wang, Shuaiyong Zheng, Linxin Su, Ran Xu, Wenxin Xiao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of global air traffic, the Asia-Pacific region has become a core hub of dense civil aviation routes, imposing stringent regionalized requirements on satellite augmentation system performance. The Satellite-Based Augmentation Service (SBAS) of the BeiDou-3 Navigation Satellite System (BDS-3), with its Asia-Pacific-optimized constellation, is a key technology for improving navigation performance on regional dense routes. This paper proposes a BDSBAS-based civil aircraft navigation framework, to systematically evaluate its application potential across typical flight phases on the Beijing-Jinan-Shanghai trunk route, a typical Asia-Pacific dense civil aviation route. By virtue of high-precision differential correction, BDSBAS effectively improves the positioning accuracy of conventional satellite navigation and optimizes satellite geometric distribution for Asia-Pacific dense air routes. Simulation results show that, compared with standalone BDS-3 standard positioning, BDSBAS on this route increases the number of visible satellites by an average of 66.7%, optimizes Dilution of Precision (DOP) values with more stable spatial geometry, and stabilizes the vertical positioning accuracy of civil aircraft to better than 1.5 meters. This work quantifies the performance improvement law of BDSBAS on typical Asia-Pacific dense civil aviation routes, establishes a solid technical basis for full-phase precision navigation from en route flight to APV on regional dense routes, and thus promotes the practical application of BDSBAS in Asia-Pacific civil aviation.

title: PGT-GAN: Privacy-Gradient Tabular Generative Adversarial Network
authors: S. Moses Dian, R. Indhumathi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Publishing a dataset is essential for various research, data analysis, and the data mining process. The third parties can access this data to make informed decisions. Many of these released datasets contain highly sensitive information, including disease, diagnoses, treatment options, and symptoms. The publication of numerous sensitive information is not acceptable since it endangers people's privacy. The main focus of the proposed method is on ensuring that the released data is private and secure. Existing privacy-preserving data publication approaches for multiple sensitive attributes do not consider the fact that sensitive attribute values may have varying levels of sensitivity. To tackle this problem, we proposed a method, Privacy-Gradient Tabular Generative Adversarial Network (PGT-GAN). We developed ML-driven identification for Low-Risk Sensitive (LRS) and High-Risk Sensitive (HRS) security levels for different sensitive attribute values that had differing sensitivity requirements. Elliptic Curve encryption combined with sensitivity-adaptive Differential Privacy is used to encrypt and allocate an adaptive privacy budget to the extremely sensitive data. Dynamic k-anonymization is utilized during the publication of LRS data that respects privacy. An experimental evaluation is conducted against baseline methods, demonstrating that the proposed method achieves a better privacy-utility trade-off and significantly minimize the information loss of published data.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Causal Inference in Climate Science: A Novel Approach to Model Modeling Complex Interactions
authors: Preeti Sethi, Bikki Kumar, Ruchi, Puneet Garg, Parveen Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Considering the limitation imposed by the nonlinear interactions and the multidimensional nature of climate data, there is an urgent need to identify the cause and effect relationships in climate science in order to have accurate modeling and make sound policy-making decisions. To facilitate the sound causal inference, this paper presents a Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) based framework, where climate variables are represented by graph nodes, and interactions among them are represented by the edge. To infer causal relationships among variables, like temperature and precipitation, this framework uses GNNs with the Peter-Clark (PC) algorithm. The established meaningful causal associations are confirmed using the experiment that is performed on the NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis dataset and the suggested framework, in turn, provides a higher model accuracy.

title: Efficient Fine-Tuning Strategies for LLMs in Low-Resource Environments
authors: Satyanarayana Gudimetla, Gazal Sharma, Afsar Jahan, Deepa Mathew K, Anshu Malhotra, K. Adilakshmi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The adaptation of large language models (LLMs) to low-resource settings is still a significant challenge because of limited data and limited computational resources. The present paper suggests a new fine-tuning methodology using adaptive selection of subnetworks and synthetic curriculum augmentation to become able to learn effectively in the face of resource miniaturization. Rather than fine-tuning the entire parameter space, the method is dynamic and finds task-sensitive subnetworks at the token level and fine-tunes them selectively, much lowering memory and compute demands. To reduce the sparsity of data, a lightweight curriculum generator learns to synthesize successively more complex pseudo-samples using minimal supervision to inform stable and task-orientation adaptation. The method is tested on the WikiANN multilingual named entity recognition dataset, which inherently allows for experiments on low-resource on a variety of languages. Experimental results prove that the given strategy can attain a competitive performance with a significant reduction of training cost and data dependency in comparison with the traditional fine-tuning approaches. The results suggest the promise of structured sparsity with curriculum-constructive augmentation to be able to use LLM with a limited number of resources in a resource-constrained environment.

title: Performance Analysis of Equal Gain Combining and Maximal Ratio Combining over Correlated Weibull fading channels – A Review
authors: Kirandeep Kaur, Sankalp Thakur, Mohammad Irfanul Hasan, Shalini Singh, Abhay Sharma, Dipali Bansal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: This paper presents an analytical investigation of M-branch diversity receivers employing Equal-Gain Combining (EGC) and Maximal Ratio Combining (MRC) over correlated Weibull fading channels. The Weibull distribution is widely recognized for accurately modeling a broad range of fading environments. This work derives closed-form expressions for the mean output Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) for both EGC and MRC schemes. These derivations fully incorporate the impact of the Weibull fading parameter (β) and the inter-branch correlation (ρ). The analysis of these expressions reveals a critical distinction; the mean output SNR of the optimal MRC scheme is proven to be independent of both channel correlation and fading severity. Conversely, the sub-optimal EGC scheme's mean SNR is shown to be dependent on both factors, exhibiting an increase as correlation rises. This paper concludes that this apparent improvement is a misleading statistical artifact that masks a degradation in practical reliability.

title: Spectrum Sensing Integration and Cross-Layer Optimisation in Cognitive Radio Routing Protocol
authors: Neha Chaudhary, Geeta Yadav, Dipali Bansal, Rashima Mahajan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: In a cognitive radio system, efficient spectrum utilization can be achieved through spectrum sensing. In this research, by employing cooperative spectrum sensing with a double dynamic threshold, the performance of the energy detector has been improved. By considering the Additive White Gaussian Noise (AWGN) over a wideband frequency range of 54 MHz to 862 MHz system is evaluated and implemented in MATLAB.This research investigates the performance of different routing protocols for packet to delivery ratio, average end-to-end delay and routing overhead. Probability of detection of primary user is achieved under key performance metrics as packet delivery ratio, average end-to-end delay and routing overhead for the Reactive, proactive and hybrid routing protocols. Experimental outcomes indicate that increasing the number of samples significantly enhances Pd, with 32-QAM outperforming 16-QAM modulation. Superior detection performance is observed at –9 dB SNR in comparison to –15 dB and –5 dB, achieving nearly a 10% improvement in Pd for 16-QAM relative to existing Simulink-based energy detector models. Furthermore, a machine learning method has been incorporated to calculate the trust score for the awareness of the spectrum for the cognitive radio AODV protocol. Initially, for an interval of 10 secs. The ML classifier is trained and considered a logistic regression value of 0.5, and gets a trust score, i.e 0.5. Monte Carlo simulations are employed to validate the robustness and statistical reliability of the proposed model across numerous random channel realizations.

title: Optimal Financial Portfolio Management Using Deep Transfer Learning with Real-Time Financial Data in Cloud Computing Platform
authors: Shailesh Sayarmal Chaudhary
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Financial portfolio management is a critical task in the modern finance, which involves the skilful manipulation of large dynamic data sets in market settings in order to optimize returns and at the same time reduce risk. Conventional methodological frameworks are usually limited in their ability to capture sophisticated market dynamics as well as their ability to react to real time perturbations and hence constrain their forecast capabilities and effectiveness of decision making processes. The current research proposes a model that optimizes the utilization of deep transfer learning and real-time financial information, which is implemented on a cloud computing platform, to support intelligent management of portfolios. The salient financial features are extracted using DefenseNet and the convergence to the most informative subset of salient features is performed with the help of the enhanced bobcat optimization (EBO) algorithm. Then the finely-tuned features are added to a complex transfer learning model, which predicts the portfolio choices in the changing market environment; real-time U.S. market assets data are also exploited to support the presented framework. Practical experience proves that the methodology significantly enhances the predictive accuracy, augments portfolio returns, and is highly adaptable to market dynamics, which provide a sound and scalable solution to the modern financial decision-making in the domain of cloud-based systems.

title: Experimental Evaluation of SMOTE for Rare Cyberattack Detection in IoT Environments
authors: Abdelaaziz Nassiri, Pr Azeddine Khyat, Pr Kamal El Guemmat, Mohamed Aazi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The increasing deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) systems in critical sectors has expanded the attack surface of connected environments and intensified the need for effective intrusion detection mechanisms. However, IoT security datasets are typically characterized by high heterogeneity, limited feature consistency across devices, and severe class imbalance, which degrades the detection of rare attack classes. In this paper, we experimentally investigate the impact of the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) on the detection of rare cyberattacks and proxy zero-day scenario in IoT environments. Our framework combines multiple sources of security relevant data. We evaluate the performance of machine learning models, including Random Forest and XGBoost, under both original and rebalanced data distributions, considering classification performance and inference efficiency. The results demonstrate that using multimodal learning and the right rebalancing methods greatly enhances detection reliability, particularly for rare,uncommon or new threats.

title: Able Vista: A Technology-Driven Platform for Enhancing Learning Outcomes Among Special Learners
authors: Akash Goel, Aditya Arora, Aniket Gautam, Aditi Sharma, Navya Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: This paper will be discussing Able Vista which is an innovative platform developed to serve the specially abled students through the application of modern technology in ensuring that there is an improved and inclusive education. Able Vista offers a combination of personal learning resources, real-time assistance, and friendly interaction area to help students struggling with such challenges as stress, anxiety, and depression during their studies. It is built with React and TypeScript and Tailwind CSS to create a secure and responsive webpage, Next.js to create pages which load quickly and handle a large amount of information, and MongoDB or another database to store the information about a user in a safe manner. It also introduces Python based applications such as text classification models to enhance the content and text to speech applications to make the system more interactive and user friendly. The primary objective is to make the teenage students comprehend their emotional, social, and mental facets by utilizing intelligent self checking devices. As opposed to the antique and dreary systems providing them with the same form of learning, Able Vista creates a nurturing and helpful environment that shuns the stigma concerning inclusive schooling and provides them with the required means to help them develop. It fantasizes a world where intelligent and free screening systems are instrumental in empowering the youth in school and life..This work speaks to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, specifically SDG 4 (Quality Education) in terms of stimulating inclusive and accessible learning, and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities) in leveraging the possibilities of intelligent technologies towards the emotional, social and academic well being of specially abled learners.

title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence Applications on Visual Effects Workflows in Virtual Reality Animated Visual Presentations
authors: Mansour El Sherif, Maha Nasr Mohamed Mansour
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping how animated visual presentations are conceived, produced, and experienced, extending visual effects (VFX) workflows from linear timelines into adaptive, data-driven systems. This review synthesizes research and practice on AI adoption in animation and virtual reality (VR), emphasizing effects authoring, neural rendering, previsualization, compositing, and audience-facing interaction. We examine how AI-augmented interfaces accelerate ideation, enable embodied camera and staging decisions inside immersive workspaces, and support real-time evaluation of lighting, particles, volumetrics, and compositing cues using learned denoisers and neural approximations of offline renders. The manuscript is framed as a review and conceptual analysis rather than an empirical study. It maps how AI supports prediction, reconstruction, and constrained generation in VFX pipelines, while also identifying constraints that complicate deployment, including hallucinated detail, model bias, temporal inconsistency, data interoperability with established digital content creation pipelines, intellectual-property and provenance risks, and the need for repeatable, version-controlled generation. Practical mitigation strategies are summarized, including hybrid VR-desktop workflows, constraint-based generation, lightweight proxy assets, standardized export paths to offline renderers, and model/seed logging for reproducibility. The review concludes with research directions on perceptual metrics, collaborative multiuser editing with AI agents, and physically based real-time neural rendering for next-generation VFX in animation.

title: Classification of Breast Cancer Types Using Machine Learning
authors: Mustapha Riad, Ayoub Charef
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) is an aggressive subtype of breast cancer (BC), lacking targeted therapies, which complicates treatment. This study applied machine learning (ML) algorithms to gene expression data to distinguish TNBC from non-TNBC. Four ML methods; Support Vector Machines (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Naïve Bayes (NGB), and Decision Trees (DT) were evaluated using accuracy, recall, and specificity as performance metrics. SVM outperformed other models, achieving 90% accuracy, 87% recall, and 90% specificity. KNN followed closely with 87% accuracy, 76 % recall, and 88 % specificity, while NGB and DT exhibited lower recall rates. A 10-fold cross-validation approach confirmed the reliability of the classification results. This study demonstrates that SVM is particularly effective in distinguishing TNBC from non-TNBC using transcriptomic data, providing a promising approach for developing gene-based diagnostic tools. Future work should focus on larger datasets and refined feature selection methods to further improve performance.

title: Architectural Invariants for Designing Multi-Agent Energy Management Systems in Microgrids
authors: Yosra M’Zabiri, Abdelhadi Raihani, Mohamed Kissaoui, Atifi Youness, Ahmed Khayat, Khalili Tajeddine
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The growing integration of distributed renewable energy sources into microgrids increases the complexity of energy management systems (EMS) due to resource variability, demand uncertainty, and energy asset heterogeneity. Traditional EMS, which are mostly centralized and deterministic, have significant limitations in terms of flexibility, resilience, and scalability in these variable environments. This paper aims to design an intelligent hierarchical EMS architecture based on an intelligent multi-agent technique. It is an architecture based on intelligent agent ensuring distributed coordination and global orchestration of energy decisions in microgrids. The proposed architecture is centered on an explicit separation between agent-to-agent (A2A) coordination, dedicated to negotiation, cooperation, and event management mechanisms. The standardized access to data and services is provided by the Model Context Protocol (MCP), used for the integration of supervision, forecasting, and optimization tools. Each subsystem of the microgrid (generation, storage, loads, and network interface) is represented by a specialized autonomous agent, while a supervisor agent ensures the overall consistency of decisions. The study focuses on a detailed description of the proposed architecture and interaction flows, of the EMS. Also, properties of the proposed architecture are qualitatively examined in terms of modularity, robustness, and extensibility. Prospects for quantitative validation and experimental deployment are identified as future work.

title: A Vehicle-Mounted Measurement Method for Longitudinal Slope of Unclassified Roads Based on Adaptive Wavelet Denoising
authors: Guoqing Yang, Guoliang Zang, Ping Li, Wenjie Yu, Yong Luo, Xiyu Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: This paper proposes a vehicle-mounted method for measuring the longitudinal slope of unclassified roads using an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU). By constructing a mathematical model correlating IMU accelerometer signals with road slopes, static road parameters are inverted from dynamic sensor data. To address the issue of IMU signal noise induced by vehicle vibration on unclassified roads, a fuzzy adaptive wavelet denoising algorithm is designed, which adjusts the denoising threshold based on vehicle speed and road roughness, enabling effective noise elimination. Finally, real-vehicle experiments demonstrate that the proposed method can accurately reflect road slope values in the calculation of longitudinal slopes on unclassified roads.

title: Fiber Optic Temperature Sensors and Machine Learning-Fused Signal Demodulation Methods
authors: Zhanlu Zhang, Feng Xia, Bo Liu, Yulong Gu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Fabry-Perot (F-P) sensors based on polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS)-filled hollow-core fiber (HCF) frequently encounter challenges such as valley aliasing and nonlinear demodulation during wide-range temperature monitoring. This paper proposes MS-ISDNet, a multi-scale deep learning architecture designed for robust spectral demodulation. The model employs a dual-channel input to fuse raw spectra with first-derivative features, enabling the simultaneous extraction of fringe visibility and phase-shift information. The core architecture utilizes parallel Inception modules with varied convolutional kernel sizes ($1 \times 3,1 \times 11,1 \times 31$) to effectively identify both global and local spectral features, resolving valley aliasing while maintaining high-precision localization. Within a temperature range of $1^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $48^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, MS-ISDNet achieves a root mean square error (RMSE) of $0.1995^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and a coefficient of determination ($R^{2}$) of 0.9998. Furthermore, through training with data augmentation simulating physical interference, the model demonstrates superior robustness against noise and light source fluctuations.

title: Towards Sustainable Textile Manufacturing: Implementing Intelligent Predictive Maintenance in the Indian Context
authors: Hirendra Singh, Bhim Singh, Ramendra Singh Niranjan, Gaurav Kumar Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Indian textile industry currently facing problems like unexpected machine backdowns, wastage of energy, and a handsome amount of maintenance expenditure which led to poor productivity and sustainability. However, it has been observed in the literature that industry 4.0 technologies integrated with predictive systems come out with lucrative benefits. Current study reflects the effect of Intelligent Predictive Maintenance (IPdM) in textile manufacturing industry in India. Digital technologies of industry 4.0 like Industrial Internet of things (IIoT), Sensors, and machine learning (ML) models were used with traditional predictive maintenance that enable real-time monitoring, anomaly detection. The results indicate 68% increase in Mean Time Between Failures, decrease about 41% in Mean Time to Repair, machine availability improved from 82% to 93%, and maintenance costs decreases about 27%, while energy consumption reduced upto 11%. Outcomes of this study reflects the economic benefits and sustainability in textile manufacturing industry. Study provides empirical evidence on IPdM for enhancing productivity and performance in the Indian textile sector.

title: An Ensemble Machine Learning Approach for Imbalanced Brain Stroke Prediction
authors: R. M. Sakthivel Suriya, V.S. Adithyan, Ramkiran B, Pavithra M, Vijayabhaskar B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Early detection of brain stroke can play an important role in reducing deaths and enhancing outcomes, but it is a challenging task owing to class imbalance in datasets, with the stroke-positive patients being minority samples (ratio of 19:1). This study evaluates and optimizes ensemble learning architectures by testing four machine learning algorithms: Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, and Neural Networks. The class imbalance issue is handled by employing hyperparameter tuning with GridSearchCV along with a unique algorithmic weighting technique that incorporates the use of both XGBoost and SVM in soft voting. Additionally, the SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) technique determined the important predictor variables, which included age and clinical factors, like hypertension and mean blood glucose values. Overall, this study has shown that ensemble methods applying gradient boosting and SVM algorithms provide better prediction results in assessing clinical stroke risk, with a final optimal accuracy rate of 95.04%.

title: Self-adaptive graph learning approach for Spectral Clustering with soft-label assignment
authors: Khoi Vo Huy, Vinh Truong Hoang, Thanh Duc Ngo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Spectral clustering, one of the most popular modern clustering algorithms, explores the connectivity between data points and optimizes the minimum graph cut on the graph, resulting in clusters in the dataset. Despite the ability to capture the geometric properties of a graph for clustering non-convex data, this algorithm relies on building an accurate initial affinity graph. Consequently, it suffers from a dependency on pre-defined parameters to correctly represent connections among data. Self-adaptive graph-based approaches have emerged to address this limitation, but they require capturing the local and global cluster information to achieve the optimal graph structure. To mitigate these problems, we introduce a self-adaptive graph clustering framework that dynamically adjusts the affinity graph structure and imposes several constraints, such as sparsity, matrix rank and node invariance. Moreover, to improve the quality of cluster results, our framework replaces traditional k-means clustering with a soft-label assignment module and optimizes it in a self-supervised manner. The experimental results demonstrate that our modifications can construct optimal graph structures and deliver remarkable improvements over the baseline model and the existing clustering methods.

title: Enhancing Response Mechanisms Against LOLBAS Threats in SCADA Environments: Ablation Study and Practical Implications
authors: Thamer Abu-Alzanat, Jafar A. Alzubi, Ashraf AlDabbas, Hussam N. Fakhouri, Hasan Kanaker, Omar A. Alzubi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Living-off-the-land binaries and scripts (LOLBAS) enable adversaries to abuse trusted system utilities to perform malicious actions while minimizing the introduction of new executables. In supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) environments, this behavior is difficult to distinguish from legitimate administration and maintenance, and overly aggressive mitigation may jeopardize availability and safety. This paper presents a LOLBAS-aware detection and response framework that fuses host command telemetry with NetFlow-style network behavior. We extract lightweight semantic features from command lines using natural language processing and combine them with real-time flow summaries derived from packet capture. Detection is performed with a weighted ensemble of Random Forest, XGBoost, LSTM, and Transformer models, where vote weights are calibrated from validation F1-scores. To preserve operational safety, the response layer operates in alert-only mode, producing evidence-backed operator recommendations instead of automated containment. Experiments on 150,000 labeled records from a hardware-in-the-loop SCADA simulation and a 6–8 week continuous evaluation window achieve 95.2% accuracy, 2.8% false positive rate, 0.95 F1-score, 0.97 ROC-AUC, and 8.5 ms average inference latency. An ablation study shows that command semantics contribute the largest performance gain, while network context and temporal modeling further reduce false alarms.

title: Manufacturing of double molded 3D freeform antennas based on Fan-Out Wafer Level Packaging (FOWLP) technologies by compression molding and direct Cu metallization
authors: Friedrich Müller, Oliver Nallaweg, Christian Tschoban, Paul Perlwitz, Karl-Friedrich Becker, Tanja Braun, Martin Schneider-Ramelow
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: The trend towards the application of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) for logistic applications drives the need for better antennas for airspace surveillance. To cover a wider range of airspace it is necessary to bring the 2D antennas on wafer/panel level to the third dimension. One possibility to solve this issue with processes from FOWLP is compression molding. This standard process for reconfigured wafers can combine the innovation of realizing 3D structures with the proven advantages of chip embedding and redistribution layer fabrication.In this paper a concept for solderable 3D freeform antenna manufacturing using FOWLP technologies will be presented and discussed on the basis of the realized process steps regarding feasibility and performance. Herein, the focus will be on the possibilities of structuring the direct metallized Cu on the 3D mold substrate regarding different resist application techniques, dry resist vacuum lamination, dip coating as well as spray coating. The exposure setting of the resist and its impact on the resolution will be also addressed. The 3D double molding with its challenges and impact on the following blind via laser drilling process will complete the considerations before showing the performance of realized 3D freeform test antennas by electrical measurement. The antennas show their performance especially in the field of view where they have an opening angle of more than 180° and are therefore about a factor two better than conventional antennas.All in all the proposed concept shows a scalable manufacturing flow supporting the functional integration of 3D antennas into the FOWLP packaging platform.

title: Dual-Mode Adaptive Memory-Anchored Target State Estimation Based on Shooting Event Triggering under Dense False Measurements
authors: Luyun Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: To address the problem of severe contamination of radar tracking filters by dense projectile false measurements during anti-aircraft weapon firing, which leads to degraded estimation accuracy and even target loss, a dual-mode adaptive memory-anchored state estimation algorithm triggered by shooting events is proposed. Distinguished from conventional probabilistic data association and interacting multiple model methods, the proposed algorithm utilizes the system’s firing signal as a prior temporal trigger condition. During firing periods, it actively suspends measurement updates and switches to a memory-anchored mode based on the mean velocity derived from a dual-buffer sliding window, thereby circumventing false measurement interference at its source. Upon cessation of firing, a linear weighted smooth transition strategy is employed to achieve rapid target reacquisition. Theoretical analysis elucidates the error propagation characteristics of the memory-anchored extrapolation and the influence mechanism of the sliding window length on estimation accuracy. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly enhances target state estimation accuracy and reacquisition capability after lock-loss in dense false measurement environments, providing a novel engineering solution for radar anti-projectile interference.

title: Influence of Geometrical Battery Electrode Defects on the Format-Flexible Laser Singulation Process
authors: Sebastian Schabel, David Kraus, Florian Schlüter, Jürgen Fleischer
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The electrification of powertrains is essential for transforming the transport sector in a resource-efficient and environmentally sustainable manner, with batteries playing a central role. However, the high costs associated with battery production and raw materials highlight the need for waste minimisation. A critical step in manufacturing is the singulation of electrodes into single sheets. While laser singulation offers wear-free and format-flexible cutting compared to mechanical punching, current handling units impose limitations on format flexibility. Additionally, geometrical defects such as wrinkles and corrugations lead to unstable cutting processes due to the small focal length of laser beams. Furthermore, geometrical defects and the process parameters during singulation highly affect the dimensional accuracy of the single sheets in width and length. This study introduces an innovative machine concept for format-flexible laser singulation of electrode sheets. The system integrates vacuum point grippers for electrode fixation and mechanical jaw grippers to control web tension. The configuration of point grippers on a magnetic base can be rapidly adapted to modify the electrode formats. Based on that, the experimental investigations assess the correlation of calendering parameters of an NMC622 cathode on the cutting quality and the dimensional sheet accuracy. The findings demonstrate that the deviation in sheet width, oriented toward the electrode web's running, exhibits a significantly stronger correlation with the investigated calendering parameter levels than the deviation in sheet length. The roller temperatures have been shown to have the most significant impact on the sheet width.

title: Ultrasonic experimental study of the water quality: Impact of salinity
authors: Abedlaaziz Ghafiri, Elhoussaine Ouacha, Hicham Banouni, Elhoussine Oukhai, Lahbib. Alahyane
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Ultrasonic technique is a powerful method for characterizing and non-destructively testing aqueous media. This technique is faster and less expensive than classical chemical methods, which require samples, reagents, and significant analysis time. This study experimentally investigates the impact of water salinity on the evolution of ultrasonic acoustic wave properties, namely ultrasound velocity and amplitude. To this end, we conducted six experiments with different samples using the monostatic configuration, in which the same transducer alternates between transmitter and receiver. The first sample, considered as a reference, is 200 g of distilled water. The subsequent samples were prepared by successively adding 10 g of commercial salt to the initial sample. The results demonstrate a direct relationship between the measured parameters and salt concentration. Significant variations in ultrasound velocity and amplitude of the backscattered signal were observed as the salt percentage increased. Ultrasound velocity increases while amplitude decreases. These results demonstrate that the proposed ultrasonic technique can efficiently control water quality under similar conditions.

title: Nonlinear Backstepping control of grid-connected wind energy system using Vienna rectifier and Half-Bridge inverter
authors: El Aaoud Amina, Mchaouar Youssef, Abouelmahjoub Younes, Gmih Yassine, Elouafi Mohamed
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In this paper, a backstepping control technique is proposed to control the Wind Energy Conversion System (WECS), which consists of a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator (PMSG), a Vienna rectifier, and a single-phase halfbridge inverter with an LCL filter. The motivation for this choice is that this configuration is highly efficient and offers natural capacitor voltage balancing, and the half-bridge configuration is a simple and efficient means to connect a WECS to a singlephase grid. The objectives of this study are to: (i) To guarantee that the rotor speed follows the reference produced by the Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) technique; (ii) To maintain balanced voltages for the capacitors while controlling the DC link voltage; and (iii) To assure that the grid current is sinusoidal and stays in phase with the grid voltage under various wind conditions. Simulation results verify the effectiveness of the approach, achieving rotor speed tracking with steady-state error ${\lt}1 \%$, DC-link regulation at $600 \mathrm{~V} \pm 2 \mathrm{~V}$. The system guarantees grid integration with high quality, ensuring accurate grid current tracking in phase with the grid voltage and always keeping the Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) below the 5% limit set by the IEEE 519 standard.

title: A Hierarchical Fusion Method Based on Dempster-Shafer Theory for Interoperability Assessment of Complex System
authors: Wen Yang, Jiajie Sun, Junhao Li, Zhekai Pang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: The interoperability assessment of complex system often involves substantial uncertainty and conflicting evidence, while conventional methods provide only limited descriptive information. This paper therefore develops a hierarchical fusion method based on Dempster-Shafer (DS) theory. Based on the characteristics of the multi-stage task processes, a hierarchical indicator system with six primary indicators and twenty-four secondary indicators is developed. Hierarchical fusion enables the method to generate the interoperability score, grade, pignistic probability (BetP) distribution, confidence, and conflict information. Simulation results show that the method distinguishes among different interoperability states, including high-level balance, stage transition, and local imbalance. Compared with the weighted average method, it is more sensitive to transitional characteristics and critical weak links. A correlation-aware conflict refinement mechanism is further introduced at the secondary-indicator level to alleviate conflict overestimation caused by highly correlated indicators. Monte Carlo perturbation experiments and $95 \%$ confidence intervals further demonstrate the robustness of the proposed method under input fluctuations. The proposed method provides a more interpretable and informative assessment of interoperability in complex systems.

title: Mining Mathematical Misconceptions Using Deep Learning and Large Language Models
authors: Aya Lyousfi, Asmaa Mourhir, Naeem Nisar Sheikh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Mathematical misconceptions represent one of the most persistent and consequential barriers to STEM education, systematically blocking student progression from elementary arithmetic to advanced calculus. Automated detection of these errors remains difficult because traditional classification approaches struggle with the extreme class imbalance inherent in misconception detection, where thousands of possible errors must be distinguished from limited training examples. This paper presents a retrieval-based system that combines dense neural search with Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning to match student errors to known misconceptions. A finetuned MathBERT bi-encoder retrieves the top- 25 semantically relevant misconceptions from 2,587 candidates, achieving 93% recall. Two LLM-based reranking strategies refine these results: ReAct-style reasoning with chain-of-thought prompting, and pairwise comparison with majority voting. The ReAct agent using DeepSeek-R1 improves mean average precision at rank 5 from 0.70 to 0.80. Unlike classification models that output opaque probability distributions, this approach retrieves humanreadable misconception descriptions, providing teachers with interpretable, verifiable explanations of student errors. Results demonstrate that retrieval-based architectures offer superior scalability for educational diagnosis tasks.

title: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: TamOCR: A Lightweight LLM-Assisted OCR Framework for Digitizing Tamil-Brahmi Inscriptions
authors: K Saranya, S R Nitthin Kumar, R Praveen Kumar, S Arun Nivesh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The digitisation of Tamil–Brahmi inscriptions is essential for safeguarding South India’s earliest written heritage, yet real-world OCR deployment is still challenged by weathered stone surfaces, broken or missing glyphs, inconsistent lighting, and highly variable carving styles. In response to survey findings that highlighted the need for modular and resilient OCR pipelines, this work introduces TamOCR, a lightweight, LLM-assisted OCR framework tailored for Tamil–Brahmi inscription digitisation. The framework combines a robust pre-processing stage for contrast enhancement and noise reduction, a segmentation module that isolates glyphs from complex stone backgrounds, and a recognition stage driven by compact vision models tuned for low-resource execution environments. To mitigate recognition ambiguities, TamOCR employs a lightweight language-model–based reconstruction layer that performs context-aware correction, symbol disambiguation, and transliteration refinement. We evaluate the framework on diverse inscription datasets with varying degradation levels and show substantial gains in character-level accuracy, word-level recognition stability, and robustness when compared with conventional OCR engines and heavier neural architectures. Owing to its modular design, the framework can be deployed on resource-constrained platforms, making it suitable for field archaeology, museum applications, and large-scale digital archiving. This work offers an early practical realisation of Tamil–Brahmi OCR using a lightweight LLM and provides a feasible pathway toward scaling the preservation of ancient scripts.

title: An Ethical Intent-Aware Electric Fence System
authors: S. Rathinamala, Banupriya V, Sivasian S, Vishal C. P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Electric fences have been extensively employed in protecting agricultural land and restricted areas but, traditional methods work around-the-clock, ignoring whether intrusions are intentional or unintentional. Such a method can cause unwanted harm to humans and animals. In this regard, an intention-aware electric fence system is suggested, which will be able to detect any movement or intrusion around the fence and identify if the intrusion is from a human being or from animals. Unlike other approaches, this system will not send electric pulses to the fence right away, as soon as a person or animal comes within its vicinity. Instead, this system will alert the person of its presence with an auditory signal. The fence will become electric only after identifying the intention of the intrusion. In this way, a safety protocol will be introduced prior to activating the electrical charge on the fence, which is why the proposed approach is safe.

title: Collaborative Robot Automated Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation Coil Characterization
authors: Logan Schorr, Wesley Lohr, Sabeya Neeha, Mahdi Paslar, Ravi L. Hadimani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Despite widespread advances in transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS), there exist relatively few experimental methods to evaluate how coils stimulate the brain beyond computational simulations or clinical testing. Collaborative robots offer a promising solution, providing precise coil positioning, integrated force-torque sensing for alignment, and unparalleled safety in human environments. A novel coil characterization platform is presented that integrates a collaborative robot with a custom-designed brain phantom to enable automated and reproducible qualification of TMS coils. In this platform, a collaborative robot manipulates a TMS coil around the surface of a brain phantom instrumented with embedded tri-axial probes. At each pose, the coil is pulsed and the resulting electric field is recorded, enabling automated acquisition of spatial maps that capture the relationship between coil placement and induced stimulation patterns. Using this methodology, both a figure-of-eight and a quadruple-butterfly coil were characterized, producing electric field heatmaps that demonstrated the capability of the system to resolve coil-specific differences in field distribution. By providing a safe, repeatable, and automated workflow, the platform addresses a critical bottleneck in coil development: the absence of standardized experimental methods for qualification prior to clinical use. Beyond facilitating rapid testing of new coil designs, the utility of the system can be extended to validate the fidelity of brain phantoms themselves. This work serves to highlight collaborative robots as a key asset for not only advancing TMS research, but also rapidly accelerating the development of novel stimulation hardware.

title: PathRWKV: Enhancing Whole Slide Image Inference with Asymmetric Recurrent Modeling
authors: Tianyi Zhang, Sicheng Chen, Borui Kang, Dankai Liao, Qiaochu Xue, Bochong Zhang, Fei Xia, Zeyu Liu, Yueming Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Whole Slide Imaging (WSI) has become a gold standard in cancer diagnosis, inspecting multi-scale information from cellular to tissue levels. Processing an entire WSI directly is infeasible due to GPU memory constraints; thus, Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) has emerged as the standard solution by partitioning WSIs into tiles. While recent two-stage MIL frameworks partially achieve memory efficiency by decoupling tile-level extraction from slide-level modeling, they still face four limitations: (1) the conflict between training throughput and inference memory efficiency, (2) the high susceptibility to overfitting on small-scale WSI datasets with sparse supervision, (3) the disruption of spatial structural integrity during sampling-based training, and (4) the inadequate modeling of multi-scale feature interactions within long sequences. We therefore introduce PathRWKV, a novel State Space Model designed for efficient and robust WSI analysis. To resolve the computational trade-off, we propose an asymmetric structure utilizing max pooling aggregation, enabling parallelized training for high throughput and recurrent inference with constant (O(1)) memory complexity. To mitigate overfitting, we employ random sampling to enhance data diversity, with a multi-task learning module to regularize feature learning on limited data. To restore spatial context, we introduce 2D sinusoidal position encoding to perceive the relative locations of tissue tiles. To capture comprehensive representations, we integrate TimeMix and ChannelMix modules, enabling dynamic multi-scale feature modeling across temporal and spatial dimensions. Experiments on 29,073 WSIs across 11 datasets demonstrate that PathRWKV outperforms 11 state-of-the-art methods on 10 datasets, establishing it as a scalable and solution with application potential.

title: FPGA Implementation of Face Detection System Using MTCNN on ZCU104 Board
authors: Abinash Kumar Pala, Jatin Kumar Rauto, Sthitaprajna Sahoo, Shubham Ranjan Sahoo, Srikanta Mishra, Ashutosh Rout, Dinesh Kumar Dash
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: A crucial component of many computer vision applications is face detection. These consist of automated attendance systems, surveillance, and facial recognition [9], [10]. This paper introduces a Multi-task Cascaded Convolutional Neural Network (MTCNN) based real-time face detection framework on the Xilinx ZCU104 FPGA [1], [3]. The Wider Face dataset is used by the system to evaluate detection performance in various lighting conditions, occlusions, and poses [1]. The Vitis AI workflow is used to quantize, compile, and deploy the MTCNN model on the FPGA's Deep Learning Processing Unit (DPU) [3]. In comparison to conventional CPU-based systems, experimental results demonstrate that this hardware-based implementation offers high accuracy and significantly lowers power consumption [2], [4]. The results demonstrate that FPGA-based embedded AI systems offer a scalable, energy-efficient, and successful solution for real-time face detection tasks [3].

title: Mitigation of Random Body Movement Artifacts in Radar-Based Vital Sign Monitoring
authors: Marida De Maria, Andrea Gargano, Ilde Rosa Lorato, Giuseppe Marrara, Giuseppe Araniti, Graziella D'Arrigo, Patrizia Vizza, Alberto Greco, Pierangelo Veltri, Enzo Pasquale Scilingo, Marco Mercuri
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Mitigating the effects of random body movements (RBMs) remains a major challenge for reliable radar-based vital sign monitoring, as the resulting artifacts can mask or distort subtle respiration and heartbeat signals. In this work, we present a signal processing pipeline to reduce the effect of limb artifacts. The feasibility of the proposed approach is demonstrated in vivo using a Frequency-Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radar.

title: Online Transient Frequency Prediction via Physics-Data Fusion with Swarm Intelligence Optimization
authors: Zhen Huang, Fei Peng, Kaiyuan Hou, Deming Xia, Duoqin Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Transient frequency instability in power systems poses a critical challenge to grid security. Existing online frequency response analysis methods can predict transient frequency using artificial intelligence techniques; however, they often overlook the deep correlations in time-series frequency characteristics, leading to limitations in prediction accuracy and timeliness. To address this issue, this paper proposes an online frequency response prediction method based on a swarm intelligence fusion model, which significantly enhances prediction performance through time-series feature decoupling and dynamic structure optimization. First, variables affecting transient frequency are decomposed into key state variables and system time-series data according to their temporal attributes. Second, a system frequency response model is employed as a physics-driven approach to preserve its mechanistic interpretability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms traditional approaches in both prediction accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Self-Adaptive Flood Extractor (SAFE): A Lightweight Spatiotemporal Gap-Filling Framework for Cloud-Prone Areas Using GEE
authors: Boya Xu, Qianguang Tu, Zhonghang Su, Mengxian Wu, Haiqian Yu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Optical remote sensing satellites are crucial for near-real-time flood monitoring and disaster assessment. However, severe and frequent cloud cover during extreme weather events causes extensive spatial fragmentation in optical imagery, leading to massive data gaps in extracted inundation maps. Reconstructing continuous and reliable flood extents from these cloud-contaminated observations is highly challenging. Existing gap-filling methods, such as complex deep learning models or spatiotemporal Markov Random Field optimization, are computationally expensive and heavily rely on local annotated data, hindering rapid deployment in large-scale disaster responses. To address this limitation, we propose the Self-Adaptive Flood Extractor (SAFE-Flood), a lightweight and self-adaptive flood mapping framework based on Google Earth Engine (GEE). The framework first leverages the JRC Global Surface Water dataset and an improved water index to automatically mine high-confidence water and land priors. Subsequently, the Fisher criterion is introduced to optimize multispectral features and automatically determine water-land segmentation thresholds, eliminating the reliance of traditional water indices on empirical thresholds. Finally, a minimalist spatiotemporal gap-filling strategy is constructed using a local sliding temporal window and a static physical prior. Experiments in a typical cloud-prone test site with cloud cover greater than 60 percent demonstrate that SAFE-Flood successfully reconstructs the maximum flood extent with topological continuity and improves the F1 score from 0.6078 to 0.6909. Combining high efficiency with robustness in extreme weather, this training-free and cloud-native spatiotemporal reconstruction framework provides a practical Earth observation solution for large-scale rapid disaster response.

title: Optimization of a Hybrid PV–Wind–Battery System for Off-Grid Rural Electrification in the High Atlas of Morocco
authors: Maryam Domar, Khalida Zazi, Malika Zazi, Abdelilah Zadid
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In remote and rural areas, one of the key challenges lies in providing stable and consistent sources of electricity. The present research focuses on the design of a simple hybrid model for power generation using photovoltaic cells, wind energy, and battery energy storage, which will provide electricity for an isolated village in the High Atlas mountains of Morocco ($\mathbf{3 1}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}} \mathbf{2 6} \boldsymbol{\prime} \mathbf{3 6} \mathrm{N}, 6^{\circ} 58^{\prime} 25 \mathrm{~W}$). There are 80 houses in this village requiring about 150 kWh of energy per day. To remain consistent with reality, the data on solar and wind energy was obtained for each hour of the year 2024, and daily consumption was calculated with account taken of essential needs (such as lighting, refrigeration, and minor household appliances). The system is designed with a surplus of production capacity to accommodate additional households in the future and ensure higher standards of life (heating in the winter and air conditioning in the summer). To determine the degree of reliability, we use the measure of LPSP aiming at 5%. The initial hybrid model failed to satisfy this requirement (11.98%), which necessitated the use of a large battery (capacity - 400 kWh). With Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), we managed to reach the necessary value of LPSP $\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{5. 0 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$ with smaller battery storage ($\mathbf{1 1 7. 5 ~ k W h}$) and lower costs (approximately 30%).

title: Additional Reviewers
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)

title: Breathing Rate Estimation from Data Fusion of Multiple Elevated and Tilted FMCW Radars
authors: Mareike Wendelmuth, Alexander Yarovoy, Francesco Fioranelli
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The problem of measuring vital signs with contactless radar monitoring in settings where wearables are not feasible, such as psychiatric clinics, is considered. Breathing rate estimation using three elevated and tilted FMCW radars placed at ceiling height is investigated in this paper. A dataset of 18 participants was collected, including activities with frequent posture changes. A processing pipeline to perform motion based segmentation, localization with MIMO beamforming, phase extraction, and short time window breathing rate estimation using autoregressive (AR) models is proposed. A simple fusion method to combine the individual radar estimates based on their correlation with an ideal respiration model is suggested. Robustness improvement across different body orientations and including active body movements due to multiple radar data fusion is experimentally demonstrated. A decrease of the mean absolute errors (MAE) of down to 0.88 respirations per minute (rpm) for the best-case participant, and an average of 3.33 rpm for all considered participants is achieved. The feasibility of reliable breathing rate monitoring of actively moving persons using elevated radars in realistic psychiatric care environments is shown.

title: Construction of Deep Learning-Based Dynamic Early Warning Model for Charging Resource Capacity
authors: Sihua Liu, Kai Kang, Min Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: With the rapid growth in the number of new energy vehicles, charging loads exhibit high randomness, strong volatility, and significant temporal coupling characteristics, posing severe challenges to the safe operation of distribution networks and the coordinated planning of charging infrastructure. Traditional methods for assessing charging resource capacity are often based on static or empirical models, lacking both timeliness and accuracy in early warning, and are no longer sufficient to meet the demands of refined regulation. To address these issues, this paper constructs a deep learning-based dynamic charging resource capacity early warning model. First, drawing upon multi-source data encompassing charging facility operations, user charging behaviour, and distribution network status, a dynamic charging resource capacity indicator system is established.
