This system unifies the modelling of temporal characteristics and spatial correlation features. Subsequently, a deep temporal learning model is introduced, integrating Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with attention mechanisms. This enables joint prediction of charging load evolution and capacity risk trends, alongside the construction of a hierarchical early warning criterion. Practical case studies validate that the proposed model outperforms conventional methods in both prediction accuracy and warning precision, enabling effective early identification of charging resource capacity risks. This research provides a viable intelligent warning technology support for the operational control of electric vehicle charging facilities and the safety management of distribution grids.

title: Trans-Intestinal Port for Periodic Drug Refilling: Bridging Ingestible Capsules and Implantable Devices_supp2-3699210.mp4
authors: Leonardo Ricotti
publish: N/A
abstract: Implantable devices for controlled drug release are extremely promising for the treatment of chronic diseases, such as type 1 diabetes, chronic pain, and others. However, these systems still require periodic invasive refills, which can result in clinical complications and patient discomfort. Previous works introduced the opportunity for a non-invasive refilling of these devices through ingestible capsules. However, concerns remain regarding repeated tissue perforation of the intestinal wall and the corresponding risk of fistula formation. This study presents a novel trans-intestinal port system enabling non-invasive refilling of implanted devices designed to create a stable and controlled interface between the device and the intestinal lumen. The system aims to avoid tissue damage due to repeated needle insertions, ensure proper alignment between the device and capsule, and prevent leakage of digestive fluids into the peritoneal cavity. The design and fabrication of this trans-intestinal port are presented, together with the results of bench tests and a preliminary short-term in vivo test. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed trans-intestinal port as an innovative solution to enable a non-invasive periodic refilling of implantable drug delivery systems, paving the way to a full validation of this technology in the future, which will require long-term in vivo experiments.

title: ChamVerDB: A Chameleon Hash-based Secure and Efficient Verifiable Outsourced Database
authors: Wenqi Zhang, Xiong Li, Ruirui Gao, Ting Chen, Jiguo Yu, Jinjun Chen, Xiaosong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: A verifiable outsourced database is a crucial technology for ensuring the integrity of query results in a cloud-based outsourced database. However, existing schemes struggle to balance efficient query execution with strong data security and query privacy, limiting practical deployment. To address this challenge, we propose ChamVerDB, a Chameleon Hash-based secure and efficient verifiable outsourced database. ChamVerDB restructures the database into a matrix and encrypts each record into three ciphertext segments using a modified BG encryption. For each row, it generates row parity, computes a corresponding Chameleon Hash Value and proof, while also generating individual parities for each record. The ciphertexts and parities are packaged into an encrypted database and outsourced to the server. During querying, the user utilizes a one-hot query vector to retrieve the target ciphertext row and a column vector to extract the desired ciphertext locally. Subsequently, the user and server collaboratively perform proxy re-encryption on one ciphertext segment and recover the plaintext using the remaining segments. The user then verifies the result using row parity, individual parity, proof, and the public hash value. Security analysis confirms that ChamVerDB ensures data security, query privacy, and result verifiability. Experimental evaluation on a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2^{20}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-record database demonstrates that ChamVerDB achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$42\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$32\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> faster offline processing than two encrypted outsourcing baselines. In online phase, ChamVerDB completes a query in just 1.21s, outperforming three representative baselines by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$511\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> (618.5s), <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1039\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> (1258.14s), and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$209810\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> (253871.49s), illustrating its practical usability.

title: Graph-Based Reinforcement Learning for Minimizing Population Mortality in Epidemic Networks
authors: Zhihao Dong, Yuanzhu Chen, Somayeh Kafaie, Qiao Kang, Cheng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The spread of infectious diseases in networked populations poses significant challenges for public health intervention strategies. Traditional centrality-based and heuristic network dismantling approaches prioritize highly connected nodes but often fail to account for individual mortality risk, limiting their effectiveness in minimizing overall fatalities. While recent advances in machine learning have improved intervention strategies, existing models largely focus on reducing disease transmission rather than directly targeting mortality outcomes. To address this gap, we propose a reinforcement learning-based framework that integrates graph representation learning to identify and remove high-risk nodes, thereby maximizing network fragmentation while minimizing overall deaths. The framework is trained using synthetic networks and evaluated on five synthetic and four real-world datasets, benchmarking its performance against state-of-the-art network dismantling methods [graph dismantling with machine learning (GDM), generalized network dismantling (GND), and graph enhanced reinforcement learning (GERL)]. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms baseline approaches, particularly in scale-free and community-structured networks, where targeted node removal significantly weakens network connectivity and suppresses epidemic spread. Moreover, in real-world networks, the method achieves lower cumulative death rates and higher epidemic thresholds, highlighting its robustness in controlling disease propagation. By incorporating mortality risk into network representation learning, the proposed framework offers a scalable, adaptive, and socially responsible approach to epidemic mitigation, misinformation control, and network resilience enhancement.

title: Analyzing the Impact of Service Stages on Demand and Modal Shift for Urban Air Mobility: A Case Study of Seoul Metropolitan Area
authors: Hyunsoo Kim, Kwanjung Yee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Upon the introduction of urban air mobility (UAM) services as a new transportation mode, a substantial portion of demand is expected to be generated by passengers shifting from conventional transportation modes. However, previous studies have focused on the primary mode of transportation that shifts to UAM in specific service stages, overlooking how modal shift patterns evolve as the service matures. Additionally, most prior studies have reported only total demand values, without distinguishing between different types of routes such as airport shuttles and local intra-city trips, which obscures route-specific demand characteristics. This study aims to bridge this gap by providing a comprehensive analysis of mode choice patterns and route characteristics for UAM services throughout the entire service stages. A utility-based mode choice model is applied, and a case study of the Seoul metropolitan area is conducted using empirical travel data. The results indicate that as UAM services mature, targeting the demand shift from private vehicle users has more potential to generate high demand than targeting public transport or taxi users. Furthermore, the findings indicate that routes with high demand during a specific service stage do not consistently follow high demand throughout the entire service stage. In particular, airport routes initially show higher modal shift rates due to greater time savings, their influence on overall UAM demand diminishes over time because of their inherently low traffic volume. These findings offer practical insights for policy-makers and planners, particularly in cities with mobility patterns similar to Seoul, by supporting strategic decisions on service planning and infrastructure development.

title: GPU-Accelerated Simulation of Densely Connected Tensegrity Networks for Statistical Analysis of Morphological Convergence
authors: Yanqiu Zheng, Nobuyuki Masuda, Zebing Mao, Cong Yan, Chengyan Zhao, Longchuan Li
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Densely connected tensegrity networks provide a useful testbed for morphology-driven dynamics, but their quadratic cable interactions make large-scale, long-horizon simulation prohibitively expensive for CPU-based implementations, limiting quantitative studies of morphological properties. This study presents a size-scalable dynamic model and a GPU-accelerated simulator for two-dimensional multi-rod tensegrity systems under dense connectivity, while preserving the same tension-only cable mechanics and fixed-step time integration scheme as a CPU baseline. Leveraging the resulting throughput, we conduct Monte Carlo sweeps over system size and randomized initial conditions, and quantify morphological convergence to a conservative feasibility set using simple, interpretable scalar metrics. Across the tested range, larger networks exhibit reduced variability in morphology statistics, higher convergence rates, and typically shorter convergence times. These results provide quantitative evidence that strong internal coupling supports statistically robust morphological convergence, and show that GPU acceleration enables reproducible, large-scale evaluation of morphology-driven dynamics in densely connected tensegrity networks.

title: Adaptive High-Speed Radar Signal Processing Architecture for 3-D Localization of Multiple Targets on System on Chip
authors: Aakanksha Tewari, Jai Mangal, Sumit J. Darak, Shobha Sundar Ram, Arnav Shukla
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) is a key enabler of high-speed, ultra-low-latency vehicular communication in 6G. ISAC leverages radar signal processing (RSP) to localize multiple unknown targets amid static clutter by jointly estimating range, azimuth, and Doppler velocity (3D), thereby enabling highly directional beamforming toward intended mobile users. However, the speed and accuracy of RSP significantly impact communication throughput. This work proposes a novel 3D reconfigurable RSP accelerator, implemented on a Zynq Multi-processor System-on-Chip (MPSoC) using a hardware–software co-design approach and fixed-point optimization. We propose two RSP frameworks: (1) high-accuracy and high-complexity, and (2) low-complexity and low-accuracy, along with their respective architectures. Then, we develop an adaptive architecture that dynamically switches between these two frameworks based on the signal-to-clutter-plus-noise ratio. This adaptive reconfiguration achieves up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.6\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> faster RSP compared to state-of-the-art designs. At the system level, the proposed RSP-based ISAC delivers a 24% improvement in communication throughput without increasing hardware complexity.

title: Production Atmosphere Requirements for the Use of Direct-Contact Prelithiation within Lithium-Ion Battery Production
authors: Felix Diller, Maximilian Hüesker, Maximilian Lechner, Lovis Wach, Sophie Grabmann, Rüdiger Daub
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Direct-contact prelithiation is a promising method to increase the energy density and the cycle life of lithium-ion batteries. In this process, the anode active material is brought into direct contact with lithium metal. The additional lithium compensates for the loss of active lithium due to the solid electrolyte interphase formation during cell formation and operation. The formed intermediate product, a combination of lithium metal with anode active material or already partially lithiated anode active material show increased reactivity to moisture, nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and other air components. Though, the requirements on the production atmosphere for these intermediate products are not known, hindering the industrialization of direct-contact prelithiation. The knowledge of the production atmosphere requirements is crucial to ensure process stability and material integrity, allowing for the industrial implementation of innovative materials, production processes, and intermediate products. However, the influence of different production atmospheres on the stability and electrochemical performance of anodes in direct contact with lithium metal for direct-contact prelithiation has not been sufficiently investigated. In this study, silicon-based anodes were prelithiated by applying lithium foil onto the anode surface. A calender integrated in an argon-filled glove box was used to form this anode-lithium composite. The anode-lithium composites were stored under specific atmospheric conditions, including argon, nitrogen, and dry room environments with various dew points. Storage durations of 8hours, 24hours, and one week were evaluated. For comparison, pristine lithium foil was stored under the same conditions. The stored anode-lithium composites were analyzed using optical microscopy and electrochemically tested in both half and full cells. The results indicate that the storage of prelithiated anodes has a negative impact on their electrochemical performance in all tested atmosphere conditions and should be minimized. The anode-lithium composites show a faster change in color than the pure lithium foil. Negligible degradation was observed at dry room conditions with a dew point smaller than −47°C and an exposure time of up to 24hours.

title: Feature selection method to improve time series forecasting of Moroccan GDP using deep learning
authors: Sana Hamiane, Youssef Ghanou, Hamid Khalifi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The objective of this work is to identify relevant indicators to improve the performance of the CNN-LSTM hybrid model for forecasting Morocco’s gross domestic product. Before training, we utilized a robust variable selection step by combining Mutual Information, TSFresh, and the RFECV method with XGBoost, which enabled us to select important features that are closely related to GDP, while Bayesian Optimization was used to determine optimal hyperparameters. The introduced model was tested on quarterly indicators and GDP time series for Morocco from the Haut-Commissariat au Plan, covering a 45 -year period from 1980 to 2025, consisting of 183 observations and 16 features. To ensure a realistic assessment of predictive performance, the proposed model was evaluated using an out-of-sample forecasting strategy, which simulates real-world forecasting conditions and tests the model’s ability to generalize to unseen data. Experimental results showed that the hybrid CNN-LSTM model with feature selection outperformed the hybrid model without feature selection, the individual models—namely CNN, LSTM, and TCN—as well as the traditional econometric Vector Autoregressive (VAR) model, with lower MSE, MAE, and MAPE values, and a higher value of $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$.

title: A Small-Range High-Resolution Linear Displacement Encoder Based on Electromagnetic Resonance
authors: Longjun Fang, Xuefan Bian, Baiyang Liu, Liang Wu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: To address the issues of low resolution and weak signal strength associated with traditional eddy-current linear encoders in small-range displacement measurements, this paper proposes an electromagnetically resonant linear displacement encoder designed to achieve high-precision and high-resolution measurements within a confined space. Upon application of a high-frequency alternating current to the excitation coil, the metallic rectangular coil on the moving ruler generates a reflected magnetic field that acts upon the sensing coil. During measurement, variations in the coupling area between the two coils induce a periodically varying electromotive force in the sensing coil, enabling the determination of the displacement value. The structure and measurement principle of the proposed encoder are described, and its feasibility is validated through finite-element simulations. A prototype is fabricated and subjected to performance testing. Experimental results demonstrate that the encoder achieves a peak-to-peak error of 4.9 $\mu \mathrm{m}$ over a measurement range of 2 mm (one pitch), with an optimal resolution of $0.08 \mu \mathrm{~m}$.

title: The Effects of Visual-Olfactory Interactions With Moving Particles on EEG-Based Emotional Classification in AR Environments
authors: Ye-ji Jin, Xiaoyang Mao, Masaki Omata, Won-Du Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Cross-modal perception, the integration of information from multiple senses, plays a critical role in shaping emotional experiences. This study examines the interactions between visual and olfactory stimuli and their effects on emotional responses, a topic rarely addressed in prior research. Experiments employed five distinct visual stimulation methods that were combined with olfactory stimuli. Participants' emotional responses were assessed via surveys and electroencephalography (EEG) signal analysis. The study varied the color and movement direction of augmented particles to investigate their impact on EEG signals and emotional states. The findings demonstrated significant differences in emotional state classification under the influence of visual-olfactory interactions. Specifically, with backward-moving particles with matching colors (M4), classification accuracy was comparable to that of unimodal olfactory conditions (M1). Other visual stimuli generally caused confusion in classifying emotional responses. The increased valence ratings for pleasant aromas across all visual conditions did not consistently align with EEG-based classification results, suggesting that visual stimuli may introduce complexities into neural signals. These results highlight the intricate dynamics of multisensory interactions, emphasizing the role of visual stimuli in modulating emotional responses. The findings also suggest the potential of visual-olfactory interactions in developing augmented reality (AR) systems. By aligning visual and olfactory cues, AR environments can enhance the user experience and create immersive emotional landscapes, leading to applications for mood modulation and stress relief. This study underscores the relevance of multisensory integration in advancing emotion analysis and affective computing.

title: Effect of IBR Negative-Sequence Control on Phase-to-Phase Fault Direction Determination
authors: Zhixin Miao, Fernando Calero, Lingling Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Protective relays implement algorithms to determine the direction to the fault. Phase-to-phase fault detection, can rely on negative sequence components. If the negative sequence currents provided by an inverter-based resource (IBR) are not reliable, the directional determination of the fault is not consistent. In this paper, we compare two types of IBRs: grid-following (GFL) and grid-forming (GFM), for their dynamic performance when subject to phase-to-phase faults on the high side of the step-up transformer. The GFL-IBR behaves as a positive-sequence current source, while the GFM-IBR behaves as a voltage source behind an inductive impedance in the positive-sequence circuit, and as an inductive impedance in the negative-sequence circuit. It is found that the GFM-IBR ensures proper fault ride-through, while the GFL-IBR cannot ride through such a fault. Additionally, the GFM-IBR ensures correct fault detection via negative-sequence directionality check, while this check fails for the GFL-IBR case. Therefore, the GFM design presented in this paper appears to have two advantages compared to the legacy GFL design: better capability of fault ride-through and assurance of correct fault direction determination.

title: A Fault Diagnosis and Differentiated Processing Strategy for Grid Side Overcurrent Faults in Electric Traction Drive System
authors: Qiang Ni, Jinxin Zhang, Canbing Li, Zhicheng Cai, Honghui Fang, Jie Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Grid-side overcurrent (GSOC) in the traction transformer is a system-level fault in the electric traction drive system (ETDS). Once it occurs, the traction control unit (TCU) indiscriminately trips the main circuit breaker to ensure safety, causing the train to stop and requiring inspection and maintenance by the crew. This excessive protection and manual maintenance approach reduces the availability and operational efficiency of the trains. Therefore, this paper proposes a fault diagnosis and differentiated processing strategy for GSOC. Firstly, the GSOC mechanism and the degree of deterioration are analyzed, and the different types of GSOC are classified as permanent fault or temporary fault. Secondly, the effective segment of the fault signal is selected, and the fault temporal features are extracted. Thirdly, the differentiated processing strategy is proposed based on the diagnostic results and the degree of fault deterioration. Finally, the fault diagnostic model is established based on Random Forest classifier, and then the fault scenario is reproduced on the RT-LAB and the diagnostic model is implemented using a digital signal processor (DSP) to complete the verification experiments. The experimental results show that, compared with existing methods, the proposed method can not only achieve 100% diagnostic accuracy but also significantly shorten the diagnostic time. Moreover, the differentiated processing strategy can greatly reduce the trains' stopping time and frequency caused by GSOC.

title: Quantum Software Size: What Do We Measure, How and Why? A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Tuna Hacaloglu, Hassan Soubra, Pierre Bourque, Alain Abran
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Rapid growth in quantum computing has created an urgent need to adapt traditional software engineering methodologies. Among these methodological challenges, software size measurement plays a central role in project estimation, quality assurance, and software engineering management. However, there is no consolidated understanding of how it should be applied to quantum software systems. This paper presents the first systematic literature review on quantum software size measurement, synthesizing existing approaches, and identifying key research gaps. An analysis of 16 studies shows that size measures span multiple abstraction levels, with most research focusing on adapting classical metrics, such as Lines of Code (LOC) and functional size, to quantum software systems. LOC continues to be used for sizing quantum code, while COSMIC Function Points (ISO 19761) have been extended to capture data movements in quantum components, enabling their application at the requirement, code, and circuit levels. At the specification and architectural design levels, the proposed measures include counts of quantum classes and methods as well as lines of architectural specifications. At the circuit level, quantum-specific constructs, such as qubits, gates, and measurements, are also incorporated. Additionally, software structure metrics, including cyclomatic complexity and Halstead metrics, are used to measure quantum software at both the code and design levels. Research output has grown since 2024, and is driven by academic groups, with minimal industrial participation. However, most metrics remain conceptual, lack empirical validation, and are not integrated into development life cycles, highlighting the immaturity and fragmentation of current proposals and the need for standardized frameworks.

title: Structural Node Representation on Irregular Roles
authors: Wang Zhang, Wenjun Wang, Xuan Guo, Tianpeng Li, Pengfei Jiao, Lin Pan, Jian Wu, Xue Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Roles of nodes refer to the structural patterns of relations that reflect node identities or functions in the graph or network. Learning role-based node representations has been a hot topic in the last decade, as they are helpful in fully understanding the internal mechanisms of the network and improving performance on downstream tasks. In real-world networks, the dependencies between the roles are extremely complex, where the local structures of nodes can vary considerably even if they share the same role. However, existing methods ignore the varying dependencies and fail to discover roles having the irregular structure. To fully understand the above problems, we first propose an index based on a graph kernel to measure role irregularity, and analyze its influence on downstream tasks. Then for practically overcoming the shortcomings, we propose a novel framework based on a graph neural network and a variational auto-encoder with a cross-attention mechanism (GVCAM). It leverages the graph convolutional network to preserve the varying dependencies between nodes and the variational auto-encoder to capture the structural traits of nodes. We introduce the crossattention mechanism that fuses the two parts to adaptively learn the irregular roles. Extensive experiments on one synthetic and six real-world datasets show that GVCAM achieves competitive performance across multiple tasks compared with ten state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Influence of Grafting Modification on the Intrinsic Electronic Structure of Polypropylene Cable Insulation Materials: Insights from Single-Molecule Density Functional Theory
authors: Chenyu Gao, Wenyu Ye, Qingquan Duan, Shuai Hang, Xize Dai, Jian Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Grafting modification is an effective strategy to improve the electrical insulation performance of polypropylene (PP) for high-voltage cable applications; however, the underlying microscopic electronic mechanisms remain insufficiently understood. In this work, density functional theory (DFT) calculations are employed to systematically investigate the intrinsic electronic properties of pristine PP, styrene-grafted PP (PP-g-St), and AO3052- grafted PP (PP-g-AO) under external electric fields. The molecular conformation, electron density distribution, dipole moment, polarizability, hyperpolarizability, ionization energy, electron affinity, excitation characteristics, molecular orbital distribution, energy gap, and molecular surface electrostatic potential are comprehensively analyzed. The results indicate that grafting does not induce significant field-driven conformational changes but profoundly modifies the electronic structure of PP. Compared with pristine PP, grafted systems exhibit enhanced polarization capability, reduced excitation energy, increased electron affinity, and pronounced field-dependent electronic redistribution. In particular, PP-g-AO shows the strongest electron localization around oxygen-containing functional groups, the highest electron affinity, and the formation of deep and stable electron traps under high electric fields. Molecular orbital and electrostatic potential analyses further reveal that grafting introduces low-energy unoccupied states and highly heterogeneous surface electrostatic regions, which effectively capture injected electrons and suppress charge transport. These microscopic electronic features collectively explain the experimentally observed enhancement in breakdown strength of grafted PP. This study provides a fundamental molecular-level understanding of how grafting modification improves the insulation performance of polypropylene.

title: Compact Hybrid-Mode Diplexer With Nonresonant Nodes Based on MISL Technology
authors: Yu Zhan, Yi Wu, Ming Yin, Dongqi Gu, Ruipeng Liu, Weiqi Liu, Kaixue Ma, Kiat Seng Yeo
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a highly compact hybrid-mode diplexer based on the metal-integrated suspended line (MISL) technology. By embedding nonresonant nodes (NRNs) into suspended patch resonators, independently controllable transmission zeros (TZs) are generated to improve the interchannel isolation. A double-sided dual-mode metal-integrated waveguide (MIW) is employed as the common junction, which facilitates a seamless mode transition between the TE mode of the waveguide resonator and the TM mode of the patch resonator, synergizing the spatial compactness of suspended patch resonators with the high quality-factor of MIW resonators. Consequently, mode-conversion losses are effectively suppressed while preserving a miniaturized footprint. For validation, an X-band diplexer prototype was designed and fabricated. Experimental measurements reveal dual-channel operation at center frequencies of 10.36 and 12.28 GHz, with −15-dB reflection bandwidths of 9.31% and 8.91%, respectively. The realized minimum insertion losses of two channels are 0.85 and 0.77 dB, respectively, alongside an interchannel isolation exceeding 39 dB. This work provides an effective solution for the design of low-loss, high-isolation, and miniaturized diplexers.

title: Performance Enhancement of Classic and Differential P & O MPPT Used in PV System
authors: Jayanta Kumar Sahu, Dipika Debasmita Panda, Chittaranjan Swain, Sampad Kumar Pradhan, Mrutyunjay Senapati, Jyoti Prasad Patra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The response power of the PV system is affected by temperature, load impedance, and PV cell irradiation. Under certain climatic conditions, the system must be able to function close to its maximum power point in order to excerpt the maximum power from a PV cell. The response voltage or current of the PV system is adjusted for a specific load, irradiation level, and cell temperature using Maximum Power Point Tracking. But the load does not receive all of the real electricity generated by the PV array. As a result, it's crucial for obtaining the most electricity from the PV array. The key goal of this work is to examine MPPT techniques used in standalone PV and analyze Classic and Differential Perturb. The results will be compared based on accuracy or stability and Tracking efficiency. This approach, called the maximum power point methodology, is a simple and affordable way to monitor the solar PV array's maximum power as the weather changes. It offers a cost-effective maximum power point strategy that works with all PV systems. The system operated more effectively when monitoring the PV panel's maximum power.

title: A Wideband Parasitic Patch Antenna in a Rigid-Flex Thin Substrate for on-Body Communication
authors: Alessandro Ficarra, Emanuele Cardillo
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: This paper presents a patch antenna implemented on a multilayer rigid-flex substrate for communication in wearable applications. The antenna can be connected to the rest of the circuit through a flexible transmission line, which is able to conform to the curvatures of the human body or wearable devices such as smartwatches, rings, heart-rate bands, and wristbands. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$57-64 \text{GHz}$</tex> band is an unlicensed spectrum, enabling high-speed short-range on-body communication for wearable devices. The proposed antenna exhibits a wideband of 12 GHz and a flat gain of 6 dBi, thereby reducing the bit error rate and improving signal integrity.

title: Advanced Artificial Intelligence-Based Adaptive Approaches for Personalized Education: A Literature Review and Research Perspectives
authors: Hasna El Bacha, Asmae Ouhmida, Nezha Benmoussa, Manal Ouakil, Abdelhadi Raihani, Bouchaib Cherradi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: A single teaching method cannot fit all students’ needs, as the increase of digital learning spaces has been lightly proved. According to this, tailored education is recognized as a promising paradigm to enhance learning effectiveness by customizing education paths and content for each learner based on his or her own individual profile. Therefore, AI has been extensively investigated as a technology that can be applied to the development of Adaptive Learning (AL) systems that are capable of handling the personalization process on a large scale. In this paper, we examine the AI-enabled AL systems in the context of personalized learning, and we present an evaluation of them. It covers existing techniques, such as machine learning and high-level AI techniques. It analyses the pros and cons of how transparent the two are as systems, how pedagogically relevant they are, and how effective they are in terms of personalization. This reflection opens up avenues for researching the design of more transparent, robust, and learnercentered adaptive learning systems and reveals substantial underexplored research areas. This will help the researchers and practitioners to learn about the trends and the gaps existent in personalized education.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Wireless Communication Protocols for Battery Management Systems in Electric Vehicles
authors: Challa Krishna Rao, Sarat Kumar Sahoo, Gandam Deepika Rani, Ilupothi Jahnavi, Konchada Lahari, Bora Manikantha, Boddepalli Manmadha Kumar, B. Srinivasarao, V V Appalanaidu Menda
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Recent studies identify BLE and ZigBee as promising candidates for low power, short range multi node architectures, while Wi-Fi and UWB can deliver higher through put and lower latency with careful electromagnetic and reliability optimization. NFC based and hybrid approaches are also gaining interest for secure data readout, second life battery applications, and maintenance without continuous wireless connectivity. Beyond telemetry, next generation WBMS platforms are integrating cloud connectivity, digital twins, and machine learning algorithms to enable predictive diagnostics, remaining useful life estimation, and over the air (OTA) parameter optimization. These capabilities improve state of charge (SOC) and state of health (SOH) estimation accuracy while enabling fleet level monitoring and continuous, software defined performance enhancement throughout the battery lifecycle. However, the deployment of wireless BMS introduces new design challenges concerning cyber security, functional safety, synchronization, and coexistence with other in vehicle wireless systems, along with the need for standardized, automotive qualified communication protocols and certification frameworks. This paper surveys the state-of-the-art developments in wireless communication for EV BMS, encompassing industrial implementations, experimental demonstrators, and research proposals up to 2025. It provides a comparative analysis of BLE, ZigBee, Wi-Fi, UWB, LoRa, and NFC technologies in terms of performance, scalability, cost, and safety implications. On this basis, the study identifies application oriented technology choices and highlights promising hybrid and cloud integrated architectures that are shaping the future evolution of BMS in electric vehicles.

title: Fault Diagnosis in Analog Circuits Using Quadratic Discriminant Analysis Classifier
authors: Shaktikanta Jena, Karmila Soren, Tapas Kumar Patra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Analog circuitry continues to play a crucial role in modern electronic systems; however, its long-standing reliability has been increasingly challenged by soft and parametric defects that degrade operational integrity. This study presents a machine learning-based diagnostic architecture designed to detect and classify faults in analog integrated circuits (AICs). The proposed method exploits frequency-domain features extracted from the real and imaginary components of the output voltage and supply current responses, which offer strong discriminative capability. These features are systematically generated using frequency-domain Monte Carlo simulations. Several classification techniques, including Support Vector Machine (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-NN), Random Forest (RF), and Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA), were evaluated using standard performance metrics such as precision, recall, and overall accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that QDA delivers superior performance, achieving an average classification accuracy of 99.8%, and outperforming conventional neuro-fuzzy and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) approaches. Experimental validation was conducted using three benchmark circuits: a Sallen-Key band-pass filter, a four-op-amp high-pass filter, and a leapfrog filter. All circuits were modeled in SPICE, with component variations of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 50 \%$</tex> introduced to emulate realistic parametric deviations.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Calibration Method for a Wind-Speed Measurement Unit in Power-Grid Meteorological Devices
authors: Liming Wang, Qibin Zhou, Tengfei Li, Feng Zeng, Li Kang, Lei Zhao, Zhibo Ta
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The wind-speed measurement unit of power-grid meteorological devices plays an important role in power system operation monitoring and extreme-weather risk assessment. However, due to structural flow disturbance and varying operating conditions, the raw wind-speed measurements often exhibit systematic bias, making high-accuracy and deployment-ready calibration modeling necessary. This paper proposes a wind-speed calibration method based on a "physics baseline and residual learning". A power-law model is first adopted as the physics baseline to anchor the overall trend. Then, nonlinear bias is compensated via residual learning, where the Reynolds number is introduced as a representative physical feature to characterize operating-condition differences. An ensemble strategy is further employed to improve prediction stability. Comparative experiments are conducted using wind-tunnel calibration data. Under five repeated random 8:2 train/test splits, the proposed method achieves a test-set "RMSE of 0.1220 m/s (std 0.0115 m/s)" and "MAE of 0.0761 m/s (std 0.0021 m/s)", significantly outperforming the physics-only power-law baseline. Moreover, the empirical CDF of absolute error shows "P90 = 0.1459 m/s" and "P95 = 0.1868 m/s", confirming stable accuracy for the majority of samples. Additional analyses under different wind-speed ranges and repeated random splits further verify the robustness of the proposed method.The results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively compensates nonlinear bias while preserving physically consistent trends, providing a practical solution for engineering deployment of wind-speed calibration in power-grid meteorological devices.

title: First Experimental Demonstration of Metasurface-Enhanced Detection of Small Scatterers Using an Open-Ended Coaxial Probe
authors: Eleonora Razzicchia, Milad Mokhtari, Rotem Gal-Katzir, Yunxiao Zhang, Yarden Mazor, Emily Porter
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Metasurfaces (MTSs) can improve electromagnetic coupling at material interfaces and have strong potential for enhancing microwave dielectric sensing. This work presents the first experimental demonstration of an MTS matching layer used with an open-ended coaxial probe (OECP) to improve detection of small embedded scatterers. A PEC-grid MTS, designed as an impedance-matching layer using a transmission-line analytical model, was fabricated and tested on custom samples consisting of a homogeneous background medium and a medium containing a small inclusion. Reflection measurements from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-20 \text{GHz}$</tex> show that the MTS improves field penetration and greatly increases the variations observed in the measurements between the two samples, making the embedded scatterer much more detectable. These preliminary proof-of-concept results indicate that MTS-based matching layers can enhance OECP sensitivity and provide a low-cost, practical solution for improving dielectric characterization in materials and biological tissues.

title: Comparison Study of Tianmu-1 Radio Occultation Data with ERA5 and MERRA-2 Reanalysis Datasets
authors: Xia Sheng, Zewen Ming, Guowen Ma
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The radio occultation data from the domestic Tianmu-1 constellation provide a high-quality data source for global meteorological observation. This study uses June 2024 Tianmu-1 radio occultation temperature profiles as the main dataset. ERA5 and MERRA-2 reanalysis data are adopted as reference benchmarks. We build a standardized spatiotemporal matching and quality control framework over the conterminous United States <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(25^{\circ} \mathrm{N}-49^{\circ} \mathrm{N}, 67^{\circ} \mathrm{W}-124^{\circ} \mathrm{W}\right)$</tex>. We then compare the datasets from three perspectives: overall statistics, vertical stratification, and regional spatial distribution. We also analyze detection accuracy variations and error sources at different altitude layers. The results show that the correlation coefficient between Tianmu-1 temperature data and the two reanalysis datasets reaches 0.989, and the mean biases relative to ERA5 and MERRA-2 are −1.35 K and −1.42 K, respectively. The detection accuracy in the middle and upper atmosphere (above 500 hPa) is excellent. The absolute value of bias is mostly less than 1 K. A significant systematic negative bias (−4.28 K to −5.44 K) appears in the lower troposphere (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$700-850 \text{hPa}$</tex>). This bias is caused by the inherent physical mechanism of radio occultation detection and water vapor. This study confirms that Tianmu-1 radio occultation data have good overall applicability. They can provide reliable domestic data support for meteorological observation over the conterminous United States. This work also serves as a technical reference for error correction and operational application of GNSS radio occultation data.

title: Towards a Physics-Informed Deep Learning Approach for 3D Phaseless Microscopy
authors: Nicola Cuzzocrea, Nadia Mammone, Cosimo Ieracitano, Yingying Qin, Krishna Agarwal, Martina T. Bevacqua
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The inverse scattering problem consists in reconstructing the contrast of an unknown object from measurements of the scattered field it generates in response to a given illumination. The solution of such a problem is relevant in various applications, for instance, microscopy, biomedical microwave imaging and subsurface imaging. This paper proposes a new method based on a physics-informed neural network for the solution of the inverse scattering problem underlying 3D phaseless microscopy. The network takes as input the solution obtained by means of the Born Approximation. Furthermore, a loss function is adopted that integrates the physics of the scattering phenomenon. The method is preliminarily tested against simulated data using the MNIST dataset, which contains both handwritten numerals and letters and is a standard benchmark for machine learningbased approaches.

title: Crossmodal-Bart: a Unified Vision-Language Model for Video Summarization
authors: Gautham S N, Satish K, Akshai A, Santhi S, Kalaiselvi S
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The rapid growth of online videos, crafting short and meaningful summaries is very important for easy understanding, quick viewing, and for saving storage space. Most methods developed so far have their focus either only on visual features or generate summaries directly from captions, yielding simplistic or low-context outputs. This work proposes CrossModal-BART, a video summarization framework where video visual understanding and text generation are combined into one seamless workflow. It uses BLIP to extract visual features from a collection of selected video frames, which are then converted into a few short captions. The resulting captions are ordered to keep the same order as in the video. Finally, BART takes this sequence of captions as input and creates a clear and readable summary. The proposed framework truly understands not only what is present in the video but also the context and meaning of the video. Experimental results revealed that CrossModal-BART generates more accurate, less repetitive and easier-tounderstand summaries than the traditional approaches. Comprehensive experiments on the benchmark dataset demonstrates that CrossModal-BART achieves significant improvements over state-of-the-art baselines: 61% ROUGE-1 score (vs. 52% for caption-only methods), 86% BERTScore (vs. 71%), and superior human evaluation ratings for coherence and informativeness. Applications of this system can be extended to educational videos, surveillance footages, media content, and a lot of other real-world applications.

title: Comparative study of textual feature extraction methods and machine learning models for the classification of industrial accidents
authors: Kawtar Benderouach, Idriss Bennis, Khalifa Mansouri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Workplace accidents in the energy sector are a significant issue in terms of industrial safety, given their human and financial implications. Automated analysis of accident reports could be very useful for understanding and preventing these accidents. In this study, machine learning models are compared using these two accident types’ binary classifications according to their text descriptions. This study evaluates several techniques that may be used to extract textual features, such as Bag of Words (BOW), TF-IDF, Word2Vec, BERT, and TF-IDF + BERT. Several classification models used in this research are: logistic regression, SVM, decision tree, random forest, and XGBoost. To optimize the model’s accuracy, grid search optimization is done, while to deal with class imbalance, SMOTE technique is employed. The experimental results reveal an important conclusion: despite the recognized effectiveness of contextual encodings such as BERT, the best performance in terms of accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score is achieved by using the traditional BOW representation combined with SMOTE and GridSearch. This demonstrates that simpler representations combined with appropriate optimization strategies can outperform more complex language models for analyzing the severity of workplace accidents and managing industrial risks.

title: Robust Radiometric Normalization for Hyperspectral Time-Series Analysis via Superpixel-Constrained Pseudo-Invariant Feature Regions Extraction
authors: Junyu Chen, Geng Zhang, Xiangpeng Feng, Haiwei Li, Yali Wu, Silei Lian, Siyuan Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Radiometric inconsistencies across multi-temporal hyperspectral images severely compromise the reliability of time-series analysis. To address this issue, this paper proposes a robust radiometric normalization algorithm based on superpixel-constrained extraction of object-based pseudo-invariant feature regions. The algorithm first converts multi-temporal data into surface reflectance space to reduce major environmental disturbances. A superpixel segmentation strategy is then introduced to extend pseudo-invariant feature extraction from the pixel level to the object level, improving robustness against geometric misregistration and noise. Furthermore, a joint constraint mechanism integrating statistical, spatial, and spectral information is incorporated into the IR-MAD algorithm to enhance the reliability of pseudo-invariant region selection. Finally, a histogram-matching-based radiometric mapping model is constructed to achieve consistent normalization of multi-temporal reflectance data. Experimental results on ZY-1E hyperspectral imagery demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly improves radiometric consistency, with the mean relative absolute error controlled within 4.2 %, thereby enabling more robust change detection and quantitative analysis in hyperspectral time-series applications.

title: From Industry 5.0 to Maintenance 5.0: A Roadmap for the Evolution of Maintenance Practices and the Operator 5.0 Competency Framework
authors: Soukaina Sadiki, Driss Amegouz, Said Boutahari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The transition to Industry 5.0 is bringing about a profound change in maintenance practices, which can no longer be viewed solely from the perspective of technological deployment. In this context, Maintenance 5.0 is emerging as a human-centric and resilient paradigm, aiming to coherently integrate advanced technologies, organizational processes, and human skills. However, the existing literature addresses these dimensions in a fragmented manner, without proposing a structured framework to support their joint implementation. This article proposes an integrated approach to Maintenance 5.0 based on three main contributions. First, it analyzes the adaptation and use of Industry 5.0 technologies in maintenance activities, highlighting their impact on decisionmaking, disruption management, and the resilience of industrial systems. Second, a multidimensional roadmap is proposed to describe the gradual evolution of maintenance practices towards Maintenance 5.0, integrating technological, organizational, human, physical, and environmental dimensions. Finally, this evolution leads to a redefinition of the role of the operator, formalized through the concept of Operator 5.0, for which a structured skills framework is developed, covering the physical, cognitive, social, and digital aspects necessary for the operationalization of this new paradigm.

title: Roof top PV system forecasting under urban climate of Fez, Morocco, Based on ML Models
authors: El Ouastani Nasyra, Moussaoui Mohammed Amine, Amraqui Samir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of photovoltaic (PV) energy production is essential for enhancing the operational efficiency and reliability of solar energy systems. This study addresses the challenge of PV energy prediction using advanced machine learning techniques, namely Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) models. The integration and comparative evaluation of these approaches aim to improve forecasting accuracy and support more reliable, data-driven decision-making in energy management. The analysis is based on real production data collected from a rooftop photovoltaic installation located in Fez, Morocco, combined with four key meteorological variables obtained from the Copernicus database. Unlike utility-scale solar power plants, rooftop PV systems are highly sensitive to local environmental conditions, resulting in increased variability and making their prediction more complex and less explored in the existing literature. A comparative assessment of the selected machine learning models was conducted within this specific context. The results indicate that the Random Forest model achieves the highest predictive performance ($\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.8722$), followed by XGBoost ($R^{2}=0.8606$) and SVM ($R^{2}=0.77$). These findings confirm the effectiveness of machine learning approaches for PV forecasting and highlight their potential for improving energy production predictions at the local scale.

title: Decoding the “Class Attraction” Effect in Satellite Imagery: A Quantitative Explainable AI Approach
authors: Jbrane Abrouch, Redouan Korchiyne, Rabiaa Cheikh Maoulainine, Rachid Dahmani, Mustapha Tahiri Alaoui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: While deep learning architectures have achieved remarkable success in Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) classification from high-resolution satellite imagery, their inherent “black-box” nature limits their reliability in critical Earth observation applications. Recent literature has identified the “class attraction” effect-a phenomenon where models consistently misclassify spectrally overlapping classes, such as Agriculture and Barren Land. However, traditional evaluation metrics, such as the mean Intersection over Union (mIoU), fail to elucidate the spatial reasoning behind these decision failures. In this paper, we introduce a quantitative Explainable AI (XAI) framework to decode this phenomenon. Using Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), we extract the spatial attention of four distinct Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures: U-Net, SegNet, PSPNet, and DeepLabV3+, evaluated on the DeepGlobe dataset. To transcend subjective visual inspection, we propose two novel spatial explainability metrics: Attention-Intersection over Union (A-IoU) and the Energy Ratio. Our quantitative results demonstrate that standard encoderdecoder models like U-Net exhibit severe attention scattering, capturing only 45.0% of their activation energy within the target class boundaries (A-IoU of 0.25). In contrast, DeepLabV3+ demonstrates highly focused spatial reasoning, directing 82.0% of its energy to the correct topological features (A-IoU of 0.68). These findings prove mathematically that multi-scale contextual aggregation, such as Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP), is essential not only for accuracy but for mitigating the class attraction effect by anchoring the model’s focus on global geometric consistency rather than deceptive local spectral intensities.

title: Study and Analysis of Approaches to Optimizing Coordination Between Processes in Industry 4.0
authors: Manal Tahri, Bachir Benhala, Mohamed El Khaili
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Industry 4.0 reflects a paradigm change driven by the extensive deployment of Cyber Physical Systems (CPS). The fourth revolution brings about changes in paradigms. As the world has become globalized, companies find themselves in a volatile business environment that requires coordination of processes as not a choice, but necessity. Our paper addresses the inherent complexity of managing dispersed processes, which constitutes a major challenge. The synchronization between production, logistics, and maintenance (PLM) often suffers from informational silos. In a mostly multi-site environment, the lack of seamless coordination leads to prolonged downtime and a degradation of service rates. Our research provides syntheses of proposed models capable of synchronizing these functions in real-time to minimize overall costs under resource constraints.

title: Geo-Contextual Information Extraction and Summarization System using NLP and Large Language Models
authors: B. Krishnakumar, Rajesh Kumar G, Malathi Eswaran, Vikkram R, S Sadesh, T. Kumaresan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The internet has no shortage of information about famous cities and landmarks. But ask about a small town in rural Tamil Nadu or a village in rural Bangladesh, and chances are that you will be directed to a wikipedia article or a third party unverified website that buries the interesting bits therein under an avalanche of census statistics, electoral history and administrative classifications. GCIES (Geo-Contextual Information Extraction and Summarization) was designed to correct exactly that. Given nothing more than a location name, GCIES automatically finds, filters and summarizes what that place is known and famous for. The system concurrently fetches the data on Wikipedia and OneFiveNine village database, processes the input through a custom Geographic Scoring Algorithm to select the appropriate result, filters out the noise with a SpaCy Named Entity Recognition filter, reducing the number of tokens used by more than 80 percent before it ever reaches Llama 3.3 70B, which generates 6 to 7 brief, culturally relevant insights with the help of the Groq API. Previously queried locations are served directly from an Upstash Redis cache within less than 100 milliseconds and entirely bypasses the pipeline. The entire system is a full-stack web application: React (Vite) on the frontend, Python FastAPI on the backend, deployed on Render. The architecture has been documented, along with design choices of each phase, and experimental results validating the system's token efficiency, disambiguation accuracy, and latency.

title: Latent-Heat Cloaking: Adaptive Thermal Invisibility via Phase-Change Metamaterials
authors: Ahlam Alaoui, Mohammed Lahsaini, Younes Achaoui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Thermal metamaterials enable precise manipulation of heat flux through mechanisms inaccessible to conventional materials. Among their most compelling functionalities, thermal cloaks redirect heat around a protected region while preserving the surrounding temperature field, rendering the core thermally invisible. Conventional cloak designs, however, rely on static material constituents. They remain intrinsically passive under time-varying boundary conditions, leaving the protected region unable to buffer thermal excursions autonomously. This work presents the first investigation of thermodynamic phase-change materials (PCMs) as functional constituents of a transformationthermotics cloak. It constitutes a proof of concept for a new class of adaptive thermal metamaterials. PCMs replace passive polymer layers within a concentric multilayer architecture. They absorb and release substantial latent heat across a narrow melting interval, giving the cloak a thermal shielding and storage capacity by stabilizing its core during transient excitations, without any external actuation. Transient finiteelement simulations are performed under sinusoidal boundary temperatures ranging from 10 to $40{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, across excitation periods from 30 to 240 min. The PCM-enhanced cloak reduces the core oscillation gain to $\mathbf{1. 6 \%}$, compared to $\mathbf{3 5. 1 \%}$ for the unstructured reference case. Controllable time lags reaching 57 min are achieved depending on the PCM selected. External field restoration is preserved throughout, confirming that thermal invisibility and adaptive core protection coexist within a single passive architecture.

title: Interpretable Diabetes Risk Classification using Optimized Deep Learning and Baseline Machine Learning Models
authors: Sujith Jayaprakash, Annette Nayana Nellyet, Chitra Ganesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Diabetes prediction is one of the important health-related applications in the deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) community. However, most of the studies on diabetes prediction have focused on achieving high accuracy and ignored the effect of the demographic constraints and class imbalance in the datasets. Although they achieve good results, the results are not validated. In this paper, we propose a transparent prediction model that integrates the optimal deep learning model with the traditional machine learning approaches. We applied median imputation and Min-Max normalization on the dataset and applied the SMOTE-based oversampling technique only on the training folds to avoid data leaking. We performed 5-fold cross-validation experiments on logistic regression, feedforward neural networks, and an autoencoder-augmented classifier and presented the accuracy and F1-score along with the threshold-dependent ROC–AUC and precision–recall curves. The logistic regression model is used as a baseline while the modified neural network achieved an accuracy of 85% for discrimination. The SHAP analysis provided that the glucose, BMI, and insulin are the most important features which is in line with the clinical symptoms of diabetes. The main limitation of this study is that the results are only validated in a gender-specific dataset and there is no validation of the generalizability of the findings. The advantages of the proposed model are that the prediction accuracy and interpretability can be achieved simultaneously which addresses the long-standing problem of diabetes prediction.

title: XAI for Automated Prediction Model for Cyber Incident Response and Security Support in Cloud
authors: Chandra Shekar Reddy Avula
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The rising number and sophistication of cyber incidents in the cloud environments necessitates intelligent, explainable, and automated response mechanisms to assist in making timely and dependable protection decisions. The classic incident response systems have been noted to have low flexibility, lacks visibility, and slow forensic investigation mechanisms that make them less effective in countering new threats. The paper will find a solution to these issues by providing an explainable artificial intelligence (XAI)-based automated prediction model on cyber incident response and support of security in cloud infrastructures. A domain attention (DAtt) mechanism with critical incident features, a modified electric fish optimization (MEFO) algorithm to select optimal features and policy tuning, and a deep Q-network (DQN) to be trained and analyze the patterns of complex cyber incidents to make adaptive decisions. Reinforcement learning-based post-incident investigation was supported by generating a controlled malware forensics dataset of 26 paired infected and uninfected memory dumps based on 13 isolated windows virtual machines analysed with the Volatility framework. The experimental outcomes demonstrate the high accuracy of the proposed XAI-enabled model, the high response time, and the enhanced interpretability, the effectiveness of the integration of XAI with DQN in providing reliable cloud-based cyber incident responses.

title: Adopting artificial intelligence applications in legal education- studying the case of faculty of law at University of Petra
authors: Ali Mohamed Aldabbas, Mohammad Saeed Abdallah ALsheyab, Ma’en Abdel-Rahim Juwaihan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study addresses the most important areas of use of artificial intelligence applications among students of the Faculty of Law at Petra University with the aim of enhancing legal education and exploring the sources of concern and challenges faced by students when using applications based on artificial intelligence in legal education, and the role of the faculty in guiding students towards integrating artificial intelligence applications into legal education with the aim of facilitating effective integration of artificial intelligence into curricula and preparing for the future legal scene. The study reveals an increase in awareness and reliance of students of the Faculty of Law at Petra University on applications based on artificial intelligence in various areas of legal learning. However, there are still difficulties and challenges in the use of artificial intelligence tools among students, such as insufficient training for students, weak university technological infrastructure, linguistic challenges especially the scarcity of Arabic legal applications based on artificial intelligence, and ethical concerns such as reliability, bias, and "hallucination" in artificial intelligence outputs. To cope with these issues, the study confirms the need for institutional investment in advanced digital infrastructure, training programs, and improved AI-focused curricula.

title: Real-Time Unified Spam Detection System for Email and SMS
authors: Badal Singh, Rishab Mishra, Krishnakant Nagina, Syed Rameez Ali, Rajesh Tiwari, Nikhat Raza Khan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A machine learning framework for identifying spam in emails and SMS is presented in the study. TF-IDF and Bag-of-Words embeddings are used after preprocessing techniques like tokenization, lemmatization, and stop-word removal. An 80–20 split was used to train ten classifiers, including Naïve Bayes variants, and they were assessed using ROC-AUC, accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and Z-score. With 99.20% accuracy and 0.98 ROC-AUC, ensemble models demonstrated strong reliability and outperformed other models. According to linguistic analysis, spam messages usually contain more words and characters and have intricate structures. The system demonstrated great potential for cybersecurity applications and was implemented via a graphical user interface (GUI) for voice-based and real-time spam detection

title: An Accurate Method for 3D Model Classification using the Explainable Boosting Classifier
authors: Fatima Rafii Zakani, Mohcine Bouksim, Khadija Arhid, Taoufiq Gadi, Mohamed Aboulfatah
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Accurate and efficient 3D model classification is still a major challenge in computer vision and other fields. This paper presents a new method which is expected to achieve high classification performance. The proposed approach is organised in two major phases. First, we extract a discriminative feature vector by computing three powerful geometric measures, i.e., Shape Diameter Function (SDF), dihedral angles, and the Shape Index, and fuse them into a unified representation for each 3D object. Second, this composite feature vector is used to train an Explainable Boosting Classifier (EBC), a powerful ensemble learning model, to perform the classification task. The effectiveness of the proposed method was thoroughly tested on the Princeton Benchmark dataset. Experimental results show that our proposed method can get a better classification accuracy than several leading state-of-the-art methods.

title: ICSI 2026 Author Information Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)

title: Development of a Non-invasive Blood Glucose Wristwatch System Based on PPG Signal
authors: Yutong Liu, Guangyu Zhang, Hongyu Zhu, Jian Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Continuous and non-invasive blood glucose monitoring is essential for the effective management of diabetes and the prevention of chronic complications. This paper presents a wearable wristwatch system for non-invasive blood glucose monitoring based on multi-wavelength photoplethysmography (PPG) signals. The hardware architecture integrates a multiwavelength optical acquisition module $(540 \mathrm{~nm} / 660 \mathrm{~nm} / 940 \mathrm{~nm})$, a contact pressure sensing module to ensure signal fidelity, and a low-power microcontroller unit. A hierarchical network architecture was implemented, comprising a data acquisition layer, an edge access layer via a smartphone application, and a cloud computing layer for sophisticated signal analysis. To ensure high prediction accuracy, a robust preprocessing pipeline was developed, utilizing a refined valley-detection algorithm for cycle segmentation and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to extract 11 key morphological features. The glucose regression model was established using Support Vector Regression (SVR) to map the nonlinear relationship between PPG features and glycemic levels. In vivo experiments demonstrate that the model effectively tracks blood glucose trends. This study provides a promising technical framework for mobile, non-invasive, and continuous glycemic monitoring.

title: Design of gyroidal structures for heat exchanger applications, manufactured with binder jetting using copper
authors: Daniel Mayer, Sandipan Sen, Jörg Fischer-Bühner, Daniel Beckers, Alexander Kauffmann, Stefan Dietrich, Frederik Zanger
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The thermal management of modern vehicle systems requires the use of high-performance heat exchangers that are also designed to be compact to guarantee the best possible integration into the overall system architecture. In this context, additive manufacturing opens up the possibility of using more complex structures to generate larger exchange surfaces and a 3D fluid flow using gyroid structures. Binder jetting is a process that has great potential for these applications, as its fast build rates and scalability make it suitable to produce medium quantities and easy processing of Cu. However, to enable its broad transfer of this technology to industry and to ensure the applicability of the process, it is necessary to better understand the process in terms of its production limits regarding thin-walled structures.In this work, the design limits of gyroid structures were evaluated with respect to their suitability for depowdering using various test geometries. An evaluation method was presented that makes it possible to relate the depowdering of samples to the respective slices based on CT-images. It was found that the gyroid design parameters have a decisive influence on the depowdering of the manufactured components. It was shown that the depth effect of compressed air during the depowdering step is limited and becomes more difficult with scaled component dimensions. In addition, the depth effect decreases with increasing wall thickness and decreasing unit cell size. However, stretching the unit cell of the gyroid structures along the depowdering direction has proven to be positive.

title: Large-Signal Output Tracking and Stabilization of a DC–DC Boost Using Improved Takagi–Sugeno Modeling and Gain-Scheduled LQR Control
authors: Othmane Maakoul, Abdellah Boulal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of large-signal output tracking and stabilization of DC-DC boost converters using a Takagi-Sugeno (TS) fuzzy control framework. While core techniques such as TS modeling, Parallel Distributed Compensation (PDC), Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR), and Linear Matrix Inequalities (LMIs) are well established in the literature, their effective integration for large-signal tracking with reduced computational complexity remains a relevant challenge. To this end, an improved control strategy is proposed based on a reduced-rule TS fuzzy model, which preserves accuracy while lowering implementation complexity. An augmented-state formulation with integral action is introduced to ensure zero steady-state error. Furthermore, a gain-scheduled LQR controller is developed within the PDC framework, where controller gains are adapted online according to fuzzy membership functions.The stability of the closed-loop system is guaranteed using LMI-based conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed integrated approach improves tracking performance and robustness under large-signal variations compared to conventional methods.

title: PSEF-Net: A Spiral-based Attention Edge Fusion Network for Parkinsons’ Disease
authors: Yixin Lin, Haoming Ye, Cheng Yang, Xiaolong Chai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Early Parkinson’s disease is often accompanied by a decline in fine motor control, manifesting as increased line fluctuations and unstable trajectory control during writing and drawing. To address the challenge of simultaneously capturing overall morphological information and local anomaly details in the classification of Parkinson’s spiral images, this paper proposes PSEF-Net (Parkinson’s Spiral Attention and Edge Fusion Network). This method takes the original image and edge map as input, extracts features through the ResNet50 main branch and the Canny edge branch respectively, and introduces CBAM (Convolutional Block Attention Module) after the high-level features of the main branch to enhance the response of key regions. Detection is then completed through feature fusion and a classification head. Five-fold cross-validation results on the HandPD dataset show that the model achieves average accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and AUC of $90.17\%, 89.92\%, 0.902, 0.897$, and 0.941, respectively. Compared with the ResNet50 baseline model, the proposed method improves accuracy by 7.74 percentage points, providing a low-cost, non-invasive, and repeatable preliminary risk assessment for early screening of Parkinson’s disease.

title: Characterizing and Accelerating Spacecraft Onboard Workloads on RISC-V Platform
authors: Boul Chandra Garai, Sumit Kumar Mandal, K. R. Yogesh Prasad, R Govindarajan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: This paper studies 28 production spacecraft workloads from ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) missions to find out which computations actually limit performance on resource-constrained space processors. Spacecraft onboard processors face strict power and thermal constraints while performing critical real-time calculations for navigation, control, and data processing. Using a Basic Computational Element (BCE) breakdown, we find that software trigonometric calls (up to 36% of execution time) and small matrix operations (13%–41%) are the main bottlenecks across navigation, attitude control, guidance, and remote-sensing workloads. To check whether these findings translate into useful hardware, we build two domainspecific accelerators—a CORDIC (COordinate Rotation DIgital Computer) trigonometric unit and a 3 × 3 systolic matrix accelerator—integrated into a RISC-V processor through custom instruction set extensions (RVTrig, RVMatrix) and evaluated using cycle-approximate gem5 simulation. Across 18 representative workloads, the combined accelerators give a 3.13× geometric mean speedup and 37.2% energy reduction, reaching up to 9.29× on the most accelerator-friendly workload. Section VI introduces a timestep-based roofline model that tracks algorithmic progress rather than raw operations, so that an accelerated system no longer looks slower under the standard FLOPs-based view.

title: Configuring Circular Supply Networks: Digital Technology Embedded Pathways
authors: Shenghao Xie, Atif Sarwar, Yu Gong, Eric Ka Ho Leung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Circular Supply Networks (CSNs) are essential organizational forms for advancing the transition to a circular economy and can be supported by digital technologies. However, limited understanding exists of how digital technologies become embedded in CSN configurations. This study addresses this gap by examining the structuring logics and governance dynamics of digital technology-enabled CSNs. The study employs a rigorous multiple-case study design, drawing on 30 interviews and extensive archival data analyzed through a systematic coding approach. Applying Actor–Network Theory (ANT), this research analyzes five blockchain applications across different industries to investigate how digital technologies become embedded in CSN configurations. The findings reveal four translation processes underlying CSN configuration: anchoring actor tensions, actor-centric alignment, technology-embedded governance, and the emergence of configured CSNs. These processes give rise to two distinct configuration pathways, enhancement and structuration. By integrating ANT, the study advances understanding of how digital technologies configure CSNs by unpacking the sociotechnical dynamics through which human and non-human actors co-shape CSNs. The insights highlight the importance of adopting a network-oriented perspective when implementing circular initiatives and designing governance mechanisms that align with underlying value creation.

title: Trap-Based Modeling and Mechanisms of Non-Monotonic Hot-Carrier Degradation in Cryogenic CMOS Devices
authors: Junru Qu, Xinze Li, Dong Liu, Ran Tao, Yuanxi Liao, Yuchen Gu, Di Yang, Qing Tian, Ying Sun, Yongyu Wu, Dawei Gao, Bing Chen, Yong Ding, Genquan Han, Ran Cheng
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: This work experimentally characterized and investigated the temperature dependence of hot carrier injection (HCI) at low temperature (LT) for 130 nm foundry-level CMOS with no resolvable transient self-heating-effect (SHE) induced current droop under the tested conditions. Cryogenic virtual source transistor model together with trap models at LT are developed and employed to extract the related carrier and trap parameters.
