The BLDC motors are tremendously used in the high-Power applications as a result of their high efficiency, reliability and the ratio between the torque and weight. Nevertheless, they hold nonlinear nature and responsive to parameter changes and exogenous shocks, which makes their speed control quite difficult. PI controllers are easy to understand and implement but in dynamic working conditions do not have much robustness. Conversely, SMC requires large inherent noise and model uncertainties resistance albeit the threat of chatter and complexity. To take advantage of the complementary benefits between the two control paradigms, a hybrid controller is constructed on the basis of which the PI controller is used to achieve stable, tracking, and other desired properties in the steady-state performance, and SMC is used to achieve reliable system performance, robustness, and dynamic characteristics. The suggested control architecture actively mixes the PI and SMC actions according to the working regime of a system and, in this way, optimizes performance in a wide range of regimes. In order to determine the effectiveness of the controller, a mathematical model of the BLDC motor will be simulated to perform a study that is based on nonlinear dynamics and the presence of load disturbance. The findings confirm that the hybrid SMC-PI controller attains a quicker response time, less overshoot as well as better disturbance rejection than both standalone PI and SMC controllers. Besides, the hybrid strategy has largely reduced chattering that is characteristic of SMC providing a smoother control signal. The control scheme has also high resistance to parameter changes and thus can be applied practically in the industrial field and automotive field. In this piece of work, interest has been on the possibility of intelligent hybrid control systems in promoting reliability and efficiency of sophisticated electric motor drives.

title: Based on the 3D Point Smart Recognizion of Buildings Structural Facades and Quantity its Damages*
authors: Jiayu Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The traditional building structure surface damage detection technology has the problems of low efficiency, low precision and great dependence on artificial experience. In this paper, an intelligent damage identification and evaluation method based on 3D point cloud is proposed to solve the current technical problems. The high-precision point cloud data of the target object is obtained by 3D laser scanning, and the optimized RandLA-Net neural network is used for preprocessing and dimensionality reduction, which greatly improves the data processing speed. Furthermore, a deep learning model based on dynamic graph convolution network and attention mechanism is established to analyze the common damage types such as cracks, spalling and corrosion from the aspects of curvature characteristics and normal information. Considering the differences between the actual structure and the ideal geometric model, a multi-dimensional damage quantitative evaluation index system is formed to achieve the purpose of accurate grading. From the experimental results, it can be seen that the proposed method is better than the traditional detection method in terms of positioning accuracy and measurement accuracy. The results provide important support for the innovation and development of building safety monitoring technology, and have broad prospects.

title: MRFO Based Virtual Inertia Controller for Frequency Stabilization of Islanded Micro-Grid
authors: P. Mohanty, S. Padhan, A. K. Barisal
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: In this present work, Manta Ray Foraging Optimization (MRFO) algorithm optimized ProportionalIntegral (PI) controller is used for controlling of frequency of a standalone micro grid (MG). The MG system is made up of a Diesel Engine Generator (DEG), Fuel Cell (FC), Micro Turbine (MT) and photovoltaic (PV) systems with maximum power point trackers (MPPT). The virtual inertia control (VIC) is also used to provide the inertia during transient condition. Three PI controllers are used for controlling of MPPT, VIC and Diesel engine generator. The MRFO algorithm uses an Integral of Squared Error (ISE) objective function to optimize the gains of the PI controllers. The simulation is carried out with different case studies. When compared to the results of other optimization approaches for the same MG, such as Salp Search Algorithm, Particle Swarm Optimization, and Teaching Learning Based Optimization, the MRFO-based PI controller outperforms them in all scenarios. According to the simulation findings, the MRFO technique gives better dynamic responses like overshoot, undershoot value, settling time, and Integral squared Error. It is also observed that with presence of VIC, the performance of MG improved.

title: An Efficient Preprocessing based Transfer Learning Approach for Multi-Plant Disease Recognition
authors: Vijaya Bhasker Reddy, S. Satheeskumaran
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Plant leaf diseases are a major factor affecting agricultural productivity, particularly in countries that depend heavily on agriculture. Leaf diseases occurring during the crop growth reduce the overall agricultural yield results in financial losses to farmers. Multi-plant leaf disease detection is necessary to address crop growth issues, support farmers, and improve agricultural productivity. Existing transfer learning approaches are not suitable for multi-plant leaf disease classification involving multiple disease classes. Hence, this work proposes a multi-class leaf disease detection and classification model suitable for multiple crops. Four different transfer learning models, namely Deep CNN, AlexNet, ResNet50, and VGG16, have been implemented and assessed using three different plant datasets: tomato, potato, and cotton. The collected datasets were preprocessed using median filters before classification. The preprocessing could make the data sets better for learning and classification of models. The obtained results of proposed preprocessing based VGG16 transfer learning model achieved the average classification accuracy of 95.5% which is highest among all the four models being tested, while evaluating on three selected plant datasets.

title: Feasibility of Single-camera Markerless Motion Analysis During the Long Jump Take-off Phase
authors: Koki Fujihara, Kai Kobayashi, Hongjie Zheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: Recent advances in deep learning have enabled markerless motion capture using monocular pose estimation, but its applicability to high-speed sports movements remains insufficiently validated. This study examined the feasibility of single-camera, two-dimensional (2D) motion analysis of the take-off phase of the long jump by quantitatively evaluating positional errors relative to a three-dimensional reference system. Video data captured by a single RGB camera were processed using AlphaPose to extract 2D body keypoints, and the center of gravity (CoG) was estimated from bilateral shoulder and hip keypoints. Estimation accuracy was evaluated using the mean absolute error (MAE) for the four keypoints and CoG in horizontal and vertical directions. CoG estimation yielded smaller errors than individual keypoints, indicating improved stability through multiple-keypoint integration. The error magnitude varied among the keypoints and coordinate directions, with horizontal errors generally being larger for the hip keypoints and CoG, whereas no consistent trend was observed for the shoulders. In addition, noticeable between-trial variability, as reflected by the standard deviations of MAE, was observed despite identical experimental conditions. These findings suggest that single-camera markerless motion analysis can capture the overall characteristics of CoG motion during the long jump take-off phase, supporting its practical utility in real-world sports environments.

title: Efficient Top-r Reliable Community Detection in Temporal Networks
authors: Xinyan Li, Peilong Li, Chuanyu Zong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: Community detection in temporal networks has emerged as a significant research topic in complex network management, which seeks to identify communities based on a given subgraph model. While previous studies primarily focus on detecting communities solely based on structural properties, they often ignore essential factors, such as member engagement, connection strength, and cohesion continuity, which are critical for assessing the reliability of communities in temporal networks. Motivated by these limitations, we investigate the problem of top-r reliable community detection based on the (θ, k)-core model in temporal networks, aiming to identify top-r reliable communities ranked by their reliability scores. Due to the high computational complexity, we first propose a baseline algorithm, DpBCRC, leveraging the WCF-Index and dynamic programming. We further optimize the WCF-Index and propose a novel detection algorithm, IBTCD. To enhance efficiency, we design pruning strategie based on valid edge boundaries, leading to the optimized algorithm IBTCD-OPT. Experimental results on eight real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of our methods.

title: Hybrid MCTS++: An Adaptive Optimizer for Enhanced Game-Playing Performance
authors: Faris Abuhashish, Mohammed Atoum, Isma H. Ismail
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Model-based reinforcement learning combines neural networks with planning to achieve superhuman performance in board and video games. MuZero exemplifies this by learning a model and using Monte Carlo tree search (MCTS) for planning. However, its search component employs unstable prior normalization, stochastic action selection, and a fixed simulation budget, all of which limit performance and data efficiency, particularly in low-data regimes. We propose a hybrid optimization method called Hybrid MCTS++, which improves the search element of MuZero. The method computes numerically stable priors using a masked softmax over legal actions, deterministically selects actions by taking the maximum UCB score, and adapts the number of simulations according to the entropy of the root policy distribution. This adaptive schedule allocates more simulations when the search is uncertain and fewer when the model is confident. We mathematically formulate the algorithm, provide pseudocode, and evaluate it in the Tic-Tac-Toe environment included in the MuZero repository. Against a random opponent, our hybrid search achieved 100% accuracy and sensitivity, improving the baseline accuracy of MCTS of 74% accuracy and 69.8% sensitivity. Against an expert opponent, Hybrid MCTS++ reached 86% accuracy and 36.4% sensitivity compared to baseline’s 22% accuracy and 2.5% sensitivity. These results demonstrate that Hybrid MCTS++ markedly enhances search efficiency and predictive metrics without retraining underlying models, offering a simple yet powerful upgrade for model-based reinforcement learning systems overall.

title: Vision-Driven Architecture for Translating, Segmenting and Interpreting Radar-based Satellite Imagery
authors: Naveen Karthik R, Varun Kumar G S, Ilamurgan G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar, or SAR, images allow us to visualize the Earth even when there are clouds or without sufficient lighting. However, its effectiveness is often marred by speckle noise and difficulties in interpreting backscatters. This paper proposes a three-stage deep learning process to automatically transform SAR images into a format that is easily understandable by non-experts. The process involves transforming SAR images into RGB images using a conditional GAN, then applying a semantic segmentation technique to identify specific geographic features, and lastly using an attention-based encoder-decoder model to generate a concise description of a scene. The key is to combine these three techniques into a single process, with each technique acting on the data individually. The process runs on a single GPU device and produces three final products.

title: Extreme Learning Machine Software Defect Prediction Hybrid Model
authors: Ala M. Abu-Samaha, Islam Akram Twaiqat
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid model that utilises supervised machine learning classification to facilitate Software Defect Prediction (SDP) process which aims to discover which parts of a software system may contain defects to provide in advance valuable information on software defects. The model enhances the Software Defects Prediction process performance by using Random Forest (RF) as a feature selector and Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) as a classifier. The model was empirically tested through its application to NASA’s PROMISE software engineering repository datasets. By comparing the results of the proposed model with other relative approaches (Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), Naïve Bayes (NB) algorithms as feature selectors and Decision Tree and Naïve Bayes (NB) as a classifier), the obtained results show an improved performance of the prediction process. RF-based feature selection consistently outperforms SVM and NB-based feature selection in particular for ELM and Decision Tree classifiers. On the other hand, ELM with moderate neuron size (200) offers the best trade-off between complexity and performance.

title: GlucoBuddy: Development of Optical Sensor Integrated Device for Non-Invasive Continuous Glucose Monitoring in Diabetes Management
authors: Audreen Louise A. Sanglitan, Bryant Carlo C. Mama, Shaen Dollykaeh S. Era, Fritzhie Mhae V. Cultura, Dapilaga Hilario Jr, Jonrey V. Rañada
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is a chronic disease marked by high blood glucose (blood sugar). In the Philippines, its implications are a leading cause of illness and death. Monitoring blood glucose uses invasive methods which involve sampling blood. It is accurate and widely used, but it has drawbacks particularly for continuous monitoring such as discomfort, financial burden and potential infection risks. This study addresses the need for a noninvasive continuous glucose monitoring solution through the development of an optical- based system. The system is designed as a wearable armband and through this approach, the study is seeking to deliver a more convenient solution for diabetes management.

title: Smart Healthcare: Real-Time Patient Monitoring and Emergency Response Systems for Smart Cities
authors: Pooja Gupta, Sonal Wadhwa, Sangeeta Chaudhary, Ashima Arya, Abhinav Juneja, Niranjan Devanathan R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The research paper explores the effects of the adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) on healthcare service effectiveness within the NCR Delhi area concentrating on four major determinants, which are system reliability, perceived ease of use, data security and privacy, and real-time responsiveness. The structured questionnaire was used to collect data, and the data was analyzed by the Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). It was shown that the measurement model had good reliability, convergent and discriminant validity, and could be used to analyse the structure. Findings show that data security & privacy are the factors that have the greatest impact on effectiveness of healthcare services, then perceived ease of use, system reliability, and real-time responsiveness. The results indicate that the confidence of patients in having safe data management, as well as convenient and trustworthy platforms are the key to successful IoT-mediated healthcare provision. Although real-time responsiveness will also have a positive effect, its effect is not as significant in this situation. The study can be helpful to healthcare practitioners, technology developers, and policymakers who need to improve the concept of patient-centered care by using efficient, secure, and responsive IoT solutions.

title: Cyber Attacks on Real-Time Internet of Things: A Comparative Analysis of Algorithms using Data-Driven Prognostic Algorithms
authors: Fouad Sleiby, Ahmed Abdou, Feras Abdeen, Mohammad Shakarnah, Mohammad Kouali, Khaled AlQawasmi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The fast proliferation of Real-Time Internet of Things (RT-IoT) systems has brought about critical cybersecurity issues because of the constant creation of large-volume, high-velocity, and heterogeneous network data. Conventional intrusion detection methods do not have the capacity to detect dynamic and sophisticated cyber threats in these dynamic settings. This paper assesses the performance of machine learning algorithms in detecting cyberattacks based on the RT-IoT2022 dataset. An extensive data cleaning pipeline was applied that involved data cleaning, removal of duplicates, outlier identification based on a Local Outlier Factor, label encoding, missing values, and feature scaling through standardization. Three classification models were trained and tested, namely, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression using a 70/30 train-test split, and oversampling to correct the imbalance in the classes. The findings indicate that Random Forest posted the highest performance, with the training and testing accuracy of 99.88 and 99.74, respectively, and outperforms other models in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. These results demonstrate that ensemble learning techniques are effective in intrusion detection and that they are applicable to the creation of scalable and dependable cybersecurity systems in real-time IoT systems.

title: 2026 Aerospace & Electronic Systems Society: Organization and Representatives
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine

title: The Impact of Impurities on Water Trees in XLPE Model Cable Insulation Systems
authors: Hilde Marie Syvertsen, Cedric Lesaint, Hans Helmer Sæternes, Caterina Lesaint Rusu, Sofie Brandtzæg Hårberg, Mari-Ann Einarsrud, Sverre Hvidsten
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Recently, it is reported that vented water trees have likely been initiated due to contaminants causing degradation structures within the bulk of the semiconductive (SC) screen, rather than at the interface with the insulation. The aim of this study is to reproduce the degradation phenomenon, observed as voids and nanostructured paths, to better understand its origin and mechanisms. Using a combination of disc-shaped samples, Rogowski test objects, and aged cables, the research explores how impurities and thermal -and electrical ageing influence the degradation. Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS) is used to assess Na+ diffusion, while breakdown strength testing and water tree analysis quantifies the impact of ageing on dielectric performance. Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and Energy Dispersive X-ray Spectroscopy (EDS) are used at degradation sites, and Thermomechanical Analysis (TMA) evaluates the residual stress and thermal behaviour. Results show that contaminants like NaCl can be a contributing factor to the degradation phenomenon connected to water tree initiation. The findings highlight the complex interplay of chemical, thermal, and electrical factors in the insulation system degradation and underscore the need for refined analytical methods and integrated testing to better predict long-term cable performance.

title: Learning-Assisted Radiation Pattern Optimization for Reconfigurable Antenna Arrays in Dynamic Propagation Environments
authors: Ali Ahmed Alqudaihi, Amaleswari Rajulapati, V.C.Sharmila, A.P. Pushpalatha, Anil Kumar Deepati, N.Dhasarathan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: This study aims to examine the ability of machine-assisted radiation pattern optimization in dynamically changing propagation environments for reconfigurable antennas, and to determine if it could be used to overcome the limitations of the current antenna design paradigm in environments where there is high variability in propagation channels. In environments where the conditions of the propagation channel change rapidly, the antenna designs currently being developed are unable to respond to these changes; therefore, this study implemented an adaptive learning methodology to optimize the radiation pattern of an antenna over time. This learning process utilized a simulated environment that produced randomly varying levels of fading and interference. The antenna optimized its radiation pattern by adapting to observed performance data (i.e., performance data collected) in lieu of relying on accurate models of the channel. Beamforming using a learning-based approach performed significantly better than traditional fixed beamforming techniques with regard to focusing of the beam, reduction of side lobes and maintaining SINR stability. The model also seemed to be able to adapt well to sudden environmental changes. While only synthetic simulations were used in this study (and therefore there was no use of actual experimentally-derived data), the simulated results presented here are both practical and realistic. These findings suggest that learning-based antenna models may be suitable for the development of future adaptive wireless communication systems.

title: GE-DFNet: An Ensemble CNN for a Multi- Modal Framework for Robust Deepfake Detection in Images and Videos
authors: Kondra Megha Nandi Vardhini, V Parthipan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Deepfake technologies have developed rapidly, and now it is possible to manipulate facial expressions, speech, and identity in both photos and videos with a high level of realism, which is very harmful to digital trust and cybersecurity. To address these problems, this paper proposes GE-DFNet, a multi-modal ensemble model that integrates multi-scale Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), lightweight CNN heads (LeNet, ResNet101, EfficientNet), and a meta-learning fusion strategy to learn spatial discrepancies and temporal artifacts across frames. Moreover, the watermarking using Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) guarantees the forensic traceability, whereas the confidence scoring is used to increase the reliability in the real-world implementation. Experimental results on benchmark datasets, FaceForensics++, DFDC, and Celeb-DF, show that GE-DFNet consistently outperforms the state-of-the-art methods, achieving its highest accuracy of 99.1% on the FaceForensics++ dataset. Additional studies on ablation also confirm the role of ensemble fusion and watermarking in enhancing the detection strength. The proposed framework developed an interpretable and scalable solution to multimedia forensics.

title: A Rigorous Mathematical Comparison of Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Fine-Tuning for Domain-Specific Language Model Customization
authors: Raghvendra Kumar Jha, Mamta Arora, Dipali Bansal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Finetuning (FT) are two dominant strategies for adapting large language model (LLMs) to knowledge intensive or domain specific tasks. Several studies have been conducted in recent past for comparing effectiveness of RAG vs FT affecting the performance of LLMs represented as stochastic predictors. PAC-Bayesian theory provides tools to bound the generalization performance of stochastic predictors. However, prior PAC-Bayes research has not explicitly separated the entropy or uncertainty of the retriever from the rest of the model. This paper provides a probabilistic framework comparing FT and RAG, cast retrieval as a latent variable and derive an ELBO, present a risk decomposition for both paradigms and introduce a novel PAC-Bayesian generalization bound that explicitly incorporates retriever entropy. This paper also discusses theoretical implications for sample complexity and gives a synthetic experimental plan to validate the theoretical predictions.

title: Energy-Aware Deep Neural Networks for Resource-Constrained Devices
authors: M. Shamila, Gaurav Pushkarna, Vandana Rawat, Vandana Roy, K Deepashree, Surabhi Shankar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Wearables, IoT sensors, and edge controllers are resource-constrained devices that are highly challenged by the need to deploy deep learning workloads without sufficient computation, energy, or memory capabilities. In the paper, a conceptual model of a dynamic energy-aware deep neural network (EA-DNNs) has been presented to achieve the optimization of inference efficiency, without affecting prediction reliability. Its proposed framework combines a multi-granular adaptive activation mechanism and energy-profiling guided layer selection to make an appearance of real-time adjustment of network depth and sparsity basing on available power to accomplish it. Lightweight Energy State Monitor constantly monitors processing load conditions, dynamic voltage scaling conditions and remaining battery levels and constraints to a Decision-Driven Runtime Optimizer, which optionally deactivates neurons, intermediate representation compression, and opportunistic offloading of computations. The EA-DNNs idea uses the concept of modular deployment of the models, the quantization-aware encoding of features, and the estimation of the energy consumption with the help of the hardware-friendly counters. Through a close integration of neural computation behavior with the understanding of power, the proposed solution can facilitate sustainable, single-computational, and smart inference in future mobile and edge contexts reducing its energy waste without compromising the quality of learning.

title: Optimal and Energy-Efficient Demultiplexer in QCA Nanotechnology
authors: Nandan Vaid, Vijay Kumar Sharma, Neeraj Tripathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Current complementary metal oxide semiconductor (CMOS) technology is falling stiffing scaling through sub threshold leakage, heat dissipation, and poor nanoscale performance. Quantum dot cellular automata (QCA) provide a definitive architecture and breakthrough, leveraging charge-based interactions for the information processing to achieve ultra efficient energy performance, high speed operation and extreme miniaturization. This study presents a compact QCA based demultiplexer with 20 quantum cells, utilizing the charge polarization and coulomb coupling for the precise signal routing. Simulation results demonstrate the superior power delay performance and device area over the existing works, with optimization delivering unmatched area efficiency and reliable operation. Energy dissipation, validated via QCA designer and QCA Pro matches and average of 1.15 meV per cycle according to QCA pro, total energy dissipation is significantly improved compared to the existing designs.

title: AI-Powered Colour Image Retrieval using Hybrid Descriptors
authors: Yuvi Yuvi, Prabhdip Singh, Ruhin Kaur Saini, Sant Kumar Maurya, Shaveta Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The explosive rise in the size of large-scale image repositories has led to more requests to have perceptually meaningful and accurate Content-Based Image Retrieval (CBIR) systems. The traditional CBIR methods that use handcrafted low-level features are affected by semantic gap problem, and deep learning-based methods, even though effective in capturing semantic features, tend to undercut fine-grained visual features including color and texture. To overcome this, a hybrid CBIR framework is suggested that incorporates profound semantic characteristics of a pre- trained Vgg16 with low-level characteristics-Color Auto-Correlogram (color) and Uniform Local Binary Patterns (uniformity of texture). A powerful combination of these features is obtained as the normalized and weighted hybrid of the features. The experiments with the Corel-1k dataset show that this hybrid performance is much more precise in retrieval than retrieval with deep or traditional features alone, and these results highlight the effectiveness of weighted feature fusion for accurate image retrieval.

title: Scalable and secure cloud architectures for IoT-enabled healthcare systems
authors: Ronish Patel, Sudheekar Reddy Pothireddy, Noopur Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The fast implementation of Internet of Things (IoT) in healthcare allowed constantly monitoring patients and accessing information in real-time with the interconnected medical devices and cloud platforms. Nevertheless, the mass distribution of such sensitive physiological data sends shocking information pertaining to data protection, confidentiality, and scalability in the context of IoT networks. This paper proposes a safe cloud-based architecture of IoT-enabled healthcare systems incorporating a lightweight timestamp-based secret key generation (T-SKG) scheme combined with waterwheel plant (EWP) optimization algorithm and block signcryption of secure data transmission to overcome such difficulties. The framework produces secret keys at the patient device and recreates them at the doctor device and removes the actual key exchange and minimizes the risk of key compromise. Block signcryption guarantees data secrecy, integrity and authentication simultaneously and minimal computational cost, whereas the EWP algorithm is the best algorithm to deal with secure data in the cloud system. Wearable sensors and integrated into an Arduino-based patient device are used to collect real-time physiological data, such as ECG, SpO2, and body temperature, and store and retrieve this data in an AWS-based database. The proposed framework is effective in terms of efficient and safe data transmission with a low computation time and high security attack resistance, which can be used to scalable IoT-based healthcare.

title: Enhanced Alpha-Beta Pruning and Evaluation Function Optimization for Pac-Man AI Agents
authors: Yuvraj Deshmukh, Shweta Malwe, Prachi Mehta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced Alpha-Beta Pac-Man agent made for the Berkeley Pac-Man project. The agent employs an improved evaluation function, reactive behavioral rules that enable Pac-Man escape danger or quickly chase scared ghosts, and a memory system that stops Pac-Man from getting stuck going back and forth. Testing was done on two maze layouts, medium and tricky, and compared it with the normal Alpha-Beta agent. The results showed that the agent played more smoothly, kept scores steady, and used far fewer node expansions. Overall, it cut node expansions by about 34% while still keeping good scores. This shows that better decision-making and pruning can greatly improve Pac-Man in fast and changing mazes.

title: Fault Detection in Healthcare: Patient Bed Exit Alert System using IoT
authors: Bhavik Uppal, Parul Hooda, Simarjit Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Elderly patients are likely to get themselves injured easily as they are quite vulnerable to sudden falls from their beds while trying to get up and walk on their own without any super-vision. Under certain circumstances, an individual can remain unattended and also get hurt due to lack of medical support on time. It is a non-negotiable problem and needs to be looked upon to prevent more loss of patient health. An efficient solution is being created that is bed exit alert system capable of generating timely alerts based on the pressure and detected motion nearby the patient’s bed using PIR Sensor and Force Sensitive Resistor Sensor. This is a very simple yet robust approach to detect fall or any kind of suspicious activity around the patient and can help to provide extensive care at the earliest. It can be achieved by leveraging IoT (Internet of Things) and create an environment using sensors and actuators which can handle this situation accordingly.

title: IRASET 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)

title: Lesion-aware Transformer-based Deep Hybrid Model (LTDHM) for Explainable Diabetic Retinopathy Detection
authors: Rashmi Bhardwaj, Ambuj Kumar Agrawal, Ankur Sisodia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: This article summarizes the recent advances in automated diabetic retinopathy (DR) detection using deep learning methods. It elaborates on the promising concepts of lesion-awareness and explainability in contemporary diagnostic systems. Diabetic retinopathy is one of the leading causes of blindness worldwide, making early identification and timely treatment efforts crucial. Deep learning technologies, namely those developed around convolutional neural networks and transformer architectures. Here have recently demonstrated effectiveness in identifying and qualitatively assessing the severity of DR, based on retinal fundus imaging. One recent focus of exploration has entailed combining analysis of local lesion candidates with global context, for more accurate detection of pathology and improved interpretability of models. The various outputs are integrated with attention-based fusion techniques, and a final classification considers DR stages. Explainability techniques (e.g., Grad-CAM) are also used to visually highlight the primary diagnostic features that are most salient to clinically, in an effort to provide professional transparency. Using a benchmark test suite, the performance will be compared of 16 recent DR detection models on several benchmark datasets. The review also provides a comparison of several evaluation metrics, mostly accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity, in tabular form or in table 1, table 2 and table 3. The review explicitly states limitation and challenges with regard to unbalanced datasets, interpretability, and generalizability in clinical settings. Taking all of this together, the review concludes by emphasizing the need to integrate lesion-aware segmentation, hybrid architectures, and XAI in building robust and transparent diabetic retinopathy systems.

title: Explainable Multimodal LLMs for Intelligent Human–Machine Interaction
authors: Kishore Subramanya Hebbar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Intelligent Human-Machine Interaction provides a key basis to upcoming AI systems of the next generation to work in healthcare, industry, autonomous environments, assistive technologies, and immersive digital environments. The Multimodal Large Language Models are predominant in perception, reasoning, and communicating in heterogeneous inputs although the transparency and interpretation are the outstanding issues. This paper introduces an explainable multimodal LLM model which is based on general principles of cognitive reasoning, with modality-specific encoders, common semantic projection, and attention integrated fusion to form coherent multimodal perception. The hierarchical cognitive reasoning graph offers the traceable evolution of decisions, whereas uncertainty estimation and ethical alignment of the future are the requirements of responsible behavior. The framework combines an intrinsic explanation generator which can provide faithful explanations understandable by humans and which does not reveal sensitive internal artifacts. Experimental analysis shows that it provides compelling performance enhancement with 0.98 interaction accuracy, 0.96 multi-modes alignment and 0.93 explanation fidelity with 0.06 uncertainty error and response time of 410ms performance being better than that of Vision-Language Transformer, AV-BERT, MFN, and Neuro-Symbolic models. The framework will bring the multimodal intelligence to the credible, transparent, and ethically viable human-machine cooperation.

title: Agentic AI Security in 6G Networks: A Survey of Emerging Attack Vectors, Vulnerabilities, and Defenses
authors: Hailin Feng, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu, Yuchao Xia, Yongkang Zhao, Zhihao Wen, Jijing Cai, Pronaya Bhattacharya, Kai Fang, Madhusanka Liyanage
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The emergence of autonomous, goal-driven artificial intelligence (AI) agents as core components of future sixth-generation (6G) networks fundamentally reshapes the network security landscape. Unlike earlier AI-assisted systems, these agents operate with a high degree of autonomy, enabling adaptive decision-making that current security models are not designed to handle. In this survey, we examine how agentic AI in 6G can be exploited by attackers and, conversely, how these AI systems themselves become vulnerable.We discuss how malicious actors can leverage autonomous agents to conduct intelligent, evolving attacks across the radio access network, network control functions, and digital twin environments, while also showing how the agents’ own capabilities for reasoning, memory, and tool use introduce new vulnerabilities to poisoning, hijacking, and manipulation. By integrating these two perspectives, we assess emerging defense strategies, identify gaps in existing standards and governance, and outline key research directions toward building trustworthy, AI-native 6G networks. Our goal is to lay a practical groundwork for securing future intelligent networks.

title: Automated Federated Learning: Evaluating Optuna for Global Aggregator Tuning
authors: Yousef M. Jaradat, Ahmad A. Manasrah, Ismael A. Jannoud
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables privacypreserving model training across decentralized devices, yet its performance is severely hampered by ‘client drift’ in Non-IID environments. While global aggregators like FedAvgM improve stability, their hyperparameters are notoriously difficult to tune manually. This paper proposes a lightweight AutoFL framework using Optuna to autonomously optimize server-side parameters. Utilizing a Dirichlet-based data partitioner ($\boldsymbol{\alpha}=\mathbf{0. 1}$) on the MNIST dataset, we demonstrate that a Tree-structured Parzen Estimator (TPE) sampler with Median Pruning can discover optimal configurations that outperform static baselines by 16%. Our results indicate that automated aggregator tuning is essential for robust convergence in heterogeneous federated systems. This research provides a practical roadmap for implementing robust, self-optimizing federated systems in realworld, heterogeneous environments.

title: Dynamic Simulation and DC-link Voltage Regulation of a Grid-Connected Alkaline Water Electrolyzer for Hydrogen Production
authors: Zakaria Ibrahimi, El Ayachi Chater, Najma Laaroussi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In this paper, we provide a comprehensive analysis and simulation study of a grid connected alkaline water electrolyzer fed by means of an AC to DC conversion stage (based on a PWM controlled AC/DC converter) and a regulated DC-link. The overall system is simulated within MATLAB/Simulink, based on Simscape Electrical models which include a three-phase grid model, a detailed switching rectifier model, a DC-link model and a model of an alkaline electrolyzer for hydrogen production assessment. A PLL-PI-PWM controller is used to synchronize the grid side AC converter with the grid voltage and to regulate the DC-link voltage during transient operating conditions. A ramp profile was used as the DC-link reference to reduce the stress associated with starting-up the electrolyzer and a transition from one reference to another was made to test robustness. The simulation results indicate that the DC-link voltage has reached a steady state regulated condition and therefore it can be used as a DC supply to operate safely the alkaline electrolyzer. Moreover, under regulated DC-link conditions, the estimated daily hydrogen production is stabilized at values around 16.5 to $17 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{day}$. Finally, the specific energy consumption per unit volume of hydrogen produced shows a significant increase during the start-up period due to the low level of hydrogen produced initially, but it becomes constant as soon as the hydrogen production starts; in fact, after some time, the hydrogen production stabilizes at a certain rate. Therefore, the overall study confirms the possibility of feeding an alkaline electrolyzer using a grid power source by controlling effectively the DC-link voltage and producing hydrogen consistently even under variable operating conditions.

title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence and Digital Evolution on Employees’ Performance in the Public Sector: The Mediating Role of Organizational Communication
authors: Sahem Nawafleh, Heba Ibraeem Mahmoud Ahmad
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study investigated the effect of artificial intelligence (AI) applications and a digital transformation on the output of the staff in the public healthcare sector in Jordan, and organizational messaging is a bridge between the two. To this end, a survey was carried out on 355 staff and the response was calculated with the help of common statistical tools and structural equation model. The findings showed that the basis of technology, the use of rules and guidelines, as well as the use of digital human resources, have a positive impact on output of workers. It was established that organizational interaction is an important role that mediates the effect of these factors on employee effectiveness. Recommendations of measures that action-takers and administrators could take were also proposed in the investigation. It recommended the creation of clear and flexible laws and guidelines that would guide AI and digital evolution, keeping up with the fast-global changes and protecting patient data. Moreover, the paper has highlighted the need to implement educational programs that will help medical personnel to improve their digital skills, effectively work with modern technology, and enhance excellent communication in the team.

title: Life Cycle Assessment of the Remanufacturing Process for a 15 kW Inverter in Mild-Hybrid Cars
authors: Felix Birk, Manuela Ockel, Oliver Krimmel, Nils Thielen, Jörg Franke, Florian Risch
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Inverters are a key component inside an electric vehicle; however, they are rarely the reason for machine failure and can possibly be reused. This paper evaluates the remanufacturing process of a 15-kW inverter for mild-hybrid vehicles using life cycle assessment. The focus is on the impact category global warming potential based on a 100-year timeframe. The assessment encompasses all flows, starting from logistics and cleaning to the diagnosis of the control unit, while focusing on the German market. The results indicate significantly lower greenhouse gas emissions compared to the production of a new inverter, achieving approximately a 98 % reduction in emissions. In addition, the study highlights that the primary emission drivers are attributable to logistics and energy consumption during cleaning the unit. Remanufacturing and reuse of an inverter is therefore a viable option to reduce the overall CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-impact during production, if transportation is optimized.

title: The Ice Dart : Landing on Steep Ice Using Spiny Feet, Dissipative Landing Gear and Reverse Thrust
authors: Isaac Tunney, John P. Bass, Alexis Lussier Desbiens
publish: IEEE Transactions on Field Robotics
abstract: This paper presents a strategy that enables small multirotors to reliably land on steep and irregular ice surfaces, such as those found on icebergs and glaciers. The proposed system integrates three key components: (1) ice-gripping feet equipped with opposing, passively retractable spines that generate anisotropic shear forces for both uphill- and downhill-facing landings, (2) a friction-based dissipative landing gear that absorbs kinetic energy and increases spine penetration by preventing rebound, and (3) reverse thrust at touchdown to increase normal forces and substantially increase shear forces. A detailed study of ice-spine interaction guided the selection of two distinct sets of spine properties for the downhill and uphill spines, each to meet a distinct function respectively: (1) aggressive braking on steep ice to avoid holes, crevasses, or ice protrusions, and (2) easy spine re-engagement on steep slopes under low normal loads to prevent slipping after landing. A custom quadrotor drone, the Ice Dart, was designed for high power and maneuverability in strong winds, enabling validation of the strategy on real icebergs and glaciers. Laboratory drop tests of the Ice Dart demonstrated successful landings at descent speeds of 1–3 m/s on ice slopes of up to 60°, while confirming the advantages of the specialized feet. Field trials in Iceland demonstrated the performance on diverse ice types, iceberg sizes and shapes, achieving 24 successful landings on icebergs and glaciers, at inclinations of up to 58° and heading offsets of up to 45°, in winds of up to 30 km/h, and achieving a 100% success rate.

title: A Compressive Neural Telemetry With Address-Event- μ -Packet Encoding and Duty-Cycling IR-UWB Transmitter
authors: Jiang Liu, Yuming He, Hua-Peng Liaw, Pietro Russo, Marios Gourdouparis, Xiongchuan Huang, Jie Chen, Yao-Hong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a networked wireless telemetry for 384-sensor-pixel neural data acquisition, designed to support high throughput, low power consumption, and meter-scale communication distance. The proposed event-based Address-Event-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Packet (AE<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>P) encoder with differential binary phase shift keying (DBPSK) modulation addresses the challenging throughput-power trade-off for neural telemetry without compromising transmission range and neural signal fidelity. The encoder compresses data and minimizes protocol overhead as well as memory access. In addition, a duty-cycling impulse-radio ultra-wideband (IR-UWB) transmitter is proposed as a communication link for the event-based encoder. To support the multiaccess operation, a duty-cycling frequency-locked loop (FLL) is adopted to provide fast-settling packet-level frequency control for frequency division multiplexing (FDM). The FLL achieves warm-start lock within <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s with 2.73-MHz rms variation, meeting the frequency accuracy required for DBPSK at a 125-MHz symbol rate. The measured three-access-node spectra comply with Federal Communications Commission (FCC)/ETSI masks in 6–8.5GHz. The neural signal integrity is validated by evaluating the rms error between the reconstruction signals and the original in vivo data captured by a high-pixel-count Neuropixels probe. The presented AE<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>P achieves an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$11.52\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> compression ratio. Fabricated in 65nm CMOS with an active area of 0.12mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, the presented telemetry is capable of transmitting 384 pixels of neural data with low compression error (3.6-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>Vrms), at record-low power of 1mW, and with a 6.6-m air communication range.

title: An End-to-End Neural Network for Multi-TTI MU-MIMO I2V Joint Scheduling and Beamforming with Guaranteed Data Integrity
authors: Jing Xu, Jiaping Xie, Wujie Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Inefficient resource allocation across multiple transmission time intervals (TTIs) often causes transmission disruption and bandwidth waste in vehicular communications. This study investigates the joint user scheduling and beamforming problem over multiple TTIs in a multi-user multiple-input multiple output (MU-MIMO) infrastructure-to-vehicle (I2V) system. We formulate a novel effective sum rate (ESR) maximization problem that explicitly incorporates data integrity alongside total power constraints. To solve this long-term optimization problem, we propose an end-to-end hybrid framework, PPO-CNN, which integrates proximal policy optimization (PPO) with a convolutional neural network (CNN). This framework employs two dedicated networks to optimize downlink user scheduling and beamforming, respectively, thereby circumventing computationally intensive iterative beamforming operations. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed neural network solution achieves a higher ESR while reducing computational complexity by more than an order of magnitude compared with the state of-the-art approach.

title: Investigating Wire Encapsulating Additive Manufacturing for Mechatronic Integrated Devices
authors: Markus Ankenbrand, Tanmay Ulhas Naphe, Finn Gohlke, Kok Siong Siah, Jörg Franke, Florian Risch
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: This paper investigates the use of Wire Encapsulating Additive Manufacturing (WEAM) for the production of Mechatronic Integrated Devices (3D-MID) and analyzes the achievable electrical and mechanical properties. WEAM enables the placement and encapsulation of wires directly onto 3D surfaces. The system uses a specialized tool head that combines a wire feeder with a Fused Filament Fabrication (FFF) extruder. The wire is dispensed directly beneath a stream of molten polymer extruded from the nozzle, allowing it to be fixed and encapsulated in a single step. A wire-cutting mechanism incorporated in the assembly ensures precise segmentation of the wire. The entire assembly is mounted on a rotary unit that dynamically aligns the wire with the toolpath.Material combinations including polylactic acid (PLA) and a polycarbonate–polybutylene terephthalate (PC-PBT) blend for the substrate and insulation, as well as copper and nichrome wires for the conductor, are explored. The capabilities of the WEAM tool head mounted in a CNC system are evaluated using geometrical designs relevant to industrial applications. Conductive traces capable of carrying high current densities over longer distances can be fabricated in much shorter time than with other 3D-MID manufacturing technologies. Use cases such as a heating pad and a resistive bending sensor are successfully demonstrated. Nichrome is used for fabrication with additive manufacturing to print both sensors and heaters, which is generally not possible with other 3D-MID manufacturing technologies. In addition to imparting electrical properties, the embedded wires simultaneously improve the mechanical properties of the substrate material.

title: Toward a Soft Robotic Gripper Integrating High Force Grasping and Dexterous Manipulation: The SOFRo Gripper_supp1-3693142.mp4
authors: Dongjun Shin
publish: N/A
abstract: Developing a robotic hand that integrates high fingertip force, rapid response, and multi-degree-of-freedom (DoF) motion to achieve human hand-like capabilities remains a challenge in the field of robotic hands. This study presents the SOFRo Gripper, a soft, high-force robotic gripper designed to combine these capabilities within a compact pneumatic architecture. The finger module of the SOFRo gripper incorporates synergistically arranged chambers together with a multinode tendon routing strategy that distributes actuation forces, enabling both flexion and abduction/adduction motions while enhancing fingertip force. Furthermore, a Force-Enhanced Pleated (FEP) mechanism was applied to the chambers to increase fingertip force, resulting in a 32.41% improvement over conventional chamber designs. The proposed SOFRo gripper achieves a high fingertip force of 68.76 N and dexterous motion capabilities, enabling a maximum lifting force of 400.0 N and in-hand manipulation. To validate its versatility, extensive experiments were conducted across a wide range of tasks, including object grasping, high-force manipulation (e.g., lifting heavy objects, closing a valve), delicate handling (e.g., grasping tofu, inserting a light bulb), and high-speed manipulation (e.g., spinning a top, catching a ball). These results demonstrate the SOFRo Gripper’s ability to combine high force with versatile soft robotic manipulation.

title: Comparative Study of Imprint Extraction Methods with Vision Transformer–Based Feature Fusion for Pill Image Recognition
authors: Linh Nguyen Thi My, Tham Vo, Vinh Truong Hoang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Pill image recognition is critical for medication safety, yet existing systems struggle with the effective integration of visual and textual information from pill imprints. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of six state-of-the- art Optical Character Recognition (OCR) methods for imprint extraction, including SAR, PaddleOCR, Tesseract, CRNN, RobustScanner, and TrOCR, and investigates the application of Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture for multimodal feature fusion. We systematically evaluate six OCR methods—Tesseract (traditional), CRNN (CNN-RNN), SAR (attention-based), RobustScanner (hybrid attention), PaddleOCR (hybrid architecture), TrOCR (transformer-based)—on the CURE pill dataset using identical experimental protocols to ensure fair comparison despite the dataset’s challenging uncontrolled imaging conditions. RobustScanner achieves highest standalone accuracy (95.80% test accuracy, 92.35% mAP), while PaddleOCR provides optimal accuracy-speed balance (95.66% test accuracy, 92.02% mAP, 3.06 FPS). For multimodal fusion, Vision Transformer significantly outperforms traditional approaches: when combined with PaddleOCR, ViT fusion achieves 95.66% test accuracy and 92.02% test mAP, representing substantial improvements of +3.34pp accuracy and +5.69pp mAP over Simple Sum fusion (92.32% accuracy, 86.33% mAP), and +0.86pp accuracy and +1.37pp mAP over Weighted Sum fusion (94.80% accuracy, 90.65% mAP). Averaging across all six OCR methods with ViT fusion, the framework demonstrates mean test accuracy of 95.18% at 2.30 FPS, validating practical viability for real-world deployment. This study provides the first systematic comparison of modern OCR architectures specifically for pharmaceutical imprint extraction.

title: Remote Control of a Robotic Arm Based on Human Arm Motion Using Flex Sensors and MPU6050
authors: Mohammad A. Alzubaidi, Samar Al-Shboul, Amal Alzoubi, Deema Hindawi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Gesture-based teleoperation enables intuitive control of robotic manipulators in hazardous, repetitive, or distant environments. This paper presents a low-cost wearable system for remote control of a five-degree-of-freedom (5-DOF) robotic arm that mimics human arm motion. The proposed system captures joint bending using three flex sensors mounted on the forefinger, wrist, and elbow, and measures arm orientation using an MPU6050 inertial measurement unit (IMU). Sensor values are processed on an Arduino Nano and transmitted wirelessly via STX882/SRX882 ASK RF modules to the robotic arm side. At the receiver, two Arduino Uno boards are used to avoid timing conflicts between wireless reception and servo control. Experimental validation includes scenario-based pick-and-place trials and quantitative prototype measurements. The system achieved an average end-to-end latency of 120 ms (maximum 220 ms), an average joint tracking error of 6.5° (maximum 15°), a packet loss rate of 1.8%, and a pick-and-place success rate of 86% for light objects up to 150 g. The results demonstrate that responsive motion-mimicking control can be achieved using affordable off-the-shelf components.

title: Dual-Stream Deep Learning for Non-Invasive Anemia Detection from Conjunctival Images
authors: S. Annapoorani, A. Shobanadevi, M. Geetha Jenifel, T. Veeramakali
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The diagnosis of anemia is mostly made at late stages, particularly in an environment characterized by the limited resources since conventional tests are invasive and expensive. To implement this, we are going to suggest a non-invasive scheme with the help of automated analysis of the conjunctival pictures based on the Transfer Learning with ResNet-18, DenseNet-121, and Vision Transformers. Grad-CAM was used to provide explanations as to be seen, which increases interpretability and clinical confidence. The Transfer Learn + ResNet-18 and the Vision Transformer setting were the most successful and had the highest performance, registering an accuracy of 0.84 and a recall of 0.852. Based on this, a two-stream ensemble model that used hierarchical convolutional features with a transformer-based self-attention model yielded both local spatial and global contextual features. Testing on the Eyes-defy-anemia dataset revealed that this combination method was better with standalone models achieving their highest accuracy at 84 percent and an F1-score at 87 percent and showed that Transfer Learning with ResNet-18, and Vision Transformers (ViT). Grad-CAM can be successfully combined to achieve meaningful results in medical indication and diagnosis.

title: The Role of Edge AI in Ultra-Reliable Wireless Networks
authors: Angulakshmi M, Kingshuk Srivastava, Ashutosh Madhukar Kulkarni, Mahesh Soni, Dhananjay Shripad Rakshe, Nagarajan I
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The requirements of the future communication systems namely in the Ultra-Reliable Low Latency Communication (URLLC) pose a constraint of a high degree of dependability and delay less than one milliseconds. Conventional network management systems are not able to achieve these stringent, dynamic Quality of Service (QoS) objectives. The paper has offered an empirical study simulated based on secondary network slicing data to assess the ability of Edge Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to coordinate radio resources to ensure URLLC. The DRL agent attains an improved performance by locating the cognitive control loop to the network edge. Findings indicate that the Edge DRL model can reduce the Delay Violation Rate (DVR) by up to 71.4% and increase the overall composite Reliability Score by 65.07% as compared to the traditional and non-cognitive scheduling techniques. This affirms that Edge AI is the solution needed to satisfy the deterministic URLLC needs of 6G networks.

title: Toward Trustworthy AI in Mental Health: A Hybrid Explainable Ensemble Model for Depression Screening
authors: Richa Datta, Bhupesh Gupta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: All across the globe, depression need to be identified correctly at an early stage for immediate treatment. Many machine learning techniques are black-box models that makes them less dependable and functional in healthcare business even though they seem helpful in for projecting depression. The present study aims to propose a hybrid and wholesome learning methodology for anticipating depression using survey data pertaining to mental health. The study proposed an approach that employs a performance-based weighted integrated technique to regulate and integrate probabilistic outputs from Random Forest, Ada Boost and Logistic Regression classifiers, thus magnifying prediction reliability and robustness. This method uses explainable artificial intelligence tools to help the people in understanding how the model makes decisions. It uses techniques like SHAP to show which factors are most important overall, and LIME to explain individual predictions in a simple way. After many tests, the combined model was more accurate than single models and performed better in measuring how well it can correctly identify depression. The explain ability results also show which factors play a key role in predicting depression. This makes the decision process clearer and more transparent. Overall, the proposed system provides a clear and effective tool to assess mental health risk. It can help doctors screen for depression early and make better decisions.

title: Event-Trace Cohesion Metrics for Live Co-Authoring Streams
authors: Pratik Dhameliya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Collaborative writing platforms generate extensive telemetry data, yet real-time analysis of co-authoring behavior remains challenging. This paper presents an event-trace cohesion framework that transforms high-frequency edit logs into interpretable collaboration metrics through incremental stream processing. The system parses raw editor logs into ElementaryOperations, aggregates them into author-level Operations, and maintains dynamic document structure through Paragraph and SuperParagraph tracking. A multi-stage pipeline computes real-time metrics including participation entropy, synchronous editing ratios, operation-type distributions, and semantic similarity trends using lightweight sentence embeddings. Evaluated on 45 collaborative writing sessions from university students (320,000+ ElementaryOperations), the architecture processes 1,200+ events/second with sub-100ms latency, enabling live dashboard visualization without full-history reprocessing. Key findings demonstrate that semantic similarity slopes correlate with expert-rated collaboration quality (r =0.38), participation entropy shows moderate correlation (r =0.42) with smooth collaboration, and window-based metrics capture transient patterns invisible to full-history analysis. The framework’s parameterized thresholds and incremental design support heterogeneous deployment scenarios across educational technology and collaborative systems. This work contributes an implementation-ready analytics pipeline that converts document telemetry into actionable insights for real-time monitoring, comparative analysis, and temporal trend discovery in co-authoring environments.

title: Recirculation Strategies in Convection Dryers for Enhanced Drying of Water-Based Battery Electrodes
authors: Jonas Mohacsi, Kevin Ly, Philip Scharfer, Wilhelm Schabel
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The global energy transition is driving a rapidly increasing demand for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), thereby necessitating sustainable, efficient, and cost-effective production methods. A critical step in the manufacturing process is the drying of battery electrodes, due to its high energy consumption, significant cost implications, and its crucial influence on product quality. One promising approach to significantly enhance the energy efficiency of convective drying is the recirculation of drying air—i.e., the reintroduction of a portion of the exhaust air into the fresh air stream. This strategy reduces the thermal load on air heaters and is particularly advantageous in the drying of water-based electrodes, where high recirculation rates can be effectively employed. In such cases, air recirculation leads to a notable increase in the humidity of the drying air. Although most conventional drying systems can accommodate air recirculation and the associated energy-saving potential is substantial, the effects of high recirculation rates on the drying behavior of battery electrodes have not yet been systematically investigated. This study presents, for the first time, experimental investigations into the film-side effects of increased air humidity due to high recirculation rates, using water-based graphite anodes as a model system. Elevated humidity in the drying air was found to reduce the drying rate. In the conducted experiments, this reduction was compensated either by increasing the airflow or by raising the air temperature. In both cases, a consistent trend was observed: higher drying air humidity led to increased adhesion forces of the electrode. This behavior suggests a reduction in binder migration at elevated humidity levels, with the increased film temperature identified as the primary contributing factor. These findings highlight the potential of air recirculation not only for improving energy efficiency but also for enhancing electrode quality in the drying of water-based systems.

title: A Novel Explainable Perception AI Approach to Enhance Communication Efficiency and Response Time in Smart City Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Kawtar Jellid, Tomader Mazri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles operating in smart cities must detect surrounding objects and share critical information with nearby vehicles and infrastructure within very short time windows.In dense urban traffic, the overall response time is not only affected by the speed of the perception model, but also by the cost of transmitting large perception outputs through V2V/V2I links. Many perception systems still generate rich but heavy messages, which can increase network load and delay cooperative decisions.This paper introduces a novel XAI-integrated YOLO perception framework designed to improve communication efficiency and reduce end-to-end latency in connected autonomous driving. The proposed pipeline firsts perform realtime object detection using YOLO, then applies an explainability module to highlight the visual evidence supporting each detection. These explanation cues are transformed into a lightweight relevance measure, allowing the system to rank detected objects, retain only the most decisioncritical information, and avoid unnecessary data transmission. By turning explainability into an active filtering mechanism rather than a post-hoc visualization tool, the framework aims to accelerate cooperative perception and improve responsiveness in internet of vehicles scenarios. The paper details the architecture, The implementation workflow, and an evaluation plan to measure latency reduction and communication gains under realistic smart city conditions.

title: Autoencoders versus ML Classifiers for IoT Intrusion Detection: A critical Analysis
authors: Aya Nejari, Abdelali Elmounadi, Abdelmounaim Badaoui, Naoual Berbiche
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The rapid growth of connected devices has made IoT networks more vulnerable to cyber threats. Consequently, intrusion detection has become a key security need, although developing effective solutions for IoT environments remains difficult, due to their constraining nature in terms of massive amounts of data and the heterogeneity of data sources. In this paper, we explore the use of AI-based intrusion detection techniques in IoT networks. We conducted a critical analysis to compare the performance of the supervised and the unsupervised learning approaches. On one hand, the use of Autoencoders as an unsupervised learning method showed a decreasing level of performance in terms of FPR when data is complex, despite their ability to prevent zero-day intrusions. On the other hand, the use of XGBoost as a supervised learning algorithm showed strong capabilities in terms of accuracy and precision in detecting known attacks, but it was less effective when facing new or unknown threats. This work presents a balanced comparison of both approaches and discusses the implementation insights for developing efficient IDS dedicated to real-world IoT environments.

title: Shifting Audits Left: A Framework for Enhancing AI Auditability from an Internal Auditing Lens
authors: Hamza Jamal, Abou Zakaria Faroukhi, Widad Elouataoui, Jihane Gharib, Youssef Gahi, Khalil El-Khatib
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) systems have become integrated into our lives more than ever. However, AI systems do not come without drawbacks. Over recent years, these systems have demonstrated embedded discrimination and bias, which have shaken public trust in these systems. To address these risks, auditing AI systems has emerged as a core process to regain stakeholder trust by identifying AI risks and planning mitigation strategies to address them. Despite its promising impact, this field is still immature, lacks standards and well-defined practices, and practitioners often face substantial challenges in anticipating, detecting, and mitigating harms. To address these challenges, the article highlights the importance of AI systems’ auditability and the major role internal auditing plays in enhancing this auditability. We then present an innovative framework aimed at improving the auditability of AI systems through the process of internal auditing. The proposed framework is intended to serve as an auditability enhancer and enabler to address AI harms before they propagate to the end user.

title: Toward a Soft Robotic Gripper Integrating High Force Grasping and Dexterous Manipulation: The SOFRo Gripper_supp5-3693142.mp4
authors: Dongjun Shin
publish: N/A
abstract: Developing a robotic hand that integrates high fingertip force, rapid response, and multi-degree-of-freedom (DoF) motion to achieve human hand-like capabilities remains a challenge in the field of robotic hands. This study presents the SOFRo Gripper, a soft, high-force robotic gripper designed to combine these capabilities within a compact pneumatic architecture. The finger module of the SOFRo gripper incorporates synergistically arranged chambers together with a multinode tendon routing strategy that distributes actuation forces, enabling both flexion and abduction/adduction motions while enhancing fingertip force. Furthermore, a Force-Enhanced Pleated (FEP) mechanism was applied to the chambers to increase fingertip force, resulting in a 32.41% improvement over conventional chamber designs. The proposed SOFRo gripper achieves a high fingertip force of 68.76 N and dexterous motion capabilities, enabling a maximum lifting force of 400.0 N and in-hand manipulation. To validate its versatility, extensive experiments were conducted across a wide range of tasks, including object grasping, high-force manipulation (e.g., lifting heavy objects, closing a valve), delicate handling (e.g., grasping tofu, inserting a light bulb), and high-speed manipulation (e.g., spinning a top, catching a ball). These results demonstrate the SOFRo Gripper’s ability to combine high force with versatile soft robotic manipulation.

title: Compressing for Compression with the XOR Cache
authors: Zhewen Pan, Joshua San Miguel
publish: IEEE Micro
abstract: Modern processors dedicate a large share of their resources to caching, especially to the last-level cache (LLC). We observe that today’s memory hierarchies contain untapped compression potential in the redundancy created by private caching and inclusion. To exploit it, we introduce XOR Cache, a new LLC architecture based on XOR compression. Instead of storing cache lines as-is, XOR Cache stores the bitwise XOR of line pairs, halving the number of stored lines through a form of inter-line compression. When paired with existing compression schemes, XOR Cache also boosts their intra-line compression ratios: XORing similar lines lowers the entropy of the data before it is compressed. Our evaluation shows that XOR Cache reduces LLC area by 1.93× and power by 1.92×, with only a 2.06% performance overhead compared to a larger uncompressed cache.

title: LLM-Driven Collaborative Agents for Wireless Network Management
authors: Yongchao Zhang, Jia Hu, Geyong Min
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: The management of next-generation wireless networks faces unprecedented difficulties driven by complex heterogeneous architectures, exponential device growth, higher frequency bands vulnerable to blockages, dynamic traffic fluctuations, and ever-evolving service requirements. While Large Language Model (LLM)-driven agentic AI has emerged as a transformative paradigm for autonomous network management, effective collaboration among distributed LLM agents remains a fundamental challenge. Joint training or fine-tuning of multiple LLMs is computationally prohibitive, while message-passing-based coordination incurs substantial communication overhead and privacy concerns. To address these issues, this article presents a decentralized collaboration framework that enables distributed LLM agents to coordinate effectively via reward sharing, where agents share portions of their local rewards with neighbors to encourage cooperative policies that align with system-wide performance goals. Each agent employs deep reinforcement learning to dynamically optimize sharing weights based on local network observations and historical interaction outcomes, without requiring extensive inter-agent communication. Experimental evaluation on distributed edge computing scenarios demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves stable learning and facilitates effective multi-agent collaboration in reducing system costs.

title: Comprehensive Deep Learning Framework for Indoor Localization with BLE RSSI in Dynamic Environments
authors: Yeonjeong Gong, Kiwon Jung, Yeeun Kwon, Taekyung Sung, Minseuk Kim
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) of low-cost and low-power Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), which offers high infrastructure accessibility through the ubiquitous support of mobile devices, has been widely investigated in the field of indoor localization. Although the RSSI fingerprinting-based localization is a promising alternative in Global Positioning System (GPS)-denied environments, its performance in dynamic environments is degraded by signal loss and multipath fading. To address these limitations and enhance localization accuracy, this paper presents a comprehensive localization framework that integrates data interpolation with the Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations (MICE) method, deep learning feature extension using a special Classification (CLS) token-based Transformer fed with Differential Received Signal Strength (DRSS) features, and outlier mitigation via novel filtering. Experimental results on a dynamic tunnel driving dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mean localization error of 2.376 ± 0.194 m, outperforming the 1-d Kalman + Random Forest (RF) baseline (3.206 ± 0.173 m), corresponding to an improvement of approximately 25% under the same evaluation protocol, reducing the error by 0.83 m. These results highlight the effectiveness of the proposed framework for robust BLE-based localization in highly dynamic, GPS-denied environments, particularly in vehicular scenarios.

title: Performance Analysis of Fluid Antenna–Assisted Over-the-Air Federated Learning Under Spatially Correlated Fading
authors: Mohsen Ahmadzadeh, Saeid Pakravan, Wessam Ajib, Ming Zeng, Ghosheh Abed Hodtani, Ji Wang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Fluid antenna (FA) technology has recently emerged as an effective means of exploiting spatial diversity through position-domain reconfigurability. This paper investigates the integration of FA into over-the-air federated learning (OTA-FL) systems with the aim of improving aggregation reliability and user participation under realistic channel conditions. By dynamically selecting antenna positions, FA-equipped users can exploit additional spatial degrees of freedom to realize more favorable channel conditions, thereby increasing the probability of successful contribution to the OTA aggregation process in each communication round. We consider an uplink OTA-FL framework consisting of a single fixed-antenna access point and multiple FAenabled users operating over spatially correlated fading channels. Unlike existing studies that primarily rely on optimization-based designs or numerical evaluations, we develop a tractable analytical framework that enables a rigorous performance characterization of FA-assisted OTA-FL. In particular, closed-form expressions are derived for the aggregation error outage probability and the expected number of participating users per round. Spatial channel correlation across FA ports is modeled using a copula-based approach, where the Clayton copula is adopted to capture lower-tail dependence relevant to worst-case fading conditions. Numerical results validate the analytical findings and demonstrate that FA-assisted OTA-FL significantly outperforms conventional fixed-antenna schemes in terms of aggregation reliability and participation efficiency, while providing insights under practical system considerations.

title: CONDEN-FI: Consistency and Diversity Learning-Based Multi-View Unsupervised Feature and Instance Co-Selection_supp1-3700726.pdf
authors: Tianrui Li
publish: N/A
abstract: The objective of multi-view unsupervised feature and instance co-selection is to simultaneously identify the most representative features and samples from multi-view unlabeled data, which aids in mitigating the curse of dimensionality and reducing instance size to improve the performance of downstream tasks. However, existing methods treat feature selection and instance selection as two separate processes, failing to leverage the potential interactions between the feature and instance spaces. Additionally, previous co-selection methods for multi-view data require concatenating different views, which overlooks the consistent information among them. In this paper, we propose a CONsistency and DivErsity learNing-based multi-view unsupervised Feature and Instance co-selection (CONDEN-FI) to address the above-mentioned issues. Specifically, CONDEN-FI reconstructs multi-view data from both the sample and feature spaces to learn representations that are consistent across views and specific to each view, enabling the simultaneous selection of the most important features and instances. Moreover, CONDEN-FI adaptively learns a view-consensus similarity graph to help select both dissimilar and similar samples in the reconstructed data space, leading to a more diverse selection of instances. An efficient algorithm is developed to solve the resultant optimization problem, and the comprehensive experimental results on real-world datasets demonstrate that CONDEN-FI is effective compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Strategizing At Speed: A Learned Model Predictive Game for Multi-Agent Drone Racing
authors: Andrei-Carlo Papuc, Lasse Peters, Sihao Sun, Laura Ferranti, Javier Alonso-Mora
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Autonomous drone racing pushes the boundaries of high-speed motion planning and multi-agent strategic decision-making. Success in this domain requires drones not only to navigate at their limits but also to anticipate and counteract competitors' actions. In this paper, we study a fundamental question that arises in this domain: how deeply should an agent strategize before taking an action? To this end, we compare two planning paradigms: the Model Predictive Game (MPG), which finds interaction-aware strategies at the expense of longer computation times, and contouring Model Predictive Control (MPC), which computes strategies rapidly but does not reason about interactions. We perform extensive experiments to study this trade-off, revealing that MPG outperforms MPC at moderate velocities but loses its advantage at higher speeds due to latency. To address this shortcoming, we propose a Learned Model Predictive Game (LMPG) approach that amortizes model predictive gameplay to reduce latency. In both simulation and hardware experiments, we benchmark our approach against MPG and MPC in head-to-head races, finding that LMPG outperforms both baselines.
